gmw_00004867.name: J122016.9+112627.09
gmw_00004867.overview: J122016.9+112627.09 is a candidate dual quasar system located at a redshift of z=1.889 [arXiv:2306.00068]. High-resolution imaging resolves the system into two distinct point sources separated by 2.2 kpc, which are reddened by different amounts of dust extinction [arXiv:2306.00068].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J122016.9+112627.09 as W2M J1220, a z=1.889 candidate dual QSO resolved by HST/STIS and VLBA into two reddened components separated by 2.2 kpc.

quotes.quote: One source, J122016.9+112627.09 Hereafter, W2M J1220 , appeared as two closely separated point sources (left and middle panels of Figure fig:discovery ) in both the F105W and F160W filters. The WFC3/IR observations were designed to be identical to those in Glikman15 , with W2M J1220 having exposure times of 797 s and 1597 s and reaching 3 surface brightness limits of 23.67 mag arcsec ^ 2 and 23.87 mag arcsec ^ 2 in F105W and F160W, respectively. From the ground, this source appears as a single object with =18.13 in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) and H=16.01 in 2MASS. This source possesses an optical spectrum in SDSS and was assigned a redshift of z=1.871 (see sec:qso for details on the corrected redshift), shown in the right panel of Figure fig:discovery .

We report the discovery of a dual QSO candidate, separated by 0 26 corresponding to 2.2 kpc at z=1.889 . The sources are confirmed as QSOs with a spatially-resolved STIS spectrum and high-resolution VLBA imaging at 1.4 GHz which reveal two point sources consistent with the positions in the HST images. The two components are reddened by different amounts of dust-extinction. When corrected for this extinction, the properties of the QSOs are similar, including black hole masses 10^ 7.5 M_ and radio loudness of 0.5 (though their Eddington ratios differ).

gmw_00004868.name: NGC5898
gmw_00004868.overview: NGC5898 is an early-type galaxy characterized by a faint, arc-like dust lane [arXiv:0808.0714]. It is associated with the dwarf elliptical companion NGC5898 DW1, which exhibits discy isophotes [arXiv:0903.4393, arXiv:1105.4809], and NGC5898 DW2, which displays weak contemporary star formation [arXiv:0903.4393].

sources.summary: NGC5898 is described as a dust-lane early-type galaxy whose faint arc-like dust lane gives very small V-band extinction, making its extinction curve difficult to determine from short SALT exposures.

quotes.quote: We derived extinction curves and extinction values for eight of our nine sample galaxies, with the exception of NGC5898.

For the case of the faint arc-like dust lane of NGC5898 (see B-I colour index map in Fig. \ fig:BImaps ), the measured V-band extinction per resolution element is rather small with values up to 0.1 mag. In addition, these galaxy images are relatively faint due to short exposure times (see Table t:exposure ) and therefore the extinction is poorly determined.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC5898 DW1 as a dE3 dwarf with little age gradient, flat metallicity, and discy isophotes.

quotes.quote: The extremes are formed by FCC288 (dS0(7),N), FS76 (dE1), FS131 (dE5,N), and NGC5898 DW1 (dE3), which show almost no age gradient, on the one hand, and FCC207 (dE2,N) and FCC046 (dE4,N) which show an age gradient of over 5 Gyr between the centre and 2 , on the other.
NGC5898 DW1 (dE3) and FCC046 (dE4,N) have flat metallicity profiles although they are somewhat rounder objects.

However, like FCC204 and FCC288, NGC5898 DW1 has a positive C_4 coefficient which indicates that it has discy isophotes (deviations of the isophotes from a pure elliptic shape are quantified by expanding the intensity variation along an isophotal ellipse in a fourth order Fourier series; C_4 measures the disciness/boxiness of the isophotes, see e.g. d03 ).

sources.summary: The paper reports weak contemporary star formation, a very young population, and flat outer metallicity in NGC5898 DW2.

quotes.quote: In our sample, three galaxies (FCC46 and 207, NGC5898 DW2) show H _ emission revealing the existence of a weak contemporary star formation activity.
For two galaxies, FCC046 and NGC5898 DW2, where a very young population is detected, we fitted 4 bursts to resolve the lowest age bin.

Flat metallicity profiles The galaxies standing out from the strong metallicity gradient rule are FCC288 (dS0(7)), FCC204 (dS0(6),N), NGC5898 DW2 (dE6), and FS29 (dE5), which, outside of the inner few arcseconds, have essentially flat metallicity profiles out to the last data point.

sources.summary: NGC 5898 DW1 is a dE3 with positive disciness, stronger inner flattening than outer flattening, and dE(di)-type classification.

quotes.quote: NGC 5898 DW1 = PGC7 92714 This dE3 has a positive C _4 d03 . Its flattening is stronger in the inner 10 arcsec ( 0.5) than in the outskirts ( 0.2). In the terminology of lisker2007 it would be classified as a dE(di).

gmw_00004869.name: NGC3283
gmw_00004869.overview: NGC3283 is an early-type galaxy characterized by a boxy de Vaucouleurs profile [arXiv:0808.0714]. It features a dust lane along its major axis and hosts radio jets originating from its center [arXiv:0808.0714].

sources.summary: NGC3283 is identified as a major-axis dust-lane galaxy with noisy extinction plots, a boxy de Vaucouleurs profile, radio jets from its center, and extinction-curve deviations from the Galactic law.

quotes.quote: For the case of NGC3283 and AM1118-290, the B-I colour-index maps show prominent dust lanes along the galaxies major axes. These galaxies show noisy extinction plots in all bands, thus extinction values are difficult to determine using only a linear regression fit, as has been suggested before.

While NGC3283 shows a simple de Vaucouleurs profile with a boxy structure, as also indicated by Bertola et al. \ (1988), the same authors observed radio jets from the galaxy center, implying the presence of an active central source that could produce a significant forward-scattering from the major-axis dust lane. We should note that the derived R_V values for NGC3283 and AM1118-290 are 2.42 and 4.05, respectively. The first may imply that forward scattering, which tends to increase the extinction values, may not be dominant in the case of NGC3283.
An exception is NGC3283, which shows a similar deviation from the Galactic extinction law in both extinction curves.

gmw_00004870.name: AM1118-290
gmw_00004870.overview: AM1118-290 is a disk galaxy that features a dust lane along its major axis [arXiv:0808.0714]. A two-dimensional decomposition analysis of the galaxy reveals a stellar disk, which may cause forward scattering from this dust lane [arXiv:0808.0714].

sources.summary: AM1118-290 has a prominent major-axis dust lane, noisy extinction plots, a stellar disk that may cause forward scattering, and unusually high R_V compared with Galactic extinction values.

quotes.quote: For the case of NGC3283 and AM1118-290, the B-I colour-index maps show prominent dust lanes along the galaxies major axes. These galaxies show noisy extinction plots in all bands, thus extinction values are difficult to determine using only a linear regression fit, as has been suggested before.
A 2D decomposition analysis of AM1118-290 shows a stellar disk that may be responsible for significant forward scattering from the major-axis dust lane.

We also found that NGC3283 and AM1118-290 show noisy A_ A_V plots in most bands, suggesting a possibly complex dust structure or the presence of significant forward scattering. The latter seems more reasonable for AM1118-290 which produces larger R_ values with respect to the Galactic extinction values.

gmw_00004871.name: NGC4370
gmw_00004871.overview: NGC 4370 is an edge-on early-type galaxy [arXiv:0908.3564, arXiv:1505.03150] located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:1709.00457]. It features a dust lane along its equatorial plane [arXiv:0908.3564, arXiv:1505.03150], where the dust is likely distributed in a ring around the galactic center [arXiv:1505.03150].

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC4370 as a Virgo early-type dust-lane galaxy with uncertain morphology, co-rotating gas and stars, and evidence that some gas or dust may be accreted.

quotes.quote: NGC4370 is an early type galaxy member of the Virgo B cloud goldmine forming a non-interacting pair with NGC4365. Its detailed morphological classification is rather uncertain, varying from Sa vcc to elliptical bertola88 . This is mainly due to the presence of a prominent dust lane along the equatorial plane of the galaxy. Although active star-forming regions are not clearly evident from UV and H images, we cannot exclude that we are observing an analogous of the Sombrero galaxy (NGC4594) completely edge-on. Excluding the prominent dust lane, no other evidence for interaction/accretion is found and the gas and stars in the galaxy are co-rotating bertola88 .

They propose that the amount of dust observed in the galaxy can not have been formed in situ and that at least part of the material must have been accreted during an interaction event. An external origin for the atomic hydrogen content in this galaxy is thus not excluded, as observed in other rich dust-lane elliptical galaxies oosterloo02 .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4370 as an edge-on early-type galaxy with a dust lane, finding radiative-transfer dust masses consistent with FIR/sub-mm measurements.

quotes.quote: NGC 4370 is an edge-on, early-type galaxy with a conspicuous dust lane and regular morphology, making it suitable for several (semi-)analytical modelling techniques. We aim to derive the dust mass from both optical and FIR data and to investigate the need to invoke a putative diffuse dust component. We used different methods to determine the total dust mass in the dust lane. We used our exquisite optical data to create colour and attenuation maps, which are converted to approximate dust mass maps based on simple dust geometries.

Dust is more likely to be distributed in a ring around the centre of NGC 4370 as opposed to an exponential disc or a simple foreground screen. Moreover, by using inverse radiative transfer fitting, we are able to constrain most of the parameters that describe these geometries. The resulting dust masses are high enough to account for the dust observed at FIR/sub-mm wavelengths, so that no diffuse dust component needs to be invoked.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4370 as a gas-poor outlier with a very large thin-disc scale height and a thin disc barely distinguishable from the thick disc, possibly heated by Virgo-cluster harassment.

quotes.quote: The two largest scale-heights for a thin disc correspond to gas-poor galaxies, namely NGC 1032 and NGC 4370. The scale-heights of these two galaxies are far too large to be moved close to the linear fit by accounting for v_ uncertainties. This suggests that part of the large scatter seen for gas-poor galaxies in the plots in Sect.  masses and in this subsection might have a physical origin. Visually, the thin disc of NGC 1032 is very diffuse, whereas that of NGC 4370 is barely distinguishable from the thick disc (although the distinction is much clearer in the surface-brightness profile).

This and the fact that the seven most extreme outliers are on the positive side in Fig.  residual suggests that some discs of gas-poor galaxies may have been dynamically heated, a process which is more efficient in gas-poor galaxies MOS10 . NGC 4370 is in the Virgo cluster, so harassment is a viable heating mechanism there. NGC 1032 is isolated TU15 so heating would have been caused by the merger with a companion.

sources.summary: NGC4370 is described as a pure elliptical with a major-axis dust lane, no luminous central body, and an extinction curve very similar to the Galactic law.

quotes.quote: We also note that for the case of NGC4370, another major-axis dust lane elliptical, there were no signs of a luminous central body in our 2D decomposition analysis and although the A_ A_V values measured in various dusty regions produce somewhat noisy plots, we obtain extinction values very similar to the Galactic extinction values.

The major-axis dust lane elliptical NGC4370 shows an extinction curve very similar to that of the Galaxy. While the effect of forward scattering may be important in the case of major-axis dust lane disk galaxies, it does not seem significant for the case of pure ellipticals such as NGC4370. Such a possible effect, as well as that of a more complex dust structure, might reduce the observed total extinction of NGC4370 with respect to its optical depth.

gmw_00004872.name: NGC5799
gmw_00004872.overview: NGC5799 is an early-type galaxy that does not form stars at a very high rate in its interstellar medium torus [arXiv:0808.0714]. The galaxy exhibits an exceptionally low extinction value and contains dust grains that are significantly smaller than those found in the Milky Way [arXiv:0808.0714].

sources.summary: NGC5799 has an exceptionally low R_V extinction value; the paper argues star formation cannot explain it and finds smaller grain sizes than Galactic dust.

quotes.quote: We note that the extinction value derived for NGC5799 has an exceptionally low value of R_V=1.90 0.01 ; this is similar to the lowest extinction value obtained by Patil et al. \ (2007) and Goudfrooij et al. \ (1994).

However, this may not account for the relatively low extinction values of NGC5799, for which Brosch et al. \ (1990) concluded from the relatively low ratio between the blue and 60 m flux densities that it does not form stars at a very high rate in its ISM torus, and therefore star formation should have a negligible effect on its derived extinction values.

For each of the NGC5799 and AM1459-722 galaxies Brosch et al. \ (1989) derived a flat extinction curve in the B V filters, which did not correspond with the Galactic extinction trend or values. It is therefore reassuring to derive extinction curves which run parallel to the Galactic extinction curve, though we find NGC5799 to have significally smaller grain sizes compared with the Galactic dust.

gmw_00004873.name: QSO B2202--209
gmw_00004873.overview: QSO B2202--209 is a radio-quiet quasar with a revised redshift of z=0.532 [arXiv:1611.02306]. It is characterized as X-ray loud with an exceptionally strong optical [O III] emission line, which may be explained by a nearly edge-on disk [arXiv:1611.02306].

sources.summary: The paper revises QSO B2202--209 to z=0.532, measures its black-hole mass and X-ray cutoff/coronal temperature, and interprets its X-ray loudness as a nearly edge-on disk.

quotes.quote: We present an analysis of the joint XMM-Newton and NuSTAR observations of the radio-quiet quasar QSO B2202--209. Using an optical observation from the Hale Telescope at the Palomar Observatory, we revise the redshift of the source from the previously reported z=1.77 to z=0.532 , and we estimate the mass of the central black hole, (M_ BH /M_ ) = 9.08 0.18 . The X-ray spectrum of this source can be well described by a power-law of photon index = 1.82 0.05 with E_ cut = 152_ 54 ^ +103 keV , in the rest frame of the source. Assuming a Comptonisation model, we estimate the coronal temperature to be kT_ e =42 3 keV and kT_ e = 56 3 keV for a spherical and a slab geometry, respectively.

The quasar is X-ray loud, with an unusually flat observed optical-to-X-ray spectral slope _ OX = 1.00 0.02 , and has an exceptionally strong optical [ O iii ] line. Assuming that both the X-ray emission and the [ O iii ] line are isotropic, these two extreme properties can be explained by a nearly edge-on disk, leading to a reduction in the observed UV continuum light.

gmw_00004874.name: II Zw 096
gmw_00004874.overview: II Zw 096 is a merging, luminous infrared galaxy system [arXiv:1004.3543, arXiv:2208.10647] consisting of at least two gas-rich spiral galaxies with well-developed tidal tails [arXiv:1004.3543]. The system's infrared luminosity is dominated by a compact, dust-obscured off-nuclear region [arXiv:1004.3543, arXiv:2208.10647] that hosts a starburst [arXiv:1004.3543, arXiv:1211.1674] and a suspected active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2401.02763, arXiv:2402.17479].

sources.summary: The paper describes II Zw 096 as a z=0.036 merging LIRG whose compact off-nuclear source dominates the infrared luminosity, is starburst-powered, and sits in an interacting, tidal-tail system.

quotes.quote: An analysis of data from the Spitzer Space Telescope, Hubble Space Telescope, Chandra X-ray Observatory, and AKARI Infrared Astronomy Satellite is presented for the z=0.036 merging galaxy system II Zw 096 (CGCG 448-020). Because II Zw 096 has an infrared luminosity of log (L_ IR /L_ )=11.94 , it is classified as a Luminous Infrared Galaxy (LIRG), and was observed as part of the Great Observatories All-sky LIRG Survey (GOALS http://goals.ipac.caltech.edu ).

The Spitzer data suggest that 80 of the total infrared luminosity comes from an extremely compact, red source not associated with the nuclei of the merging galaxies. The Spitzer mid-infrared spectra indicate no high-ionization lines from a buried active galactic nucleus in this source. The strong detection of the 3.3 and 6.2 PAH emission features in the AKARI and Spitzer spectra also implies that the energy source of II Zw 096 is a starburst. Based on Spitzer infrared imaging and AKARI near-infrared spectroscopy, the star formation rate is estimated to be 120 M_ yr^ 1 and > 45 M_ yr^ 1 , respectively.

From its optical morphology, II Zw 096 appears to be a merger of at least two gas-rich spirals. From the optical imaging, classify II Zw 096 as a Class III interacting galaxy, based on the system of , meaning the galaxy has two identifiable nuclei, with well developed tidal tails.

sources.summary: II Zw 096 is presented as a complex interacting luminous infrared galaxy whose D1 source dominates the mid-IR, contains a compact dusty core, and likely hides a dust-obscured AGN.

quotes.quote: II Zw 096 is a complex interacting system and luminous IR galaxy located at a luminosity distance of 159 Mpc (z=0.0362). This system consists of four main near-IR sources (A, B, C and D; e.g. Goldader97 , see also their Fig. nircam_f356 ). Goldader97 classified sources A and B as galactic nuclei, whereas they found that the remaining two sources (C and D) are strongly reddened with prominent IR emission.

Using HST /NICMOS 1.6 m imaging observations, D was resolved into two sources: D0 and D1 ( Inami10 ), with the majority of the total IR luminosity coming from D1. Inami22 demonstrated that D1 is indeed the dominant source of the system in the mid-IR as probed by James Webb Space Telescope and Mid-IR Instrument ( JWST /MIRI). These authors also confirmed that the location of D1 coincides with the OH megamasers detected in II Zw 096 ( Migenes11,Wu22 ).

The dusty structure bolometric luminosity is 6.8 10 ^ 10 L _ and its IR surface brightness is 3.6 10 ^ 8 L _ /pc ^2 , suggesting the presence of a compact and dust-obscured AGN in D1. The exceptionally high covering factor of the dusty structure inhibits the direct detection of AGN emission.

sources.summary: IIZw96 is described as a major merger with dusty star-forming clumps and a buried SW nucleus whose unusual spectrum shows weak PAHs, near-IR lines, and hot star-forming dust.

quotes.quote: Finally, IIZw96 is also a major merger, with a dusty eastern region consisting of numerous compact, dusty star-forming clumps Inami2010, Inami2022 , separate from the two main nuclei of the interacting galaxies. The SW nucleus is thought to host a buried AGN from modelling IR rovibrational lines of the molecular bands Garcia-Bernete2023b .

IIZw96 The spectrum of the SW nuclei of IIZw96 is highly unusual Inami2022, Garcia-Bernete2023b , with very weak PAH features and a plethora of emission features in the near-infrared (< 3 m). Indeed, Shipley2016 noted this object as an outlier in the PAH vs SFR correlation, due to its weak PAH emission as probed by Spitzer. From the dust distribution we find evidence for a cold, buried continuum source, which shows a gradient, which would again suggest a smooth obscuring structure.

The star-forming component is strong, which dilutes this obscured source, filling up the silicate feature. Additionally, the star-forming component is hot, leading to silicate emission at 9.8 m, consistent with the higher molecular gas temperature of 900 K. The 4.6 m CO band appears in emission rather than absorption Garcia-Bernete2023b .

sources.summary: The paper presents IRAS 20550+1656 as a class IIIb starburst merger whose infrared output partly arises outside the nuclei and whose X-ray spectrum suggests alpha enrichment.

quotes.quote: Similarly, IRAS 20550+1656 is a class IIIb merger system. The separation between the optical nuclei is uncertain, but believed to be about 11.^ 7 Arribas04 . From the RBGS fluxes, we calculate luminosities of (L_ FIR / ) = 11.66 and (L_ IR / ) = 11.94 for a redshift of z=0.036, corresponding to a distance of 161 Mpc Armus09 . Optical Baan98 and IR Inami10 spectroscopy and line ratio diagnostics indicate that this system is a starburst. The Spitzer data in particular Inami10 suggests that 80 of the IR luminosity comes from a region outside the nuclei of the two interacting galaxies in this merger.

Our highest quality spectrum of IRAS 20550+1656 suggests super-solar abundance of alpha elements at 2 significance, with _ = [ ] = 0.4 0.2 . This is suggestive of recent enrichment with Type II supernovae, consistent with a starburst environment.

sources.summary: The paper describes CGCG448-020 as a merging LIRG whose infrared output is dominated by a dust-obscured region resolved by JWST into compact mid-infrared clumps.

quotes.quote: ( CGCG448-020 , IRAS20550+1656 ) is a merging, luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG) at z=0.0361 with an infrared (IR) luminosity of L_ IR,8-1000 m =8.7 10^ 11 L_ , one of the more than 200 LIRGs in the Great Observatories All-sky LIRGs Survey GOALS; ArmusL2009 . Previous imaging with the Spitzer Space Telescope revealed that the majority (up to 80 ) of the infrared luminosity of the entire system comes from a region outside of the merging nuclei InamiH2010 , making it an even more extreme analog of the well known Antennae Galaxies MirabelIF1998,BrandlBR2009 . The system consists of regions A, C, and D (Figure fig:MIRI_imgs b) along with a merging spiral galaxy to the northwest GoldaderJD1997 .

Our JWST imaging has revealed the complexity of the dustiest region of the merging galaxy, . The three main components in the perturbed region show a wide variety of optical-infrared colors and morphologies, with a mix of bright, unresolved clumps and diffuse emission. This suggests a range in properties, such as extinction, SFR, age, and dust temperature in this ongoing merger. Although ID 7 (D0) is the brightest source at 1.6 , the JWST mid-IR data demonstrate that ID 8 (D1) generates the bulk of the total IR emission in .

sources.summary: The paper presents IRAS 20550+1656 as a tentative dual OH and water megamaser LIRG/ULIRG merger whose broad masers likely relate to starburst activity.

quotes.quote: IRAS 20550+1656 We tentatively find broad megamasers in the LIRG/ULIRG irregular galaxy IRAS 20550+1656 (II Zw 96; v_ LSR =10837 10 km s ^ 1 1993AJ....105.1271G , D_ L =148 Mpc, z=0.036 ). With Arp 299, IRAS 20550+1656 may be the second galaxy that hosts megamasers of both the OH and H _2 O species. The sensitivity was 5.8 mJy per 0.3 km s ^ 1 channel and 1.5 mJy after 16-channel Gaussian smoothing.

The object is an ongoing merger or close binary system with a peculiar rotation curve that is known to host an OH MM with 26 mJy peak and 83.2 L_ luminosity 1992AIPC..254..617A,klockner . H 1 is seen in emission with a FWHM of about 200 km s ^ 1 2001AA...368...64V . XMM-Newton observations found a single X-ray source and a high abundance of alpha process elements suggestive of starburst activity, but could not rule out an AGN or AGN-starburst composite.

sources.summary: The paper confirms water maser emission from IIZw96, describing it as a complex interacting merger whose obscured starburst clump and dual megamasers resemble Arp 299.

quotes.quote: IIZw96 IIZw96 is a complex merger between at least two galaxies. The OH megamaser is offset from obvious galactic nuclei (Migenes et al. 2012), in a 10^9 M_ reddish clump of gas. Soft X-ray emission and strong IR emission lines (Goldader et al. 1997) suggests this clump is an extra-nuclear, obscured starburst, similar to that found in Arp 299, regions C and C' in T11 (Imani et al. 2010). IIZw96 resembles Arp 299 morphologically: Goldader et al. (1997) observes that both galaxies possess similar projected size (10-15 kpc), and are composed of distinct nuclei which are strongly interacting and are in a similar, very short ( 10 Myr) ``intermediate" stage of merging. Water emission toward IIZw96 was tentatively detected by Wagner (2013) but could not be confirmed above 5 as integration time was only 12 minutes.

sources.summary: The paper describes CGCG 448-020 as a complex interacting system with a dominant obscured off-nuclear starburst and possible AGN-like super-Eddington behavior.

quotes.quote: CGCG 448-020 : This is an interacting system showing a complex morphology. It is still not clear whether there are two or more systems interacting. It hosts an off-nuclear starburst (north-east component in Figure fig:fig1 ) which contributes 80 of the total infrared luminosity of the galaxy at infrared wavelengths (Stierwalt et al. in prep). In the final map (i.e., the one with the highest resolution, not shown in this work), this off-nuclear starburst is still only partially resolved, even at 0 08, while the more extended component (south-west) is resolved out.
Note that CGCG 448-020 is a special case since it shows super-Eddington values independent of the gas fraction, indicating the potential presence of an AGN.

sources.summary: The paper cites II Zw 096 as a luminous merger with two star-cluster age populations, a recent cluster-forming merger event, an approximately 2 Gyr dynamical age, and very young star clusters.

quotes.quote: In another luminous merger, II Zw 096 ( CGCG 448-020 ), there are two populations of clusters with ages of 1-5 Myr and 20-500 Myr Inam10 .

The younger star clusters ( 1-5 Myr) in II Zw 096 are assumed to have been generated at the most recent merger event. While the dynamical age of II Zw 096 is around 2 Gyr, it does host extremely young star clusters.

gmw_00004875.name: J0822+4605
gmw_00004875.overview: J0822+4605 is a compact dwarf galaxy with a high specific star formation rate [arXiv:1806.00551]. Its dense, cluster-like starburst region contains an active population of approximately 1,000 hot O stars within a small radius of 33 parsecs [arXiv:1806.00551].

sources.summary: The paper uses J0822+4605 as a representative compact XMP, estimating its recent-burst mass, high sSFR, active O-star population, small radius, and cluster-like starburst properties.

quotes.quote: Consider the system J0822+4605, a galaxy with stellar mass in its recent burst of M/M_ = 10^ 5.9 0.1 and sSFR 40 18 Gyr^ 1 typical of our sample. Despite its relatively low stellar mass in stars produced recently, this system has had sufficient recent star formation to produce a significant population of hot stars. Under nominal assumptions ( Z/Z_ =0.1 and the fiducial BPASS IMF, which is approximately Kroupa1993 ), the BPASS v2.0 stellar synthesis models Eldridge2017 indicate that this star formation history corresponds to 1000 active O stars. This galaxy has a measured effective radius of 1.3 at 52 Mpc distant (Figure fig:cutouts ), suggesting that this substantial young stellar population occupies a region 33 pc in radius. The bulk properties of the dense burst in J0822+4605 are comparable to individual super star clusters observed in the canonical XMP SBS 0335-052, which is similarly undergoing a particularly intense phase of star formation Reines2008 .

gmw_00004876.name: J0845+0131
gmw_00004876.overview: J0845+0131 is an isolated, compact dwarf galaxy characterized by an extremely low metallicity [arXiv:1806.00551]. It hosts a dominant young stellar population with a low stellar mass and exhibits very strong hydrogen-alpha emission [arXiv:1806.00551].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0845+0131 as a compact, isolated, very low-metallicity XMP with a dominant young stellar population, strong H emission, and low stellar mass.

quotes.quote: Among the 32 confirmed XMPs in our sample, we have identified two new systems in this regime: J0845+0131 at 12+ O/H =7.30 0.13 and J1005+3722 with 7.25 0.22 are compact systems in the realm of the lowest-metallicity galaxies discovered to-date. Though both are characterized by prominent massive star populations, J0845+0131 stands out, with a dominant young stellar population likely formed within the last several Myr and as a result the highest equivalent width H emission in our sample ( 296 63 ). This system is one of only three known at 12+ O/H 7.30 with H equivalent width 300 , the other two being one of the star-forming regions in SBS0335-052E and J0811+4730 Izotov2009,Guseva2017,Izotov2018 .

The lowest-metallicity galaxies in our sample present the faintest absolute magnitudes and lowest stellar masses as well, with J0845+0131 at M_ =-10.8 and 10^ 4.73 0.08 M_ ; and J1005+3722 at M_ =-7.8 and 10^ 3.65 0.13 M_ in recently-formed stars. Adopting large aperture photometry and assuming a higher mass-to-light ratio (Section sec:phot ) still yields very low total stellar masses of 10^ 6.4 and 10^ 4.7 M_ , respectively.

Deeper HSC-SSP photometry allowed us to identify J0845+0131, an isolated and compact system with stellar mass 10^5 M_ at particularly low metallicity 12+ O/H =7.30 0.13 and with very strong nebular line emission (H equivalent width 296 63 ) suggestive of individual super star clusters in SBS 0335-052E e.g.

gmw_00004877.name: J1005+3722
gmw_00004877.overview: J1005+3722 is a compact dwarf galaxy with a very low redshift of z=0.0013 [arXiv:1806.00551]. It is one of the lowest-metallicity galaxies discovered to date, possessing an extremely low total stellar mass [arXiv:1806.00551].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1005+3722 as one of the lowest-metallicity compact XMPs, with very low stellar mass, uncertain low-redshift distance, and potential proximity for resolved stellar studies.

quotes.quote: We note that this uncertainty is most significant for J1005+3722, which is the only galaxy with inferred distance <15 Mpc (at z=0.0014 , corresponding to 2.7 Mpc in our model).

Among the 32 confirmed XMPs in our sample, we have identified two new systems in this regime: J0845+0131 at 12+ O/H =7.30 0.13 and J1005+3722 with 7.25 0.22 are compact systems in the realm of the lowest-metallicity galaxies discovered to-date.

The lowest-metallicity galaxies in our sample present the faintest absolute magnitudes and lowest stellar masses as well, with J0845+0131 at M_ =-10.8 and 10^ 4.73 0.08 M_ ; and J1005+3722 at M_ =-7.8 and 10^ 3.65 0.13 M_ in recently-formed stars. Adopting large aperture photometry and assuming a higher mass-to-light ratio (Section sec:phot ) still yields very low total stellar masses of 10^ 6.4 and 10^ 4.7 M_ , respectively. Due to its very low redshift z=0.0013 , the mass of J1005+3722 is subject to large systematic uncertainties from our adopted local velocity flow model; but even a factor of two underestimate leaves its total stellar mass 10^5 M_ and distance <6 Mpc. This places it as potentially one of the nearest known population of massive stars in this metallicity regime Z/Z_ <0.05 , at a similar distance as the gas-rich XMPs Leo P 2.0 Mpc; Skillman2013 and the Leoncino Dwarf 7--20 Mpc; Hirschauer2016 discovered through surveys.

gmw_00004878.name: AzTEC-ASTE-SXDF1100.001
gmw_00004878.overview: AzTEC-ASTE-SXDF1100.001, also known as Orochi or HXMM.02, is a bright submillimeter galaxy [arXiv:1009.1455, arXiv:2207.06967]. It is a gravitationally lensed, optically dark source at a redshift of approximately 3.4 that exhibits extreme starburst activity [arXiv:1009.1455, arXiv:2207.06967].

sources.summary: The paper treats HXMM.02/Orochi as a Herschel-selected lensed DSFG; its HCN band suffered strong RFI, making the deepest observation less sensitive than expected.

quotes.quote: The three sources selected from the Herschel surveys H-ATLAS survey Negrello2010, Negrello2017 and HerMES Oliver2012, Wardlow2012 are SDP.81, SDP.130, and HXMM.02 (also known as Orochi, Ikarashi2011 ). All three targets have HCN(1--0) line observable in the JVLA K-band, robust lens models based on high-resolution ALMA imaging, and existing CO(1--0) measurements.

The data quality was very good with the exception of HXMM.02. For this source, three out of four observing blocks have strong RFI in the lower sideband (which contains HCN, HCO ^+ and HNC). We mitigated the RFI using Casa 's tfcrop flagging mode, followed by manual flagging in the time domain. With 13:30 hr of observing time, this is the deepest observation in our sample; unfortunately, the resulting sensitivity is much worse than expected.
For HXMM.02, only the upper sideband is used for the continuum imaging.

sources.summary: The paper reports AzTEC-ASTE-SXDF1100.001 as an extremely bright SMG pinpointed by CARMA/SMA, likely a lensed optically dark z~3.4 source with very high apparent infrared luminosity, star formation, and rapid gas consumption.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of an extremely bright ( 37 mJy at 1100 m and 91 mJy at 880 m) submillimeter galaxy (SMG), AzTEC-ASTE-SXDF1100.001 (hereafter referred to as SXDF1100.001 or Orochi), discovered in 1100 m observations of the Subaru/XMM-Newton Deep Field using AzTEC on ASTE. Subsequent CARMA 1300 m and SMA 880 m observations successfully pinpoint the location of Orochi and suggest that it has two components, one extended (FWHM of 4 ^ ) and one compact (unresolved).

Based on the analysis of the derived spectral energy distribution from optical to radio wavelengths of possible counterparts near the SMA/CARMA peak position, we suggest that Orochi is a lensed, optically dark SMG lying at z 3.4 behind a foreground, optically visible (but red) galaxy at z 1.4 . The deduced apparent (i.e., no correction for magnification) infrared luminosity ( L_ IR ) and star formation rate (SFR) are 6 10^ 13 L_ and 11000 M_ yr ^ 1 , respectively, assuming that the L_ IR is dominated by star formation. These values suggest that Orochi will consume its gas reservoir within a short time scale ( 3 10^ 7 yr), which is indeed comparable to those in extreme starbursts like the centres of local ULIRGs.

gmw_00004879.name: ESO 499-G005
gmw_00004879.overview: ESO 499-G005 is a barred spiral galaxy of type SAB(s) with an absolute brightness of -19.7 magnitudes [arXiv:1605.03136]. The galaxy exhibits a metallicity gradient, showing solar-like oxygen abundance near its center that decreases to sub-solar values in its outer regions [arXiv:1605.03136].

sources.summary: ESO 499-G005 is described as a SAB(s) host with MB=-19.7 mag; spectra show a metallicity gradient and sub-solar oxygen abundance near SN 2009mw.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of SN 2009mw, ESO 499-G005 is a type SAB(s) galaxy, with the absolute brightness of M_B=-19.7 mag. The SN is located in the outskirts of the galaxy (see Fig. picture ), the ratio between its deprojected position and r_ 25 is d_ SN /r_ 25 =1.4 . We were able to extract a spectrum of an H ii region close to the position of the SN from the spectrum taken with the du Pont telescope (Sec. sec_spectroscopy ).

As part of a project to determine the metallicity of parent populations of SNe, there were VLT + FORS2 spectrum taken of this galaxy, aiming for another SN it hosted, SN 2008H (Anderson et al., in prep.). SN 2008H was located in the center of the galaxy, that is the position where the slit was placed. We were able to extract spectra at several positions along the slit and used the relations of marino_metallicity to determine the oxygen abundance. The derived metallicity is solar-like close to the center of the galaxy and decreases along the deprojected distance from it. None of our measurements extend as far as the deprojected distance of SN 2009mw, but by extrapolating the gradient we can estimate the oxygen abundance at the distance of SN 2009mw as N2=8.32 0.08 dex, a somewhat sub-solar value (see Fig. Z_grad ).

gmw_00004880.name: HS 1202 + 3538
gmw_00004880.overview: HS 1202 + 3538 is a nitrogen-rich quasar candidate [arXiv:1803.06362]. It exhibits rapid nitrogen-to-carbon abundance-ratio variability and X-ray behavior that are consistent with a possible tidal disruption event [arXiv:1803.06362].

sources.summary: HS 1202 + 3538 is treated as the N-rich quasar candidate whose rapid abundance-ratio variability, archival light curves, and X-ray behavior are consistent with a possible TDE.

quotes.quote: Here we report the discovery of the first N-rich quasar with rapid N/C variability that could be caused by a TDE. Two spectra separated by 1.7 years (rest-frame) show that the / \ intensity ratio decayed by 86 14 (1 ). Optical (rest-frame UV) light-curve and X-ray observations are qualitatively consistent with the TDE hypothesis; though, the time baseline falls short of a definitive proof.

A Chandra observation of HS 1202 + 3538 (observation ID=3070) on 2002 July 21 (MJD=52476) serendipitously caught \ at an off-axis angle of 10.7 arcmin. The observed-frame soft (0.5--2 keV) and hard (2--8 keV) counts in the 6.7 ks (4.5 ks corrected for vignetting) exposure were 16 and 9, respectively Gibson2008 . We derive an unabsorbed X-ray flux (corrected for the Galactic column density) of F_ 0.5-10 keV =2.6 10^ 14 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 for _ X = 4 , or F_ 0.5-10 keV =4.4 10^ 14 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 for _ X = 2 . The X-ray counts are too few for a robust measurement of _ X .

gmw_00004881.name: SF-1
gmw_00004881.overview: SF-1 is a spiral galaxy characterized by extensive ongoing star formation within its disk [arXiv:2310.10719]. The galaxy contains clumpy structures associated with its spiral arms and hosts an active galactic nucleus with intrinsically weak emission lines [arXiv:2310.10719].

sources.summary: SF-1 is a spiral X-ray AGN candidate dominated by disk star formation; MUSE line ratios and SFR indicate intrinsically weak AGN lines rather than aperture dilution.

quotes.quote: Visually, SF-1 is a spiral galaxy (Figure fig:SF-1 ) with extensive ongoing star formation in its disk. Compared to other X-ray AGNs, SF1 has a low L _ X /SFR ratio and mass and a high sSFR (Figures fig:lxsfr and fig:ssfrm ). In the maps of its ionized gas emission (Figure fig:SF-1 ), there are clear clumpy structures associated with spiral arms.

Its measured line ratios in the various apertures (Figure fig:bpts ) are all concentrated around the same region and so the inclusion of extra light in the aperture does not appear to be changing the result. Interestingly, the star formation rate for SF-1 determined from the H emission is consistent ( <0.1 dex difference) with that from the SED, further suggesting there is no hidden AGN component altering the measurement.

In summary, SF-1's AGN lines are intrinsically weak and are not simply overwhelmed by SF. As a matter of fact, if there was no SF, this object would belong to our second category of objects that we explore in this paper and thus scenario (ii) is the most likely of the explanations.

gmw_00004882.name: WL-1
gmw_00004882.overview: WL-1 is an edge-on lenticular galaxy that hosts an X-ray active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2310.10719]. The galaxy exhibits strong optical emission in its central region, though its active galactic nucleus emission lines are intrinsically weak [arXiv:2310.10719].

sources.summary: WL-1 is an edge-on S0 X-ray AGN host whose MUSE data show central optical emission and aperture shifts, but weak SDSS lines are intrinsic rather than host-swamped.

quotes.quote: WL-1 Visually, WL-1 is an edge-on S0. WL-1 has an L _ X /SFR ratio typical of other X-ray AGNs (Figure fig:lxsfr ). Its mass is on the low side, and its mass and sSFR place it on the lower side of the main sequence (Figure fig:ssfrm ).
One can clearly see strong optical emission in its central region. [ OIII ] , [ NII ] , and H are well measured among many spaxels whereas H is poorly measured outside of the central region.

In summary, WL-1 does exhibit aperture effects in the sense that the position on the BPT diagram shifts towards the top of the AGN branch as the aperture is decreased. However, the principal reason for it being classified as weak-lined (low SNR) in SDSS is due to the AGN lines being intrinsically weak, rather than being overpowered by host light. Indeed, the SNR of [ OIII ] does not increase when the aperture is reduced.

gmw_00004883.name: WL-2
gmw_00004883.overview: WL-2 is an early-type galaxy that hosts an X-ray active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2310.10719]. The galaxy exhibits strong optical emission in its central regions, a very low dust content, and an extremely low specific star formation rate [arXiv:2310.10719].

sources.summary: WL-2 is an early-type X-ray AGN host with high LX/SFR and low sSFR; MUSE reveals central emission, while weak [OIII] is not due to aperture dilution.

quotes.quote: WL-2 WL-2 is an early type galaxy. It has an extremely high L _ X /SFR ratio and an extremely low sSFR compared to the average X-ray AGN (Figure fig:lxsfr and Figure fig:ssfrm ) or even compared to a general population of galaxies.

With the MUSE data, one can see that WL-2 possesses strong optical emission in its central regions. [ OIII ] , [ NII ] , and H are well measured among many spaxels but H remains the limiting factor for BPT classification outside of the very central region. In fact, WL-2 could have been classified as an AGN based on log( [ NII ] /H ) alone, being greater than the cutoff of 0.35 .
This excludes the host dilution scenario and again points to intrinsically weak [ OIII ] . In summary, WL-2 has a very low dust content and consequently a very low sSFR.

gmw_00004884.name: WL-3
gmw_00004884.overview: WL-3 is an edge-on lenticular galaxy with a specific star formation rate typical of transitional galaxies [arXiv:2310.10719]. It is characterized by a nearly lineless spectrum, containing little gas and no obvious dust signature [arXiv:2310.10719].

sources.summary: WL-3 is an edge-on S0 with transitional sSFR and nearly lineless spectra; the paper argues its missing lines are not caused by continuum swamping.

quotes.quote: WL-3 WL3 is an edge-on S0 and has a L _ X /SFR typical for X-ray AGNs (Figure fig:lxsfr ). It has sSFR typical of a transitional galaxies, which have some gas and SF, but far below what is typical on the main sequence (Figure fig:lxsfr ). In the SDSS spectrum of WL-3, the BPT emission lines all have measured S/N levels less than 1. It is essentially a lineless galaxy, and the maps in Figure fig:WL-3 demonstrate that there is not a concentrated structure of ionized gas in the center of the galaxy, except for a nuclear patch of [ NII ] emission. Emission lines remain un-detected in WL-3 in the smallest aperture.

To summarize, WL-3 is either not a proper X-ray AGN, or is another example of an AGN that is powerful in the X-rays, but no matter how closely we isolate the nuclear region we do not find any emission lines, thus excluding the host swamping scenario. The host is an early type with little gas and not any obvious dust signature, which again suggests the picture in which the ionizing radiation simply does not have anything to ionize and we are left with a lineless AGN. Although WL-3 has weak lines, it is plausible that it belongs to the [ OIII ] underluminous tail of the [ OIII ] luminosity distribution at its X-ray luminosity, like other XBONGs.

gmw_00004885.name: WL-EXT-1
gmw_00004885.overview: WL-EXT-1 is an elliptical galaxy that exhibits a quiescent specific star formation rate [arXiv:2310.10719]. The system is likely a member of a galaxy cluster, with its extended X-ray emission probably originating from hot intracluster gas rather than an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2310.10719].

sources.summary: WL-EXT-1 is described as an elliptical, quiescent system with extended X-ray emission probably from hot intracluster gas rather than an AGN.

quotes.quote: WL-EXT-1 Visually, WL-EXT-1 looks like an elliptical galaxy. It occupies a region where there are few X-ray AGN in L _ X --SFR diagram (Fig 1). Its sSFR is consistent with being rather quiescent.

It has some emission detected but none of the lines are detected in coincident spaxels so a full classification with the BPT diagram in any spaxel is impossible. In a visual assessment of the aperture spectra, they do not appear to show any genuine emission lines.

It is worth to note here as well that this object is probably a member of a cluster of galaxies at z=0.11 bahar2022 and so the emission is likely associated with the hot gas in the intracluster medium. To summarize, WL-EXT-1 is an elliptical galaxy with X-ray emission probably related to hot gas, not AGN.

gmw_00004886.name: Mrk 205
gmw_00004886.overview: Mrk 205 is a Seyfert 1 galaxy and quasar [arXiv:1904.06476] that lies between the starburst and main-sequence regimes [arXiv:2307.06087]. The active nucleus is powered by a supermassive black hole operating at a low Eddington rate [arXiv:1904.06476], with neutral emission lines arising from a Compton-thick torus [arXiv:1904.06476].

sources.summary: The paper models Mrk 205 as a Seyfert/quasar with Fe K emission, Compton-thick torus reflection, ionized absorption, low Eddington rate, and no firm broad relativistic Fe line detection.

quotes.quote: We have carried out an X-ray spectral analysis of the Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk 205 using the observations from and telescopes. Previous studies by 2001A&A...365L.134R and 2003MNRAS.343.1241P could not affirmatively confirm the presence of broad emission line and the authors proposed that the higher ionization broad line could also be a blend of several higher ionization Fe K emission lines, mimicking a broad feature. In this study, for the first time, using broad band spectra ( 0.5-30 ) from and and physical models such as relxill , we investigate the presence of a broad emission line in the source Mrk 205. The super massive black hole at the center of the galaxy Mrk 205 has a mass 10^ 8.32 1.0 2012MNRAS.422L...1T,2016MNRAS.462..511K . The 2-10 unabsorbed luminosity of Mrk 205 is L_ 2-10 =10^ 43.8 .
Using the bolometric correction factor of =20 2007MNRAS.381.1235V , we find that the bolometric luminosity of the AGN is 10^ 45.10 , which implies an Eddington rate of _ Edd =0.03 .

In Mrk 205 we find that the MYTorus model, which models the narrow emission line simultaneously with the Compton hump (peaking at 20 ) has a best fit reflection column density of 2.15 10^ 24 . This result corroborates the fact that the neutral emission line of Mrk 205 arises from the Compton-thick torus.

sources.summary: The paper says Mrk 205 lacks SDSS and IRAS/Herschel ancillary data, has uncertain CIGALE properties, and lies between starburst and main-sequence regimes.

quotes.quote: For Mrk 205, there is no ancillary data from SDSS therefore we used the photometry from the Hubble Source Catalog https://catalogs.mast.stsci.edu/hsc/ ( Hubble_catalogue ; WFPC2 images with filters F439W, F555W, F814W). The near- and mid-infrared photometry were taken from 2MASS (bands H, J, K _S ; 2MASS ) and WISE (bands W1, W2, W3, W4; WISE ), respectively. Finally, for the far-infrared (FIR), we used Herschel/PACS and SPIRE data when available PACS,SPIRE , otherwise we used IRAS in the 60 and 100 m bands IRAS . For I Zw 1, PG 1211 and PG 1448, the Herschel photometry comes from , while for IRAS 13349 and Mrk 110 we derived the fluxes following the method of . For Mrk 205, we could only used the photometry from Spitzer/MIPS at 70 m as there is no ancillary data from IRAS and Herschel.

We thus conclude that some of the properties derived with CIGALE for IRAS 17020, Mrk 205, Ark 564 and Mrk 1044 may have been under/overestimated, in particular in the case of Mrk 205 for which the predictions of the FUV and FIR fluxes deviate significantly from the SED fitting (Figure cigale-all ). However, with the current data, it is not possible to say whether this is the case or not.
The last galaxy (Mrk 205) lie in between the two regimes.

gmw_00004887.name: HS 1630+2355
gmw_00004887.overview: HS 1630+2355 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2009.11865]. It is identified as a candidate host galaxy for a supermassive black hole binary [arXiv:2009.11865].

sources.summary: The paper highlights HS 1630+2355 as an AGN SMBHB candidate, notes its favorable pulsar alignment, and says IPTA2025 could detect or constrain it if the binary is real.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, Saade2020 identified HS 1630+2355 (also known as FBQS J163302.6+234928) as the only AGN in their sample that could host a SMBHB since its semi-major axis is a 57r_g , making it a tantalizing SMBHB candidate, which we highlight moving forward.

In general we leave the RMS values of the adopted IPTA pulsars unchanged, except for PSR J1640+2224 which is extremely close in sky position to HS 1630+2355 , and the two best timers in this region of the sky, PSR J1909-3744 and PSR J1713+0747.

Using h_ 1 we find that 3C66B, SDSS J164452.71+4307 and HS 1630+2355 should have S/N >3 with IPTA2025 data if they are real binaries Note that the strain calculations for SDSS J164452.71+4307 do not have mass errors, therefore h_0 h_ 1 . If not detected, we can start to constrain their masses, and eventually rule out these AGN as SMBHB host galaxies.

gmw_00004888.name: Mrk 504
gmw_00004888.overview: Mrk 504 is an active galactic nucleus located at a distance of approximately 160 Mpc [arXiv:2009.11865]. It is identified as an optically periodic supermassive black hole binary candidate with an estimated period of 1410 days [arXiv:2009.11865].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 504 as an optically periodic SMBHB candidate, constrains its binary mass using PTA strain limits, and notes its reverberation-mapping potential.

quotes.quote: We show Mrk 504's EM periodicity in Figure Fig:example_LC , along with the photometry extracted from CRTS . It has a 10-yr data baseline, and we find its period to be 1410 days, very close to the Graham2015b value of 1408 days. The data, ending at MJD 56580, could therefore cover about 2 binary orbital periods.

We also compute the strain needed for an S/N=3 detection in Mrk 504's sky position using H19 (based on the NANOGrav 11-yr data) at f_ gw =16.4 nHz (corresponding to an orbital period of 1410), and find this to be h<4.4 10^ 15 . Using the source's distance of 160 Mpc, we can now constrain the chirp mass to be M _c<1.4 10^9 M_ . Assuming an equal mass ratio, q=1 , we can limit to the total mass of the binary to be M<3.3 10^9 M_ . Ho2008 uses the H--I emission line method Greene&Ho2005b to find the BH mass of Mrk 504 (alternative name, PG 1659+294), log (M_ BH /M_ )=6.69 0.5 . Mrk 504 is also in process of reverberation mapping campaign (Lick AGN Monitoring Project) which could yield more accurate mass estimate Pancoast2019 .

gmw_00004889.name: NuSTAR J053449+2126.0
gmw_00004889.overview: NuSTAR J053449+2126.0 is a high-redshift active galactic nucleus that belongs to a recently identified sub-group of quasars [arXiv:2302.08994]. The power supply for this quasar is favored to be an accreting supermassive black hole with a mass of approximately 7e8 solar masses [arXiv:2302.08994].

sources.summary: The paper identifies an optical counterpart to NuSTAR J053449+2126.0, argues it is probably a high-redshift AGN/quasar, and estimates a central black hole mass near 7e8 solar masses.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage In this work, we present the identification of the optical counterpart to the X-ray source NuSTAR J053449+2126.0, which was discovered by 2022ATel15171....1T . To search for an optical counterpart of NuSTAR J053449+2126.0 (J0534 in short), we observed the source with the 1.5-m Telescope (RTT150). Using the B , V , R and I images of J0534, we discovered the optical counterpart of J0534 and determined, based on our spectral analysis, the source distance for the first time. J0534 could be a high-redshift member of an Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN) sub-group recently identified as a new class of quasar. Our analysis favors an accreting black hole of mass 7 10^8 M_ as a power supply for the quasar in J0534.

gmw_00004890.name: MACS0417-z5BBG
gmw_00004890.overview: MACS0417-z5BBG is a gravitationally lensed galaxy located at a redshift of z=5.2 [arXiv:2310.03065]. It is a low-mass system that has experienced a sudden drop in star formation, likely leading to a quiescent phase [arXiv:2310.03065].

sources.summary: The paper treats MACS0417-z5BBG as a z=5.2 lensed low-mass quiescent galaxy whose SED-inferred burst lies far below the supernova quenching threshold.

quotes.quote: Interpreting MACS0417-z5BBG MACS0417-z5BBG is a z=5.2 lensed galaxy observed by ; it has a low stellar mass, M_ =4.3 10^7 . Just as JADES-GS-z7-01-QU, it exhibits a recent sudden SF drop, likely leading to a quiescent phase. Considering the SED fitting from , the SFR is equivalent to a top hat with SFR 1 and t_ b 40 Myr . The estimated halo mass and virial temperature are M_h=10^ 10.5 and T_ vir = 10^ 5.5 K. Its stellar metallicity is rather high, Z= (see Table tab:gal_properties ).

We see that the galaxy falls by a factor 50 below the critical SFR_ min line, implying that SN energy cannot be responsible for the observed SF drop also in this case. Stated differently, SF could be quenched by SNe in this galaxy only if it would experience a 50 burst of SF.

gmw_00004891.name: PKS B2252-089
gmw_00004891.overview: PKS B2252-089 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar hosting an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1511.03067]. Observations of the host galaxy reveal neutral hydrogen absorption with a broad blue wing, which may trace either an inflow of cool gas toward the nucleus or an atomic outflow driven by radio jets [arXiv:1511.03067].

sources.summary: BETA observations of PKS B2252-089 confirm H i absorption previously seen by GBT, including a blue wing; the gas may trace inflow toward the nucleus or jet-driven atomic outflow.

quotes.quote: In Fig. figure:PKS2252-089_full_spectrum we show an example spectrum towards the flat spectrum quasar PKS B2252-089 from an 8.5 h on-source integration with five of the BETA antennas. The source has a continuum flux density of 1 Jy at 800 MHz. This example spectrum demonstrates the power of the telescope for detecting H i gas in absorption, with no strong radio frequency interference evident in this band. The absorption feature originally detected with the Green Bank Telescope ( Curran:2011a ) and associated with the host galaxy of the radio source is clearly evident at a frequency of 883.6 MHz. The striking agreement between the BETA and GBT spectra can be seen in Fig. figure:PKS2252-089_comparison_spectrum , with the former spectrum confirming the broad wing seen bluewards of the peak absorption.

The H i is redshifted by 200 km s ^ 1 with respect to the optical [ O III ] λλ4959, 5007 emission lines ( Drinkwater:1997 ), which might be indicative of an inflow of cool neutral gas towards the nucleus along the line-of-sight. Alternatively if the peak absorption against the compact radio source is a more accurate indicator of the AGN redshift, then the blue wing would suggest that the radio jets in this source ( Liu:2002 and references therein) could be driving the atomic gas in an outflow.

gmw_00004892.name: UDS10275
gmw_00004892.overview: UDS10275 is a faint active galactic nucleus located at a high redshift of approximately 4.1 [arXiv:1802.01953]. Spectroscopic observations of the galaxy have successfully detected flux in its Lyman continuum region [arXiv:1802.01953].

sources.summary: The paper adds UDS10275 as a serendipitous z 4.1 faint AGN, derives its spectroscopic redshift from Magellan data, and detects LyC-region flux in a two-hour LDSS3 exposure.

quotes.quote: In addition to the 16 known AGN described above, we have found a serendipitous faint AGN at z 4.1 (UDS10275) during a spectroscopic pilot project with the Magellan-II Clay telescope. Since this object is relatively bright ( I=22.27 ) and the available observations cover the wavelength region where LyC is expected, we decided to include this AGN in our final sample.
The spectroscopic redshift for UDS10275 has been derived by the Magellan data described in this paper.

In September 2017 a relatively faint AGN (UDS10275, I 22.3 ) at z 4.1 has been serendipitously observed with the VPH-All grism and the LDSS3 instrument mounted at the Magellan-II Clay 6.5m telescope. The sensitivity of this grism peaks at =7000 and covers the wavelength range between 4200 and 10000 . While the sensitivity in the blue is slightly reduced w..t. the VPH-Blue grism, it allows to cover a relatively larger spectral window, which is useful for the characterization of the properties of this target. With an exposure time of approximately 2 hours we were able to detect flux in the LyC region, as we will discuss in detail in the following sections.

gmw_00004893.name: COSMOS1710
gmw_00004893.overview: COSMOS1710 is an active galactic nucleus that was observed using multi-object spectroscopy [arXiv:1802.01953]. Although previously cataloged at a redshift of z=3.567, deep FORS2 observations revised its spectroscopic redshift to z=1.547 [arXiv:1802.01953].

sources.summary: The paper says COSMOS1710 was observed with the COSMOS MXU targets but its published z=3.567 redshift was revised to z=1.547, excluding it from the final LyC sample.

quotes.quote: Three targets (SDSS04, SDSS20, SDSS27) were observed in long slit configuration, while the remaining AGNs (COSMOS955, COSMOS1311, COSMOS1710, COSMOS1782) were observed with multi object spectroscopy (MXU): in the COSMOS pointings, indeed, there are also many X-ray sources from Marchesi et al. (2016) and Civano et al. (2016) without spectroscopic identification. Given the legacy value of these targets, we carried out MXU observations in order to measure the redshifts of these fillers.

COSMOS1710 had a spectroscopic redshift z_ orig =3.567 from the work by Marchesi et al. (2016) and Civano et al. (2016), but from our deep FORS2 spectrum we derive an updated spectroscopic redshift of z=1.547 (see Fig. cosmos1710 ). This object is not included in our final sample where we have measured the LyC escape fraction for AGN at z 3.6 .

gmw_00004894.name: COSMOS1311
gmw_00004894.overview: COSMOS1311 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1802.01953]. High-quality spectra of the source have allowed researchers to refine its spectroscopic redshift by approximately 0.019 [arXiv:1802.01953].

sources.summary: The paper uses good spectra of COSMOS1311 to refine its spectroscopic redshift by z 0.019 and argues this correction does not bias the measured LyC escape fraction.

quotes.quote: Only for the AGNs COSMOS1311 and SDSS04 the spectroscopic redshifts have been revised by small amounts, z 0.019 and 0.004 respectively.
In our case, only for two AGNs (COSMOS1311 and SDSS04) we have good quality spectra which allow us to refine the spectroscopic redshifts.

The shift in redshift applied to COSMOS1311 and SDSS04 is not affecting our LyC f_ esc estimate. To avoid mismatch for the position of the LyC region, we decided to measure the escape fraction relatively far from the LyC break, i.e. between 892 and 905 rest frame. For example, considering the z 0.019 correction for COSMOS1311, it will move the 905 rest frame wavelength to 908 .

gmw_00004895.name: SDSS04
gmw_00004895.overview: SDSS04 is an active galactic nucleus that was observed in a long-slit configuration [arXiv:1802.01953]. Its spectroscopic redshift was revised by a small amount of approximately 0.004, which does not affect its Lyman continuum escape fraction estimate [arXiv:1802.01953].

sources.summary: SDSS04 was observed in long-slit mode; the paper revises its spectroscopic redshift by z 0.004 and states that this shift does not affect the LyC escape-fraction estimate.

quotes.quote: Three targets (SDSS04, SDSS20, SDSS27) were observed in long slit configuration, while the remaining AGNs (COSMOS955, COSMOS1311, COSMOS1710, COSMOS1782) were observed with multi object spectroscopy (MXU): in the COSMOS pointings, indeed, there are also many X-ray sources from Marchesi et al. (2016) and Civano et al. (2016) without spectroscopic identification.
Only for the AGNs COSMOS1311 and SDSS04 the spectroscopic redshifts have been revised by small amounts, z 0.019 and 0.004 respectively.
The shift in redshift applied to COSMOS1311 and SDSS04 is not affecting our LyC f_ esc estimate.

gmw_00004896.name: SDSS J004300.63--091346.3
gmw_00004896.overview: SDSS J004300.63--091346.3, also known as RAD12, is a red elliptical galaxy at a redshift of 0.07628 [arXiv:2210.06100]. It hosts a one-sided radio jet that interacts with a larger companion galaxy, terminating to form a mushroom-shaped radio bubble [arXiv:2303.06416, arXiv:2210.06100].

sources.summary: The paper presents RAD12 as a citizen-science-discovered low-excitation radio galaxy whose one-sided jet appears stopped by a larger companion, producing a mushroom-shaped radio bubble.

quotes.quote: RAD12, the RAD@home citizen science discovery with GMRT being presented here, is not only a new system being added to nearly half a dozen rare cases known so far but also the first case where the neighbouring galaxy is not a minor/dwarf companion but a galaxy bigger than the host of the radio jet. Additionally, the jet appears to be one-sided and the jet after interaction completely stops and forms a bubble inflating laterally which is unlike previous cases of minor deviation or loss of collimation. Since the nature of radio jet-ISM coupling is poorly understood so far, more discovery of objects like RAD12 will be important to the understanding of galaxy evolution through merger and AGN feedback.

Here, we briefly report one extraordinary case, named RAD12, where radio jet outflow is seen interacting with the neighbouring galaxy in a possible major dry merger of two early-type galaxies. This was initially discovered by the RAD@home citizen science research collaboratory Hota2014 and then followed up with the upgraded Giant Meterwave Radio Telescope (GMRT).

GMRT detected the core-plume structure of the FIRST and resolved the NVSS blob to be a mushroom-shaped radio bubble. The tail of radio emission does not extend further to the west but shows a termination peak, supporting obstruction by the companion galaxy. The accompanying deep optical image (Figure. \ fig:color b) from the legacy https://www.legacysurvey.org/acknowledgment/ survey clearly shows multiple stellar shells in the western companion galaxy. In order to understand the overall radio spectral nature of RAD12, we have plotted the integrated flux densities of RAD12 with the available archival data ranging from 150 MHz to 5000 MHz as seen in Figure. \ fig:SIFIT . It is clear that the least square fit to the whole spectrum gives a spectral index of 0.59.

sources.summary: The eastern galaxy SDSS J004300.63--091346.3 is identified as RAD12's host: its nucleus coincides with the radio core, has redshift 0.07628, and has red elliptical colors.

quotes.quote: While the radio core coincide with the nucleus of the eastern galaxy (SDSS J004300.63--091346.3) hereafter host galaxy, a faint point radio source can be identified to coincide with the larger and brighter western galaxy (SDSS J004254.69--091349.4) hereafter companion galaxy. The spectral index of this point source from GMRT and MeerKAT data is -0.5 (see Fig. \ fig:ALLIMS ) confirming its association with the nucleus of the companion. The spectroscopic redshift of the host and companion galaxies are 0.07628 0.00001 and 0.07618 0.00001, respectively.

The u and band SDSS magnitudes of the host galaxy are u (18.21 0.03) and (15.36 0.00), and that for the brighter ( 1 mag.) companion galaxy are u (17.23 0.03), (14.22 0.00). Thus, the u- colours, 2.85 and 3.01, put both of them in the typical red and dead elliptical galaxy category.

gmw_00004897.name: SDSS J004254.69--091349.4
gmw_00004897.overview: SDSS J004254.69--091349.4 is a gas-poor elliptical companion galaxy involved in a major merger [arXiv:2210.06100]. It is larger and brighter than its merging partner and is currently being hit by a radio jet [arXiv:2210.06100].

sources.summary: The western companion SDSS J004254.69--091349.4 is larger and brighter, shares RAD12's redshift, has red elliptical colors, and appears to be the gas-poor galaxy hit by the radio stem.

quotes.quote: While the radio core coincide with the nucleus of the eastern galaxy (SDSS J004300.63--091346.3) hereafter host galaxy, a faint point radio source can be identified to coincide with the larger and brighter western galaxy (SDSS J004254.69--091349.4) hereafter companion galaxy. The spectral index of this point source from GMRT and MeerKAT data is -0.5 (see Fig. \ fig:ALLIMS ) confirming its association with the nucleus of the companion. The spectroscopic redshift of the host and companion galaxies are 0.07628 0.00001 and 0.07618 0.00001, respectively.

The radio-optical overlay as seen in Fig. \ fig:cfht , the stem seems to terminate, with steep gradient of radio contours, in a region touching (in projection) the outer boundary of the companion galaxy. Furthermore, the terminal peak has north-south orientation unlike the middle peak on the stem. Thus, the radio mushroom seem to inflate to both north and south direction relatively easily after getting obstructed possibly by the gaseous halo or stellar shells (Fig. \ fig:ALLIMS and Fig. \ fig:cfht ) of the companion galaxy or the gas in the orbital plane of the galaxy merger.
This is possibly the first clear case of a major merger, where the companion galaxy, which is being hit by the jet, is a gas-poor bigger and brighter elliptical galaxy.

gmw_00004898.name: NGC 5322
gmw_00004898.overview: NGC 5322 is an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1801.03660, arXiv:2206.14734] classified as a slow rotator with a depleted stellar core [arXiv:1801.03660, arXiv:1802.00014]. It hosts a counter-rotating inner stellar disc [arXiv:1801.03660, arXiv:1802.00014], an edge-on nuclear dust disc [arXiv:1801.03660], and symmetric, low-power radio jets [arXiv:1801.03660, arXiv:astro-ph/0611858].

sources.summary: The paper links NGC 5322's depleted core, counter-rotating disc, dust disc, AGN, and low-power radio jets to dry mergers followed by gas-rich satellite cannibalism.

quotes.quote: We have analysed a new high-resolution e-MERLIN 1.5 GHz radio continuum map together with HST and SDSS imaging of NGC 5322, an elliptical galaxy hosting radio jets, aiming to understand the galaxy's central structure and its connection to the nuclear activity. We decomposed the composite HST + SDSS surface brightness profile of the galaxy into an inner stellar disc, a spheroid, and an outer stellar halo. Past works showed that this embedded disc counter-rotates rapidly with respect to the spheroid. The HST images reveal an edge-on nuclear dust disc across the centre, aligned along the major-axis of the galaxy and nearly perpendicular to the radio jets.

After careful masking of this dust disc, we find a central stellar mass deficit M_ def in the spheroid, scoured by SMBH binaries with final mass M_ BH such that M_ def /M_ BH 1.3 - 3.4 . We propose a three-phase formation scenario for NGC 5322 where a few ( 2-7 ) `dry' major mergers involving SMBHs built the spheroid with a depleted core. The cannibalism of a gas-rich satellite subsequently creates the faint counter-rotating disc and funnels gaseous material directly onto the AGN, powering the radio core with a brightness temperature of T_ B,core 4.5 10^ 7 K and the low-power radio jets ( P_ jets 7.04 10^ 20 W Hz ^ 1 ) which extend 1.6 kpc. The outer halo can later grow via minor mergers and the accretion of tidal debris.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5322 as a slow rotator with a kinematically distinct component whose inner and outer components counter-rotate while remaining aligned with the photometric axis.

quotes.quote: We find kinematically distinct components (KDC) in NGC 507 and NGC 5322. Both are slow rotators with k_1^ max 50 km s ^ 1 and _e below 0.1. The KDC of NGC 507 was reported in our earlier paper Vealeetal2017a . The KDC of NGC 5322 was reported in Bender1988 based on long-slit observations and was observed as part of the ATLAS ^ 3D survey krajnovicetal2011 .

For the KDC in NGC 5322, Fig. fig:5322 shows that the inner kinematic component extends up to radius 10'' and is rotating at k_1 40 km s ^ 1 around a median PA _ kin of 100 . The outer kinematic component is counter-rotating with a lower speed in almost exactly the opposite direction (median PA _ kin of 270 ) as the inner component. Similar features for the inner 35'' were also reported in krajnovicetal2011 . The rotation axis for both components is aligned with the photometric axis (PA _ phot =92 from NSA) and the misalignment angle is =4.5 , so this galaxy is classified as aligned. Our HST WFC3 data for NGC 5322 shows a constant PA _ phot of identical value as the NSA out to R 70'' Goullaudetal2018 .

sources.summary: The paper reports faint, 350 kpc FR I radio jets in NGC 5322, arguing the low-luminosity elliptical is unusually large because of weak entrainment in a low-density environment.

quotes.quote: The discovery of faint FR I radio jets in the nearby elliptical galaxy NGC 5322 is reported here using 144 MHz LOFAR image. The jets have an angular extent of 40 arcmin or a projected physical extent of 350 kpc. The faint jets remain well collimated and disappear in the intergalactic medium, without any visible hotspot or radio lobes. The jets detected up to 20 kpc extent at higher frequencies are relatively bright within the optical extent of the galaxy but become faint abruptly outside, where detection is made only in the LOFAR image.

The 144 MHz radio luminosity of the faint jets outside the optical extent is estimated to be 7.1( 2.0) 10^ 21 W Hz ^ 1 . The extent of the jets for its radio luminosity is abnormally large when compared to the general population of radio galaxies. It makes NGC 5322 a member of a rare population of radio galaxies, previously not detected in other radio surveys. A combined effect of stellar core depletion and low-density environment around the jets, inferred from previous studies in other wave-bands, resulting into weak entrainment of surrounding material to the jets could be responsible for its large size despite a low radio luminosity.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5322 as a bright E3 central galaxy with symmetric radio jets, compact asymmetric X-ray emission, and possible entropy/pressure substructure.

quotes.quote: NGC 5322, z=0.0065 The central galaxy NGC 5322 is a bright, E3 galaxy (de Vaucouleurs et al. 1991). Early radio observations show that this galaxy is a weak radio source (Feretti Giovannini 1980; Hummel 1980) with symmetric jets emanating from an unresolved core located at the optical center (Feretti et al. 1984). The bright part of the X-ray emission is rather compact ( 1^ ), peaked on NGC 5322, and extends only to the south of the galaxy. There is a dip in the entropy map 1.2 arcminutes to the south-west from the center with no optical counterpart in DSS2 images. Insufficient quality of the data only allowed us to select 6 regions for the spectral analysis, precluding us from confirming the entropy dip. The enhancement in the pressure 3.7^ to the south-east of NGC 5322 is only marginally seen in the spectral analysis.

gmw_00004899.name: Mkn 104
gmw_00004899.overview: Mkn 104 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1012.4540] that contains three distinct, bright star-forming regions [arXiv:0903.0793]. It exhibits a distorted velocity field, an extended gas envelope, and a separate northern cloud, suggesting the galaxy has experienced tidal forces [arXiv:1012.4540].

sources.summary: Mkn 104 is studied photometrically and spectroscopically; it has three bright star-forming regions, a 0.02 solar-mass-per-year peak star-formation rate, and a central 500 Myr underlying stellar population.

quotes.quote: Mkn 104 is found to contain three distinct bright star forming regions, whereas I Zw 97 is found to contain three bright and two faint star forming regions. Medium resolution spectra of three bright H ii regions in the two galaxies were obtained. Estimation of oxygen abundance in these regions yields a value equal to log(O/H)+12 = 8.5 ( Z=Z_ /2.7 ). Star formation rates in these star forming regions are estimated. The highest star formation rate for I Zw 97 is found to be 0.04 M_ yr ^ 1 and for Mkn 104, it is 0.02 M_ yr ^ 1 .

A spectrum of the central region of Mkn 104 gives a hint about the underlying old stellar population. The age of this underlying population using the U-B vs V-I two colour diagram is estimated to be 500 Myr.

sources.summary: The paper maps Mrk 104 as a BCD with extended gas, a distorted velocity field, a northern cloud, and evidence suggesting it has experienced tidal forces.

quotes.quote: Mrk 104 : Figure fig:mrk104_r (a) and (b) show the column density and velocity field of Mrk 104. The extent ( D_ H i , diameter of upto a column density of 5 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 ) is about 2.7 times the optical size ( D_ 25 , diameter at 25 mag arcsec ^ 2 ) of the galaxy. A small offset is noticed in the kinematic centre with respect to the optical centre. The mass is estimated to be 2.2 10^8 M_ (refer Table tab:obs_hi ) which is within a factor of 2 of the mass estimated from single dish measurements.
A separate cloud-like entity is detected to the north of Mrk 104. The galaxy has a distorted velocity field. All of the above seem to suggest that Mrk 104 has experienced tidal forces.

gmw_00004900.name: I Zw 97
gmw_00004900.overview: I Zw 97 is a cometary blue compact dwarf galaxy with an underlying dwarf irregular structure [arXiv:0903.0793]. The system contains five distinct star-forming regions [arXiv:0903.0793] and has been detected in the radio band at 610 MHz [arXiv:1012.4540].

sources.summary: I Zw 97 is treated as a cometary blue compact dwarf with multiple star-forming regions, peak star formation of 0.04 solar masses per year, and episodic star formation atop older populations.

quotes.quote: Mkn 104 is found to contain three distinct bright star forming regions, whereas I Zw 97 is found to contain three bright and two faint star forming regions. Medium resolution spectra of three bright H ii regions in the two galaxies were obtained. Estimation of oxygen abundance in these regions yields a value equal to log(O/H)+12 = 8.5 ( Z=Z_ /2.7 ). Star formation rates in these star forming regions are estimated. The highest star formation rate for I Zw 97 is found to be 0.04 M_ yr ^ 1 and for Mkn 104, it is 0.02 M_ yr ^ 1 .

To conclude, both of these galaxies are not young systems; instead they are undergoing episodic star formation superposed on a faint older component. I Zw 97 is a cometary blue compact dwarf galaxy where the underlying low surface brightness (LSB) galaxy is a dwarf irregular observed during a major stochastic enhancement of its otherwise moderate star formation activity, a phenomenon widely accepted as self propagating stochastic star formation. Both these galaxies are very similar in their stellar content, showing an older 4 Gyr population, an intermediate 500 Myr population and the current burst of star formation of age 5-13 Myr.

sources.summary: The paper reports the first radio-band detection of I Zw 97, finds it only at 610 MHz, and derives star-formation rates from FIR, 610 MHz, and H fluxes.

quotes.quote: I Zw 97 : We do not detect the galaxy at 240 MHz upto a 5 limit of 7.5 mJy on the flux density of the galaxy. We detect radio emission from this galaxy at 610 MHz (see Figure fig:izw97_r (a)) with an integrated flux density of about 1.14 mJy. This is the first detection of this galaxy in radio bands to the best of our knowledge. We note that it has not been detected in the NVSS condon98 and FIRST beck95 surveys at 1.4 GHz.

I Zw 97: The galaxy, I Zw 97 is detected only at 610 MHz and is undetected in any other frequency. Due to this, the modelling of the spectra could not be done. The SFR calculated using the FIR luminosity is 0.17 0.05 (Table tab:fir_rad ) and using 610 MHz flux 0.023 0.023 . Using the H flux quoted in , we find the SFR is 0.001-0.03 \ if we consider the production rate of stars of masses greater than 10 M_ .

gmw_00004901.name: J1439B
gmw_00004901.overview: J1439B is a massive active galactic nucleus (AGN) host galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.4189 [arXiv:1703.03807]. It exhibits a star formation rate that is a factor of nine lower than typical galaxies of similar mass and redshift, with broad circumgalactic medium absorption suggesting gas ejected by AGN feedback [arXiv:1703.03807].

sources.summary: J1439B is presented as a massive z=2.4189 AGN host near a metal-rich sub-DLA, with unusually low star formation and broad CGM absorption interpreted as gas likely ejected by AGN feedback.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a massive (M/M_ )=10.74^ +0.18 _ 0.16 galaxy at the same redshift as a carbon-monoxide-bearing sub-damped Lyman absorber (sub-DLA) seen in the spectrum of QSO J1439+1117. The galaxy, J1439B, is located 4 7 from the QSO sightline, a projected distance of 38 physical kpc at z=2.4189 , and exhibits broad optical emission lines ( _ [ O III ] =303 12 ) with ratios characteristic of excitation by an active galactic nucleus (AGN). The galaxy has a factor of 9 lower star formation than is typical of star-forming galaxies of the same mass and redshift.

Metallic absorption within the circumgalactic medium of the sub-DLA and J1439B is spread over a velocity range v > 1000 , suggesting an energetic origin. We explore the possibility that a different galaxy could be responsible for the rare absorber, and conclude that it is unlikely based on imaging, integral-field spectroscopy, and high- z massive galaxy pair statistics. We argue that the gas seen in absorption against the QSO was likely ejected from the galaxy J1439B and therefore provides a unique observational probe of AGN feedback in the distant universe.

gmw_00004902.name: CXO J101527.2+625911
gmw_00004902.overview: CXO J101527.2+625911 is a bulge-dominated elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1907.10161, arXiv:1704.05549] and a merger remnant located at redshift z=0.3504 [arXiv:1907.10161]. The system hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1907.10161] and may contain either a dual black hole system [arXiv:1907.10161, arXiv:1704.05549] or a recoiling supermassive black hole [arXiv:1704.05549].

sources.summary: The paper describes CXO J101527.2+625911 as a z=0.3504 QSO-hosted elliptical merger remnant whose radio and Keck spectra favor a dual black hole system over a recoiled SMBH.

quotes.quote: We report the results of an investigation to determine the nature of the offset active galactic nucleus (AGN) found in the source CXO J101527.2+625911. Hubble Space Telescope and Chandra X-ray observatory data had suggested that the offset AGN, which has an angular separation of only 0 26 from the center of the host galaxy, is a recoiled Super Massive Black Hole (rSMBH).
