We carried out high angular resolution observations with both the VLBA (1.54 GHz) and the VLA (10.0 GHz 33.0 GHz) and detected a single compact radio source in the center of the host galaxy, with no radio continuum emission associated with the offset AGN. The detected radio source has a high brightness temperature value of T_b=7.2 10^ 7 K, indicating that the radio emission is associated with an AGN. Furthermore, we present the decomposition of high-resolution KECK spectra of the [ O III ] 5007 line into two narrow emission line components, which is a characteristic sign of a dual black hole system.

CXO J101527.2+625911 is a QSO hosted merger remnant located at z =0.3504. Its morphological type is a bulge-dominated elliptical, with two nuclei in its center - a bright northern nucleus harboring AGN and is spatially offset by 1.26 kpc (0.256 ) from the less bright southern nucleus that was identified as the center of the host galaxy. Spectral decomposition of the KECK spectra suggests H broad emission line is redshifted by 175 25 km s ^ 1 with respect to the systemic velocity (Kim et al. 2017).

sources.summary: CXO J101527.2+625911 is described as a z=0.3504 potential recoiling SMBH system in a bulge-dominated elliptical host, with spatial and velocity offsets and weak disturbance suggesting a late-stage merger.

quotes.quote: From the survey, we have detected a potential rSMBH, 'CXO J101527.2+625911' at z=0.3504. The CXO J101527.2+625911 has a spatially offset (1.26 0.05 kpc) active SMBH and kinematically offset broad emission lines (175 25 km s ^ 1 relative to systemic velocity). The observed spatial and velocity offsets suggest this galaxy could be a rSMBH, but we also have considered a possibility of dual SMBH scenario.

This, along with the null detection of X-ray source in the nucleus supports our hypothesis that the CXO J101527.2+625911 is a rSMBH. Our GALFIT analysis shows the host galaxy to be a bulge-dominated elliptical. The weak morphological disturbance and small spatial and velocity offsets suggest that CXO J101527.2+625911 could be in the final stage of merging process and about to turn into a normal elliptical galaxy.

gmw_00004903.name: SDSS J074352
gmw_00004903.overview: SDSS J074352 is an active galactic nucleus that exhibits a hard X-ray spectrum [arXiv:2401.07895]. Its spectrum is modeled with both Galactic and intrinsic absorption [arXiv:2401.07895], and its continuum and line light curves showed clear dips during 2016–2017 [arXiv:1802.03022].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J074352.02+271239.5 as a harder-spectrum SEAMBH outlier, fitting intrinsic absorption and citing a Swift 2-8 keV photon-index measurement.

quotes.quote: Six out of eight Chandra SEAMBHs show a negative hardness ratio implying soft spectra, whereas two (SDSS J074352.02+271239.5 and SDSS J101000.68+300321.5) show hard spectra.

Six Chandra SEAMBHs and the Archived SEAMBH (SDSSJ075051.72+245409.3) show soft, steep spectra well fit by using a power-law model with Galactic absorption. SDSS J074352.02+271239.5 SDSS J101000.68+300321.5 appear as outliers and have much harder spectra than the other SEAMBHs. We fit their spectrum using a power-law model with both Galactic and intrinsic absorption ( xsphabs.abs1 * xszphabs.zabs1 * powlaw1d.p1 ). SDSS J101000.68+300321.5 likely needs a more complex model, but the low photon count prevents us from uniquely doing so.
Except for SDSS J074352.02+271239.5, an F-test confirms that adding intrinsic absorption component to the Galactic-absorbed power-law model does not improve the fit significantly.

sources.summary: SDSS J074352 shows clear 2016-2017 continuum and line-curve dips; its seasonal CCF lag is adopted because the single-season continuum ACF is narrower than the full-light-curve ACF.

quotes.quote: SDSS J074352: Both the continuum and \ light curves show very clear dips during 2016--2017. In general, the time lag measured from the light curves in 2016--2017 is consistent with the lag measured from its entire light curves, within the uncertainties. However, the ACF generated solely from its continuum light curve in 2016--2017 is much narrower than the ACF obtained from its entire continuum light curve. Considering that the season gap between 2016 Jun to 2016 Oct may influence the \ lag measurement, we adopt the CCF analysis of the light curves in 2016--2017 as the final result of this object.

gmw_00004904.name: SDSS J075051
gmw_00004904.overview: SDSS J075051 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1802.03022, arXiv:2401.07895] that has been observed in the X-ray band by the Chandra observatory [arXiv:2401.07895]. It exhibits a soft X-ray spectrum [arXiv:2401.07895] and possesses a high-quality photometric light curve [arXiv:1802.03022].

sources.summary: The paper processes archival Chandra data for SDSSJ075051.72+245409.3, finding a soft spectrum and consistency with a published Chandra photon-index measurement.

quotes.quote: SDSSJ075051.72+245409.3 has archival ASIC-S Chandra observation from Cycle 21 (PI: Gordon Garmire, see Table tab:table1 for details). In the absence of simultaneous optical-UV observation for this target, we use L_ 2500 measurement from .

Except for SDSSJ075051.72+245409.3, the other five extreme SEAMBHs are part of the Liu et al. (2021) sample and we refer the readers to their Section 2 for details of X-ray and optical-UV analysis. We process the X-ray data of SDSSJ075051.72+245409.3 along with the eight Chandra SEAMBHs and present the details in Section section3a .
SDSSJ075051.72+245409.3: Huang+2023 finds the 0.3-8 keV photon index of 2.22 0.19 using Chandra data. Our 0.35-8 keV measurement is consistent with their findings.

sources.summary: SDSS J075051 has a higher-quality photometric light curve than its 5100 continuum curve, a stronger photometry-to-line correlation, and needs more observations to improve its lag measurement.

quotes.quote: SDSS J075051: The quality of its photometric light curve is superior to the 5100 \ continuum light curve. The r_ max of photometry vs. \ is higher than the value ( 0.4 ) of 5100 \ vs. . More observations are needed to improve its lag measurement in the future.

gmw_00004905.name: SDSS J075101
gmw_00004905.overview: SDSS J075101 is a radio-quiet, type 1 quasar with a redshift of z=0.1208 [arXiv:1904.12876]. It is classified as an active galactic nucleus with a super-Eddington accreting massive black hole candidate [arXiv:1701.08781, arXiv:1904.12876], exhibiting extreme X-ray variability [arXiv:1904.12876].

sources.summary: SDSS J075101.42+291419.1 is one of four high-accretion RM-selected AGN observed with XMM-Newton; the paper fits its UV-optical SED with a thin disk and finds ordinary soft X-ray behavior.

quotes.quote: Here we present, for the first time, X-ray observations of four RM-selected candidates to have slim AD (SDSS J075101.42+291419.1, SDSS J080101.41+184840.7, SDSS J081441.91+212918.5, and SDSS J100055.71+314001.2) that have been observed recently by XMM-Newton (PI: Shai Kaspi). These new AGN with the highest accretion rates >20 were observed for the purpose of testing the slim-disc idea in the most extreme known cases. We follow the work of paper  I to fit the IR-optical-UV SED of these RM-selected AGN (see Appendix  appendix for a more explanations where the spectral analysis is presented). We found that the UV-optical SED of these four RM-selected AGN can also be fitted by thin AD models presenting moderate amount of reddening ( A_ V 0.4 ). We also found that the soft X-ray emission, as well as the Comptonized power law, of these AGN with the largest dimensionless accretion rate ( = 20 -- 200, are surprisingly similar to those from the parent sample ( 3) that have lower accretion rates.

sources.summary: SDSS J075101.42+291419.1 is presented as a radio-quiet, super-Eddington type 1 quasar with extreme X-ray variability, reverberation-mapped black hole mass, NLS1-like spectra, and possible SEAMBH classification.

quotes.quote: Here we report the discovery of extreme X-ray variability in another quasar, SDSS J 075101.42+291419.1 (hereafter SDSS J 0751+2914 ). The source has a redshift of z=0.1208 , and it is a radio-quiet (radio loudness R=1.29 ) The radio loudness parameter is defined as R=f_ 6 cm /f_ 2500 , where f_ 6 cm and f_ 2500 are flux densities at 6 cm and 2500 , respectively . The value is adopted from . quasar that possesses spectroscopic characteristics similar to NLS1s; e.g., a relatively narrow H line ( FWHM=1679 km s^ 1 ), strong optical Fe 2 emission, and weak [ O 3 ] lines. It is one of the targets in the reverberation mapping (RM) campaign targeting super-Eddington accreting massive black holes SEAMBHs; Du2015,Du2016,Du2018 . Its virial BH mass constrained from the H RM is 1.6 10^7 M_ , and its dimensionless mass accretion rate derived from the standard thin disk model Shakura1973 is M = M ^2/ L_ Edd = 20.1 ( _ 44 / i )^ 3/2 (M_ BH /10^7M_ )^ 2 =28.2 , where M is the mass accretion rate, L_ Edd =1.5 10^ 38 (M_ BH /M_ ) erg s^ 1 is the Eddington luminosity for solar composition gas, _ 44 = L_ 5100 / 10^ 44 erg s^ 1 is the rest-frame 5100 luminosity in units of 10^ 44 erg s^ 1 , M_ BH is the BH mass, and i (adopted as cos i =0.75 ) is the inclination angle of the disk e.g., Du2015,Du2018 .
According to the criterion of M >3 used for identifying SEAMBH candidates Wang2014a,Du2015 , SDSS J 0751+2914 can be classified as a SEAMBH candidate.

sources.summary: The new SDSS J075101 reverberation measurement agrees with its 2013-2014 lag but has smaller errors; a prominent peak gives a very high correlation coefficient.

quotes.quote: SDSS J075101: It was monitored previously in 2013 Nov -- 2014 May (Paper du2015 ), yielding an \ time lag of 33.4_ 5.6 ^ +15.6 days in the rest frame. The new measurement in the present paper is consistent with the previous observations, within the uncertainties, but the error bars are smaller. The peak around Julian date 550 (from the zero point of 2457300 in Figure fig:light_curves , similarly hereinafter) is prominent, and its r_ max is very high (0.96).

gmw_00004906.name: SDSS J075949
gmw_00004906.overview: SDSS J075949 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1802.03022]. The galaxy was monitored over a three-year period from 2014 to 2017 to constrain its rest-frame time delay and analyze its light curves [arXiv:1802.03022].

sources.summary: SDSS J075949 is monitored over three years; new observations better constrain its lag, and 2015-2016 light curves give the correlation result used for relation analysis.

quotes.quote: SDSS J075949: We have monitored it for three years, from 2014 to 2017. The result of the first year was published in Paper du2016V , showing relatively large error bars in its \ time delay ( 55.0_ 13.1 ^ +17.0 days in the rest frame). We also plot its old light curves in 2014--2015 in Figure fig:light_curves for comparison. The new observation in this paper gives better constraints on the \ lag.

The light curves in 2015--2016 give a higher correlation coefficient ( r_ max =0.83 ) than the value ( r_ max =0.74 ) obtained from its entire light curves (including the data in 2014--2015). We thus use the CCF results from 2015--2016 in the analysis of the \ relation.

gmw_00004907.name: SDSS J081441
gmw_00004907.overview: SDSS J081441 is an active galactic nucleus selected for reverberation mapping [arXiv:1701.08781, arXiv:1802.03022]. Its ultraviolet-optical spectral energy distribution is well-fitted by a thin accretion disk model [arXiv:1701.08781], and its light curves have been monitored to improve lag measurements [arXiv:1802.03022].

sources.summary: SDSS J081441.91+212918.5 is one of four high-accretion RM-selected AGN observed with XMM-Newton; the paper fits its UV-optical SED with a thin disk and finds ordinary soft X-ray behavior.

quotes.quote: Here we present, for the first time, X-ray observations of four RM-selected candidates to have slim AD (SDSS J075101.42+291419.1, SDSS J080101.41+184840.7, SDSS J081441.91+212918.5, and SDSS J100055.71+314001.2) that have been observed recently by XMM-Newton (PI: Shai Kaspi). These new AGN with the highest accretion rates >20 were observed for the purpose of testing the slim-disc idea in the most extreme known cases. We follow the work of paper  I to fit the IR-optical-UV SED of these RM-selected AGN (see Appendix  appendix for a more explanations where the spectral analysis is presented). We found that the UV-optical SED of these four RM-selected AGN can also be fitted by thin AD models presenting moderate amount of reddening ( A_ V 0.4 ). We also found that the soft X-ray emission, as well as the Comptonized power law, of these AGN with the largest dimensionless accretion rate ( = 20 -- 200, are surprisingly similar to those from the parent sample ( 3) that have lower accretion rates.

sources.summary: For SDSS J081441, the paper replaces a sparse 2013-2014 light-curve tail with more convincing 2016-2017 light curves, producing a much better lag measurement.

quotes.quote: SDSS J081441: Its light curves from 2013 Nov -- 2014 May were published in Paper du2015 . In the \ 2013 -- 2014 light curve, there are only several points after the peak around Julian day 160 (see Figure 1 of Paper du2015 ), because the altitude of the source was already too low to observe at the end. The new 2016 -- 2017 light curves look more convincing, and the lag measurement is much better (with smaller error bars).

gmw_00004908.name: SDSS J083553
gmw_00004908.overview: SDSS J083553 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1802.03022]. Its light curves lack visible primary peaks around Julian day 300, but they exhibit a clear, small dip near Julian day 450 [arXiv:1802.03022].

sources.summary: SDSS J083553 has missing primary continuum and line peaks, but a clear smaller dip near Julian day 450 motivates adopting the 2016-2017 CCF result.

quotes.quote: SDSS J083553: It is a little unfortunate that the primary peaks around Julian day 300 in the continuum and \ light curves are invisible. However, the small dip close to Julian day 450 is observed clearly. So, we adopt the CCF analysis from the 2016--2017 data to be the final result for this object. Its ACF is narrower and the r_ max is a little higher than the analysis obtained from the entire light curves from 2015 to 2017.

gmw_00004909.name: SDSS J093302
gmw_00004909.overview: SDSS J093302 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1802.03022]. Its continuum light curve displays a clear dip and response, which makes its lag measurement reliable [arXiv:1802.03022].

sources.summary: For SDSS J093302, the paper changes the comparison star after bright-star contamination, improving the continuum light curve; its clear dip and response make the lag measurement reliable.

quotes.quote: For SDSS J093302, we used a comparison star ( R_ * =184 .6 ; P.A.= 284^ .3 ) different from the one listed in Table tab:obj before 2016 Dec 12. We discovered that our previous observations were adversely affected by another very bright star in the slit, located between the target and our previous comparison star, which contributed a very high background to both the target and the comparison star. This reduced the S/N of the previous spectra and increased the scatter of the light curves. After changing the comparison star to the one listed in Table tab:obj , the quality of the new light curves, especially the continuum, is relatively better.
SDSS J093302: The big dip and its response are very clear in the light curves. The lag measurement is pretty reliable.

gmw_00004910.name: SDSS J100402
gmw_00004910.overview: SDSS J100402 is a luminous quasar located at a redshift of approximately 0.33 [arXiv:2106.05655, arXiv:1307.6799]. It is a super-Eddington accreting system that exhibits narrow H-beta emission lines and low variability in each velocity bin of the H-beta line [arXiv:2106.05655].

sources.summary: PG 1001+291 is a luminous quasar at z=0.3272 with narrow H-beta, prior SEAMBH monitoring under an SDSS alias, a symmetric lag structure, and super-Eddington accretion.

quotes.quote: PG 1001+291 has a redshift of z=0.3272 , m_ g =15.9 mag , and A_ V = 0.06 mag Schlafly2011 . Similar to PG 0923+201, PG 1001+291 also shows prominent emission lines with 1.17 and weak \ line Du2019 . However, its H line has a much narrower FWHM, only about 2100 . PG 1001+291 was monitored between 2015 and 2017 by the Super-Eddington Accreting Massive Black Holes (SEAMBH) campaign The selection criteria for SEAMBHs were: strong optical , relatively narrow H , and weak lines (see Du2014, Du2018 ). ( Du2018 , who used the alternative SDSS name for PG 1001+291, namely, SDSS J100402.61+285535.3).

PG 1001+291 The lag structure of PG 1001+291 is relatively symmetric and also shows smaller delays in both wings of the line profile, basically agreeing with the virial envelopes. However, this object has a low variability in each velocity bin of the H line, resulting in broad ICCFs and large lag errors. Further spectroscopic monitoring on PG 1001+291 is warranted to strengthen the evidence on the velocity-delay structure.
This implies that PG 0923+201 is a sub-Eddington accretor whereas PG 1001+291 is a super-Eddington accretor.

sources.summary: Ton 28 has an associated absorption-line system detected only in the doublet, with velocity-aligned profiles and coverage analysis consistent with full occultation.

quotes.quote: Ton 28 ( =0.3302) -- This associated absorption-line system is detected only in the 1031.926, 1037.617 doublet. The 1238.821, 1242.804 doublet is covered but not detected. Figure fig:ton28 shows velocity-aligned flux profiles of the transitions (left) and an evaluation of velocity-dependent partial coverage (right) in the doublet. The doublet is consistent with fully occulting the quasar central engine.

sources.summary: SDSS J100402 has a near-zero full-light-curve CCF peak centroid because of a two-year gap and flat 2016-2017 light curves, giving the largest lag uncertainty.

quotes.quote: SDSS J100402: The centroid of the peak of the CCF calculated from the entire light curves is nearly zero because of the the gap between the two years and the very flat light curves in 2016 -- 2017. Therefore, we adopt the light curves in 2015 -- 2016 to measure the time lag. The uncertainty of its \ lag is the largest among all of the objects in this paper.

gmw_00004911.name: SDSS J101000
gmw_00004911.overview: SDSS J101000 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a supermassive black hole with a super-Eddington accretion rate [arXiv:2401.07895]. It exhibits a hard X-ray spectrum and is characterized as X-ray weak, possibly due to shielding by a puffed-up slim accretion disk [arXiv:2401.07895].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J101000.68+300321.5 as a hard-spectrum, X-ray-weak SEAMBH whose low photon count limits more detailed X-ray modeling.

quotes.quote: Six Chandra SEAMBHs and the Archived SEAMBH (SDSSJ075051.72+245409.3) show soft, steep spectra well fit by using a power-law model with Galactic absorption. SDSS J074352.02+271239.5 SDSS J101000.68+300321.5 appear as outliers and have much harder spectra than the other SEAMBHs. We fit their spectrum using a power-law model with both Galactic and intrinsic absorption ( xsphabs.abs1 * xszphabs.zabs1 * powlaw1d.p1 ). SDSS J101000.68+300321.5 likely needs a more complex model, but the low photon count prevents us from uniquely doing so.
Only SDSS J101000.68+300321.5 appears as X-ray weak, with _ ox = -0.23 . The limited photon count for this object hinders fitting more complex models to correct for absorption.

One of our targets, SDSS J101000.68+300321.5, exhibits a flatter/harder _ 2-8 keV and appears as X-ray weak ( _ ox <-0.2 ). It could be that the X-ray data for this object is not good enough, as indicated by large error bars, or the possibility that the X-ray emission is shielded by a puffed-up slim accretion disk. explains X-ray weakness in highly accreting AGNs through an orientation effect in a slim accretion disk.

sources.summary: For SDSS J101000, the 2016-2017 line light curve has smaller scatter and errors, so that seasonal lag is selected while remaining consistent with the full campaign.

quotes.quote: SDSS J101000: The scatter and the error bars of the \ light curve in 2016 -- 2017 are smaller than those in 2015 -- 2016. Thus, we select the lag measurement using the light curves in 2016 -- 2017 as the final result. In general, the lags obtained from the light curves in 2015 -- 2017 and in 2016 -- 2017 are consistent with each other.

gmw_00004912.name: NGC 973
gmw_00004912.overview: NGC 973 is an edge-on Sb or Sbc spiral galaxy [arXiv:1305.3130, arXiv:1507.03095] classified as a Seyfert 2/LINER object [arXiv:1305.3130, arXiv:1804.09071]. It features a dust lane spanning its entire disk [arXiv:1305.3130, arXiv:1804.09071], a boxy or peanut-shaped bulge [arXiv:1507.03095, arXiv:1804.09071], and an X-shaped central structure indicating the presence of a bar [arXiv:1507.03095, arXiv:astro-ph/0512244].

sources.summary: NGC 973 is described as an edge-on Sb/Sbc Seyfert 2/LINER with a prominent dust lane, extraplanar gas detections, shallow FIR profiles, and a sharp PACS central peak.

quotes.quote: NGC 973 [ UGC 2048 ] NGC 973 is located at a distance of 63.5 Mpc and has a D_ 25 diameter of 4 03. Its morphological class is estimated to be Sb or Sbc, due to the inherent uncertainty of the precise structure of edge-on galaxies, and it is classified as a Seyfert 2/LINER galaxy. The inclination is found to be 89 6 0 1 1997A&A...325..135X and the galaxy is showing a prominent dust lane 1992A&AS...93..255G over its entire span, from the centre to the edges. Although 1994ApJ...427..160P claimed not to have found any extraplanar emission-line gas in H , 2003ApJS..148..383M did detect some extraplanar emission from gas, but also mentioned there is no suggestion of a widespread diffuse emission above the disc plane. It is assumed the dust shows the same or at least similar behaviour.

This galaxy demonstrates a very peculiar behaviour in its horizontal profiles compared to the other galaxies in the sample. For one thing, the surface brightness shows a relatively shallow decline, out to about 1 4 on the SW side and about 1 4 to 1 6 -- depending on the wavelength -- on the NE side of the disc and then falls down dramatically, suggesting a possible truncation of the dust disc. In addition to this, the emission shows a very sharp and strong central peak at PACS wavelengths, which has almost completely vanished from 350 m \ onwards, partly because of the poorer spatial resolution at these latter wavebands.

sources.summary: NGC 973 is an Sb/Sbc galaxy with a boxy/peanut bulge and possible bar; the new H I data show flat stellar and gas disks with asymmetric high-density structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 973 NGC 973 (UGC 2048) is located at a distance of 63.5 Mpc The distances we use here are taken from NED, as explained in section 2.1 of Paper I. . It is classified as an Sb or Sbc galaxy and has a boxy/peanut-shaped bulge ( lutticke00 classify it as a `bulge which is on one side boxy and on the other peanut-shaped'). After subtracting an axisymmetric model from their I-band image of NGC 973, find an X-shaped pattern in the central regions which they interpret as evidence for the presence of a bar. So far, the structure and kinematics of the atomic gas disk of NGC 973 have never been studied.

Both the stellar and the H i disk are remarkably flat, even in the outer regions, but some asymmetry is visible at high contour levels in the total H i map. This is also visible in Fig. fig:N973_models_mom0 . Indeed, on the approaching side only one density peak is observed (at a major axis offset of +1 in Fig. fig:N973_models_mom0 ).

sources.summary: The paper models the optical-to-far-infrared SED of UGC 2048 as an edge-on spiral and finds young stars dominate dust heating while normalized star-formation rates resemble comparable disk galaxies.

quotes.quote: Using tools previously described and applied to the prototype galaxy NGC 891, we model the optical to far-infrared spectral energy distributions (SED) of four additional edge-on spiral galaxies, namely NGC 5907, NGC 4013, UGC 1082 and UGC 2048. Comparing the model predictions with IRAS and, where available, sub-millimeter and millimeter observations, we determine the respective roles of the old and young stellar populations in grain heating. In all cases, the young population dominates, with the contribution of the old stellar population being at most 40 , as previously found for NGC 891.

In the present paper we have extended the analysis from Paper I to a sample of four additional edge-on galaxies (NGC 5907, NGC 4013, UGC 1082, UGC 2048) and proved that the solution obtained for NGC 891 is generally valid. We have also shown that NGC 891 is not an exceptional system in terms of its SFR density. In all the systems the dust is predominantly heated by the young stellar population. The contribution of the old stellar population can account for at most 40 .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 973 as a massive edge-on Sb spiral with a regular dust lane, B/PS/X central structure, Sy2/LINER classification, and relatively successful panchromatic simulations.

quotes.quote: NGC 973 This galaxy classified as an Sb spiral (NED) is the most massive galaxy in our sample, with a radius of the outermost isophote of 25 mag/arcsec ^2 up to 40 kpc. It is seen almost exactly edge-on; the dust lane is fairly regular, without strong bending or clumpiness. A clear thick B/PS/X structure in the centre is a distinctive feature of the galaxy 2000A&AS..145..405L which points to the presence of a strong bar 2006MNRAS.366.1121P . Also, it is classified as a Sy2/LINER object with significant X-ray emission detected by SWIFT and INTEGRAL see e.g. 2007A&A...462...57S, 2012ApJ...761..184W . According to 2015A&A...582A..18A , this galaxy does not show strong disc warping in H i , nor in optical observations.

sources.summary: The paper uses UGC 2048, identified in the paper as NGC 973, as one of three edge-on disks whose residual profiles reveal X-like morphology attributed to a bar.

quotes.quote: In a pilot survey we compare the orbital structures which lead to the appearance of ``peanuts'' and ``X''-like features with the edge-on profiles of three disc galaxies (IC 2531, NGC 4013 and UGC 2048). The subtraction from the images of a model representing the axisymmetric component of the galaxies reveals the contribution of the non-axisymmetric terms. We find a direct correspondence between the orbital profiles of 3D bars in models and the observed main morphological features of the residuals.
We focus our attention to the structures observed in the central regions of IC 2531, NGC 4013 and UGC 2048 (NGC 973).

The main conclusion of the present study is that the careful subtraction of axisymmetric components from the profiles of the three edge-on disk galaxies (NGC 4013, IC 2531 and UGC 2048) reveals an ``X''-like morphology, which indicates the presence of a bar.

gmw_00004913.name: J1702+1301
gmw_00004913.overview: J1702+1301 is an X-ray-bright, radio-loud active galactic nucleus at a redshift of approximately 5.5 [arXiv:2212.10113, arXiv:2402.19202]. Its high radio loudness, flat-to-rising radio spectrum, and rapid flux variability support its classification as a blazar [arXiv:2212.10113, arXiv:2402.19202].

sources.summary: J1702+1301 is presented as an X-ray-bright z=5.5 quasar whose compact radio counterpart, high radio loudness, flat/rising spectrum, and rapid variability support a blazar classification.

quotes.quote: Here we report the radio properties of an X-ray bright z=5.5 quasar, SRGE J170245.3+130104 (J1702+1301). Our high-resolution radio images reveal the radio counterpart at the optical position of J1702+1301, while another radio component is also detected at 23.5 \ to the southwest. Our analysis suggests that this southwest component is associated with a foreground galaxy at z 0.677 , which is mixed with J1702+1301 in low-frequency low-resolution radio images. After removing the contamination from this foreground source, we recalculated the radio loudness of J1702+1301 to be R> 1100, consistent with those of blazars. J1702+1301 exhibits a flat radio spectrum ( = -0.17 0.05 , S ^ ) between 0.15 and 5 GHz; above 5 GHz, it shows a rising spectrum shape, and the spectral index ^ 8.2 _ 4.7 appears to be correlated with the variation of the flux density: in burst states, ^ 8.2 _ 4.7 becomes larger.

J1702+1301 displays distinct radio variability on timescales from weeks to years in the source's rest frame. These radio properties, including high radio loudness, rising spectrum, and rapid variability, tend to support it as a blazar. galaxies: active – galaxies: high-redshift – galaxies: jets – quasars: individual: SRGE J170245.3+130104 Recently, the eROSITA telescope 2021A&A...647A...1P on-board the Spectrum Roentgen Gamma SRG; 2021A&A...656A.132S space observatory discovered a luminous quasar, SRGE J170245.3+130104 (J1702+1301), during its first half-year X-ray All-sky Survey 2021AstL...47..123K .

sources.summary: The paper studies SRGE J170245.3+130104 as a z≈5.5 X-ray luminous, radio-loud blazar whose properties probe early powerful jets and SMBH growth.

quotes.quote: Petersburg 191187, Russia Received 30/01/2024; accepted 26/02/2024 The X-ray luminous and radio-loud AGN SRGE J170245.3+130104 discovered at z 5.5 provides unique chances to probe the SMBH growth and evolution with powerful jets in the early Universe.

Our research focuses on SRGE J170245.3+130104 (hereafter J1702+1301), one of the highest-redshift and high-luminosity X-ray quasars recently discovered by the eROSITA telescope on-board the Spectrum Roentgen Gamma (SRG; Merloni2020 ) during its first half-year X-ray All-sky Survey Khorunzhev2021 .
J1702+1301 exhibits powerful X-ray and radio emission with a radio luminosity of 10^ 27 W Hz^ 1 , a radio loudness of R>1100 , a rapidly varying radio flux density, and a flat radio spectrum An2023 .

gmw_00004914.name: J1702-SW
gmw_00004914.overview: J1702-SW is a foreground galaxy that displays multiple structures in DECaLS z-band imaging [arXiv:2212.10113]. Its infrared colors place it in the transition region between spiral and starburst galaxies, indicating that it is an active galaxy [arXiv:2212.10113].

sources.summary: J1702-SW is identified as the southwest infrared/radio component; photometric redshifts favor a foreground galaxy, and WISE colors place it between spiral and starburst active galaxies.

quotes.quote: The NE component is close to the optical position of , and the SW component is close to an infrared (IR) source WISEA J170243.84+130052.0 (J1702-SW hereafter).

We then estimated the photometric redshift of J1702-SW to be 0.677 based on the Sloan Digital Sky Survey SDSS, 2022ApJS..259...35A and Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer (WISE) photometry data 2014yCat.2328....0C using a public software program of Easy and Accurate Redshifts from Yale (EAZY, 2008ApJ...686.1503B ). We used a set of nine templates from that combine the Simple Stellar Population Models 2009ApJ...699..486C,2005MNRAS.362..799M with varying dust attenuation and ages along with emission line models. The photometric redshift obtained using the DECaLS data 2019AJ....157..168D for the SED fitting is slightly lower. However, the southwest source displays multiple structures in the DECaLS z band image, which causes a larger uncertainty in the estimation of the photometric redshift. Therefore, the photometric redshift results suggest that J1702-SW is a foreground galaxy.

We calculated WISE W1-W2 and W2-W3 of J1702-SW. We found that it is located in the transition region between spiral and starburst galaxies in the IR colour-colour diagram (Fig. fig:J1702-SW ), indicating that it is an active galaxy.

gmw_00004915.name: host of GRB 130603B
gmw_00004915.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 130603B is a perturbed spiral galaxy undergoing tidal interactions [arXiv:1308.2984, arXiv:1605.02438]. Located at a redshift of z=0.3565 [arXiv:1308.2984], it exhibits moderate star formation activity [arXiv:1308.2984, arXiv:2205.13940] and near-solar oxygen abundance [arXiv:1308.2984, arXiv:1605.02438].

sources.summary: The host of GRB 130603B is a disrupted, moderately star-forming galaxy at z=0.3565 with near-solar to 0.5-solar metallicity; the burst occurred off the strongest star-forming region but in dense gas.

quotes.quote: Together with multiwavelength photometry we study the host and environment of GRB 130603B. From these spectra we determine the redshift of the burst to be z=0.3565 0.0002 , measure rich dynamics both in absorption and emission, and a substantial line of sight extinction of A_V=0.86 0.15 mag. The GRB was located at the edge of a disrupted arm of a moderately star forming galaxy with near-solar metallicity. Unlike for most long GRBs (LGRBs), N_ H_X /A_V is consistent with the Galactic ratio, indicating that the explosion site differs from those found in LGRBs. The merger is not associated with the most star-forming region of the galaxy; however, it did occur in a dense region, implying a rapid merger or a low natal kick velocity for the compact object binary.

The metallicity of the host is similar throughout the system with a value of 0.5 solar at the GRB site and the core, and marginally higher ( 0.7 solar) at the side opposite the core of the GRB region, depending on the calibrator (these values are slightly lower than the ones obtained by cuc13 , which is mostly due to the use of a different calibrator).

The host of GRB 130603B has a somewhat higher metallicity than expected for a galaxy of this mass, but also a slightly higher SFR. This could both be due to a starburst at the time of the tidal interaction which is now declining but has already lead to an over-enrichment with heavier elements.

sources.summary: The paper describes the SGRB 130603B host as a perturbed interacting spiral whose emission-line ratios were modeled, yielding near-solar O/H, lower N/H/S/H, and an unusually high magnetic field.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy is a perturbed spiral due to interaction with another galaxy (de Ugarte Postigo et al). In the spectrum taken by X-shooter the afterglow dominates the continuum, but the lines emitted from the host were used in their modelling. Therefore, we refer to line ratios and not to line fluxes.

The spectrum observed by Cucchiara et al (2013) for SGRB 130603B is also reported in Table 12 for comparison. The models show shock velocities and pre-shock densities in the norm. However, to best reproduce all the line ratios we adopted a magnetic field 0 higher by a factor of 3 than for the LGRB galaxies presented in the previous sections. A higher 0 prevents compression in the downstream gas, but its effect on the line ratio results is different than that obtained reducing the preshock density (see Contini 2009). We find O/H near solar in all the positions in agreement with de Ugarte Postigo et al results, and N/H and S/H lower than solar by factors 2.

sources.summary: The paper reports DOT B and R detections of the GRB 130603B host and SED modeling that gives high mass, dust extinction, and moderate star formation.

quotes.quote: We observed the host galaxy of short GRB 130603B using 3.6m DOT in B and R filters with an exposure time of 2x300 s in each on 23^ rd March 2017. We detected a bright galaxy (see the upper-right panel of Fig. host_findingchart ) in both the filters.

Therefore, in this work, we performed the SED modeling of GRB 130603B using an advanced software called Prospector , and we derive following host parameters: log(M/M _ ) = 10.63 ^ +0.09 _ 0.10 , log(Z/Z_ ) = -1.50 ^ +0.40 _ 0.36 , age of the galaxy = 7.09 ^ +1.76 _ 2.14 Gyr, A_ V = 1.65 ^ +0.29 _ 0.23 mag, and log( ) = 1.78 ^ +0.76 _ 0.72 . We calculated the star formation rate of the galaxy = 11.57 M _ yr^ 1 . Our results suggest that the host galaxy of GRB 130603B has a high mass galaxy with a moderate star formation activity, consistent with those reported in .

gmw_00004916.name: TKRS4389
gmw_00004916.overview: TKRS4389 is a bright, blue-cloud starburst galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.694 [arXiv:0907.0231, arXiv:1008.3397] that shows signs of recent merger activity [arXiv:1304.6405] and hosts a weak active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0907.0231, arXiv:1304.6405]. The galaxy exhibits intense star formation [arXiv:1008.3397] and drives a substantial, isotropic outflow of cool gas [arXiv:0907.0231, arXiv:2005.03017].

sources.summary: The paper describes TKRS4389 as a bright blue-cloud background galaxy with high star formation, near-solar oxygen abundance, weak AGN emission, and spectral evidence for cool-gas outflow.

quotes.quote: TKRS4389 (Background Galaxy) The background galaxy is one of the brightest galaxies in the B_ 435 band in the TKRS Wirth2004 at its redshift and is among the bluest objects in the ``blue cloud" in the galaxy color-magnitude diagram (CMD), as shown in the left panel of Figure figd . We derive a star formation rate (SFR) of 80 M_ yr^ 1 using the luminosity of the H line measured in Weiner2007 and assuming a CharlotFall2000 dust attenuation curve with effective V-band optical depth _V 1.5 as derived from the stellar population modeling discussed below. We assume case B recombination to calculate an H luminosity and apply the calibration of Kennicutt1998 to convert this to SFR. KK2004 measure an oxygen abundance of 12 + log(O/H) = 8.76 0.15 , close to the solar abundance value of 8.72.

Our LRIS spectrum reveals a weak [ Ne 5 ] 3426 emission line, which indicates the object is host to an AGN e.g., Ho2008 . AGN activity may therefore affect the SFR estimate and the estimate of the oxygen abundance. Because the [ Ne 5 ] emission is weak, a Gaussian fit to the line profile results in a rest-frame dispersion of 125 40 km s^ 1 ; this value is within 0.8 of the instrumental velocity dispersion at this wavelength.
As noted previously (see sec.thepair ), the spectrum of TKRS4389 exhibits evidence for a substantial outflow of cool gas.

sources.summary: The paper maps TKRS4389's Mg II circumgalactic emission to tens of kiloparsecs, finding P-Cygni absorption near the galaxy and modeling the data as an isotropic outflow with declining wind velocity.

quotes.quote: We present spatially-resolved spectroscopy from the Keck Cosmic Web Imager (KCWI) of a star-forming galaxy at z=0.6942 , which shows emission from the \ 2796, 2803 \ doublet in the circumgalactic medium (CGM) extending 37 kpc at 3 significance in individual spaxels (1 detection limit ). After deconvolution with the seeing, we obtain 5 detections extending for 31 kpc measured in 7-spaxel (1.1 arcsec ^2 ) apertures. Spaxels covering the galaxy stellar regions show clear P-Cygni-like emission/absorption profiles with the blueshifted absorption extending to relative velocities of v = -800 km s ^ 1 ; however, the P-Cygni profiles give way to pure emission at large radii from the central galaxy.

We have performed three-dimensional radiative transfer modeling to infer the geometry and velocity and density profiles of the outflowing gas. Our observations are most consistent with an isotropic outflow rather than biconical wind models with half-opening angles 80^ . Furthermore, our modeling suggests that a wind velocity profile that decreases with radius is necessary to reproduce the velocity widths and strengths of \ line emission profiles at large circumgalactic radii.

Because a primary focus of our work is on constraining the spatial extent of the wind in TKRS4389, we generated six versions of this model, changing the value of r_ outer to range between 5 and 30 kpc in increments of 5 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper describes TKRS4389 as a bright z=0.694 starburst observed with Keck/LRIS, showing intense star formation and Mg II/Fe II absorption from a cool outflow.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we examine the line emission from a bright starburst at z = 0.694 known to exhibit a P Cygni profile in Mg 2 and Fe 2 * emission Rubin2009 .

Details of our observations of the target galaxy (TKRS4389) and data reduction are given in Rubin2009 . We obtained spectroscopy of this galaxy using the Low Resolution Imaging Spectrometer (LRIS) on Keck 1 Cohen1994 .

As noted in Rubin2009 , this galaxy is exceptionally bright for its redshift, with a star formation rate of 80 M_ yr^ 1 . Stellar population modeling indicates that the spectrum is dominated by light from the intense star formation activity. The deepest parts of the Mg 2 and Fe 2 resonance absorption lines are blueshifted by 200 - 300 km s^ 1 , with high velocity tails extending to 800 , indicating that these ions trace an outflow (see Figures fig.PCygni and fig.feii ).

sources.summary: The paper compares TKRS 4389 to 32016857 as another distant galaxy with extended emission, noting its exceptional brightness, larger mass, merger signs, AGN evidence, and blue color.

quotes.quote: Many properties of 32016857 and TKRS 4389 Rubin:2011p1660 , the only other distant galaxy detected in extended \ emission, are similar. In particular, suggest that TKRS 4389 is exceptionally bright for its redshift of z = 0.6943 . It is slightly bluer and more luminous than 32016857 and has about three times as much stellar mass.

An HST image of TKRS 4389 shows signs of recent merger activity. cite the weak [ Ne 5 ] emission, W_r = -0.84 0.1 , as evidence that TKRS 4389, in contrast to 32016857, hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus (AGN). The very blue colors of 32016857 and TKRS 4389 are typical of \ emitters, which have lower masses, bluer colors (i.e., less reddening), and higher sSFR than the average star-forming galaxy at intermediate redshifts Rubin et al., in prep Erb:2012p26,Martin:2012 .

gmw_00004917.name: NGC 1448
gmw_00004917.overview: NGC 1448 is an Scd spiral galaxy featuring a truncated disk, spiral arms, and a possible bar structure [arXiv:1003.3015]. The galaxy hosts an obscured active galactic nucleus that is offset from the apparent stellar nucleus due to high interstellar extinction [arXiv:2103.14280].

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 1448 with MUSE, finding the obscured AGN offset from the apparent stellar nucleus, probable outflows, and a possible fossil line-emitting region.

quotes.quote: We present the analysis of an optical data cube of the central region of NGC 1448, obtained with the Multi Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE). Chandra X-ray data indicate that the active galactic nucleus (AGN) is not located at the apparent stellar nucleus of the galaxy, but at a projected distance of 1.75 0.22 arcsec ( 139 17 pc). This is probably caused by the high interstellar extinction in the surroundings of the AGN, which corresponds to the true nucleus of the galaxy, as also proposed by previous studies.

Two probable outflows from the AGN were detected along the region of the two ionization cones. The AGN position does not coincide with the brightest line-emitting region at the centre of NGC 1448. That may be a consequence of the high obscuration from the AGN towards the observer (the AGN is actually Compton thick), mostly caused by a nearly edge-on torus. An additional hypothesis is that the AGN reduced its luminosity, during the last 440 yr, to nearly half of the value in the past. In this case, the brightest line-emitting region corresponds to a ``light echo'' or a ``fossil'' of the AGN in the past.

sources.summary: The paper derives an SMBH mass estimate for NGC 1448 from K-band bulge luminosity, noting spiral arms, a truncated disk, and possible bar residuals.

quotes.quote: The measurement for NGC 1448 (Scd) is estimated from the -L _ K,bul relation (see 3.2.1) due to the lack of sufficient spectroscopic data to measure a stellar velocity dispersion.
For the bulge-disc decomposition image analysis, we have obtained archival K band imaging for ESO 121-G006, NGC 1448 and NGC 1792.

The residual of NGC 1448 highlights the existence of its spiral arms and shows the presence of a truncated disc combined with a possible bar structure which our simplified modeling technique is incapable of fitting; however, the bulge fit does not appear to be compromised. Indeed, our derived estimations for ESO121-G006 and NGC 1448 are consistent with the upper limits obtained from stellar mass-to-light ratio analyses ratnam00 .

gmw_00004918.name: HS 1603+3820
gmw_00004918.overview: HS 1603+3820 is a bright quasar at a redshift of approximately 2.5 [arXiv:astro-ph/9908134, arXiv:0710.0647]. It features a mini-broad absorption line system [arXiv:1407.5998, arXiv:1007.0448] and a rich complex of associated heavy-element absorbers that exhibit strong variability [arXiv:astro-ph/9908134, arXiv:astro-ph/0504663].

sources.summary: The paper describes HS1603+3820 as a mini-BAL quasar whose C IV profile varies across the full velocity range, likely from changing ionization due to shielding material.

quotes.quote: HS1603+3820 ( = 2.542 ), mini-BAL system at 9,500 \ ( 2.43 ): This system was first discovered by dob99 , and then monitored with high-resolution spectra for 1.2 yrs in the quasar rest frame mis05,mis07b . The C 4 mini-BAL shows both partial coverage mis03 and time variability mis05,mis07b . The C 4 profile changes across the entire range of velocity, 2000 , at once (see Figure fig:varplo_HS1603 ). Among three possible scenarios for the variation: i) a gas motion, ii) a variable scattering material, and iii) a change of ionization condition, the first two cannot be applicable for this quasar (see 1). The preferred explanation of changing ionization parameter is likely to be caused by variable shielding material between the continuum source and the absorber, since a quasar of this luminosity does not vary enough to explain the change.

Warm absorbers are a candidate for the shielding material as evidenced by X-ray observations of this quasar mis10,roz13 . Summary: There is significant variability of C 4 over the full velocity range on timescales as short as months in the quasar rest frame. The variability is likely to be caused by changing ionization parameter due to variable shielding material that modulates the quasar flux.

sources.summary: The paper studies HS 1603+3820 with two Subaru spectra, finding rich narrow absorption systems, strong variability in intrinsic System A, and possible broad-absorption-line formation.

quotes.quote: We present a new spectrum of the quasar HS 1603+3820 taken 1.28 years (0.36 years in the quasar rest frame) after a previous observation with Subaru+HDS. The new spectrum enables us to search for time variability as an identifier of intrinsic narrow absorption lines (NALs). This quasar shows a rich complex of \ NALs within 60,000 \ of the emission redshift. Based on covering factor analysis, Misawa et al. found that the \ NAL system at \ = 2.42--2.45 (System A, at a shift velocity of = 8,300--10,600 \ relative to the quasar) was intrinsic to the quasar. With our new spectrum, we perform time variability analysis as well as covering factor analysis to separate intrinsic NALs from intervening NALs for 8 \ systems.

Only System A, which was identified as an intrinsic system in the earlier paper by Misawa et al., shows a strong variation in line strength ( W_ obs 10.4 19.1 ). We speculate that a broad absorption line (BAL) could be forming in this quasar (i.e., many narrower lines will blend together to make a BAL profile). We illustrate the plausibility of this suggestion with the help of a simulation in which we vary the column densities and covering factors of the NAL complex.

sources.summary: The paper presents Subaru/FOCAS spectropolarimetry of quasar HS 1603+3820, measuring low polarization across its C IV mini-BAL and using that result to disfavor scattering as the variability driver.

quotes.quote: We report the results of a spectropolarimetric observation of the C 4 ``mini-broad'' absorption line (mini-BAL) in the quasar HS 1603+3820 ( z_ em = 2.542 ). The observations were carried out with the FOCAS instrument on the Subaru telescope and yielded an extremely high polarization sensitivity of p 0.1 , at a resolving power of R 1500 . HS 1603+3820 has been the target of a high-resolution spectroscopic monitoring campaign for more than four years, aimed at studying its highly variable C 4 mini-BAL profile.

An important result of our observations is that the degree of polarization does not change across the trough of the C 4 mini-BAL in HS 1603+3820. This result has direct implications for some of the scattering mechanisms that may operate in this object, as we discuss below.

Considering the results of our spectropolarimetric observation, we can reject with some confidence the large-angle scattering scenario (Figure 1A) as the cause of partial coverage, hence the origin of variability of the C 4 mini-BAL in HS 1603+3820. The primary argument against this scenario is that the polarization level remains low in the mini-BAL trough and similar to the level out of the trough.

sources.summary: The paper presents HS 1603+3820 as a bright z=2.51 quasar with a rich C iv absorber complex, several associated heavy-element absorbers, and one of the richest known associated absorption systems.

quotes.quote: During the course of Hamburg/CfA Bright Quasar Survey we discovered a bright ( B=15.9 ), high redshift ( z_ em =2.51 ) quasar HS 1603+3820. The quasar has a rich complex of C iv absorbers, containing at least five systems, all within 3000 km s ^ 1 from one another. Despite large ejection velocity ( v_ ej >5000 km s ^ 1 for all components) the complex is likely to be associated with the quasar. There are at least three more associated heavy element absorbers, two of which have z_ abs >z_ em . Together, they make one of the richest known complexes of associated heavy element absorbers, and the richest in objects with B<16 .

sources.summary: The paper presents HS1603+3820 as a bright 2.54 quasar from the Hamburg/CfA survey and emphasizes its unusually rich heavy-element absorption spectrum.

quotes.quote: HS1603+3820 \ is a =2.54 quasar discovered during the Hamburg/CfA Bright Quasar Survey dobrzycki1996 . With B=15.9 , it is among the top few brightest quasars known in the =2-3 redshift interval veron2006 . The most striking feature of \ is, however, the extreme richness of its heavy element absorption spectrum, and in particular the absorption.

sources.summary: The paper reports that HS1603+3820 has a short low-count Chandra observation, bins its spectrum, and agrees with an independent analysis of the same data.

quotes.quote: HS1603+3820. --- The short \ observation of this quasar yielded only 137 counts. The spectrum was binned with a minimum of 10 counts per bin. An independent analysis of the same X-ray data by Dobrzycki et al. (2007) yields results consistent with ours.

gmw_00004919.name: PKS 1954 388
gmw_00004919.overview: PKS 1954 388 is a blazar active galactic nucleus that hosts a radio jet with superluminal motion [arXiv:1705.02067]. Multiwavelength monitoring reveals long-term radio flux density variability, including a strong radio flare that lagged nine months behind a prolonged gamma-ray high-state [arXiv:1705.02067].

sources.summary: The paper presents multiwavelength observations of blazar PKS 1954 388, finding high brightness temperature, Doppler boosting, jet motion, radio variability, gamma-ray/radio lag, and SED models.

quotes.quote: We present results from a multiwavelength study of the blazar PKS 1954 388 at radio, UV, X-ray, and gamma-ray energies. A RadioAstron observation at 1.66 GHz in June 2012 resulted in the detection of interferometric fringes on baselines of 6.2 Earth-diameters. This suggests a source frame brightness temperature of greater than 2 10 ^ 12 K, well in excess of both equipartition and inverse Compton limits and implying the existence of Doppler boosting in the core. An 8.4 GHz TANAMI VLBI image, made less than a month after the RadioAstron observations, is consistent with a previously reported superluminal motion for a jet component. Flux density monitoring with the Australia Telescope Compact Array confirms previous evidence for long-term variability that increases with observing frequency.

A search for more rapid variability revealed no evidence for significant day-scale flux density variation. The ATCA light-curve reveals a strong radio flare beginning in late 2013 which peaks higher, and earlier, at higher frequencies. Comparison with the Fermi gamma-ray light-curve indicates this followed 9 months after the start of a prolonged gamma-ray high-state --- a radio lag comparable to that seen in other blazars. The multiwavelength data are combined to derive a Spectral Energy Distribution, which is fitted by a one-zone synchrotron-self-Compton (SSC) model with the addition of external Compton (EC) emission.

gmw_00004920.name: NGC253-SNFC-dw1
gmw_00004920.overview: NGC253-SNFC-dw1 is an extremely diffuse satellite galaxy located approximately 3.62 Mpc away within the stellar halo of NGC 253 [arXiv:2403.16011]. It is dominated by an old, metal-poor stellar population and displays asymmetrical tidal extensions that suggest the system is highly disrupted [arXiv:2403.16011].

sources.summary: The paper discovers NGC253-SNFC-dw1 as an extremely diffuse, old metal-poor satellite of NGC 253 with tidal extensions, a 3.62 Mpc distance, and bound-halo location.

quotes.quote: We present the discovery of NGC253-SNFC-dw1, a new satellite galaxy in the remote stellar halo of the Sculptor Group spiral, NGC 253. The system was revealed using deep resolved star photometry obtained as part of the Subaru Near-Field Cosmology Survey that uses the Hyper Suprime-Cam on the Subaru Telescope. Although rather luminous ( = -11.7 0.2 ) and massive ( M_* 1.25 10^7 ), the system is one of the most diffuse satellites yet known, with a half-light radius of = 3.37 0.36 kpc and an average surface brightness of 30.1 mag arcmin ^ 2 within the . The colour-magnitude diagram shows a dominant old ( 10 Gyr) and metal-poor ( =-1.5 0.1 dex) stellar population, as well as several candidate thermally-pulsing asymptotic giant branch stars. The distribution of red giant branch stars is asymmetrical and displays two elongated tidal extensions pointing towards NGC 253, suggestive of a highly disrupted system being observed at apocenter.

We have used the TRGB method to derive consistent distances to SNFC-dw1 and NGC 253, finding them to lie at 3.62 0.2 Mpc and 3.5 0.2 Mpc, respectively. This places SNFC-dw1 at a 3D radius of 146 kpc on the far side of the NGC 253 halo and well within its estimated virial radius of 330 kpc 2021ApJ...918...88M , confirming it is a bound satellite. We find SNFC-dw1 to be dominated by an old metal-poor population; additionally, there is tentative evidence for an associated TP-AGB population.

gmw_00004921.name: J 133658.3--295105
gmw_00004921.overview: The radio source J 133658.3--295105 has been described as the core of an FR II radio source with two detected lobes [arXiv:0811.4750]. It is proposed to be either a distant radio galaxy or quasar, or an object ejected from the nucleus of M 83 [arXiv:0811.4750].

sources.summary: The paper presents Gemini imaging and spectroscopy of J 133658.3--295105, testing whether it is a high-redshift FR II radio source or a locally ejected object linked to M 83.

quotes.quote: We present Gemini optical imaging and spectroscopy of the radio source J 133658.3--295105. This source has been suggested to be the core of an FRII radio source with two detected lobes. J 133658.3--295105 and its lobes are aligned with the optical nucleus of M 83 and with three other radio sources at the M 83 bulge outer region.

The radio source J 133658.3--295105 (object 28 in list) in M 83, is described as the core of an FR II radio source whose radio lobes are objects 27 and 29 in the same list. The source presents characteristics of a radio galaxy or a quasar at a distance z 1 as discussed by . These authors have found that at a distance d _L = 4.7 Gpc ( z =1, q _0 = 0.5, H = 75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ), J 133658.3--295105 L_ 20 cm and L_ 6 cm fit reasonably well with the expected FR II radio-sources luminosities.

Two different scenarios are proposed: the radio source is at z 2.5 , a much larger distance than the previously proposed lower limit z 1.0 , or the object was ejected by a gravitational recoil event from the M 83 nucleus. This nucleus is undergoing a strong dynamical evolution judging from previous 3D spectroscopy.

gmw_00004922.name: LRG 1059+4039
gmw_00004922.overview: LRG 1059+4039 is a galaxy with a nearby quasar sightline located at a projected distance of 29 kpc [arXiv:2310.11717]. Its circumgalactic medium exhibits strong H I absorption along with various metal ions, and the surrounding environment contains several neighboring galaxies [arXiv:2310.11717].

sources.summary: The paper places a QSO sightline 29 kpc from LRG 1059+4039 and reports strong H I, several metal ions, an additional high-velocity component, and nearby galaxies.

quotes.quote: LRG 1059+4039: QSO sightline is located at 29 kpc from the LRG. Absorption lines from the LRG-CGM system include: H 1 transitions (strong H 1 with EW( H 1 1215) 5.63 , and N_ HI between 17.69 and 20.4); intermediate ions C 3 and Si 3 ; O 6 ; low ions C 2 , Si 2 , N 2 , Mg 2 , and Ca 2 ; and Mg 1 . We note that we measure EW( C 3 977) in two ways, by including and by not including the central part of the line, since that part might be blended.

Then, we found an average value between these two measurements. Because we do not know if the line is blended, the error in EW includes both cases, and is larger than it would be for the individual measurements. One additional component was found at 2500 km s ^ 1 , which shows H 1 and C 3 lines. Around the QSO sightline and close to the LRG's redshift, a few other galaxies are located: one blue NG, one NG close to the QSO sightline, one another LRG, and two red NGs.

gmw_00004923.name: LRG 1144+0714
gmw_00004923.overview: LRG 1144+0714 is a galaxy with a circumgalactic medium probed by a quasar sightline located 98 kpc away [arXiv:2310.11717]. This sightline reveals strong H I, intermediate-ion, low-ion, and O VI absorption, with no neighboring galaxies detected nearby [arXiv:2310.11717].

sources.summary: The paper places the sightline 98 kpc from LRG 1144+0714 and reports strong H I, C III, Si III, O VI, low-ion absorption, and no neighboring galaxies.

quotes.quote: LRG 1144+0714: QSO sightline is located at 98 kpc from the LRG. Absorption lines from the LRG-CGM system include: H 1 transitions (strong H 1 with EW( H 1 1215) 2.06 , and N_ HI between 17.91 and 19.4); intermediate ions C 3 and Si 3 ; O 6 ; and low ions C 2 , Si 2 , and Mg 2 . No any NGs are found around the QSO sightline.

gmw_00004924.name: LRG 1351+2753
gmw_00004924.overview: LRG 1351+2753 is a galaxy with a circumgalactic medium that exhibits strong H I, C III, Si III, and O VI absorption lines [arXiv:2310.11717]. The system also contains low-ionization absorption lines and is surrounded by two blue neighbor galaxies [arXiv:2310.11717].

sources.summary: The paper places the sightline 99 kpc from LRG 1351+2753 and reports strong H I, C III, Si III, O VI, low-ion absorption, possible Fe III, and two blue neighbors.

quotes.quote: LRG 1351+2753: QSO sightline is located at 99 kpc from the LRG. Absorption lines from the LRG-CGM system include: H 1 transitions (strong H 1 with EW( H 1 1215) 1.45 , and N_ HI 17.37 ); intermediate ions C 3 and Si 3 ; O 6 ; and low ions C 2 and Mg 2 . We also find possible Fe 3 , but define it as an upper limit. Two blue NGs are found around the QSO sightline.

gmw_00004925.name: LRG 1520+2534
gmw_00004925.overview: LRG 1520+2534 is a quenched galaxy associated with a nearby red neighbor galaxy [arXiv:2310.11717]. Its circumgalactic medium, probed by a sightline at a distance of 225 kpc, exhibits strong H I absorption along with intermediate and low ions [arXiv:2310.11717].

sources.summary: The paper places the sightline 225 kpc from LRG 1520+2534 and reports strong H I absorption, C III, Si III, Mg II, Fe II, and one red neighbor.

quotes.quote: LRG 1520+2534: QSO sightline is located at 225 kpc from the LRG. Absorption lines from the LRG-CGM system include: H 1 transitions (strong H 1 with EW( H 1 1215) 7.41 , and N_ HI between 17.86 and 20.65); intermediate ions C 3 and Si 3 ; and low ions Mg 2 and Fe 2 . One red NG is found around the QSO sightline.

gmw_00004926.name: LRG 1106-0115
gmw_00004926.overview: LRG 1106-0115 is a quenched, red sequence galaxy [arXiv:2310.11717]. Its circumgalactic medium, probed at a distance of 383 kpc, contains strong neutral hydrogen, intermediate ions, and low ions, and the galaxy is accompanied by a red neighbor [arXiv:2310.11717].

sources.summary: The paper places the sightline 383 kpc from LRG 1106-0115 and reports H I, C III, Si III, low ions, an additional H I component, and one red neighbor.

quotes.quote: LRG 1106-0115: QSO sightline is located at 383 kpc from the LRG. Absorption lines from the LRG-CGM system include: H 1 transitions strong H 1 with EW( H 1 1215) 0.55 , and N_ HI 17.26 ); intermediate ions C 3 and Si 3 ; low ions C 2 , Si 2 , and Mg 2 . One additional component was found at 600 km s ^ 1 (shows only H 1 ). One red NG is found around the QSO sightline.

gmw_00004927.name: LRG 1440-0157
gmw_00004927.overview: LRG 1440-0157 is a galaxy with a circumgalactic medium containing hydrogen, carbon, silicon, oxygen, and magnesium [arXiv:2310.11717]. Four neighboring galaxies, consisting of two red and two blue systems, have been identified around its nearby quasar sightline [arXiv:2310.11717].

sources.summary: The paper places the sightline 343 kpc from LRG 1440-0157 and reports H I, C III, Si III, O VI, Mg II, possible O IV and Ne VIII, and four neighbors.

quotes.quote: LRG 1440-0157: QSO sightline is located at 343 kpc from the LRG. Absorption lines from the LRG-CGM system include: H 1 transitions (strong H 1 with EW( H 1 1215) 1.7 , and N_ HI 17.02 )); intermediate ions C 3 and Si 3 ; O 6 ; and Mg 2 . We also detect possible O 4 and Ne 8 . Two red and two blue NGs are found around the QSO sightline.

gmw_00004928.name: LRG 1121+4345
gmw_00004928.overview: LRG 1121+4345 is a galaxy surrounded by a circumgalactic medium that contains strong neutral hydrogen and intermediate ions [arXiv:2310.11717]. This surrounding medium also exhibits low ions, oxygen VI, and potentially iron transitions, with both a blue and a red neighbor galaxy located nearby [arXiv:2310.11717].

sources.summary: The paper places the sightline 80 kpc from LRG 1121+4345 and reports strong H I, intermediate and low ions, O VI, possible Fe lines, and blue and red neighbors.

quotes.quote: LRG 1121+4345: QSO sightline is located at 80 kpc from the LRG. Absorption lines from the LRG-CGM system include: H 1 transitions (strong H 1 with EW( H 1 1215) 1.72 , and N_ HI between 17.89 and 19.2); intermediate ions C 3 and Si 3 ( Si 3 is blended with a strong H 1 1025 from a higher redshift); O 6 ; and low ions C 2 , Si 2 , N 2 , and Mg 2 . We also possibly detect Fe 2 and Fe 3 . One blue and one red NG are found around the QSO sightline.

gmw_00004929.name: LRG 1237+1106
gmw_00004929.overview: LRG 1237+1106 is a galaxy with a nearby quasar sightline located at a projected distance of 23 kpc [arXiv:2310.11717]. This sightline exhibits likely H I 1215 absorption at the galaxy's redshift, and one red neighboring galaxy has been identified nearby [arXiv:2310.11717].

sources.summary: The paper places the sightline 23 kpc from LRG 1237+1106 and identifies likely H I 1215 absorption at the LRG redshift, with one red neighboring galaxy.

quotes.quote: LRG 1237+1106: QSO sightline is located at 23 kpc from the LRG. Only H 1 1215 line is detected at a significance level > 3 . Since we detected only one line at wavelength that corresponds to the H 1 1215 at the LRG redshift, we compared apparent column density profiles for H 1 1215 and H 1 1025, and concluded that the absorption line is likely H 1 1215 at the LRG redshift. One red NG is found around the QSO sightline.

gmw_00004930.name: LRG 1549+0701
gmw_00004930.overview: LRG 1549+0701 is a quenched galaxy with a nearby quasar sightline located at an impact parameter of 120 kpc [arXiv:2310.11717]. No circumgalactic medium absorption lines are detected along this sightline, which is surrounded by three red neighboring galaxies [arXiv:2310.11717].

sources.summary: The paper places the sightline 120 kpc from LRG 1549+0701, reports no detected CGM absorption lines, and notes three red neighboring galaxies in the field.

quotes.quote: LRG 1549+0701: QSO sightline is located at 120 kpc from the LRG. No any CGM lines are detected. Three red NGs are found around the QSO sightline.

gmw_00004931.name: LRG 1306+3421
gmw_00004931.overview: LRG 1306+3421 is a galaxy with a quasar sightline located 184 kpc away [arXiv:2310.11717]. No circumgalactic medium absorption lines are detected along this sightline, which is surrounded by two red neighboring galaxies [arXiv:2310.11717].

sources.summary: The paper places the sightline 184 kpc from LRG 1306+3421, reports no detected CGM absorption lines, and notes two red neighboring galaxies in the field.

quotes.quote: LRG 1306+3421: QSO sightline is located at 184 kpc from the LRG. No any CGM lines are detected. Two red NGs are found around the QSO sightline.

gmw_00004932.name: LRG 1217+4931
gmw_00004932.overview: LRG 1217+4931 is a galaxy surrounded by a circumgalactic medium that contains relatively strong neutral hydrogen and intermediate-ion carbon III [arXiv:2310.11717]. Three red neighbor galaxies are located near the sightline of a nearby quasar [arXiv:2310.11717].

sources.summary: The paper places the sightline 208 kpc from LRG 1217+4931 and reports relatively strong H I and C III, while treating a possible O VI feature as interloping or blended.

quotes.quote: LRG 1217+4931: QSO sightline is located at 208 kpc from the LRG. Absorption lines from the LRG-CGM system include: H 1 transitions (relatively strong H 1 1215 with EW 1.45 , however for N_ HI we measure only an upper limit), and intermediate ion C 3 . We found an absorption line close to the wavelenght that corresponds to the O 6 1031 at the LRG's redshift, but concluded that the line is likely an interloping line from another redshift or blended. Three red NGs are found around the QSO sightline.

gmw_00004933.name: LRG 1102+4543
gmw_00004933.overview: LRG 1102+4543 is a galaxy with a quasar sightline located 193 kpc away [arXiv:2310.11717]. This sightline reveals absorption lines from the galaxy's circumgalactic medium, including hydrogen, carbon, and silicon transitions, and is surrounded by four neighboring galaxies [arXiv:2310.11717].

sources.summary: The paper places the sightline 193 kpc from LRG 1102+4543 and reports H I, C III, Si III, possible unconfirmed Ne VIII, an O VI-like interloper, and four neighbors.

quotes.quote: LRG 1102+4543: QSO sightline is located at 193 kpc from the LRG. Absorption lines from the LRG-CGM system include: H 1 transitions (relatively strong H 1 1215 with EW 2.52 , however for N_ HI we measure only an upper limit), and intermediate ions C 3 and Si 3 . We found a line close to the wavelenght that corresponds to O 6 1031 at the LRG's redshift, but concluded that the line is likely an interloping line from another redshift or blended. We also found that there is a possible Ne 8 770, however we were not able to confirm this detection, so we define it as an upper limit. One blue and three red NGs are found around the QSO sightline.

gmw_00004934.name: LRG 1251+3025
gmw_00004934.overview: LRG 1251+3025 is a galaxy with a nearby quasar sightline located at a distance of 281 kpc [arXiv:2310.11717]. No circumgalactic medium absorption lines are detected along this sightline, which features one blue and one red neighboring galaxy [arXiv:2310.11717].

sources.summary: The paper places the sightline 281 kpc from LRG 1251+3025, reports no detected CGM absorption lines, and notes one blue and one red neighboring galaxy.

quotes.quote: LRG 1251+3025: QSO sightline is located at 281 kpc from the LRG. No any CGM lines are detected. One blue and one red NG are found around the QSO sightline.

gmw_00004935.name: LRG 0226+0014
gmw_00004935.overview: LRG 0226+0014 is a galaxy with a nearby quasar sightline located at a projected distance of 357 kpc [arXiv:2310.11717]. No circumgalactic medium absorption lines are detected along this sightline, which also features one neighboring red galaxy [arXiv:2310.11717].

sources.summary: The paper places the sightline 357 kpc from LRG 0226+0014, reports no detected CGM absorption lines, and notes one red neighboring galaxy near the sightline.

quotes.quote: LRG 0226+0014: QSO sightline is located at 357 kpc from the LRG. No any CGM lines are detected. One red NG is found around the QSO sightline.

gmw_00004936.name: LRG 0855+5615
gmw_00004936.overview: LRG 0855+5615 is a galaxy with a circumgalactic medium that exhibits absorption lines of neutral hydrogen, oxygen VI, and potentially weak nitrogen II and carbon III [arXiv:2310.11717]. The system is surrounded by six neighboring galaxies, consisting of four red and two blue galaxies [arXiv:2310.11717].

sources.summary: The paper places the sightline 315 kpc from LRG 0855+5615 and reports H I, O VI, possible weak N II/blended C III, an additional H I/O VI component, and six neighbors.

quotes.quote: LRG 0855+5615: QSO sightline is located at 315 kpc from the LRG. Absorption lines from the LRG-CGM system include: H 1 transitions (relatively strong H 1 1215 with EW 1.32 , however for N_ HI we measure only an upper limit), O 6 , and possibly weak N 2 and blended C 3 . We find one additional component (absorption system) at 1000 km s ^ 1 , that shows H 1 and O 6 absorption. Four red and two blue NGs are found around the QSO sightline.

gmw_00004937.name: host galaxy of GRB 030528
gmw_00004937.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 030528 is a stellar system located at a redshift likely of the order of unity or less [arXiv:astro-ph/0408210]. Observations of its size and color suggest that it is a blue compact star-forming galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0408210].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the GRB 030528 host, measures optical and near-IR magnitudes, and argues it is probably a blue compact star-forming galaxy at roughly order-unity redshift.

quotes.quote: The center of the source is located at RA(2000)=17h04m00.3s, DEC(2000)=--22 37 10s. The positional coincidence with the afterglow suggests that this is the underlying host galaxy of GRB 030528. We cannot exclude a residual point-like contribution to the total flux, including the afterglow and possibly an additional supernova (Zeh 2004). A free fit to the K band afterglow light curve gives a host magnitude of K =19.9 0.7. Assuming that after about 10 days all fluxes at shorter wavelengths ( V - J ) are exclusively due to the host galaxy, we find a host magnitude of J =20.8 0.1, an average I band magnitude of I =21.3 0.3, R =22.0 0.2 and V =21.9 0.2.

It is thus clear that the redshift of the host of GRB 030528 is by no means established. However, it seems reasonable to interpret the observations to imply redshifts of the order of unity or less. At z =0.4 the angular extent of 1 5 of the host (see Fig. fig:Khost ) implies a linear dimension of 8.7 kpc (assuming standard cosmology). This would suggest the host to be a blue compact star forming galaxy.

gmw_00004938.name: host galaxy of GRB 090328
gmw_00004938.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 090328 is a starburst galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.7354 [arXiv:1003.3885]. It has an estimated stellar mass of approximately 10^9.4 solar masses and exhibits dust reddening [arXiv:1003.3885].

sources.summary: The paper fits the GRB 090328 host as a starburst galaxy, deriving dust reddening, absolute magnitudes, stellar mass near 10^9.4 solar masses, and elevated specific star formation.

quotes.quote: The redshift of the galaxy was fixed at z=0.7354 , which is consistent with the apparent 4000 Balmer break being within the ^ band. The host galaxy of GRB 090328 is best fit with a starburst galaxy template, but all other templates provide acceptable fits. Similar to the afterglow SED, there is evidence for dust reddening from the host observations of the order of A_V^ host = 0.5_ 0.3 ^ +0.5 in the starburst galaxy (although A_V^ host is not well constrained ), with a best-fit age of the dominant stellar population of 50 Myr. By folding the restframe best-fit template spectrum with a typical B and K filter response, we derive absolute AB magnitudes of the host galaxy of GRB 090328 of M_B =-20.67 and M_K = -20.17 , well within the samples of previous long GRB host galaxies e.g., 2004A&A...425..913C, 2009ApJ...691..182S .

The stellar mass of the host can be approximated from the best fitting template to M_ 10^ 9.4 M _ . When using the empirical relation derived by 2009ApJ...691..182S between K band absolute magnitude and stellar mass: M_ = -0.467 M_K - 0.179 , we derive M_ 10^ 9.24 M _ with a typical uncertainty of a factor 2, consistent with the previous estimate and similar to the stellar mass of the LMC. Using the dust-corrected star formation rate SFR =4.8 M _ yr ^ 1 (see note above on slit loss) and the above stellar mass, the specific star formation rate per unit mass rate is 2.8 Gyr ^ 1 again well within the sample of previous long burst host galaxies 2009ApJ...691..182S .

gmw_00004939.name: host galaxy of GRB 090510
gmw_00004939.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 090510 is an elliptical galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of 0.903 [arXiv:1003.3885]. It has a stellar mass of approximately 10^9.6 to 10^10.2 solar masses and exhibits modest star formation with slight evidence of dust extinction [arXiv:1003.3885].

sources.summary: The paper associates GRB 090510 with a nearby extended galaxy and fits its host SED as an acceptable elliptical template with modest star formation, dust, mass, and luminosity.

quotes.quote: The SED fitting for the host of GRB 090510 has been performed in a similar manner to that of GRB 090328 (Section 090328hostsec ). The redshift of the galaxy was fixed to the spectroscopic value of z=0.903 . Again all galaxy templates provide an acceptable fit, where the best fit is obtained with an elliptical galaxy. We note that the SFR is in the range of local star-forming elliptical galaxies 2009MNRAS.398.1651H .

From the best fit we derive AB absolute magnitudes M_B =-19.91 and M_K = -20.51 , and slight evidence for a dust extinction reddening similar to the SMC with a visual extinction of A_ V ^ host = 0.7^ +0.2 _ 0.4 . The stellar mass of the host can be estimated directly from the galaxy templates to M_ 10^ 9.6-10.2 M _ . The dust-corrected star formation rate per unit mass is 0.04 to 0.15 Gyr ^ 1 , significantly below that of the host galaxy of GRB 090328. Using the galaxy distribution function in the corresponding redshift interval from z=0.8 -1 from 2006ApJ...647..853W , the luminosity of the GRB host is estimated to 0.26-0.30L_* , and we obtain a dust-corrected star formation rate per unit luminosity of SFR / L_B 2 M _ yr ^ 1 L_*^ 1 for this host galaxy, consistent with the albeit wide range of SFR / L_* values for the short bursts presented by 2009ApJ...690..231B .

gmw_00004940.name: J1203+1624
gmw_00004940.overview: J1203+1624 is a peculiar active galactic nucleus at a redshift of z=0.1656 [arXiv:1811.06674]. It is classified as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 [arXiv:1811.06674] or narrow-line Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:2105.13672] that exhibits a compact host galaxy [arXiv:1811.06674] and a high degree of polarization [arXiv:2105.13672].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1203+1624 as a peculiar NLS1/type-2 AGN with strong narrow lines, polarized broad lines, high Eddington rate, and a compact host galaxy.

quotes.quote: We present spectropolarimetric and spectrophotometric observations of the peculiar active galactic nucleus (AGN) SDSS J120300.19+162443.7 (hereafter J1203+1624) at z=0.1656 . Its optical total flux spectra clearly show broad emission lines (BELs) in H and H . After removal of narrow emission lines (NELs), the full width at half maximum (FWHM) of the Lorentzian BEL is FWHM_ BEL 1270 , fulfilling the conventional definition of a narrow-line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) galaxy. However, its NELs are unprecedentedly strong when compared to type 1 AGNs.

This, together with its large MIR excess ( g-W_4 = 13.172 mag), implies that the observer and the NEL region might see a different ionization continuum. Our optical spectropolarimetry confirms its type 2 nature by detecting a polarized blue continuum and Balmer BELs ( FWHM_ Polarized BEL 1,183 ), with a high polarization degree of > 20 in the blue wing. The mass and Eddington rate of the central black hole are estimated based on both transmitted and scattered AGN radiation, which is M_ < 2.9 10^7 M_ and L_ bol /L_ Edd > 1.5 . Severe extinction of the AGN emission also enables a clear view of the compact host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1203+1624 as an NLS2 identified by spectropolarimetry, with strong scattered continuum, broad lines, and high polarization similar to NGC 1068.

quotes.quote: Recently, a NLS2 SDSS J120300.19+162443.7 (J1203+1624 hereafter) is identified via optical-IR spectrophotometry and spectropolarimetry.
Spectral analysis found out that the scattered featureless continuum and broad emission lines in J1203+1624 are so strong that they can be directly measured in the optical spectrum.

The first NLS2 reported of having broad Balmer emission lines with FWHM<2000 , i.e. J1203+1624, also shows a high degree of polarization of ( P 7.3 ). After the removal of starlight, the intrinsic polarization of the nucleus is around 13.6 , similar to NGC 1068.

gmw_00004941.name: NGC 4143
gmw_00004941.overview: NGC 4143 is a lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1610.02330] classified as a LINER [arXiv:1104.4891, arXiv:1201.5660] that hosts a nuclear bar [arXiv:1610.02330]. Its active nucleus exhibits compact subarcsecond radio core emission [arXiv:1201.5660] and ionized gas with a kinematic component consistent with an outflow [arXiv:2201.05080].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4143 as an S0 barlens galaxy whose central color peak is likely tied to an unresolved nuclear bar with roughly matching radial extent.

quotes.quote: NGC 4143 (nb): this is an S0 galaxy with an inclination of inc = 43.8 ^ . It hosts a nuclear bar with a radius of _ nb = 2 lauri2011 . Looking at its (i-z) color profiles, it seems likely that the observed central peak is related to this feature. This is suggested by the peak radial extension, which roughly corresponds to that of the nuclear bar.

This can also be seen in Appendix B, given that _ nb , normalized to the size of the main bar ( _ bar ) is 0.1, which is roughly the same extension of the central peak. However, in this case the central peak is unresolved because the FWHM is larger than the peak extension.

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 4143 snapshot and XMM observations with power-law models, finding no required intrinsic absorption in one fit and modest absorption in another.

quotes.quote: terashima03apj:rloud fit the \ snapshot observation with an absorbed power-law with N_ H <10^ 21 cm ^ 2 and 1.7 . We fit the same snapshot observation with a power-law without a requirement of an intrinsic absorption and found a similar power-law photon index within the error bars, 1.9 .
