We fit the \ observation with an absorbed power-law and found a N_ H =6 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 and a 2.2 .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4143 requires a truncated thin disk for optical emission, with a transition radius near 70 and an unusually small jet-model particle index.

quotes.quote: NGC 4143 The contribution of the truncated thin disk is required in order to account for the optical emission. The required transition radius in particular is r_ tr = 70 . The JD model requires a value of p smaller than the usual values suggested by relativistic shock theory, as was the case of NGC 266.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4143 as a LINER with a slightly inverted-to-flat radio spectrum, compact subarcsecond core emission, no extended jet, and high core brightness temperature.

quotes.quote: NGC 4143 has a slightly inverted to flat radio spectrum between 20 cm and 2 cm nagar00apj:radioliner . At the MERLIN resolution of sub-arcsecond, NGC 4143 shows a core emission, lacking any sign of extended jet emission. The core has a high surface brightness temperature of about 2 10^8 K.

sources.summary: NGC 4143 is modeled with a small-radius truncated thin disk or old stellar population for its optical point, while a jet origin for X-rays is disfavored.

quotes.quote: NGC 4143 The optical data point can be reproduced by emission from the truncated thin disk emission with the somewhat small transition radius of r_ tr = 70 . Alternatively, the optical observation can be fitted with an old stellar population with mass 5 10^7 M_ . Since the jet model requires p<2 to reproduce the X-ray spectrum, a jet origin for the X-ray emission in disfavored.

sources.summary: The atlas labels NGC 4143's H-alpha morphology Unclear because extended gas may be disk emission or outflowing material; its nuclear spectrum shows two components, the broadest consistent with outflow.

quotes.quote: NGC 4143 (see Fig. Figure_AB4 ): Our H morphology is classified as Unclear , given that the image shows extended emission along the galaxy disc, but part of the gas (or even the whole emission) could be associated to a possible outflow see Cazzoli2018 . The nuclear spectra of this galaxy was studied by based on TWIN/CAHA observations. They found two kinematic components in the ionised gas, with the broadest component consistent with an outflow.

gmw_00004942.name: NGC 3380
gmw_00004942.overview: NGC 3380 is a low-inclination S0/a lenticular galaxy that hosts a barlens structure [arXiv:1610.02330]. It features a blue central nucleus and a bar that is blue in its outer regions where it intersects a surrounding ring-like structure [arXiv:1610.02330].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3380 as a low-inclination S0/a barlens galaxy with a blue central region and nucleus, plus a blue outer bar where it crosses a ring-like structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3380 (B): this galaxy has Hubble stage S0/a and galaxy inclination of inc = 20.8 ^ . Belonging to category B, the barlens has a blue central region within a few arcseconds inside a redder barlens. The blue nucleus within a few arcseconds appears in both colors, but it is particularly clear in (g-) , indicating that it consists of younger stellar populations than the barlens. The bar is blue in its outskirts, and in the zone where it crosses the surrounding ring-like structure (rs).

gmw_00004943.name: JADES-GS-53.07597-27.80654
gmw_00004943.overview: JADES-GS-53.07597-27.80654 is a high-redshift galaxy system at a photometric redshift of approximately 11.27 [arXiv:2306.02468]. It consists of two bright, connected clumps that may represent interacting seed galaxies or early star-forming regions [arXiv:2306.02468].

sources.summary: The paper highlights JADES-GS-53.07597-27.80654 as a z_a=11.27 system of two bright connected clumps also separated in FRESCO medium-band data.

quotes.quote: Finally, JADES-GS-53.07597-27.80654 ( z_a = 11.27 , third row, right column) consists of two, bright, connected clumps separated by 0.2^ (580 pc at this photometric redshift). The sources are detected as separate clumps in the relatively shallower FRESCO F182M and F210M data as well. These sources could be interacting seed galaxies or star-forming clumps in the very early universe. z_ phot > 12 Candidates We find 33 galaxies and candidate galaxies across both the JADES GOODS-S (23 sources) and GOODS-N (10 sources) footprints at z > 12 .

gmw_00004944.name: JADES-GN-189.15981+62.28898
gmw_00004944.overview: JADES-GN-189.15981+62.28898 is a high-redshift candidate galaxy located in the GOODS-N field [arXiv:2306.02468]. It is identified as an F200W dropout galaxy and is clearly detected in multiple long-wavelength filters [arXiv:2306.02468].

sources.summary: The paper calls JADES-GN-189.15981+62.28898 the highest-redshift candidate in the sample, an F200W dropout clearly detected in multiple long-wavelength filters with strong high-redshift evidence.

quotes.quote: We include brief discussions of two of the spectroscopically confirmed galaxies from robertson2022 and curtislake2022 , JADES-GS-z12-0 and JADES-GS-z13-0, but we refer the reader to these papers for more detailed discussions of these sources. z > 12 Candidates, Part I \ \ \ \ \ \ JADES-GN-189.15981+62.28898 ( z_a = 18.79 ) This F200W dropout, the highest-redshift candidate in our sample, is clearly detected in multiple LW filters. There is no detection in the F200W filter, and we calculate a dropout color assuming a 2 upper-limit on the F200W flux of m_ F200W m_ F277W > 1.29 . While this source lies in the relatively shallower GOODS-N NW portion of the survey, the large ^2 provides strong evidence for this source being at high redshift.

gmw_00004945.name: JADES-GS-53.12692-27.79102
gmw_00004945.overview: JADES-GS-53.12692-27.79102 is a relatively bright Lyman-break galaxy identified as an F150W dropout [arXiv:2306.02468]. Photometric analysis suggests a high redshift of approximately 15.77, though weak F115W flux means it could alternatively be located at a redshift of 5 [arXiv:2306.02468].

sources.summary: The paper describes JADES-GS-53.12692-27.79102 as a bright, intriguing F150W dropout at z_a=15.77, while cautioning that weak F115W flux leaves a z=5 alternative.

quotes.quote: JADES-GS-53.12692-27.79102 ( z_a = 15.77 ) This is one of the more intriguing objects in our sample, as it is a relatively bright ( m_ F277W, Kron = 29.37 ) F150W dropout detected at greater than 16 in all of the detection bands. While there may be F115W flux observed in the thumbnail, it is only at SNR = 1.76. Caution should be exercised in adopting the derived redshift for this source as a result, since this object's fluxes are consistent with it being at z = 5 .

gmw_00004946.name: JADES-GS-53.19592-27.7555
gmw_00004946.overview: JADES-GS-53.19592-27.7555 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy with an estimated redshift of 15.32 [arXiv:2306.02468]. This slightly extended source is isolated from bright objects and is characterized by a non-detection in the F150W filter [arXiv:2306.02468].

sources.summary: The paper presents JADES-GS-53.19592-27.7555 as a slightly extended F150W dropout at z_a=15.32, isolated from bright sources and constrained by a non-detection in F150W.

quotes.quote: JADES-GS-53.19592-27.7555 ( z_a = 15.32 ) This slightly extended F150W dropout has SNR >5 in three filters: F277W, F356W, and F444W. It is also over 3^ away from any bright sources. Because of the non-detection in F150W, we estimate that m_ F150W m_ F200W > 0.74 given a 2 upper limit on the observed F150W flux.

gmw_00004947.name: JADES-GS-53.07557-27.87268
gmw_00004947.overview: JADES-GS-53.07557-27.87268 is a faint candidate high-redshift galaxy with a photometric redshift estimate of 14.4 [arXiv:2312.10033] or 15.31 [arXiv:2306.02468]. It is surrounded by brighter, lower-redshift sources [arXiv:2306.02468] and has a non-negligible low-redshift probability of being at redshift 3.8 [arXiv:2312.10033].

sources.summary: The paper presents JADES+53.07557-27.87268 as a faint z=14.4 candidate, but flags an unusual SED and non-negligible low-redshift probability.

quotes.quote: JADES+53.07557-27.87268; NIRCam ID 376946 Figure fig:sed_376946 shows the best-fit SED for object JADES+53.07557-27.87268 (NIRcam ID 376946). This faint ( m_ AB =30.5 ) object at redshift z_a=14.4 is slightly redder than most of the other candidates. JADES+53.07557-27.87268 displays an unusual SED in that either the F182M and F210M fluxes must be biased low by several sigma to be consistent with the F200W flux, and the high-redshift solution does not match well the observed F182M, F200W, and F210M data. The best solution at low redshift has z_ low = 3.8 with nearly 8 of the EAZY probability, although it overpredicts the observed F150W flux.

sources.summary: The paper identifies JADES-GS-53.07557-27.87268 as one of the faintest z>12 candidates, detected in three filters and surrounded by brighter lower-redshift sources.

quotes.quote: JADES-GS-53.07557-27.87268 ( z_a = 15.31 ) This is one of the faintest z > 12 sources ( m_ F277W, Kron = 30.04 ), although it is observed at SNR > 5 in three filters: F277W, F356W, and F444W. In the thumbnail we show how this candidate is surrounded by other, brighter sources. The sources to the northwest and southeast are both at z_a 1.0 , while the source with multiple components to the northeast is an F435W dropout at z_a = 3.74 .

gmw_00004948.name: JADES-GN-189.16733+62.31026
gmw_00004948.overview: JADES-GN-189.16733+62.31026 is a galaxy identified as a bright F150W dropout with an estimated redshift of 14.33 [arXiv:2306.02468]. However, because it is located near another dropout source, its apparent Lyman break could instead be a Balmer break [arXiv:2306.02468].

sources.summary: The paper describes JADES-GN-189.16733+62.31026 as a bright F150W dropout at z_a=14.33, but warns its apparent break could instead be a Balmer break.

quotes.quote: JADES-GN-189.16733+62.31026 ( z_a = 14.33 ) This F150W dropout is very bright in F277W ( m_ F277W, Kron = 27.28 ), pushing it above the distribution at these redshifts as seen in Figure mag_vs_za . It is within 0.5^ of an F435W dropout at z_a = 4.34 . As a result, the potential Lyman- break for this object could be a Balmer break if these two sources are associated at similar redshifts.

gmw_00004949.name: JADES-GS-53.06475-27.89024
gmw_00004949.overview: JADES-GS-53.06475-27.89024 is a high-redshift candidate galaxy with a photometric redshift estimated near z=12.9 [arXiv:2312.10033] to z=14.0 [arXiv:2306.02468]. It is identified as an F150W dropout [arXiv:2306.02468] and its best-fit template shows Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:2312.10033].

sources.summary: The paper presents JADES+53.06475-27.89024 as a z=12.9 candidate whose NIRCam photometry peaks strongly at high redshift and whose low-redshift fit conflicts with multiple blue filters.

quotes.quote: JADES+53.06475-27.89024; NIRCam ID 13731 Figure fig:sed_13731 shows the best-fit SED for object JADES+53.06475-27.89024 NIRCam ID 13731, hainline23 . The long-wavelength JWST/NIRCam photometry shows m_ AB 29.5 , and constrains the posterior photometric redshift distribution to be peaked strongly near z_a=12.9 . The best-fit low-redshift solution at z_ low =3.5 would exceed the F090W, F115W, F150W, and F162M photometry by several standard deviations. The best-fit template shows Lyman- emission.

sources.summary: The paper presents JADES-GS-53.06475-27.89024 as a promising z_a=14.0 F150W dropout, strongly detected in F277W and lacking WFC3 or ACS detections.

quotes.quote: JADES-GS-53.06475-27.89024 ( z_a = 14.0 ) This F150W dropout ( m_ F150W m_ F200W > 2.27 ) is detected in the F277W filter at 19.9 , is found in the exceptionally deep GOODS-S JADES 1210 Parallel. In this region, there are no medium band observations from either JEMS or FRESCO for this source, and we do not see any detection in any of the WFC3 or ACS bands. This source a quite promising high-redshift candidate, with a ^2 65 .

gmw_00004950.name: JADES-GS-53.08016-27.87131
gmw_00004950.overview: JADES-GS-53.08016-27.87131 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate with a photometric redshift of approximately 16.74 [arXiv:2306.02468]. It is detected faintly at shorter wavelengths but is relatively bright at longer wavelengths, though its photometry may be affected by a nearby pair of interacting galaxies [arXiv:2306.02468].

sources.summary: The paper highlights JADES-GS-53.08016-27.87131 as an interesting z_a=16.74 proximate-source candidate, bright at long wavelengths but possibly contaminated by nearby interacting galaxies.

quotes.quote: Notably, there are three galaxies at z_a > 12 : JADES-GS-53.08016-27.87131 ( z_a = 16.74 ), JADES-GS-53.09671-27.86848 ( z_a = 12.03 ), and JADES-GN-189.23121+62.1538 ( z_a = 12.16 ). JADES-GS-53.08016-27.87131 has ^2 < 4 , although this source is the most interesting due to its photometric redshift. This source has a very faint F200W detection ( SNR > 4 in an 0.2^ diameter aperture), but is relatively bright at longer wavelengths (with an F277W AB magnitude of 29.20). This source lies 3^ 4^ north of a pair of interacting galaxies at z_ spec = 1.1 bonzini2012, momcheva2016 , and flux from the outskirts of these galaxies may be contributing to the aperture photometry for this object, leading to an artificially red UV slope. We caution that this source may be a stellar cluster associated with this pair or the dusty galaxies that are to the north of its position.

gmw_00004951.name: JADES-GN-189.19772+62.25697
gmw_00004951.overview: JADES-GN-189.19772+62.25697 is a high-redshift galaxy with a systemic redshift of approximately 8.28 [arXiv:2404.06569, arXiv:2306.02468]. It is a Lyman-alpha emitter that exhibits strong oxygen emission lines and a very small Lyman-alpha peak velocity offset [arXiv:2404.06569].

sources.summary: The paper presents JADES-1899 as a z=8.28 Ly emitter with small velocity offset, strong Ly flux, and evidence for reduced IGM attenuation.

quotes.quote: Here we find another z 8 galaxy (JADES-1899 at z=8.28 ) with an even smaller peak velocity, potentially requiring very different surrounding IGM than most z 8 galaxies discovered to date. This galaxy is also recently reported in Witstok2024 . In the top of Figure fig:z8p3_lae_spec , we show the medium resolution grating spectrum of JADES-1899. Strong [ O III ] 4959 and 5007 emission lines are clearly seen in its NIRSpec G395M/F290LP spectrum.

By fitting [ O III ] with Gaussian profiles we derive a systemic redshift of z_ sys =8.2791 . In the G140M/F070LP spectrum, we detect the Ly emission line with z_ Ly =8.2801 . This indicates a Ly peak velocity close to the systemic redshift, with v_ peak =32 47 km s ^ 1 . The line profile is asymmetric, cutting off sharply at line center with minimal blue-sided emission.

sources.summary: The paper notes JADES-GN-189.19772+62.25697 has brown-dwarf-like evidence, but its FRESCO [OIII] emission-line redshift rules out the brown dwarf hypothesis.

quotes.quote: Of these objects, only two sources are both unresolved and have stellar fits within ^2 < 4 : JADES-GS-53.0353-27.87776 ( z_a = 10.82 ) and JADES-GN-189.19772+62.25697 ( z_a = 8.61 ). The latter source, which is detected with HST WFC3/IR, was identified as the Y-dropout candidate GNDY-6474515254 in bouwens2015 . While this object has evidence for being a brown dwarf, FRESCO identified both [ OIII ] 5007,4959 emission lines ( z_ spec = 8.28 ), ruling out the brown dwarf hypothesis.

gmw_00004952.name: JADES-GS-53.16863-27.79276
gmw_00004952.overview: JADES-GS-53.16863-27.79276 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate [arXiv:2306.02468]. Photometric analyses estimate its redshift to be approximately 11.71 [arXiv:2306.02468].

sources.summary: The paper says JADES-GS-53.16863-27.79276 is a newly noted sample member at z_a=11.71, independently recovered by Donnan et al., with literature photometric redshifts similar to this study.

quotes.quote: The remaining two sources were not previously known, and also appear in our sample: JADES-GS-53.13918-27.78273 ( z_a = 10.49 ) and JADES-GS-53.16863-27.79276 ( z_a = 11.71 ). donnan2023 perform a similar search, and find two additional candidates that fall into our sample: JADES-GS-53.17551-27.78064 ( z_a = 9.66 ), and JADES-GS-53.12166-27.83012 ( z_a = 9.42 ) (they also independently recover JADES-GS-53.16863-27.79276). The photometric redshifts presented in bouwens2023 for JADES-GS-53.13918-27.78273 (XDFH-2334046578 in their sample, z_ EAZY = 11.8^ +0.4 _ 0.5 ) and JADES-GS-53.16863-27.79276 (XDFJ-2404647339 in their sample, z_ EAZY = 11.4^ +0.4 _ 0.5 ) are broadly similar to our values, but we measure a much lower redshift for the former due to the availability of the F150W flux from JADES. Similarly, donnan2023 , estimate similar photometric redshifts to what we find for JADES-GS-53.17551-27.78064 (UDF-21003 in their sample, z_ phot = 9.79^ +0.15 _ 0.13 ) and JADES-GS-53.16863-27.79276 (UDF 16748 in their sample, z_ phot = 11.77^ +0.29 _ 0.44 ), but they claim a much higher redshift for JADES-GS-53.12166-27.83012 (UDF-3216 in their sample, z_ phot = 12.56^ +0.64 _ 0.66 ), which is inconsistent with the measured F150W flux.

gmw_00004953.name: JADES-GS-53.12166-27.83012
gmw_00004953.overview: JADES-GS-53.12166-27.83012 is a high-redshift galaxy with an estimated photometric redshift of 9.42 [arXiv:2306.02468]. This object is located in close proximity to a brighter source [arXiv:2306.02468].

sources.summary: The paper includes JADES-GS-53.12166-27.83012 in the sample at z_a=9.42, rejecting a higher Donnan et al. redshift and flagging proximity to a brighter object.

quotes.quote: The remaining two sources were not previously known, and also appear in our sample: JADES-GS-53.13918-27.78273 ( z_a = 10.49 ) and JADES-GS-53.16863-27.79276 ( z_a = 11.71 ). donnan2023 perform a similar search, and find two additional candidates that fall into our sample: JADES-GS-53.17551-27.78064 ( z_a = 9.66 ), and JADES-GS-53.12166-27.83012 ( z_a = 9.42 ) (they also independently recover JADES-GS-53.16863-27.79276).

Similarly, donnan2023 , estimate similar photometric redshifts to what we find for JADES-GS-53.17551-27.78064 (UDF-21003 in their sample, z_ phot = 9.79^ +0.15 _ 0.13 ) and JADES-GS-53.16863-27.79276 (UDF 16748 in their sample, z_ phot = 11.77^ +0.29 _ 0.44 ), but they claim a much higher redshift for JADES-GS-53.12166-27.83012 (UDF-3216 in their sample, z_ phot = 12.56^ +0.64 _ 0.66 ), which is inconsistent with the measured F150W flux. We note that this latter candidate appears in our catalog of sources proximate to brighter objects, although with ^2 = 4.32 .

gmw_00004954.name: JADES-GS-53.0143-27.88355
gmw_00004954.overview: JADES-GS-53.0143-27.88355 is a faint galaxy with an apparent Kron magnitude of 29.3 [arXiv:2306.02468]. Although initially identified as a potential high-redshift dropout at z ~ 12.51, its red UV slope and a flux detection in the F115W band suggest a dustier, lower-redshift solution at z ~ 3.41 [arXiv:2306.02468].

sources.summary: The paper uses JADES-GS-53.0143-27.88355 as a visually rejected high-redshift example: it appears like an F150W dropout but its red UV slope and F115W flux favor lower redshift.

quotes.quote: JADES-GS-53.0143-27.88355 ( m_ Kron, AB = 29.3 ), appears from the thumbnails and from the EAZY minimum ^2 fit to be an F150W dropout at z_a = 12.51 . However, the red UV slope indicates that perhaps this object is much dustier and at low-redshift ( z_ alt = 3.41 ), where the H emission line was boosting the observed F277W flux. This UV slope could also arise from the bright source to the southeast at z_ spec = 0.2472 , cooper2012 . In addition there is what appears to be a flux detection in the F115W thumbnail which helps to rule out the high-redshift solution.

gmw_00004955.name: JADES-GS-53.08294-27.85563
gmw_00004955.overview: JADES-GS-53.08294-27.85563, also identified as JADES-GS-z14-0, is a high-redshift galaxy at z = 14.32 [arXiv:2405.18462]. It contains approximately half a billion solar masses in stars and experienced a strong burst of star formation in its most recent few million years [arXiv:2405.18462].

sources.summary: The paper treats JADES-GS-53.08294-27.85563 as a rejected high-redshift case near another object, with possible Balmer-break contamination and planned or forthcoming follow-up.

quotes.quote: JADES-GS-53.08294-27.85563 ( m_ F277W, Kron = 26.8 ) appears to be a bright F150W dropout clump immediately adjacent to another object. The SED is well fit at z_a = 14.51 , and the fit constrained to be at z < 7 is significantly worse ( z_ alt = 3.56 ). The secondary source, which is detected with all five of the JADES HST/ACS bands (although only at 2 significance for F435W), has an EAZY template redshift z_a = 3.4 . This redshift puts the Balmer break between the NIRCam F150W and F200W filters, and we cannot rule out the possibility that this is what we are observing for JADES-GS-53.08294-27.85563. This source appeared in a sample of ``HST dark'' galaxies in williams2023b , where they present a photometric redshift of z_ phot = 3.38 , although they use a larger aperture for measuring photometry which may introduce flux from the nearby source.

JADES-GS-53.08294-27.85563 was further imaged in six NIRCam medium-band filters as part of the JWST Cycle-2 GO observations of the JADES Origins Field JOF, PID 3215; eisenstein2023b , and a more detailed analysis of this source will be presented in a forthcoming paper from the collaboration (Robertson, B. et al. in prep.). Both sources will be observed by the NIRSpec prism in JWST GTO program 1287 (Willott, C. in prep).

sources.summary: The paper reports MIRI 7.7 micron photometry of JADES-GS-z14-0 at z=14.32, inferring about half a billion solar masses, recent star formation, rest-optical line contribution, rapid assembly, and metal enrichment.

quotes.quote: We report the photometric detection of the most distant spectroscopically confirmed galaxy JADES-GS-z14-0 at z = 14.32^ +0.08 _ 0.20 with MIRI at 7.7 \ m . The most plausible solution for the stellar population properties is that this galaxy contains half a billion solar masses in stars with a strong burst of star formation in the most recent few million years. For this model, at least one-third of the flux at 7.7 \ m comes from the rest-optical emission lines H and/or [ OIII ] 4959,5007 . The inferred properties of JADES-GS-z14-0 suggest rapid mass assembly and metal enrichment during the earliest phases of galaxy formation.

gmw_00004956.name: JADES-GS-53.20055-27.78493
gmw_00004956.overview: JADES-GS-53.20055-27.78493 is a galaxy located southeast of a brighter source [arXiv:2306.02468]. Although it initially appeared to be an F200W dropout at a redshift of 15.89, this interpretation was ruled out due to the detection of F090W flux [arXiv:2306.02468].

sources.summary: The paper says JADES-GS-53.20055-27.78493 appears like an F200W dropout at z_a=15.89, but F090W flux rules out the F200W-dropout interpretation.

quotes.quote: JADES-GS-53.20055-27.78493 ( m_ F277W, Kron = 28.8 ) appears to be an F200W dropout at z_a = 15.89 southeast of another, brighter source. While positive flux is observed at the 1 - 2 nJy level in F115W and F150W, this is at a SNR < 0.8 in both cases. This source was ruled out as an F200W dropout because of the detection at the 4 of F090W flux, which can be seen in the thumbnail.

gmw_00004957.name: JADES-GN-189.30986+62.20844
gmw_00004957.overview: JADES-GN-189.30986+62.20844 is a galaxy with a faint F115W detection [arXiv:2306.02468]. It is located proximate to a brighter, lower-redshift galaxy that exhibits a complex morphology [arXiv:2306.02468].

sources.summary: The paper describes JADES-GN-189.30986+62.20844 as best fit at z_a=11.36, but proximity to a brighter galaxy and F115W detection make that solution erroneous.

quotes.quote: JADES-GN-189.30986+62.20844 ( m_ F277W, Kron = 27.4 ) is best fit at z_a = 11.36 , placing the observed Lyman- break at 1.5 m. This object is proximate to another, brighter galaxy with an EAZY fit at z_a = 1.87 with a complex morphology first observed as part of the GOODS survey in giavalisco2004 . This is at a lower redshift than the alternate EAZY result for JADES-GN-189.30986+62.20844, z_a = 2.58 , but the faint F115W detection (SNR = 3.64) demonstrates that the minimum ^2 redshift solution for this object is erroneous.

gmw_00004958.name: NGC 5557
gmw_00004958.overview: NGC 5557 is a massive, round core elliptical galaxy classified as a slow rotator [arXiv:1408.6846, arXiv:2001.11277]. The galaxy is a relaxed merger remnant that exhibits multiple shells, disturbed outer isophotes, and an elongated eastern tidal tail hosting candidate tidal dwarf galaxies [arXiv:1105.5654, arXiv:1403.0626].

sources.summary: The paper fits HST imaging of NGC 5557 as a secure core elliptical, reproducing its light profile and noting tidal-tail evidence for a past gas-rich major merger.

quotes.quote: We demonstrate the software by fitting the images of NGC 5557 and NGC 5813 , which have been previously identified as galaxies by their 1D radial light profiles. These two examples are representative of different dust obscuration conditions, and of bulge/disk decomposition.

We present here a practical application of our code on two galaxies: NGC 5557 and NGC 5813 , selected from the catalogue of ``secure'' core galaxies of dullo:2013b . These objects have been chosen because of their relatively large break radii ( r_ b ), which best suits the illustrative purposes of this example.

Contrarily, using deep MegaCam exposures, found that NGC 5557 shows clear evidence for extremely elongated tidal tails (for a total extent of 375 kpc), and high fine-structure index schweizer , which they attributed to a recent (z < 0.5) gas-rich major merger event. Though, the authors acknowledge that this hypothesis can be reconciled with the mild rotation of NGC 5557 only under the specific assumption that the merger collision had a low impact parameter.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5557 as a massive slow rotator with a 160 kpc eastern tidal tail, western debris, shells, HI clouds, and candidate tidal dwarf galaxies.

quotes.quote: Among them, a gigantic 160 kpc long, narrow, tail East of NGC 5557 hosts three gas-rich star-forming objects, previously detected in HI with the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope and in UV with GALEX.

NGC 5557 The most striking optical structures of NGC 5557 are, to the East, a long straight, narrow, filament (Tidal Tail-E on Fig. fig:N5557-struct ), and to the West a broader, more complex structure, apparently composed of an extended object at its tip (TO2-W), a ring (R-W) and a bridge (TO1-W). We will argue in the following that some of these features are the optical counterparts of the apparent free-floating HI gas clouds that had previously been detected around the ETG by the WSRT. The Eastern tail stretches out from the diffuse halo around the elliptical; it is lost 15 arcmin further to the East in a region of the MegaCam frame with a high local background that could not be removed (see Fig. fig:deep-field ). Its projected distance is at least 160 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5557 as a relaxed elliptical merger remnant with long tidal tails whose debris hosts candidate old tidal dwarf galaxies.

quotes.quote: Among the selected ETGs, NGC 5557 appears as the most promising system for hosting old TDGs. Indeed, as shown in , the galaxy exhibits on MegaCam images several prominent features typical of merger remnants such as 200 kpc long stellar filaments, plumes and shells. Its eastern tidal tail hosts three blue objects, and associated with them, three isolated \ clouds, referred later as NGC 5557-E1, E2 and E3.

The inner region of their host galaxy, NGC 5557, is fully relaxed. It does not contain any specific kinematical features Krajnovic11 - the galaxy is a slow rotator -- dust lanes, \ or CO clouds, or even young stellar populations that would indicate a recent major gas-rich merger.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 5557 separately as an additional massive ETG and finds evidence for an outward decrease in IMF slope, supported by Na-index gradients.

quotes.quote: As shown in Appendix sec:NGC5557 , the additional massive galaxy (NGC 5557) also exhibits a decrement of _ b between the central part and the outer region, with _ b =-1.5 (-0.8) , from the centre to 1(0.7) .

The Figure also plots the Na I 8190 gradient for the additional massive system, NGC 5557, observed as part of the same observational campaign as NGC 4387 and NGC 4552, but analyzed apart in Appendix sec:NGC5557 . Remarkably, for the low-mass ETG, no radial gradient in Na I 8190 is detected, while a strong decrease is observed with galactocentric distance for the high-mass galaxies. As shown in Fig. fig:na , the metallicity gradient of NGC 4552 cannot explain, by itself, the Na I 8190 gradient (as also seen by comparing the size of the orange arrow, to the range of values for the black curves, in the upper panel of Fig. fig:nall ). Moreover, the Na I 8190 index is expected to decrease with [ /Fe ] (according to CvD12 stellar population models), whereas NGC 4552, as well as NGC 5557, both have high [ /Fe ] in the centre, and a very high Na I 8190 line-strength. In conclusion, the radial gradients of Na features, for high-mass galaxies, also point to a significant radial variation of the stellar IMF, with an excess of low-mass stars confined to their central regions.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5557 as the early-type host of three TDGs, estimating its virial mass from a JAM rotation velocity while warning that its halo profile is uncertain.

quotes.quote: NGC 5557 Duc2014 found three TDGs around the early-type galaxy NGC5557.

Cappellari2013a derived a maximum value of the circular velocity for NGC5557 of v_ max = 340 km s ^ 1 from their best-fitting mass-follow-light JAM (Jeans Anisotropic Multi-gaussian expansion) model. The virial mass of NGC 5557 can therefore be estimated with M_ vir = 2.33 10^5 v_ max km s ^ 1 ^3 for an NFW potential Navarro1998 .
As the estimates on the mass and DM profile of NGC 5557 are only based on one measurement of the maximum rotation velocity, no precise statements can be made yet about the individual objects.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5557 as a massive, round core slow rotator with a gas-rich tidal tail, young central stellar age, and formation history linked to dissipative merging.

quotes.quote: Furthermore, it is useful to recall that some core slow rotators could also be created through dissipative mergers. NGC 5557 is one such case 2011MNRAS.417..863D , a relatively massive (  2 10^ 11 M _ ) and round core slow rotator . This galaxy, very typical of slow rotators in its central regions, has a long and narrow tidal tail featuring gas-rich and star-forming objects, which are likely tidal dwarf galaxies. The long-lived tidal structures in NGC 5557 are likely related to the event that formed the present galaxy, as the luminosity-weighted stellar age in the central regions is only 1-2 Gy old, even though no evidence of a starburst remains and the surface brightness core is detectable. NGC 5557 is likely a special case and could have ended as a core fast rotator .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5557 as a rich shell galaxy with about nine MATLAS-visible shells plus irregular tidal features reaching roughly 160 kpc.

quotes.quote: NGC 5557, Fig. fig:lsma , is another rich shell galaxy with around nine shells visible in the MATLAS image. There are more tidal features of irregular shapes found up to about 13 (160 kpc) from the center see also duc11 . In the MATLAS survey, NGC 5557 is classified as having shells, tails, and disturbed outer isophotes matlas20 .

gmw_00004959.name: NGC 5485
gmw_00004959.overview: NGC 5485 is an early-type lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1407.7278] that exhibits prolate-like rotation [arXiv:2001.11277] and contains a dust lane perpendicular to its photometric major axis [arXiv:1407.7278]. The galaxy features a disturbed, asymmetrical stellar halo with a stream or tail [arXiv:2103.05006], yet it possesses an unusually low cold gas-to-dust ratio [arXiv:1407.7278].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 5485 as a dust-lane lenticular with Herschel dust detections, stringent H I and CO non-detections, and an unusually low gas-to-dust ratio.

quotes.quote: In this Letter we focus on NGC 5485, a dust-lane ETG at a distance of 25.2 Mpc Throughout this Letter we use this distance for NGC 5485; all luminosities and masses are converted to be consistent with this value. 2001ApJ...546..681T, 2011MNRAS.413..813C . It is home to a prominent dust lane, perpendicular to the photometric major axis (Fig. NGC5485- , top left). Given the prominent dust lane, one would expect NGC 5485 to contain a large cold interstellar matter reservoir.

The galaxy is detected by IRAS, with a corresponding dust mass of about 10^6 M_ 2007A&A...461..103P, 2010MNRAS.407.2475F . Similarly to a third of the dust-lane ETGs from the 2002AJ....123..729O sample, NGC 5485 has a low H i content: in the frame of the ATLAS ^ 3D campaign, 2012MNRAS.422.1835S observed the galaxy at 21 cm with the WSRT, but did not detect it. The corresponding upper limit to the atomic gas mass is M_ H i < 1.5 10^7 M_ . Surprisingly, also no molecular gas is detected: 2011MNRAS.414..940Y observed NGC 5485 in the CO(1-0) and CO(2-1) lines with the IRAM 30m telescope, but did not detect it in either line.
Combining the dust mass resulting from the Herschel flux densities with the molecular and atomic gas measurements discussed in 1, we find a cold gas-to-dust upper limit M_ gas /M_ d < 14.5 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5485 as a massive core fast rotator with prolate-like rotation and a polar nuclear gas disc, likely requiring a special dissipation-less merger configuration.

quotes.quote: Alternative examples are provided by NGC 4649 and NGC 5485. The former is the third most luminous galaxy in the Virgo cluster and the latter has a prolate-like (around the major axis) rotation 2011MNRAS.414.2923K . These two galaxies are massive and have relatively high _e . This is especially the case for NGC 4649, which is one of the most massive galaxies in the ATLAS ^ 3D sample, the most massive fast rotator (see Fig  f:ms ), and also has one of highest _e in the sample. The prolate-like rotation of NGC 5485 requires a special merging configuration, possibly favouring a dissipation-less type of merger as it also has a nuclear gas disc in polar configuration 2017A&A...606A..62T,2017ApJ...850..144E,2018MNRAS.473.1489L . These galaxies are much more similar to core slow rotators , and it is possible that their classification depends on the definition of fast rotators as much as their angular momenta depend on the chance outcome of the formation process.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5485 as having a disturbed asymmetric stellar halo with a stream or tail, possible shell, central ring-like dust, and MATLAS disturbance classifications.

quotes.quote: In the MATLAS image, Fig. fig:lsma , NGC 5485 has a disturbed asymmetrical stellar halo with a stream or tail pointing towards a possible faint shell in the southeast. A deep color image of NGC 5485 shows a ring-like dust structure in the central region oriented along the minor photometric axis Figure D.49 in mky2020 . Due to the inclination, the dust structure looks like an eyebrow. In the MATLAS survey, NGC 5485 is classified as having streams, disturbed outer isophotes, prominent dust lanes, and an unsure detection of shells matlas20 .

gmw_00004960.name: NGC 0661
gmw_00004960.overview: NGC 0661 is an early-type galaxy that has been classified in literature as a possible cD galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9705214, arXiv:2001.11277]. It is characterized as a slow rotator with a counter-rotating core, which can also be modeled as a system of two counter-rotating stellar disks [arXiv:2001.11277].

sources.summary: The paper applies its decomposition code to NGC 661, treating it as a possible cD galaxy; weak disk fits are ambiguous, so a pure bulge profile is preferred for reliable parameters.

quotes.quote: NGC 661 NGC 661 has been classified as a possible cD galaxy with total magnitude in the B system m_ B = 12.88 in the literature (RC3). We used images in two bands, V and R. We ran the processing twice in each band, once with the center masked out, and once with the center left unmasked. The results are shown in Table tab:661R and, for the particular of the R band analysis with the central pixels included, in Figure fig:661R .

We have included a bulge and a disk in our fits to NGC 661. It is seen from Table tab:661R that a weak disk, with total luminosity 4 of the bulge, is detected. The disk has a scale length comparable to the FWHM of the PSF. It is therefore not possible to really distinguish between the disk and a point source.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 0661 as a counter-rotating-core slow rotator that could be considered a two-counter-rotating-disc system without changing the overall kinematic conclusions.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, NGC 0661 (a CRC) and NGC 7454 (a NRR) could also be considered 2 galaxies. For these two galaxies, fig. 12 2016ARA&A..54..597C constructed successful dynamical models made of two counter-rotating discs. If we assumed that NGC 0661 and NGC 7454 were such objects, the fraction of various kinematics classes of core-less slow rotators would change: f(CRC)=0.15, f( 2 )=0.3, and f(NRR)=0.2, but the overall conclusions remain the same.

gmw_00004961.name: NGC 7454
gmw_00004961.overview: NGC 7454 is classified as an early-type galaxy [arXiv:2001.11277]. It exhibits the kinematics of a non-regular-rotator slow rotator, which can be dynamically modeled as a system containing two counter-rotating stellar disks [arXiv:2001.11277].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7454 as a non-regular-rotator slow rotator that could be modeled as a two-counter-rotating-disc system without changing the overall conclusions.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, NGC 0661 (a CRC) and NGC 7454 (a NRR) could also be considered 2 galaxies. For these two galaxies, fig. 12 2016ARA&A..54..597C constructed successful dynamical models made of two counter-rotating discs. If we assumed that NGC 0661 and NGC 7454 were such objects, the fraction of various kinematics classes of core-less slow rotators would change: f(CRC)=0.15, f( 2 )=0.3, and f(NRR)=0.2, but the overall conclusions remain the same.

gmw_00004962.name: HG20190714A
gmw_00004962.overview: HG20190714A is a fast radio burst host galaxy that has been imaged at high resolution by the Hubble Space Telescope [arXiv:2304.10377]. Observations of the galaxy have detected carbon monoxide emission, revealing a gas mass of 0.2 to 3.13 billion solar masses [arXiv:2304.10377].

sources.summary: The paper includes HG20190714A among HST-imaged FRB hosts and reports CO emission detected above 3 sigma, with detected-host gas masses, fractions, and depletion times.

quotes.quote: gave a high resolution view of HG20180924B, HG20190102C, and HG20190714A along with several other FRB host galaxies using HST to examine the position of the FRBs within their host galaxies.
emission from the host galaxies HG20180924B, HG20190714A, and HG20200430A, was detected above 3 significance with linewidths of FWHM 320 km s ^ 1 .
The detected hosts HG20180924B, HG20190714A, and HG20200430A have M_ gas =0.2-3.13 10^9M_ , _ gas =0.08-0.13 , and t_ dep =0.65-5.05 Gyr.

gmw_00004963.name: NGC 4038/4039
gmw_00004963.overview: NGC 4038/4039 is a highly evolved interacting galaxy system [arXiv:1402.5151]. The system is undergoing a coalescence phase characterized by a highly active starburst with high optical depth [arXiv:1402.5151].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4038/4039 as a highly evolved interaction whose AISM SEDs better match overall, while simulations struggle because coalescence phases are brief and optically complex.

quotes.quote: Some of our most evolved systems (e.g. NGC 4038/4039, and NGC 5394/5395) have better overall matched SEDs from the AISM set than from the DISM set, although their photometry hints at the presence of typically cooler dust than found in the simulations as their FIR emission peaks at longer wavelengths. These systems also typically have excess absorption in the UV relative to the observed photometry, and have very few matches.

Systems with very few good matches (e.g. NGC 3690/IC 694, NGC 4038/4039, NGC 5394/5395) also have the largest number of worst matches, many of which originate from the interactions between the three less massive simulated galaxies. The ^2 distributions for these galaxies (see Figure chi_by_model ) is flatter than those of other systems, particularly at the low-mass (M0) end, indicating many similarly bad matches. Many of these models are not representative of these three systems in part due to a significant difference in the stellar mass, which has a broad normalizing effect on SEDs. Further, these three systems are among our most evolved and there are only a few snapshots in each interaction simulation that capture the coalescence in its most active phases during which the SED evolves rapidly and variation with viewing angles can become significant because the optical depth of the central starburst can be very high.

gmw_00004964.name: DLA0201+365g
gmw_00004964.overview: DLA0201+365g is an absorption-selected galaxy at a redshift of z = 2.4604 [arXiv:2206.10623]. It possesses a high molecular gas mass but exhibits faint ultraviolet emission, suggesting the galaxy is either heavily obscured by dust or has an exceptionally long molecular gas depletion time [arXiv:2206.10623].

sources.summary: The paper identifies DLA0201+365g as a z=2.4604 absorption-selected galaxy with CO emission, high molecular gas mass, faint UV emission, and likely heavy dust obscuration.

quotes.quote: The newly-identified z 2.4604 selected galaxy, DLA0201+365g, has an impact parameter of 7  kpc to the QSO sightline, and an implied molecular gas mass of (5.04 0.78) 10^ 10 ( _ CO /4.36) (r_ 31 /0.55) \ M _ . Archival Hubble Space Telescope Wide Field and Planetary Camera 2 imaging covering the rest-frame near-ultraviolet (NUV) and far-ultraviolet (FUV) emission from this galaxy yield non-detections of rest-frame NUV and FUV emission, and a 5 upper limit of 2.3 M _  yr ^ 1 on the unobscured star formation rate (SFR). The low NUV-based SFR estimate, despite the very high molecular gas mass, indicates that DLA0201+365g either is a very dusty galaxy, or has a molecular gas depletion time that is around two orders of magnitude larger than that of star-forming galaxies at similar redshifts.
We detect CO(3--2) emission from one galaxy (hereafter, DLA0201+365g, at RA=02h04m55.55s, Dec.=+36 ^ 49 17.3 , z = 2.4604 ) in the field of the z= 2.4628 DLA towards QSO B0201+365.

gmw_00004965.name: host of GRB 090926A
aliases_external: host galaxy/line of sight of GRB 090926A
gmw_00004965.overview: The host of GRB 090926A is a high-redshift, low-mass irregular galaxy [arXiv:1108.1061, arXiv:1903.01353] with an estimated age of 1.15 billion years [arXiv:1903.01353]. Its interstellar medium has a very low metallicity [arXiv:1108.1061], a low star formation rate [arXiv:1903.01353], and may be dust-rich [arXiv:1903.01353].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 090926A host as a high-redshift, low-metallicity irregular galaxy whose absorbing ISM has multiple metal-line species, two velocity components, and a modest inferred star-formation timescale.

quotes.quote: The gas residing in the GRB host galaxy is responsible for many of the features observed in the GRB 090926A afterglow spectrum. Metallic features are apparent from neutral (OI, MgI, CaI), low-ionization (CII, MgII, AlII, AlIII, SiII, SII, CaII, FeII, NiII), and high-ionization (CIV, NV, OVI, SiIV, SIV) species (see fig. 2). In addition, strong absorption from the fine structure levels of CII, OI, SiII, FeII and from the metastable level of NiII is identified, suggesting that the intense radiation field from the GRB excites such features. The probed ISM of the host galaxy is resolved into two components separated by 48 km s ^ 1 , which contribute to the absorption system.
Despite this, very low metallicity values with respect to the solar ones, between 4.2 10^ −3 and 1.4 10^ −2 , have been derived. These are among the lowest values ever observed for a GRB host galaxy.

Therefore, if we compare the measured abundance ratios in the host of GRB 090926A with predictions from detailed chemical evolution models, we should be able to understand the nature of the host. These models show that the host of GRB 090926A is probably an irregular galaxy with baryonic mass 10^8 M_ and evolving with star formation rate (the inverse of the timescale of star formation) of 0.05 Gyr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper models the GRB 090926A host as a low-mass, low-star-formation irregular galaxy, possibly dust rich, and the oldest host in the sample.

quotes.quote: GRB 090926A Looking at Figure f:090926 , the three upper panels showing [ /Fe ] vs. [ Fe/H ] behaviours show very low abuandance values for elements (however, Mg is a lower limit). This suggests ( time-delay model") we have to deal with an irregular galaxy. Among all the models considered in Table t:models , we found that the low mass and SF irregular one (I l ) is the best to describe this host.

However, looking at what happens in spiral galaxies increasing the dust production rate by CC-SNe, the hypothesis of having a very dust rich host can be considered reliable. We found for this host galaxy an age 1.15 Gyr . This makes the host of GRB 090926A the oldest in terms of galactic age among those studied in this work.

gmw_00004966.name: ESO-LV 1890070
gmw_00004966.overview: ESO-LV 1890070 is a high-surface-brightness spiral galaxy characterized by two symmetrical spiral arms and no central bar [arXiv:1501.07178]. The galaxy's bulge exhibits a shallow negative metallicity gradient [arXiv:1501.07178], while its disc displays a negative luminosity-weighted age gradient and a decreasing fraction of old stars in its outer regions [arXiv:1506.05315].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO-LV 1890070 as a high-surface-brightness spiral whose bulge spectra were analyzed; no bar was required, its arms shape the profile, and its bulge shows a shallow negative metallicity gradient.

quotes.quote: ESO-LV 1890070, ESO-LV 4460170, ESO-LV 4500200, and ESO-LV 5140100 turned out to host a high surface-brightness disk and therefore were not considered in the subsequent analysis morelli2012 . Since the long-slit spectra of the four neglected galaxies were available to us, we decided to derive the stellar population properties of their bulges.

A bar was detected and modelled in ESO-LV 4460170 and ESO-LV 4500200, whereas no bar was needed to improve the surface-brightness models of ESO-LV 1890070 and ESO-LV 5140100. The bump in the surface-brightness profile associated to both a significant peak in the ellipticity and a roughly constant position angle is the photometric signature of the bar in ESO-LV 4460170 and ESO-LV 4500200 (see aguetal09 for a detailed discussion). The flat surface-brightness profile of ESO-LV 1890070 and the increase of ellipticity measured between 30 and 60 arcsec are due to the two prominent and symmetrical arms characterizing the galaxy spiral pattern.

Shallow gradients in age and no gradient in /Fe ratio were found for all the sample galaxies, whereas a negative shallow gradient in metallicity was observed for ESO-LV 1890070, ESO-LV 4460170, and ESO-LV 5140100. This was somehow expected for the bulges of spiral galaxies. Indeed, the measured gradients are consistent with the distributions obtained by moreetal08 (Fig. fig:histgrad_ama ).

sources.summary: The paper reports that ESO-LV 1890070 has a negative luminosity-weighted age gradient, exceptional metallicity-gradient behavior, and an old-star fraction that decreases in the disc outskirts.

quotes.quote: ESO-LV 1890070, IC 1993, and PGC 377759 display a negative gradient, whereas NGC 1366 has a remarkably strong positive gradient. The metallicity gradient of all the discs is negative or null, except ESO-LV 1890070 and IC 1993.
The fraction of old stars strongly decreases in the outer regions of the disc of ESO-LV 1890070, IC 1993, and PGC 37759 and sharply increases in ESO-LV 400037 and NGC 1366.

gmw_00004967.name: IC 1993
gmw_00004967.overview: IC 1993 is a low-luminosity member of the Fornax Cluster [arXiv:1004.5063] classified as an (R')SA(s)b galaxy [arXiv:1004.5063]. Its structure features a flocculent outer pseudoring dominated by discrete HII regions [arXiv:1004.5063], and its stellar disc displays a negative age gradient [arXiv:1506.05315].

sources.summary: A low-luminosity Fornax Cluster galaxy whose flocculent outer pseudoring appears as discrete HII regions and dominates its H-alpha output.

quotes.quote: IC 1993 (Figure img4 , bottom row). This galaxy is a low-luminosity member of the Fornax Cluster and is classified as an (R ^ )SA(s)b. The broadband image is taken from Buta Crocker (1991) and shows the flocculent but distinctive outer pseudoring, which appears as multiple spiral arms winding into each other. The outer pseudoring appears in the H image mainly as discrete HII regions, although many also lie inside the feature. The brightest of the HII regions is on the western side of the galaxy, which corresponds to the side with relatively stronger and less flocculent spiral structure in the B band.

sources.summary: The paper reports that IC 1993 has a negative age gradient, exceptional metallicity-gradient behavior, decreasing old-star fraction at larger disc radii, and a shallow positive enhancement gradient.

quotes.quote: ESO-LV 1890070, IC 1993, and PGC 377759 display a negative gradient, whereas NGC 1366 has a remarkably strong positive gradient. The metallicity gradient of all the discs is negative or null, except ESO-LV 1890070 and IC 1993.
The fraction of old stars strongly decreases in the outer regions of the disc of ESO-LV 1890070, IC 1993, and PGC 37759 and sharply increases in ESO-LV 400037 and NGC 1366.

Only the discs of ESO-LV 5140100 and IC 1993 are characterised by a shallow positive gradient of \ enhancement, whereas the discs of all the other galaxies show a \ gradient consistent with zero within the errors.

gmw_00004968.name: COS55-128636
gmw_00004968.overview: COS55-128636 is a high-redshift quiescent galaxy candidate with a spectroscopic redshift of 3.757 [arXiv:2307.09590]. It is the brightest quiescent candidate in its sample and exhibits detected Balmer-line absorption features [arXiv:2307.09590].

sources.summary: The paper identifies COS55-128636 as the brightest quiescent candidate, securely at z=3.757 from absorption features, with no significant alternative redshift solution and detected Balmer-line absorption.

quotes.quote: Quiescent Candidate COS55-128636, z_ spec = 3.757 This is the brightest quiescent candidate in our sample ( = 23.2 AB). The spectroscopic redshifts derived from both \ and \ are based on the presence of a strong absorption lines at 2.062 m and 2.31 m, which we identify as H and \ at z=3.757 . The spectroscopic redshift coincides with the secondary peak of its photometric redshift solution P(z) (see Figure fig:spectra ). There are no alternative redshift solutions of any significant likelihood based on either the photometry nor spectroscopy. In this galaxy, we detect \ and H , which is blended with the G-band.

gmw_00004969.name: COS55-129098
gmw_00004969.overview: COS55-129098 is a faint, low-mass quiescent candidate galaxy at a spectroscopic redshift of z = 3.336 [arXiv:2307.09590]. Its rest-frame colors are consistent with an older stellar population aged 1.5 to 2 Gyr, which formed through a gradual star formation history [arXiv:2307.09590].

sources.summary: The paper describes COS55-129098 as a faint, low-mass tentative quiescent galaxy at z=3.336, with old rest-frame colors, gradual star formation history, and low-S/N absorption evidence.

quotes.quote: Quiescent Candidate COS55-129098, z_ spec = 3.336 This is the lowest redshift quiescent candidate in our sample and the second faintest ( = 24.0 AB). With a stellar mass of log M_ /M_ = 10.3 , it is also one of two quiescent galaxies at z > 3 with log M_ /M_ < 10.5 for which there is spectroscopy . While it exhibits the rising star formation history typical of quiescent galaxies at high redshift, the \ fit favors a SFH that is more gradual than those of the other candidates (which are at higher redshift and > 0.5 dex more massive). Its rest-frame \ and \ colors are consistent with a stellar population with an age of 1.5-2 Gyr. This is older than the other galaxies in our sample, and could be evidence for galactic ``downsizing'' thomas2005 , where the more massive the galaxy, the earlier its formation epoch and the faster its formation time.

While its extracted 1D spectrum is low S/N (median is 0.9/pixel, maximum is 4.2/pixel), this is sufficient to allow us to accurately determine the spatial distribution of the source light (see Figure fig:profile_test ) and hence derive a spectroscopic redshift to within 1 using detected stellar absorption features. If this galaxy is quiescent, the most plausible feature (based on its telluric correction) lies at 2.1 - 2.2 m in the observed frame. This is best fit by at z_ spec = 3.336 .

gmw_00004970.name: COS55-126981
gmw_00004970.overview: COS55-126981 is a high-redshift quiescent galaxy candidate with a weak detected continuum [arXiv:2307.09590]. Its spectroscopic redshift is tentatively identified at z=4.673 based on Balmer absorption features, with an alternative solution at z=4.862 [arXiv:2307.09590].

sources.summary: The paper presents COS55-126981 as a tentative z=4.673 quiescent candidate with Balmer absorption features, an alternative z=4.862 solution, stable stellar-mass estimates, and a weak detected continuum.

quotes.quote: Quiescent Candidate COS55-126981, z_ spec = 4.673 The \ spectroscopic redshift of this galaxy is based on the presence of H , H (blended with Ca H K), and H , observed at SNR 2 - 3 . The \ spectroscopic redshift allows a larger range of solutions, primarily because the SNR of the spectroscopic data is relatively low. The peak of the P(z) from the \ fit coincides with z_ spec = 4.862 , which is closer to the galaxy's photometric redshift. We show the star formation histories corresponding to both redshifts in Figure fig:sfh . The total stellar mass formed for each SFH ( corresponding to the two redshift solutions ) only changes by log M_ /M_ = 0.07 .

Although the continuum is weak in the 2D spectrum, we see a clear continuum peak in the spatial profile of the galaxy from four out of five masks (see Figure fig:profile_test ). Based on the best-fit SED, we expect to see rising continuum toward the red end of the spectrum, as it captures the region above the Balmer break. As expected, there is a shallow, but visible, slope in this spectrum, broadly consistent with the Balmer break strength , D _B = 1.75 using the definition of worthey1997 , measured from the photometry.

gmw_00004971.name: NGC 4379
gmw_00004971.overview: NGC 4379 is an early-type galaxy [arXiv:2311.13747] that contains a supermassive black hole at its nucleus [arXiv:2311.13747]. It is also identified as a galaxy exhibiting gas counterrotation [arXiv:astro-ph/0105266].

sources.summary: The paper observes NGC 4379 with Gemini/NIFS, includes it among galaxies whose JAM models require a black hole, and presents it as one of five early-type black-hole measurements.

quotes.quote: We observed four of our galaxies, NGC4458, NGC4623, NGC4379 and NGC4262, using the Near-Infrared Integral Field Spectrometer NIFS, nifspaper on the Gemini-North telescope.

Despite the degeneracy between parameters, the models for the galaxies NGC 4262, 4379, 4458, and 4550 exclude the no black hole case at 3 , thus these galaxies have SMBH detections with 99.7 certainty.

In this paper we present black hole measurements for 5 early type galaxies (NGC 4262, NGC 4379, NGC 4458, NGC 4550 and NGC 4623) using the NIFS and Osiris instruments from the Keck and Gemini telescopes respectively. Our aim is to obtain black hole masses in a mass-selected galaxy sample to test claims of bias in black hole scaling relations. Our results are follows. We detect and measure black hole masses in 4 of the 5 galaxies we observed (Table tab:sample ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4379 as the outlier whose exclusion strengthens the environmental-density distinction for gas-counterrotating galaxies, while its inclusion weakens that statistical result.

quotes.quote: In particular, excluding NGC 4379, all the remaining galaxies with gas counterrotation belong to groups where the density of galaxies _ xyz < 0.5 galaxies Mpc ^ 3 and _ NED < 1.8 galaxies Mpc ^ 3 .

This segregation/concentration for galaxies with gas counterrotation in Fig. plot1 is confirmed by a Kol gomorov--Smirnov test applied to the galaxy densities around the whole sample, except for NGC 4379. The population of galaxies with gas counterrotation and the population of normal galaxies appear to be different at a significance level of 93.7 . However, there is no a priori justification for the exclusion of NGC 4379. Its inclusion reduces the significance level to 84 and weakens the difference between populations.

gmw_00004972.name: IRAS 17132+5313
gmw_00004972.overview: IRAS 17132+5313 is a system composed of two distinct galaxy components [arXiv:1705.10801]. The north-east galaxy is extended, while the south-west galaxy is compact and contributes 40 percent of the system's integrated flux density [arXiv:1705.10801].

sources.summary: The paper resolves IRAS 17132+5313 into two components, with the north-east galaxy extended and resolved out at the highest resolution and the south-west galaxy compact.

quotes.quote: IRAS 17132+5313 : This system has two components. The galaxy towards the north east is extended and resolved out in the highest resolution image (0 08 0 07). We had to taper the map in order to recover its emission. The galaxy towards the south west is compact and contributes 40 of the integrated flux density of the system.

gmw_00004973.name: VII Zw 031
gmw_00004973.overview: VII Zw 031 is a galaxy characterized by highly extended emission [arXiv:1705.10801]. At high resolution, this emission is completely resolved out, requiring heavy tapering of the image to be recovered [arXiv:1705.10801].

sources.summary: The paper notes VII Zw 031 is completely resolved out at the highest-resolution image and is one of the most extended systems in the sample.

quotes.quote: VII Zw 031 : At the highest resolution image (0 8 0 6, done with natural weighting) the emission was completely resolved out. We had to taper the image heavily in order to recover the emission. This is one of the most extended systems in our sample along with VV 340a.

gmw_00004974.name: VV 250
gmw_00004974.overview: VV 250 is a luminous infrared galaxy system undergoing a major merger between two spiral galaxies [arXiv:1807.04298, arXiv:1511.05406]. This low-speed coplanar interaction has produced long tidal tails [arXiv:2106.15041, arXiv:1511.05406] and triggered nuclear starbursts with enhanced star formation efficiencies [arXiv:2106.15041, arXiv:2404.18999].

sources.summary: The paper describes VV 250 as a two-component system dominated by VV 250a, with a fainter north-west component requiring low-resolution tapered imaging for size recovery.

quotes.quote: VV 250 : This system has two well separated components, south-east (VV 250a) and north-west (VV 250b). In Figure fig:fig1 , we only show VV 250a since it contains 86 of the total flux density of the system (obtained by adding the flux density of both components). The north-west component is faint with an 11 peak detection.

To recover A _ 50 for this faint component, we used the tapered D array map ( 10" resolution) since we could not recover half of the integrated flux density of this component in higher resolution maps. Even in this low resolution map, we recover A _ 50 for C_ 50 =3.2 , which is lower than our conservative limit of 5 , however we inspected this contour and made sure the emission within it looked real. For the north-west component, A_ 50 =64.8 arcsec^ 2 in a map with A_ beam =113.4 arcsec^ 2 , i.e., it is unresolved, and then A _ 50 is only an upper limit.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1315 + 6207 as a LIRG major merger whose two galaxies show radio emission, broad absorption, unequal SFRs, and clumpy partial gas coverage.

quotes.quote: J1315 + 6207 This is a major merger (also known as UGC 8335, Arp 238) between two galaxies at a projected separation of 21 kpc. Based on Herschel far-infrared and sub-millimetre imaging observations, the two interacting galaxies have SFRs of 8 and 55 M _ yr ^ 1 and dust masses of 6 and 7 10^ 7 M _ cao2016 . The nuclei of both the interacting galaxies show radio emission in our GMRT map. We detect broad absorption towards the stronger radio source. Single component Gaussian gives best fit to the absorption.
Thus, the higher optical depth we measure points towards the clumpy nature of the absorbing gas and partial coverage of the radio source.

sources.summary: The paper finds Arp 238's CO confined to compact central sources with high SFEs, broad multi-peak profiles, tidal tails, and a low-speed coplanar interaction that drives nuclear starbursts.

quotes.quote: In contrast, the CO emission in Arp 238 is detected only in two compact sources at the galactic centers. Compared to the control sample, Arp 238-E shows an SFE enhancement of more than 1 dex whereas Arp 238-W has an enhancement of 0.7 dex. We suggest that the extended CO distribution and the large SFE variation in Arp 142 are due to an expanding large-scale ring triggered by a recent high-speed head-on collision between the spiral galaxy and the elliptical galaxy, and the compact CO sources with high SFEs in Arp 238 are associated with nuclear starbursts induced by gravitational tidal torques in a low-speed coplanar interaction.

Because neither of the two galaxies in Arp 238 is well resolved, we show only the spectra of their integrated emissions in Figure fig:Arp238-spectra . Both sources have broad (FWHM 400 km s^ 1 ) and multi-peak line profiles, suggesting complex kinematics within them. This is consistent with the strong interaction between them revealed by the long tidal tails shown in optical maps, and with their high SFR which can cause strong turbulence and feedbacks.

sources.summary: LARS 03/Arp 238 is described as a well-studied merging LIRG with two nuclei; the observation covers the southeast nucleus and finds emission whose EW rises in larger apertures.

quotes.quote: LARS 03 Arp 238 is among the most well-studied galaxies in the LARS sample. The galaxy comprises two merging nuclei and with ACS/SBC we were able to point at just one of the two cores. Our observation therefore covers only the south-eastern nucleus. It is a luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG) and with a (L_ FIR /L_ ) = 11.8 is close to the domain of ultra luminous infrared galaxies (ULIRGs).
Arp 238 does show \ in emission with an EW of 16 \ at twice r_ P20 and this rises to 40 \ when large apertures are considered.

sources.summary: The paper describes LARS 3 as a weak Lyman-alpha emitter with a broad blue absorption component, saturated Si II absorption, high inferred H I column density, and conspicuous fluorescent-line uncertainties.

quotes.quote: LARS 3 is a weak Ly emitter, both globally and in the aperture. The Ly line shows a weak P Cygni profile with a relatively broad blue absorption component, suggesting high H i column density. All the Si ii transitions exhibit a deep, broad absorption profile, almost or completely saturated. The averaged profile is the widest in the sample, apart from LARS 6, centered at 100 km s ^ 1 but with significant absorption out to 450 km s ^ 1 on the blue side and 150 km s ^ 1 on the red side. The strong red wing means that the O I* 1305 fluorescent line stands out more conspicuously than on most other galaxies, giving rise to the large uncertainties on f_C in the red wing shown in Figure fig:covfracs , panel 3, as well as artificially slightly raising the residual intensity in the red wing of the averaged profile.

sources.summary: The paper identifies LARS 3 as the southeastern nucleus of interacting pair Arp 238, a dusty infrared galaxy with weak Ly emission and an extended H I tidal tail.

quotes.quote: LARS 3 ( Arp 238 ) LARS 3 is the south-eastern nucleus of the violently interacting pair of similar sized spiral galaxies Arp 238 . With a global f_ esc ^ Ly = 0.1 and L_ Ly =10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 this dust rich luminous infrared galaxy is a relatively weak Ly emitter. VLA H i imaging reveals an extended tail towards the west. This tidal tail is much larger than the optical dimensions of the pair Arp 238 see also for similar extended tidal H i structures around the Ly emitting star bursts Tol 1924-416 and IRAS 08339+6517 Cannon2004 . In H the main kinematical axis runs from west to east and the radial velocity field appears symmetric, although slightly disturbed towards the north and the south.

sources.summary: The paper cites Arp 238 as a close S+S major-merger pair with resolved CO emission, nuclear-concentrated star formation, enhanced SFE, and a low-speed coplanar orbit whose tidal torque triggers nuclear starbursts.

quotes.quote: resolved the CO (1-0) emission in two close major-merger pairs, Arp 238 (S+S) and Arp 142 (S+E). They found significant differences in local SFE among the two systems.
The SFR in Arp 238 is concentrated in the two nuclei, with an SFE enhancement of 0.7 dex.

These authors attribute this difference to the very different interaction orbits: while the low-speed coplanar orbit of Arp 238 results in strong tidal torque which triggers nuclear starbursts, the high-speed head-on collision in Arp 142 produces a density-wave shock that suppresses the SFR in the system.

gmw_00004975.name: VV 340a
gmw_00004975.overview: VV 340a is a bright, edge-on disk galaxy located in the northern part of the VV 340 galaxy pair [arXiv:1705.10801]. It features extended emission that is resolved out at high angular resolutions, requiring heavy tapering to be recovered [arXiv:1705.10801].

sources.summary: The paper identifies VV 340a as the edge-on northern component of VV 340, requiring heavy tapering to recover its extended emission near the primary-beam edge.

quotes.quote: VV 340a : In the final combined image, where we achieved an angular resolution of 0 5 0 4 (using natural weighting), the emission from this system was completely resolved out. To recover the extended emission, we had to taper the image heavily. VV 340 has two components, an edge on galaxy to the north (VV 340a), shown in Figure fig:fig1 , and a face on galaxy to the south (VV 340b). Inconveniently, the pointing of the VLA observation was centered on VV 340b, from which we tentatively detected an off nuclear feature at a 3 level in our lowest resolution image. The bright edge on galaxy, VV 340a, is clearly detected, although it was hard to perform the Gaussian fit since the source fell close to the edge of the primary beam.

gmw_00004976.name: NGC 742
gmw_00004976.overview: NGC 742 is a companion galaxy to NGC 741 [arXiv:0712.1150] and is classified as a head-tail radio galaxy [arXiv:1707.02985]. It features twin radio jets that bend westward [arXiv:0712.1150, arXiv:1707.02985], a structure likely shaped by a close encounter with NGC 741 that stripped its associated gas [arXiv:1707.02985].

sources.summary: The paper interprets NGC 742 as an infalling head-tail radio galaxy whose AGN jets, stripped gas, and radio tail trace its recent interaction with NGC 741.

quotes.quote: NGC 742 appears to be a head-tail radio galaxy with strongly bent jets to the west. The emission is brightest around NGC 742 and becomes fainter toward the west. We also see the v-shaped structure of the jet around NGC 742, typical for bent radio jets. The radio surface brightness decreases toward NGC 741, beyond which we do not detect small scale radio emission. NGC 741 itself does not show any radio jets and is only visible as a point source.

We conclude from the spectral index map of the south-west radio tail that NGC 742 approached the galaxy group with an approximate velocity of 1400 km s^ 1 . Its path is characterized by the remnant radio emission of the AGN, although the shape some of the more distant regions of the south-west tail might have been disturbed by gas motions in the surrounding ICM. The surface brightness deficiency (classified as a smaller cavity in Section ch:smallcavity ) might also be caused by the encounter of NGC 742 dragging gas away from this region. The fact that the deficit in counts in this region is larger than expected for an AGN inflated bubble, might point out that it is a superposition of multiple effects. The close encounter with the BCG and the much denser ICM of NGC 741 caused the stripping of any gas associated with NGC 742 and may have also displaced some of the gas from the group core. The activity of the NGC 742 AGN may also have have been enhanced, leading to the prominent head-tail structure.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 742 as NGC 741’s companion and favors it as the narrow-angle-tail radio source with twin jets feeding the extended radio emission.

quotes.quote: The BGG, identified as NGC 741, and its companion galaxy NGC 742 are separated by 45 arcsec (17 kpc), with NGC 742 lying almost exactly to the E of NGC 741, together with a much smaller third companion approximately 9 arcsec (3.4 kpc) to the NE of NGC 741 1995A&A...297...28B .

However, twin jets are clearly visible emerging from the nucleus of NGC 742 and bending westwards to merge into the larger-scale extended emission, while there is no small-scale extension of any kind associated with the nucleus of NGC 741.
We therefore agree with 1994ApJ...436...67V that it is entirely plausible that the extended radio emission in the NGC 741/2 system comes only from a narrow-angle tail source associated with NGC 742.

gmw_00004977.name: ARK66
gmw_00004977.overview: ARK66 is a group galaxy that exhibits an unusually high X-ray luminosity [arXiv:1707.02985]. Its X-ray emission displays a head-tail structure, which is likely gas that has been ram-pressure stripped as the galaxy moves through its environment [arXiv:1707.02985].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ARK66 as a group galaxy with an unusually high X-ray luminosity and a head-tail structure likely produced by ram-pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: Only ARK66 is bright enough for a spectroscopic analysis and we measure a temperature of 0.33^ +0.09 _ 0.04 keV and a luminosity of L_ X, 0.5-2.0 = 9.4^ +1.9 _ 1.4 10^ 39 erg s^ 1 . For the other two galaxies, we are able to constraint only the luminosity with a reasonable precision. For SRGb119.030 we obtain L_ X, 0.5-2.0 = 2.6^ +0.7 _ 0.4 10^ 39 erg s^ 1 , and for ARK65 we obtain L_ X, 0.5-2.0 = 3.2^ +0.4 _ 0.4 10^ 39 erg s^ 1 . Based on the K-band luminosities, ARK66 has an unusually high X-ray luminosity. In the case of ARK66, the X-ray emission has a head-tail structure, suggesting that the galaxy is moving toward the south-west. This head-tail structure is likely to be gas that has been ram-pressure stripped from the galaxy (also see recently discovered X-ray tails associated with other galaxies, e.g., 2006ApJ...637L..81S,2015arXiv151003708S ). From measured redshifts by , we find a line-of-sight velocity difference between ARK66 and NGC 741 of 772(9) km s^ 1 . Since we see an extended tail behind ARK66, the trajectory of ARK66 cannot be exactly along the line-of-sight. If we assume a 45^ orbital inclination relative to the plane of the sky, then the 3D velocity must be about 1100 km s^ 1 , which is relatively high given the projected distance from the group center of 32 kpc .

gmw_00004978.name: HE0359-3959
gmw_00004978.overview: HE0359-3959 is an extreme radiating quasar located at an intermediate redshift of z = 1.5209 [arXiv:1801.04570]. The active galactic nucleus hosts an outflow with an extreme blueshifted velocity of approximately -6000 km/s [arXiv:1801.04570].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HE0359-3959 as an extreme radiating z=1.5209 quasar with powerful blueshifted outflows, stratified emission-line regions, and evidence for a slim disk.

quotes.quote: We present a multiwavelength spectral study of the quasar HE0359-3959, which has been identified as an extreme radiating source at intermediate redshift ( z =1.5209). Along the spectral range, the different ionic species give information about the substructures in the broad line region. The presence of a powerful outflow with an extreme blueshifted velocity of -6000 500 \ is shown in the \ emission line. A prominent blueshifted component is also associated with the 1900 \ blend, resembling the one observed in .

We find that the physical conditions for the low, intermediate and high--ionization emission lines are different, which indicate that the emission lines are emitted in different zones of the broad line region. The asymmetries shown by the profiles reveal different forces over emitter zones. The high--ionization region is strongly dominated by radiation forces, which also affect the low and intermediate--ionization emitter region, commonly governed by virial motions. These results support the idea that highly radiating sources host a slim disk.

gmw_00004979.name: PG 1404+226
gmw_00004979.overview: PG 1404+226 is a luminous Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9811049] and an extreme narrow-line Fe II quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0409495]. The source exhibits strong soft X-ray variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0409495] and displays absorption features near 1.1 keV [arXiv:astro-ph/9808205, arXiv:astro-ph/9905323].

sources.summary: The paper studies PG 1404+226's unusual 1.1 keV absorption, testing warm-absorber photoionization scenarios as alternatives to relativistic outflow and finding some improved but imperfect fits.

quotes.quote: Recently, several cases of spectral complexity around 1.1 keV have been reported (e.g., Hayashida 1997). Among these is PG 1404+226. Its high-state and spectrum show evidence for unexpectedly strong 1.1 keV absorption (Ulrich Molendi 1996, Comastri et al. 1997). If interpreted in terms of blueshifted oxygen, exceptionally high outflow velocities are implied (e.g., Leighly et al. 1997a); an exciting possibility. Here, on the basis of detailed photoionization modelling of the absorbing material under various conditions, we explore several scenarios to account for the 1.1 keV absorption without invoking relativistic outflow of the warm absorber (WA):

WA with non-solar O/Ne ratio One way to clearly change the depth of individual absorption edges, and particularly to make the neon absorption dominate over oxygen in strength, is a deviation from solar abundances (Fig. mod ), of either overabundant neon or underabundant oxygen. Several deviation factors were studied between an abundance of up to O = 0.2 solar and up to Ne = 4 solar. These models strongly improve the quality of the fit to PG 1404 up to acceptable values (Tab. 2).

sources.summary: The paper explores non-relativistic explanations for PG 1404 strong 1.1 keV absorption, finding abundance-altered warm absorber models improve the fit but have edge-location problems.

quotes.quote: The high-state and spectrum of the luminous Sy galaxy PG 1404 show evidence for unexpectedly strong 1.1 keV absorption (Ulrich Molendi 1996, Comastri et al. 1997, Leighly et al. 1997a). Here, on the basis of detailed photoionization modelling of the absorbing material under various conditions, we explore several scenarios to account for the 1.1 keV absorption without invoking relativistic outflow: a `standard' warm absorber of high ionization parameter, including a strong contribution from emission and reflection; non-solar abundances (over-abundant neon or under-abundant oxygen); an additional soft excess overlapping with the absorption features (soft excess added to the incident continuum that illuminates the warm absorber).

In application to PG 1404, fitting such a model improves the quality of the fit, but the high-state data are still not well matched. (ii) One way to clearly change the depth of individual absorption edges, and particularly to make the neon absorption dominate over oxygen in strength, is a deviation from solar abundances, of either overabundant neon or underabundant oxygen. Several deviation factors were studied between an abundance of up to O = 0.2 solar and up to Ne = 4 solar.

A potential problem for this model description, besides a difficulty to explain strong deviations of O/Ne from the solar value in terms of nucleosynthesis, is the location of the deepest edge. In order to weaken sufficiently the oxygen absorption, a rather high ionization parameter is required with the consequence that the deepest neon edges are those of highly ionized species, around 1.36 keV, instead of 1.1 keV.

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 1404+226 as an extreme narrow-line Fe II quasar with strong soft X-ray variability and absorption features interpreted as an outflowing accretion-disk wind.

quotes.quote: PG 1404+226 (V=15, M=-23.4, z=0.098) is one of the most extreme narrow line Fe II quasar.

In this Letter we present the XMM-Newton and CHANDRA observations of PG 1404+226. We detect absorption features in the XMM-Newton data and find some evidence for such lines in the CHANDRA data. We have reanalyzed ASCA data which corroborates this conclusion. The source shows high variability in all the three observations and most remarkably, the line features show variability in time scales of 5000 s, giving a direct dynamical size constraint of 100 R_g for the absorbing medium.

sources.summary: The paper reports significant absorption in PG 1404+226, with a single edge near 1.07 keV, possible second edge, and acceptable broad Gaussian absorption-line modeling.

quotes.quote: Significant evidence is found for an absorption feature in PG 1404+226. The addition of a single edge to the best-fitting continuum model (PL+BB) yields ^ 2 / =172/165 , an improvement of ^ 2 =29 , with an edge energy of E =1.07 0.03 keV and optical depth =0.84 0.28 , consistent with the results of Leighly et al. (1997b). A slight improvement ( ^ 2 =8.1 ) is also achieved by adding a second edge, although the edge energy of E =2.4 0.3 keV seems rather high. The 1 keV feature can also be modelled with a broad Gaussian absorption line, giving ^ 2 / =170/164 with fit parameters as in Table 4.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PG 1404+226 as a narrow-line outlier with a large [OIII] width, poor-quality [OIII] profiles, and possible broad blueshifted [OIII], then excludes it.

quotes.quote: It is clearly shown in Figures 1 and 2 that five NL objects, Mrk 507, Mrk 684, IRAS 15462-0450, MS 22102+1827, and PG 1404+226, having large [ OIII ] widths, deviate from other NL objects.

Only poor quality [ OIII ] profiles are available for MS 22102+1827 and PG 1404+226, and VVG mentioned that broad blueshifted [ OIII ] profiles should not be overlooked in PG 1404+226. These five objects will be excluded in the following statistic analysis.

gmw_00004980.name: PKS 1343--601
