gmw_00005118.overview: Q1218+472 is a quasar located at a redshift of z=0.40 [arXiv:astro-ph/0004068]. It is situated seven arcminutes to the west of the galaxy NGC 4258 and shows no temporal variability in its X-ray light curve [arXiv:astro-ph/0004068].

sources.summary: The paper treats Q1218+472 as a serendipitous z=0.40 quasar in the NGC 4258 field, extracting GIS counts, finding no variability, and fitting an absorbed power-law X-ray spectrum.

quotes.quote: The point source 7 arcmins to the west of NGC 4258 is readily identified with the z=0.40 quasar Q1218+472. By choosing a slightly smaller extraction radius than normal (2.5 arcmins), we can isolate the GIS counts from this source with relatively little contamination from NGC 4258. The resulting GIS background subtracted count rate is 4 10^ 3 cps per detector. No temporal variability was observed in the resulting light curve, although the low count rate results in weak limits on possible variability. This source is not within the field of view of the SIS.

The two GIS spectra were fitted with a model consisting of a power-law subjected to both Galactic absorption (with a column of N_ Gal =1.2 10^ 20 ) and intrinsic absorption with the redshift of the quasar. The resulting best fit parameters are =1.40^ +0.24 _ 0.14 and N_ H <5 10^ 21 (see Fig. 5 for the confidence contours that result from this fit).

gmw_00005119.name: Ark 227
gmw_00005119.overview: Ark 227 is a low-mass galaxy [arXiv:2310.13048] located at a distance of approximately 35 Mpc [arXiv:2310.13048]. It is surrounded by an extended stellar halo [arXiv:2310.13048] and is associated with an ultra-faint dwarf companion galaxy named Ark227-UFD1 [arXiv:2310.13048].

sources.summary: The paper reports JWST detection of Ark 227's extended stellar halo and accretion shelves, using surface-brightness, color, and metallicity profiles to infer multiple minor mergers.

quotes.quote: We report the serendipitous discovery of an extended stellar halo surrounding the low-mass galaxy Ark 227 ( M_ =5 10^9 ; d=35 Mpc) in deep JWST NIRCam imaging from the Blue Jay Survey. The F200W-F444W color provides robust star-galaxy separation, enabling the identification of stars at very low density. By combining resolved stars at large galactocentric distances with diffuse emission from NIRCam and Dragonfly imaging at smaller distances, we trace the surface brightness and color profiles of this galaxy over the entire extent of its predicted dark matter halo, from 0.1-100 kpc.

We observe such a feature in Ark 227 at 10-20 kpc, which, according to models, could be caused by a merger with total mass ratio 1:10. The metallicity declines over this radial range, further supporting the minor merger scenario. There is tentative evidence of a second shelf at _V 35 mag arcsec ^ 2 extending from 50-100 kpc, along with a corresponding drop in metallicity. The stellar mass in this outermost envelope is 10^7 . These results suggest that Ark 227 experienced multiple mergers with a spectrum of lower-mass galaxies -- a scenario that is broadly consistent with the hierarchical growth of structure in a cold dark matter-dominated universe.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Ark227-UFD1 as an ultra-faint dwarf associated with Ark 227, with metal-poor sources, surface brightness 28.1 mag arcsec^-2, 230 pc half-light size, and stellar mass about 10^5.

quotes.quote: The sources are clearly consistent with a metal-poor population at the distance of Ark 227. The surface brightness within the half-light radius is _ V,e =28.1 mag arcsec ^ 2 . Assuming that these sources are associated with Ark 227, the physical half-light size is 230 pc. Summing up the flux from the 17 sources and accounting for the unresolved flux from fainter sources implies a luminosity of M_V=-7.0 and a stellar mass of M_ 10^5 , assuming M/L_V=2 . The size and luminosity are consistent with the properties of ultra-faint dwarf (UFD) galaxies Simon19 ; we therefore consider this object a UFD associated with Ark 227 and refer to it as Ark227-UFD1.

sources.summary: The paper describes 551648 as a catalogued galaxy with redshift 1793 km/s, but argues its regular elliptical appearance and globular clusters suggest a larger distance.

quotes.quote: 551648 (ARK227) This previously catalogued galaxy has a spectroscopic redshift of 1793 km/s, putting it at a distance of D 26 Mpc, in the same group as 26058 and 677414, and giving it an absolute magnitude of 17.8 . It seems likely this redshift is incorrect, however, as the galaxy appears to be an intrinsically bright, regular elliptical with a large population of globular clusters. The brightest of these have magnitudes of i^ + 23--24, suggesting a distance up to two times further away.

gmw_00005120.name: 3C 88
gmw_00005120.overview: 3C 88 is the brightest central galaxy of a galaxy group [arXiv:1806.04692] and is optically classified as a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region [arXiv:1806.04692]. Morphologically categorized as an FR II radio galaxy [arXiv:1806.04692, arXiv:0711.4773], it features a diffuse halo of line emission [arXiv:2010.11195] and an eastern X-ray cavity with a diameter of 50 kpc [arXiv:1806.04692].

sources.summary: The paper studies AGN feedback in 3C 88, finding a 50 kpc X-ray cavity and identifying the central LINER as an FR I/II transition radio galaxy.

quotes.quote: We present results from the deep \ observation (105 ksec), together with new Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope and Very Large Array data of the AGN outburst in the radio-loud galaxy group 3C 88. The system shows a prominent X-ray cavity on the eastern side with a diameter of 50 kpc at 28 kpc from the nucleus. The total enthalpy of the cavity is 3.8 10^ 58 erg and the average power required to inflate the X-ray bubble is 2.0 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 .

3C 88 (UGC 02748) is the brightest central galaxy of a galaxy group at z=0.0302 , which is included in the 400 square degree \ PSPC galaxy cluster survey catalog Burenin07 . It has been studied at many wavelengths. Optically, it is classified as a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region LINER; Lewis03 , containing a low-power AGN with the 2-10 keV luminosity of 5 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 Gliozzi08b .

In the radio band, the FR classification of 3C 88 is arguable. Based on its morphology, 3C 88 is classified as an FR II radio galaxy e.g., Martel99,Marchesini04 , while based on its radio power it is considered as the transition source between FR I and FR II e.g., Baum92,Gliozzi08b .

sources.summary: 3C 088 is described as an FR II/LEG with a diffuse line-emitting halo around its central ELR, significant rotation, and centrally enhanced line ratios.

quotes.quote: 3C 088 (UGC 2748): FR II/LEG, z=0.030, 1 = 0.60 kpc (see Fig. 3c088 ). A diffuse halo of line emission, 3 kpc of radius, surrounds the central ELR. It shows significant rotation, with an amplitude of 170 . The velocity dispersion reaches 250 \ on the nucleus but elsewhere is rather low. The / \ ratio is higher in the central region than in the diffuse halo.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 88 as a morphologically FRII radio galaxy, notes no statistically required local absorber, and warns its flat X-ray spectrum may indicate substantial absorption.

quotes.quote: Two ( \ and ) are FRI and two (3C 88 and c4) FRII galaxies, although the classification of 3C 88 is only morphological.
Table 3 already indicates that there is evidence for moderate absorption ( N_ H 10^ 21 ) in , , and c4, whereas in 3C 88 a local absorber is not statistically required.

However, the latter result might be hampered by the limited photon statistics, since the unusually flat photon index may indicate that the X-ray spectrum of 3C 88 is affected by substantial absorption.

gmw_00005121.name: J0423 0120
gmw_00005121.overview: J0423 0120 is a blazar [arXiv:1105.3345] and quasar [arXiv:1412.2625] located at a redshift of z = 0.915 [arXiv:1105.3345]. The active nucleus exhibits confirmed intranight optical variability [arXiv:1105.3345], long-term brightness fluctuations [arXiv:1105.3345], and a radio jet with a core electric field aligned parallel to the jet direction [arXiv:1212.3423].

sources.summary: The paper newly monitors J0423 0120 across seven years, finding confirmed or probable intranight variability plus strong long-term fading and later brightening.

quotes.quote: J0423 0120 (PKS 0420 01; z = 0.915 ) This blazar has been newly monitored by us on 3 nights, covering a 7 years time span (Goyal et al. \ 2011) It showed a confirmed variability on 19 Nov. \ 2003, with an INOV amplitude of 2 per cent and was a `probable variable' on 25 Oct. \ 2009 ( C test). The F test shows it to be a confirmed variable on all the 3 nights. As for LTOV, it faded by 1.9-mag between the first 2 epochs of monitoring roughly a year apart and then brightened by 0.8-mag when we last monitored it on 25 Oct. \ 2009.

sources.summary: The paper models 0420-014 as a gamma-ray-flaring blazar/QSO, exploring axial magnetic field strength, shocked-flow Lorentz factor, and polarization constraints.

quotes.quote: The selected sources were the QSO 0420-014, the BL Lac object OJ 287, and the FSRQ 1156+295.

The parameter sequence shown in Figure fig2 (animation online) spans the range 0.05 B_z 1.0 , and is based on the model for 0420-014, for which a value of B_z =0.4 was adopted. (Note that in the animation the sample values are not uniformly distributed in the adopted range.) The `fiducial' value corresponds to a magnetic energy density B_z^2 0.16 , so that the mean field contributes less than 20 of the energy density contributed by the random component.

The spectral shape during flaring changes along this sequence because the Lorentz factor of the shocked flow differs from that of the quiescent flow, the spectral characteristics of which are used to normalize the opacity. (The quiescent flux rises along the sequence because the peak flux is normalized to that of the modeled data, and the spectrum becomes shallower.) For the model of 0420-014 the Lorentz factor of the shocked flow is 8 , required to yield the necessary shock compression, in the forward shock case.

sources.summary: The paper reports that 0420--014 shows no transverse jet RM variation, a monotonic central-region RM decrease, and core electric-field alignment with the jet.

quotes.quote: Polarization observations of 6 radio--quasars (0133+476, 0420-014, 0745+241, 0906+430, 1633+382 and 1954+513) and 2 BL Lac objects (0256+075 and 1823+568) were carried out in a 24 hour session on November 2, 2008 using the VLBA.

0420--014 shows no indications of variations transverse to the jet but does show the RM monotonically decreasing from positive ( +1000 rad/m ^2 ) to negative ( 1000 rad/m ^2 ) values as we move further from the central region.
In several sources, the electric field in the core seems to be either perpendicular (0745+241, 1954+513) or parallel (0133+476, 0256+075, 0420--014, 0906+430) to the direction of the jet.

gmw_00005122.name: Antlia II
gmw_00005122.overview: Antlia II is an exceptionally diffuse, low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxy orbiting the Milky Way [arXiv:2002.05161, arXiv:2405.09104]. It features a large core radius [arXiv:2002.05161], a low velocity dispersion [arXiv:2206.02821], and a velocity gradient indicative of tidal elongation and gas stripping [arXiv:2106.12656, arXiv:2311.05687].

sources.summary: The paper uses Antlia II as a low-surface-brightness dwarf-galaxy test of MOG, finding that modified gravity struggles to reproduce its stellar dynamics.

quotes.quote: Recently, using the second data release of the astrometric satellite Gaia , it has been discovered a very diffuse, cored, and low surface brightness dwarf galaxy in orbit around Andromeda Torrealba2019 . Antlia II has a core radius of 2.9 kpc and an exceptionally small velocity dispersion 6 km/s. Its low measured surface brightness ( 32.3 mag/arcsec^2 ) makes it 100 times more diffuse than the ultra diffuse galaxies. Since the dynamics of stars in these extremely low-surface-brightness dwarfs is dominated by the dark matter distribution, it has been argued that the formation of such a large core may require alternatives to the standard cold dark matter, such as ultra-light bosons idm2019 . Thus, Antlia II represents an exceptional laboratory where to carry out tests of modified gravity and, in particular, of those theories that aim to avoid the introduction of a dark matter component, such as MOG.

Here, we have investigated the dynamics of stars in Antlia II in the framework of MOG theory. Antlia II is a recently discovered low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxy being 100 more diffuse than ultra diffuse galaxies. It has a very wide core 3 kpc, and it is supposed to be strongly dominated by dark matter ( Torrealba2019, idm2019 ).

sources.summary: The paper models Antlia 2 as a diffuse low-surface-brightness satellite and finds it can be reproduced in CDM or SIDM, requiring low CDM concentration while SIDM core formation enhances tidal mass loss.

quotes.quote: The newly discovered Antlia 2 satellite galaxy is the lowest surface brightness galaxy known to date torrealba2019 . Its half-light radius is 2.9 kpc , making it two orders of magnitude more diffuse than ultra-diffuse galaxies Koda:2015gwa . It is located near the Galactic plane, at a galactic latitude of 11^ . Because of these unique properties, the Antlia 2 galaxy has been dubbed ``hidden giant."

Considering the above observations, we find that Antlia 2 could be realized in both CDM and SIDM with well-motivated model parameters. In our simulations, we chose the initial stellar and halo masses using the mass-metallicity and abundance-matching relations, respectively, and adopted the stellar scale radius consistent with predictions from hydrodynamical simulations with realistic feedback prescriptions. We find that the initial halo concentration in CDM should be lower than the cosmological mean to be consistent with the observations of Antlia 2. In SIDM, core formation enhances the tidal mass loss for both dark matter and stars; it also enhances the expansion of the stellar distribution.

sources.summary: The paper describes Antlia 2 as an unusually diffuse Milky Way satellite with exceptionally low velocity dispersion, low dark matter density, and a diffuse dark matter halo.

quotes.quote: In terms of tidal effects, Antlia 2 and Crater 2 have drawn attention as unusually diffuse satellite galaxies in the Milky Way 2016MNRAS.459.2370T,2019MNRAS.488.2743T . More surprisingly, these galaxies have exceptionally low velocity dispersion, even though they have large half-light radii 2017ApJ...839...20C,2021ApJ...921...32J . A central question is whether such diffuse galaxies can be explained by tidal forces in the context of CDM paradigm. Some simulation works presented that CDM models can reproduce the stellar properties of the diffuse galaxies with strong tidal stripping, especially if the dark matter halo has cored central density induced by stellar feedback and/or the halo has extremely low concentration e.g., 2015MNRAS.449L..46E,2019ApJ...886L...3R,2019MNRAS.488.2743T,2020ApJ...892..137S,2022NatAs...6..496M .

Thirdly, dark matter densities of the diffuse galaxies (Antlia 2 and Crater 2) are around one order of magnitude less dense than those of the other dwarf satellites. In particular, Antlia 2 favors having the diffuse dark matter halo as well as its stellar component. The dark matter scale density ( _0) of Antlia 2 is consistent with the result from 2019MNRAS.488.2743T .

sources.summary: The paper presents Antlia 2 as an ultra-diffuse, very low-surface-brightness Local Group system whose distance and RR Lyrae membership remain uncertain and motivate LSST observations.

quotes.quote: Antlia 2 is located behind the Galactic disc at a galactic latitude of b 11^ and spans about 1.26 degrees in size, which corresponds to about 2.9 kpc at a distance of 130 kpc estimated for the galaxy in the discovery paper. This new stellar system was identified using a combination of astrometry, photometry and variability data (RR Lyrae stars) from Gaia Data Release 2, and its nature confirmed with deep archival DECam imaging, which revealed a conspicuous BHB signal, in very good agreement with distance the authors estimated from Gaia RR Lyrae stars. While similar in extent to the Large Magellanic Cloud, Antlia 2 would be orders of magnitude fainter with M_ V = 8.5 mag, making it by far the lowest surface brightness system known (at 32.3 mag/arcsec ^2 ), 100 times more diffuse than the so-called ultra diffuse galaxies.

However, controversy exists on the actual distance of this system because there was an error in the calculation of the distance from the RR Lyrae stars. The Gaia RR Lyrae stars that triggered the discovery are at about 80 kpc in distance. Therefore, either they do not belong to Antlia 2, or if they are members the distance to the galaxy is more on the order of 80-90 kpc, or, as lately suggested by the authors, the Gaia RR Lyrae stars lay in front of the dwarf along the line of sight and likely represent the near side of an extended cloud of tidal debris spawn by Antlia 2 (V. Belokurov, private communication).

sources.summary: The paper treats Antlia II as an unusually large, low-mass, low-density dwarf whose stellar motions favor an ultralight-boson soliton core near the Jeans-scale limit for galaxy formation.

quotes.quote: This puzzle is exacerbated by the discovery of a very large but barely visible, dark matter dominated galaxy Antlia II orbiting the Milky Way, uncovered by tracking star motions with the Gaia satellite. Although Antlia II has a low mass, its visible radius is more than double any known dwarf galaxy, with an unprecedentedly low density core. We show that Antlia II favors dark matter as a Bose-Einstein condensate, for which the ground state is a stable soliton with a core radius given by the de Broglie wavelength. The lower the galaxy mass, the larger the de Broglie wavelength, so the least massive galaxies should have the widest soliton cores of lowest density. An ultra-light boson of m_ 1.1 10^ 22 eV, accounts well for the large size and slowly moving stars within Antlia II, and agrees with boson mass estimates derived from the denser cores of more massive dwarf galaxies.

For this very light boson, Antlia II is close to the lower limiting Jeans scale for galaxy formation permitted by the Uncertainty Principle, so other examples are expected but none significantly larger in size.

sources.summary: The paper presents new spectroscopy of Antlia 2, finding a velocity gradient, revised metallicity, and tidal-disruption evidence while noting tension with models requiring extreme stellar mass loss.

quotes.quote: We present new spectroscopic observations of the diffuse Milky Way satellite galaxies Antlia 2 and Crater 2, taken as part of the Southern Stellar Stream Spectroscopic Survey ( ). The new observations approximately double the number of confirmed member stars in each galaxy and more than double the spatial extent of spectroscopic observations in Antlia 2. A full kinematic analysis, including EDR3 proper motions, detects a clear velocity gradient in Antlia 2 and a tentative velocity gradient in Crater 2.

Furthermore, the orbit and kinematics of Antlia 2 require a model that includes the reflex motion of the Milky Way induced by the Large Magellanic Cloud. We also find that Antlia 2's metallicity was previously overestimated, so it lies on the empirical luminosity-metallicity relation and is likely only now experiencing substantial stellar mass loss. Current dynamical models of Antlia 2 require it to have lost over 90 of its stars to tides, in tension with the low stellar mass loss implied by the updated metallicity.

sources.summary: The paper models Antlia II as an extended, low-surface-brightness Milky Way dwarf whose gas stripping and tidal shocks after pericenter produce elongation and a velocity gradient.

quotes.quote: Antlia II is the only MW dwarf galaxy that has been discovered using Gaia DR2 proper motions Torrealba2019 . It possesses a very large radius ( r_ half = 2.54 kpc), an extremely low surface brightness even when compared to the most ultra-faint dwarfs, and an ellipticity of 0.6. Its velocity dispersion is 5.98 km s ^ 1 , and it shows a large radial velocity gradient (2.49 km s ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 ). Antlia II has a positive Galactic radial velocity Li2021,Ji2021 and is leaving its pericenter toward its apocenter.

Since the simulated Antlia II has left its pericenter where it has suffered strong tidal shocks and gas stripping, the dwarf has been stretched as shown by the elongated morphology in Panels (), (d), and (e). In panel (e), the major axis (red line) and the proper motion direction (black arrow, after correcting the solar reflex motion) shares roughly the same direction with a difference of 5 degrees, which is comparable to the observational value ( 15 degrees from Ji2021 ).

sources.summary: The appendix describes Antlia 2 as a diffuse dwarf galaxy found with Gaia kinematic filtering, gives a SkyMapper-Gaia CMD selection, reddening, visible red giant branch, and a distance near 110 kpc.

quotes.quote: Antlia 2 is a diffuse dwarf galaxy discovered using a Gaia kinematic filter (Torrealba et al 2018). Such a filter can be added to a query such as the one in 2.1 and for a radius of 0.63 degrees around the centre of Antlia 2, requiring < 0.1 mas, PMRA(cos ) > -1.5 mas/yr and | PMDEC | < 1.5 mas/yr yields the CMD in Figure A1. The reddening of Antlia 2 is E(B-V) = 0.16 mag according to Schlegel et al (1998). The red giant branch is clearly seen in the SkyMapper-Gaia photometry and is not seen in a control sample annulus 1 degree from Antlia 2. Evidently, Antlia 2 is closer to 110 kpc away than the 129.4 6.5 kpc measured by Torrealba et al.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Antlia 2 as an unusually large, low-dispersion feeble-giant satellite whose properties violate tidal-limited dwarf expectations and challenge LCDM assumptions.

quotes.quote: In particular, the large size of kinematically-cold ``feeble giant'' satellites like Crater 2 or Antlia 2 cannot be explained as due to tidal effects alone in the Lambda Cold Dark Matter scenario.

These are galaxies of unusually low velocity dispersion for their size, like the MW ``feeble giant'' satellites Crater 2 and Antlia 2, as well as the M31 satellites And 19, And 21 and And 25. Our results imply that tidal effects are not a viable explanation for these galaxies in the context of LCDM.
Reconciling systems like Crater 2, Antlia 2, or And 19 with LCDM seems to require substantial revision to at least one of the assumptions of this work.

sources.summary: Antlia II is treated as a very low surface brightness Local Group satellite whose properties may reflect tidal stripping plus a cored dark matter profile.

quotes.quote: Antlia II Antlia II is a satellite of the Milky Way and resides in the Local Group. Due to its extremely low surface brightness, it is unlikely that similar analogues will be found outside of the local group. Dynamical modelling by torrealba2019 suggests that a combination of tidal stripping and a cored dark matter profile can explain the properties of Antlia II. Due to the suggestion of tidal stripping, any dynamical mass calculated with the data should be treated with caution.

sources.summary: The paper uses Antilla II as a diffuse satellite example whose low-density stellar component can be explained by SIDM low-concentration halos that develop large cores.

quotes.quote: This correlation between the stellar extent and the DM core provides a natural explanation for diffuse stellar components of galaxies like Crater II and Antilla II . Conversely, halos that have undergone core collapse, after significant tidal stripping, have small DM core sizes and high DM core densities.

The key difference between CDM and SIDM low-concentration halos that suffer significant tidal mass loss is that SIDM models unequivocally predict a large core for these subhalos, which likely host satellites like Crater II and Antilla II.

sources.summary: The paper discusses Antlia II as a large, low-dispersion dwarf whose velocity gradient and likely tidal elongation make tidal effects more evident than in Crater II.

quotes.quote: Similarly, it has been proposed that an ultra-light boson of m_ ^2 = ( 0.6 - 1.4 ) 10^ 22 eV accounts for the large size and modest velocity dispersion stars within Antlia II, consistent with boson mass estimates for more massive dwarf galaxies with smaller dark cores Schive2014 and places Antlia II close to the lower limiting Jeans scale for galaxy formation permitted by the Uncertainty Principle for this very light boson mass. New spectroscopy data has revealed that Antlia II has a systematic velocity gradient that is comparable with its velocity dispersion Ji2021 indicating that it has suffered tidal elongation or possibly rotating, at least in the outskirts where the sign change of the velocity gradient is apparent Ji2021 . The tidal field of Crater II is also thought to have had a significant effect given the small pericenter that is now established for Crater II in the latest 'Gaia' based analysis Ji2021 , though it does not show any clear evidence for a velocity gradient nor visible elongation along its orbit, unlike the case of Antlia II where tidal effects appear more evident Ji2021 .

gmw_00005123.name: SPT-S 233227-5358.5
gmw_00005123.overview: SPT-S 233227-5358.5 is a gravitationally lensed, dusty star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.73 [arXiv:1305.0614]. Observations of its CO(1-0) line emission reveal a substantial molecular gas mass and a spatially resolved structure split into two main peaks [arXiv:1305.0614].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SPT-S 233227-5358.5 as a gravitationally lensed z=2.73 dusty star-forming galaxy with CO(1-0) emission, substantial molecular gas, and resolved lensed submillimeter structure.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The phase tracking centers were _ 2000 =23^h32^m27.8^s , _ 2000 =-53^ 58'37'' , and _ 2000 =05^h38^m16.8^s , _ 2000 =-50^ 30'52'' , respectively, based on the prior positions obtained with LABOCA at 870 m.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of CO(1-0) line emission in the bright, lensed star-forming galaxies SPT-S 233227-5358.5 ( z=2.73 ) and SPT-S 053816-5030.8 ( z=2.78 ), using the Australia Telescope Compact Array (ATCA). Both galaxies were discovered in a large-area millimeter survey with the South Pole Telescope (SPT) and found to be gravitationally lensed by intervening structures. The measured CO intensities imply galaxies with molecular gas masses of (3.2 0.5) 10^ 10 ( /15)^ 1 (X_ CO /0.8) M_ and (1.7 0.3) 10^ 10 ( /20)^ 1 (X_ CO /0.8) M_ , and gas depletion timescales of 4.9 10^7(X_ CO /0.8) yr and 2.6 10^7(X_ CO /0.8) yr, respectively, where corresponds to the lens magnification and X_ CO is the CO luminosity to gas mass conversion factor.

The CO(1-0) line toward SPT \ 2332-53 is well detected and spatially resolved into two main peaks separated by 30'' (Fig. fig:co_image ). The CO profiles of both peaks (east and west) are coincident in frequency and line shape suggesting that they arise from the same lensed background object (Fig. fig:co_line ). A comparison with the SABOCA image from greve12 indicates that the CO distribution corresponds to the blended IR components A, B, and C, following the nomenclature adopted by Greve et al.

gmw_00005124.name: SPT-S 053816-5030.8
gmw_00005124.overview: SPT-S 053816-5030.8 is a gravitationally lensed, dusty star-forming galaxy at a redshift of z = 2.78 [arXiv:1305.0614]. It exhibits properties compatible with a scaled-up local ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1305.0614] and is identified as a possible ongoing major merger [arXiv:1906.05469].

sources.summary: The paper treats SPT-S 053816-5030.8 as a lensed z=2.78 dusty star-forming galaxy with CO and radio continuum detections, high intrinsic SFR, and ULIRG-like compact starburst properties.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The phase tracking centers were _ 2000 =23^h32^m27.8^s , _ 2000 =-53^ 58'37'' , and _ 2000 =05^h38^m16.8^s , _ 2000 =-50^ 30'52'' , respectively, based on the prior positions obtained with LABOCA at 870 m.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of CO(1-0) line emission in the bright, lensed star-forming galaxies SPT-S 233227-5358.5 ( z=2.73 ) and SPT-S 053816-5030.8 ( z=2.78 ), using the Australia Telescope Compact Array (ATCA). Both galaxies were discovered in a large-area millimeter survey with the South Pole Telescope (SPT) and found to be gravitationally lensed by intervening structures. The measured CO intensities imply galaxies with molecular gas masses of (3.2 0.5) 10^ 10 ( /15)^ 1 (X_ CO /0.8) M_ and (1.7 0.3) 10^ 10 ( /20)^ 1 (X_ CO /0.8) M_ , and gas depletion timescales of 4.9 10^7(X_ CO /0.8) yr and 2.6 10^7(X_ CO /0.8) yr, respectively, where corresponds to the lens magnification and X_ CO is the CO luminosity to gas mass conversion factor.

In the case of SPT-S 053816-5030.8, we also obtained significant detections of the rest-frame 115.7 and 132.4 GHz radio continuum. Based on the radio to infrared spectral energy distribution and an assumed synchrotron spectral index, we find that 42 10 and 55 13 of the flux at rest-frame 115.7 and 132.4 GHz arise from free-free emission. We find a radio-derived intrinsic star formation rate (SFR) of 470 170 M_ yr ^ 1 , consistent within the uncertainties with the infrared estimate. Based on the morphology of this object in the source plane, the derived gas mass and the possible flattening of the radio spectral index towards low frequencies, we argue that SPT-S 053816-5030.8 exhibits properties compatible with a scaled-up local ultra-luminous infrared galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper observes SPT0538-50's water line with ALMA, describes it as a possible ongoing major merger with CO observations, and includes it among similar lensed SPT targets observed in Cycles 3 and 4.

quotes.quote: We observed the \ 987.927 GHz line in SPT0529-54 ( z = 3.369), SPT0532-50 ( z = 3.399) and SPT0538-50 ( z = 2.782) with ALMA.

Sample Selection The three SPT targets were selected such that they are at a similar redshift and within 10 ^ of each other on the sky. This selection was chosen to observe the same line transition in the three galaxies and to make observations efficient for resolved ALMA Band 9 continuum observations, which where A-rated in Cycle 5, but not yet observed. All three sources have ALMA 870 m imaging and lens models spilker16 . SPT0538-50 is a possible ongoing major merger as seen from dust continuum models bothwell13 and has resolved CO (1-0) and CO (3-2) ATCA observations aravena13,spilker15 . SPT0529-54 and SPT0532-50 have resolved CO (6-5) observations from ALMA dong19 which we make use of in this work.
Data Reduction and Imaging For SPT0532-50 and SPT0538-50, the observations are taken with ALMA in Cycle 3 (2015.1.01578.S) and Cycle 4 (2016.1.01554.S).

gmw_00005125.name: A1413-1
gmw_00005125.overview: A1413-1 is a gravitationally lensed, compact quiescent galaxy at a redshift of z=1.71 [arXiv:1306.2496]. It hosts an old stellar population with an estimated age of 2.4 Gyr and exhibits negligible ongoing star formation [arXiv:1306.2496].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes A1413-1 as a gravitationally lensed compact quiescent high-redshift galaxy, deriving z=1.71, negligible ongoing star formation, an old stellar population, and high stellar mass.

quotes.quote: The resulting redshifts are z=1.71 0.02 for A1413-1 and z=2.15 0.01 for MACS2129-1.

The model fit for A1413-1 indicates no on-going star formation (with an upper limit of 0.2 M_ yr^ 1 ), and for MACS2129-1 the fit allows for a star formation rate of 2 2 M_ yr^ 1 which according to the resulting specific star formation rate of (1.8 1.8) 10^ 11 yr^ 1 means that MACS2129-1 can be regarded as a passively evolving galaxy.

From SED modeling, we derived the ages of the stellar populations to be in the range of 2.4^ +0.8 _ 0.6 Gyr (A1413-1) and 1.7 0.3 Gyr (MACS2129-1). This is an intriguing result, as they turn out to be older and thus exhibit higher mass-to-light ratios than most galaxies from other studies 2008ApJ...677..219K,2009ApJ...700..221K,2010ApJ...718L..73V,2011ApJ...736L...9V,2012ApJ...754....3T,2012arXiv1211.3424V . This is mostly due to the fact that younger galaxies are easier to detect. indeed argue that their sample is biased towards young galaxies, compared to a mass-limited sample. By scaling to the intrinsic luminosities, taking into account the enhancement of the observed brightness by the lensing effect, and including the uncertainty on the lensing magnification in quadrature into the error budget, we infer their stellar masses to be (7.6 1.1) 10^ 10 M_ (A1413-1) and (1.1 0.28) 10^ 11 M_ (MACS2129-1).

gmw_00005126.name: MACS2129-1
gmw_00005126.overview: MACS2129-1 is a compact, gravitationally lensed galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.15 [arXiv:1306.2496]. It is classified as a passive lenticular galaxy with a disky profile and very little ongoing star formation [arXiv:1306.2496].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes MACS2129-1 as a lensed compact quiescent galaxy at z=2.15, finding little ongoing star formation and a passive, disky lenticular morphology.

quotes.quote: The resulting redshifts are z=1.71 0.02 for A1413-1 and z=2.15 0.01 for MACS2129-1.

The model fit for A1413-1 indicates no on-going star formation (with an upper limit of 0.2 M_ yr^ 1 ), and for MACS2129-1 the fit allows for a star formation rate of 2 2 M_ yr^ 1 which according to the resulting specific star formation rate of (1.8 1.8) 10^ 11 yr^ 1 means that MACS2129-1 can be regarded as a passively evolving galaxy. We emphasize that this value for star formation is solely based on the best-fit model with the assumption of an exponentially declining star formation rate. This approximation does most likely not represent reality 2012arXiv1212.4153A and as a consequence the computed star formation rates should not be taken too literally.

The structural analysis of galaxy MACS2129-1 reveals a profile which is more disky than an exponential disk with n=0.88 . This shape classifies it as an S0 like galaxy. Only relatively recently it has been possible to identify disk-like galaxies around z 2 e.g. 2004ApJ...605...37S,2008ApJ...672..146S,2006Natur.442..786G, 2011ApJ...730...38V, 2013ApJ...762...83C . Combined with the constraints on potentially on-going star formation it constitutes the first spectroscopically confirmed passive lenticular galaxy at z>2 .

gmw_00005127.name: SDSS J224113-012108
gmw_00005127.overview: SDSS J224113-012108 is a changing-look quasar at a redshift of z=0.059 [arXiv:2107.09214]. It is identified as the bluest known object of its class, exhibiting long-term photometric variability and spectral type transitions that are not well explained by dust obscuration [arXiv:2107.09214].

sources.summary: The paper reports SDSS J224113-012108 as the bluest changing-look QSO, with type-transition spectra, measurable host velocity dispersion, discrepant virial and M-sigma black-hole masses, variable photometry, and dust-obscuration disfavored.

quotes.quote: In this manuscript, based on multi-epoch SDSS spectra, we report a new and so-far the bluest CLQSO SDSS J224113-012108 (=SDSS J2241) at z=0.059 , of which both the host galaxy properties and the bright nuclei can be well determined during its type transitions,leading to quite different virial BH mass from the mass determined through the M-sigma relation (a detailed review can be found in kh13 ), which will provide further clues on physical mechanism of type transitions in CLAGN.

Based on the multi-epoch SDSS spectra from 2011 to 2017, the flux ratio of broad H to broad H has been changed from 7 \ in 2011 to 2.7 \ in 2017, leading SDSS J2241 with spectral index _ 5.21 0.02 ( < 4000 ) in 2017 to be so-far the bluest CLQSO. Based on the SDSS spectrum in 2011, the host galaxy contributions with stellar velocity dispersion 86 km/s can be well determined, leading to the M-sigma relation expected central BH mass 3 10^6 M_ . However, through properties of the broad H , the virial BH mass is 10^8 M_ , about two magnitudes larger than the mass through the M-sigma relation.

The different BH masses through different methods indicate SDSS J2241 is one unique CLQSO. Meanwhile, the long-term photometric light curve shows interesting variability properties, not expected by DRW process commonly applied in AGN but probably connected to a central TDE. Furthermore, based on continuum emission properties in 2017 with no dust obscurations, only considering the moving dust clouds cannot be preferred to explain the CLQSO SDSS J2241, because the expected intrinsic reddening corrected continuum emissions were unreasonably higher than the unobscured continuum emissions in 2017.

gmw_00005128.name: MRC 0316-257
gmw_00005128.overview: MRC 0316-257 is a high-redshift radio galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 3.13 [arXiv:astro-ph/0401357, arXiv:1203.2196]. It resides near the center of a massive protocluster [arXiv:0807.0152, arXiv:1002.4198] characterized by an overdensity of Lyman-alpha, O III, and Lyman-break galaxies [arXiv:1203.2196, arXiv:1002.4198].

sources.summary: The paper describes MRC 0316--257 as a z 3.13 radio galaxy marking a Ly emitter overdensity with small, blue, mostly sub-L* galaxies and elevated star-formation-rate density.

quotes.quote: In total seven proto-clusters were discovered, including a proto-cluster around the radio galaxy MRC 0316--257 at z 3.13 . This structure has an excess of Ly emitters by a factor of 3 as compared to the field, and the derived mass is 2--5 10^ 14 M _ . The Ly emitters in the proto-cluster are on average bluer than Lyman Break Galaxies (LBGs). Also, the galaxies are faint (sub L_* ) and small (half light radii < 1.7 kpc, which is smaller than the average size of LBGs).

One of the targets in our program was the radio galaxy MRC 0316--257. There were several additional reasons that this radio galaxy was chosen. First of all, it was already known that the object had two spectroscopically confirmed companions . Secondly, the redshift of the radio galaxy ( z=3.13 ) allows for an efficient search for Lyman Break Galaxies (LBGs) and for [ OIII ] 5007 \ emitters using a K band narrowband filter mounted on an infrared camera.

sources.summary: The paper treats MRC 0316-257 as a z=3.13 high-redshift radio galaxy whose field hosts Lyman-alpha, O III, and LBG overdensities, including possible foreground structure.

quotes.quote: We present spectroscopic follow-up observations of Lyman Break Galaxies (LBGs) selected in the field surrounding the radio galaxy MRC 0316-257 at z 3.13 (0316). Robust spectroscopic redshifts are determined for 20 out of 24 objects. Three of the spectroscopically confirmed galaxies have 3.12<z<3.13 indicating that these objects reside in a protocluster structure previously found around the radio galaxy.

One of the HzRG fields for which the LBG selection criterion has been used is MRC 0316-257 at z=3.13 . This is one of the best studied HzRGs at z>2.5 and it has been shown to host an overdensity of Ly emitters hereafter V05 venemans2005 . A study by hereafter M08 maschietto2008 has found tentative evidence for a similar overdensity of [ O iii ] emitters. In an attempt to obtain a complete galaxy census of the overdensity, hereafter K10 kuiper2010 assembled photometry of the field in 18 bands ranging from U band to Spitzer 8 m.

sources.summary: The paper describes MRC 0316--257 as a z=3.13 radio galaxy embedded in a protocluster, with nearby emitters and an overdensity tracing a larger forming structure.

quotes.quote: Venemans2005 found evidence for an overdensity of Ly emission line galaxies associated with the radio galaxy MRC 0316--257 at z=3.13 indicating the presence of a massive protocluster. Narrow-band infrared imaging was used to select candidate [ O iii ] emitters in a 1.1 1.1 Mpc ^2 region around the radio galaxy. The three newly confirmed objects lie within a few hundred km s ^ 1 of each other, but are blueshifted with respect to the radio galaxy and Ly emitters by 2100 km s ^ 1 .

This article presents observations of the protocluster surrounding the PROCESS radio galaxy MRC 0316-257. The associated 1.5 \ Jy radio source was listed in the 408 MHz Molonglo Reference Catalogue Large1981 and was optically identified with a galaxy at z=3.13 by McCarthy1990 . LeFevre1996 spectroscopically confirmed two Ly emitting companions to the HzRG, indicating that the radio galaxy is located in a dense environment.

sources.summary: The paper observes the z = 3.13 radio galaxy MRC 0316--257 and its surrounding field, finding nearby Lyman-alpha emitters whose overdensity marks a likely protocluster environment.

quotes.quote: Observations of the radio galaxy MRC 0316--257 at z = 3.13 and the surrounding field are presented. Using narrow- and broad-band imaging obtained with the VLT, 77 candidate \ emitters with a rest-frame equivalent width of > 15 \ were selected in a 7 7 \ field around the radio galaxy. Spectroscopy of 40 candidate emitters resulted in the discovery of 33 emission line galaxies of which 31 are \ emitters with redshifts similar to that of the radio galaxy, while the remaining two galaxies turned out to be [ O ii ] emitters.

Here we report on observations of the radio galaxy MRC 0316--257. This 1.5 Jy radio source was listed in the 408 MHz Molonglo Reference Catalogue lar81 and optically identified by mcc90 . Its discovery spectrum yielded a redshift of 3.13 mcc90 .

sources.summary: The paper presents MRC 0316--257 as a z=3.13 radio galaxy in an overdense environment with 31 nearby Ly-alpha emitters and a massive protocluster.

quotes.quote: MRC 0316--257, z = 3.13 This 1.5 Jy radio source from the Molonglo Reference Catalogue lar81 lies at a redshift of 3.13 by mcc90 . This target was especially interesting, because lef96 found two bright \ emitters close to the radio galaxy, which indicated that this radio galaxy is in an overdense environment. Narrow- and broad-band imaging of this field with the VLT resulted in the discovery of 77 candidate \ emitters. Follow-up spectroscopy revealed 33 emission line galaxies of which 31 are \ emitters near the radio galaxy, while the remaining two are foreground galaxies. By comparing the number density of \ emitters near the radio galaxy to that of the field, the overdensity of emitters near 0316 is estimated to be a factor 3.3^ +0.5 _ 0.4 times the field density (V05).
In V05 we have shown that the confirmed emitters are members of a protocluster at z 3.13 with an estimated mass of > 3 10^ 14 .

sources.summary: The paper studies galaxy populations around the high-redshift radio galaxy MRC 0316--257, finding surrounding LBG and BBG evidence for an early-stage protocluster centered near the radio galaxy.

quotes.quote: We present a population study of several types of galaxies within the protocluster surrounding the radio galaxy MRC 0316--257 at z 3.1 . In addition to the known population of Ly emitters (LAEs) and [ O iii ] emitters, we use colour selection techniques to identify protocluster candidates that are Lyman break galaxies (LBG) and Balmer break galaxies (BBGs). The radio galaxy field contains an excess of LBG candidates, with a surface density 1.6 0.3 times larger than found for comparable blank fields.

The most massive and intensely star forming galaxies are located primarily near to the radio galaxy indicating that proximity to the radio galaxy influences galaxy evolution. No other high mass and high SFR regions are found in the 0316 field, indicating that the radio galaxy is near the centre of the protocluster.

gmw_00005129.name: Object 12
gmw_00005129.overview: Object 12 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy candidate [arXiv:1203.2196]. It has a single strong absorption feature and evidence of a Lyman break, which indicates an adopted redshift of z=3.1127 [arXiv:1203.2196].

sources.summary: Object 12 is discussed as an LBG candidate with an ambiguous single absorption feature; comparison to other spectra leads the authors to adopt redshift z=3.1127.

quotes.quote: There is one object where the redshift is possibly ambiguous. Object 12 has only one identifiable absorption feature and evidence for a break. Since the Lyman break is a poor redshift indicator, this makes it difficult to set an accurate redshift. The single absorption feature is very strong and based on the approximate location of the break, the line can be identified as either the O i /Si ii doublet at 1303 or C ii at 1335 . This indicates its redshift is either z 3.11 or z 3.01 .

To ascertain which is the more likely, the 2D spectrum of 12 is compared to the 2D spectra of other objects with strong breaks and clear O i /Si ii and C ii features. The former option better resembles the other 2D spectra and therefore we conclude that the redshift of 12 is z=3.1127 .

gmw_00005130.name: HE0108-4743
gmw_00005130.overview: HE0108-4743 is a barred galaxy that features an exponential-like bar structure [arXiv:1905.05214]. Despite having a large Sersic index, the galaxy exhibits high star formation activity that is continuously distributed across its entire body rather than being restricted to the bar or spiral arms [arXiv:1905.05214].

sources.summary: HE0108-4743 is presented as an outlier with high bar-region star formation despite a large Sersic index, probably affected by galaxy-wide star formation contamination.

quotes.quote: The galaxy HE0108-4743 stands out from the general trend found for the other galaxies in the sample. Despite a rather large S ersic index, it shows high star formation activity in the bar region. However, the H found in this galaxy is not limited to the bar region or spiral arms, but rather seems to be a continuous feature across the whole galaxy. Possibly, there has been a burst of star formation that occurred everywhere in the galaxy. This could mean that different processes are in place as compared to just the typical evolution of star formation in galaxy bars.
The SFR of the bar should therefore be considered as an upper limit.

By purely examining the major axis profiles, we find only four bars that are exponential-like, i.e. HE0021-1819, HE0108-4743, HE2222-0026, and HE2233+0124. The S ersic indices of these bars are between 0.55 n_ bar 0.97 , thus, they are among the larger values.

gmw_00005131.name: HE 1108-2813
gmw_00005131.overview: HE 1108-2813 is a bulgeless, barred grand-design spiral galaxy [arXiv:1707.01081] classified as a Seyfert 1.5 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1707.01081]. While the central region is dominated by the active nucleus [arXiv:1905.05214], star formation drives the emission in its bar and spiral arms [arXiv:1905.05214].

sources.summary: HE 1108-2813 is used to demonstrate emission-line analysis; its AGN-dominated center contrasts with bar and spiral-arm H-alpha from star formation.

quotes.quote: We show in Fig. fig:BPT the spatially resolved emission line diagnostic for the galaxy HE 1108-2813. Each point in the left panel of this figure corresponds to one spectrum in the MUSE cube. The right panel shows the spatial location of the data points from the BPT diagram overplotted on the i band image of the galaxy. Regions are coloured according to the fraction of H that comes from star formation.
The figure shows that all spaxels that are most affected by the AGN are centrally concentrated and the H from the bar region and the spiral arms is almost exclusively caused by star formation.

The final SFR map for the galaxy HE 1108-2813 overplotted with the bar region is shown in Fig. fig:SFR_map . The extent of the bar is defined by the parameters of the photometric decomposition and the measurement of the bar length.

sources.summary: The paper describes HE 1108-2813 as a BLS1/Seyfert 1.5 galaxy at z=0.02401, with a barred dusty grand-design spiral host, bulgeless morphology, central dust, and star-forming bar edges.

quotes.quote: HE 1108-2813 is a BLS1 galaxy with a redshift of z = 0.02401 0.00004 Theureau2005 and with Balmer broad line widths of about 5000 km s ^ 1 Kewley2001,Crenshaw2003,Wang2007 . Kollatschny1983 classify it as Seyfert 1.5 galaxy based on the Balmer line peak ratio of the broad and narrow line components. They find the nucleus to have a steep (negative) continuum slope toward the UV, corresponding to the absence of higher ionization lines (e.g., C iv , C ii ] ), on the one hand, but strong optical and UV Fe ii ] ) multiplets on the other.

The faint host see, e.g., dominant AGN component; Yuan2010 is a barred and dusty grand design spiral galaxy Deo2006 of Hubble type SBc/d Malkan1998 and seems to be bulgeless Orban2011 . The dust is mainly distributed in chaotic regions in the central kiloparsec and the bar, and in large scale lanes, along the leading edges of the bar and in the main spiral arms. Prominent star-forming regions are found along the bar edges Deo2006 .

gmw_00005132.name: HE2222-0026
gmw_00005132.overview: HE2222-0026 is a galaxy characterized by an exponential-like bar structure [arXiv:1905.05214]. The galaxy exhibits a global lack of fueling gas, resulting in very little H-alpha emission and an absence of star formation along its bar [arXiv:1905.05214].

sources.summary: HE2222-0026 is treated as a non-star-forming bar special case whose weak H-alpha emission and low molecular gas mass explain the lack of bar star formation.

quotes.quote: Another special case is HE2222-0026. In contrast to the other galaxies with non-SF bars in our sample, in this galaxy we do not observe any H in the outer disc and we only observe very little along the bar. Furthermore, the total molecular gas mass is rather low M(H_2) = 0.5 10^ 9 M_ Bertram2007a . In this special case, the absence of star formation in the bar is simply explained by a global lack of fueling gas.

By purely examining the major axis profiles, we find only four bars that are exponential-like, i.e. HE0021-1819, HE0108-4743, HE2222-0026, and HE2233+0124. The S ersic indices of these bars are between 0.55 n_ bar 0.97 , thus, they are among the larger values.

gmw_00005133.name: mask1-16
gmw_00005133.overview: The high-redshift galaxy mask1-16 is an active galactic nucleus characterized by broad emission lines [arXiv:2002.06207]. It is a Lyman-alpha emitter that has been detected in X-rays by Chandra and at 20 cm in the radio by the VLA [arXiv:2002.06207].

sources.summary: The paper identifies mask1-16 as a CC2.2 member with Chandra X-ray detection, broad emission lines, Ly emission, and a VLA 20 cm radio detection.

quotes.quote: The match to the COSMOS2015 catalog shows that three of the members, mask1-1, mask1-15, and mask1-16 have Chandra X-ray detections Elvis09,Civano16,Marchesi16 .
All three have broad emission lines, indicative of their AGN nature and they are all Ly emitters as well Matthee16,Sobral17 .
mask1-16 also has a VLA 20 cm radio detection Schinnerer10 .

gmw_00005134.name: DA 55
gmw_00005134.overview: DA 55 is a highly polarized, flat-spectrum radio quasar located at a redshift of 0.859 [arXiv:1510.08153, arXiv:1510.08150]. The source is dominated by a compact core and features a continuous northwestern radio jet [arXiv:1510.08153, arXiv:1510.08150] with a 2.2-year variability timescale at 22 and 37 GHz [arXiv:0807.1796].

sources.summary: The paper presents 0133+476 as a flat-spectrum radio quasar whose KaVA map shows a northwestern jet, limited resolved structure, three components, and 1.73 Jy total flux.

quotes.quote: 0133+476 (DA55) Figure fig:fig2 shows 0133+476 (DA 55), a flat-spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ) with a redshift of 0.859 (similar to 1055+018). The corresponding angular scale is 7.7 pc/mas. The source is also known as a ray emitter cf. and references therein Nagai2013 . Lister2013 found a maximum apparent jet speed of 15.4c .

Our map shows a jet in north-west direction extending out to 4 mas. The jet shows a rather continuous shape; this indicates that the angular resolution of KaVA at 22 GHz is not enough to resolve the detailed structure. Even though, we were able to detect three model components (including the core). The total flux of the source is 1.73 Jy, with a peak intensity of 1.63 Jy/beam.

sources.summary: The VLBA analysis describes 0133+476 as a radio quasar with boundary-core rotation measure structure, an ambiguous sub-beam gradient, and core electric field aligned with the jet.

quotes.quote: Polarization observations of 6 radio--quasars (0133+476, 0420-014, 0745+241, 0906+430, 1633+382 and 1954+513) and 2 BL Lac objects (0256+075 and 1823+568) were carried out in a 24 hour session on November 2, 2008 using the VLBA.

0133+476 is a quasar showing the RM in the boundaries of the VLBI core. The values obtained for the RM span from -4000 to 1000 rad/m ^2 . Although the slice taken shows a very clear gradient with a large SNR ( 15 ), the region where a possible gradient is seen is very limited, less than one beam width, making this very ambiguous. According to the simulations performed in the Appendix, and together with the fact that it is located in the core, this is not a reliable gradient.
In several sources, the electric field in the core seems to be either perpendicular (0745+241, 1954+513) or parallel (0133+476, 0256+075, 0420--014, 0906+430) to the direction of the jet.

sources.summary: The paper describes DA 55 as an HPQ monitored at Metsahovi, with jet VLBI studies and a 2.2-year 22/37 GHz wavelet timescale close to prior UMRAO results.

quotes.quote: DA 55 ( 0133+476 ) : This HPQ source has been monitored in Mets ahovi for over 20 years at 22 and 37 GHz and for almost 15 years at 90 GHz. It has shown high apparent pattern velocity of 18c in the jet piner07 and has been also a target of many other studies using Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) observations e.g. lister01, kellermann04, lister05 . It is also included in the wavelet study of which uses the UMRAO 4.8, 8, 14.5 GHz monitoring data. They found a timescale of 2.3 years for the source at 14.5 GHz. This is very close to the timescale of 2.2 years which we obtained for the source at both 22 and 37 GHz.

sources.summary: The paper describes DA 55 as a compact, highly polarized quasar dominated by its core, with significant linear polarization mapped at 22 and 43 GHz.

quotes.quote: DA 55 DA 55 is a compact and highly polarized quasar at redshift z=0.859 . It has a compact structure and is dominated by the core (Figure fig:da55_imaps ). Although the structure is only marginally resolved, we detected and mapped significant linear polarization at both 22 and 43 GHz. Figure fig:da55_polmaps shows the spatial distribution of m_ L and polarized intensity. At both frequencies, DA 55 shows m_ L 4 and well-ordered polarization angles across the source.

gmw_00005135.name: OL 093
gmw_00005135.overview: OL 093 is a high-redshift quasar and quasi-periodic blazar candidate located at a redshift of 0.888 [arXiv:1510.08153, arXiv:1707.05829]. The source features a core-dominated radio structure with a northwestern parsec-scale jet that is strongly misaligned with its southward kiloparsec-scale jet [arXiv:1510.08153, arXiv:1101.5822].

sources.summary: The paper maps quasar 1055+018 with KaVA, finding a core-dominated northwestern jet extended to 7 mas and three Gaussian components in a recent high-resolution image.

quotes.quote: 1055+018 (4C+01.28) 1055+018 (4C+01.28) is a quasar with a redshift of 0.888 Shaw2012 . The corresponding angular scale is 7.78 pc/mas. Lister2013 found a maximum apparent jet speed of 8.05c (with being the speed of light) based on four epochs of Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) 15.4 GHz observations obtained within the frame of the MOJAVE program from 1998 to 2003. In our data (shown in Figure fig:fig1 ), 1055+018 shows a core-dominated structure and a bulky jet in north-western direction. The jet is extended up to 7 mas.

The total flux of the entire source is 3.86 Jy with a peak intensity of 3.6 Jy/beam. Including the core, three model components can be found. Since the MOJAVE program stopped monitoring this source in October 2012, our map is one of the most recent high-resolution images of 1055+018 (the most recent observation being from December 2014 by the Boston University blazar monitoring VLBA program at 43 GHz).

sources.summary: The paper reports a stacked radio map of 1055 + 018 with asymmetric edge polarization, models a February 2014 orphan flare, and notes later blob ejection.

quotes.quote: Figure fig13 illustrates a stacked radio map of the blazar 1055 + 018. In this stacked map the peak of the polarized intensity is co-spatial with the radio core. The EVPAs within the radio core are roughly aligned with the axis of the jet (in red) indicative of shock acceleration compressing and aligning the magnetic field perpendicular to the jet flow. Slightly downstream of the polarized emission in the core is a second polarimetric feature on the edge of the jet (to the west). Within this second feature the EVPAs are inclined to the jet axis shown in red. This feature is not mirrored on the other side of the jet as in the map of 4C 71 . 07 (Figure fig6 ). The sheath polarization that we do detect is probably Doppler beamed to some extent.

The Fermi LAT detected an orphan ray flare with no optical counterpart in 1055 + 018 during February 2014. The corresponding ray and optical light curves are shown in Figure fig14 . Table tab2 lists the Ring of Fire model parameters used to obtain a fit to this orphan ray flare, with the model light curves overlaid in red. The nature of the blob's acceleration was adjusted until a fit to this asymmetric orphan flare profile (with a more gradual flare onset and a steeper flare decay) was obtained. As with the other orphan flare models presented in this paper, the ring parameters were held constant and the blob's injection power was altered to fit the corresponding level of flux. There is a slight ``bump'' in the synthetic optical light curve due to the Doppler boost of the emission gained from the blob's acceleration down the jet spine. The synchrotron emission in the co-moving frame of the blob, however, remains constant due to the fixed nature of the magnetic field within the blob during the simulation. The VLBA-BU-Blazar program detected the ejection of a blob from the radio core of 1055 + 018 214 88 days after this flare with a bulk Lorentz factor of 15 (Jorstad - private communication).

sources.summary: The paper describes a strongly misaligned pc- and kpc-scale jet system, fast pc-scale jet components, a generally southward kpc-scale jet, and no extended X-ray emission on arcsecond scales.

quotes.quote: 1055 + 018 (4C +01.28) This source was in the sample. It is included in our analysis for completeness. This source is another case where the pc-scale jet is strongly misaligned with the kpc-scale jet.

Jet components are found from 1-10 mas from the core along a PA of -50 , moving at up to 11 2009AJ....138.1874L,2009ApJ...706.1253H . The kpc scale jet is oriented generally to the south Fig. 1 murphy . We detect no extended X-ray emission associated with the radio emission on a few arcsec scale.

sources.summary: The paper describes OL 093 as an HPQ whose 22 and 37 GHz monitoring shows a 4.3-year timescale, while its 37 GHz flares remain more complex.

quotes.quote: OL 093 ( 1055+018 ) : This HPQ source has been observed in Mets ahovi for over 20 years at 22 and 37 GHz and for almost 13 years at 90 GHz. The jet of this source is suggested to have a spine-sheath structure attridge99,pushkarev05 . We obtained a timescale of 4.3 years for the source at both of the 22 and 37 GHz frequencies. The timescale repeated 4.4 times and is present for 85 and 80 of the flux curve at 22 and 37 GHz, respectively. Very similar timescales are measured in Paper I, where a DCF timescale of 4.7 and 4.4 years are obtained at 22 and 37 GHz, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 4C +01.28 as the sample's highest-redshift quasar, compact at all observed frequencies, with no significant polarization but a faint jet.

quotes.quote: 4C +01.28 The quasar 4C +01.28 has the highest redshift among all sources in our sample ( z=0.888 ). Accordingly, the source appears compact at all frequencies (cf. Figure fig:4c0128_imaps ). We did not detect significant polarization. A faint extended jet in northwestern direction was detected at 22 GHz.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 4C +01.28 as a new high-redshift quasi-periodic blazar candidate, with a GLS power peak at 442 days and possible binary-SMBH relevance.

quotes.quote: The four other blazars, 4C +01.28, S5 0716+71, PKS 0805-07, and PKS 2052-47, are new candidates to quasi-periodic blazars. Three of the four candidates are located at high redshifts of z=0.89 (4C +01.28), 1.84 (PKS 0805-07), and 1.49 (PKS 2052-47), and are much more distant than the previously reported quasi-periodic blazars. Taking that binary SMBH systems are expected to be more frequent at high redshifts into account, these distant blazars are potential candidates to binary systems of SMBHs.

We computed the GLS periodograms of these sources and found that their power peaks are at 442 days for 4C +01.28, at 340 days for S5 0716+71, at 676 days for PKS 0805-07, and at 642 days for PKS 2052-47.

gmw_00005136.name: IRAS 01298 0744
gmw_00005136.overview: IRAS 01298 0744 is an advanced-merger ultraluminous infrared galaxy that displays residual, optically confirmed tidal tails [arXiv:1103.3212, arXiv:2101.12058]. It is a probable active galactic nucleus candidate with a GHz-peaked nonthermal radio spectrum that steepens at higher frequencies [arXiv:2101.12058].

sources.summary: IRAS 01298 0744 is described as an advanced-merger ULIRG with residual tidal tails, a striking X-ray non-detection, and column-density limits implying heavy AGN absorption.

quotes.quote: IRAS 01298 0744 This is undoubtedly the most puzzling object of the present selection, since the outcome of our X-ray observation is at first a striking non detection, as also confirmed with the ciao `wavdetect' tool. A serendipitous unidentified faint source is found 15 away from the ULIRG position, but any physical relation between the two can be ruled out since this angular distance is equivalent to over 30 kpc at the redshift of IRAS 01298 0744, which is actually an advanced merger with only residual tidal tails. By considering the background count rate, we can provide an upper limit to the observed 2--10 keV source excess of 2 10^ 15 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , corresponding to a marginal detection with 2 confidence level. Such a limit is significantly lower than the related entries of Table t2 , and yet reconcilable with a SB component with a far-IR luminosity of 2 10^ 11 L_ , that would account for 10 per cent of the overall IR emission. Ascribing instead the upper limit to an AGN with an intrinsic X-ray flux as that predicted, the resulting column density has to be larger than 8 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS 01298 0744 as a probable AGN candidate because its nonthermal component is GHz-peaked, its spectrum steepens at high frequency, and tidal tails are confirmed.

quotes.quote: In Figure fig:spectra , the nonthermal components of IRAS 00091 0738, IRAS 01004 2237, and IRAS 01298 0744 show a GHz-peaked spectrum. Although supernova remnants show similar peaked spectra e.g. 1998ApJ...502..218A,2004ApJ...604L..97C , such a spectrum from AGNs has also been found in ULIRGs e.g. 2004AJ....127..239G,2011AJ....142...17H , and thus these objects are still probable candidates for AGNs.
However, in the spectra of IRAS 00091 0738, IRAS 00188 0856, and IRAS 01298 0744, we find steepening toward higher frequencies (see ^ 5.5 _ 9.0 and ^ 9.0 _ 14.0 in Table tbl:result ).
This scenario could be the case for IRAS 00091 0738 and IRAS 01298 0744, whose tidal tails are optically confirmed by 2002ApJS..143..315V .

gmw_00005137.name: IRAS 12127 1412
gmw_00005137.overview: IRAS 12127 1412 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy containing two nuclei separated by 10 arcseconds [arXiv:1609.01291]. The northeast nucleus hosts a luminous buried active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1609.01291] with complex X-ray emission [arXiv:1103.3212] and a strong radio excess [arXiv:0907.4370].

sources.summary: IRAS 12127 1412 has an unusual absorbed mid-IR spectrum and the clearest new AGN X-ray detection, with complex emission pointing to ionized reflection.

quotes.quote: IRAS 12127 1412 IRAS 12127 1412 is one of the few objects in our large IR sample whose 5--8 m spectral shape cannot be reproduced by means of the standard AGN and SB templates. In fact, the observed emission is characterized by a broad formless absorption trough affecting most of the range of interest, and the putative continuum is slightly flatter than the assumed AGN hot dust component. This prevents us from measuring _6 from the reddening, and yet the AGN obscuration is likely to be substantial: first, because a stepwise correlation between the continuum reddening and the presence of individual absorption features is reasonably expected (e.g. Sani et al. 2008; Nardini et al. 2009); second, because the steeper trend seen shortwards of 5 m (Fig. f1 ) hints at a change in the slope of the extinction law (e.g. Nishiyama et al. 2009) rather than at vanishing obscuration. Based on these considerations, the intrinsic X-ray flux quoted in Section 2 for the AGN component of IRAS 12127 1412 can even be underestimated. It is therefore remarkable that the only clear AGN detection among our new observations unfolds in this source (Fig. f4 ).

Indeed, the X-ray spectrum is quite complex: a steep power-law ( 3.0 1.5 ) or thermal component is needed at soft energies, while the hard emission can be modelled with a prominent iron feature at 6.77^ +0.50 _ 0.33 keV on top of an absorbed ( N_ H 9^ +17 _ 5 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 ) continuum. All the parameters cannot be adequately constrained, but the characteristic flatness of a cold reflection spectrum is not observed, since the photon index of the AGN component is 2.0^ +1.1 _ 0.4 . Also, the line energy is more consistent with He-like iron than with cold neutral material, pointing to ionized reflection.

sources.summary: IRAS 12127 1412 is described as a double-nucleus ULIRG whose northeast nucleus likely contains a luminous buried AGN and molecular-line detections.

quotes.quote: IRAS 12127 1412 ( z = 0.1332) is a ULIRG with L _ IR 10 ^ 12.2 L _ (Table 1). It shows two nuclei (NE and SW) with a separation of 10 arcsec kim02,ima14a . The optical classification of this ULIRG is a LINER vei99 or an HII-region yua10 . The NE nucleus is thought to contain a luminous buried AGN, based on various infrared energy diagnostic methods ima06a,ima07a,vei09,ima10,nar08,nar09,nar10 .

The results of our ALMA Cycle 0 observations of IRAS 12127 1412 at HCN/HCO ^ + /HNC J=4--3 lines were reported in ima14b . These molecular emission lines were detected at the NE nucleus, but not at the SW nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper links IRAS 12127--1412NE to starburst-like systemic neon excitation, strong radio excess indicating a powerful AGN, and deep silicate absorption toward its nucleus.

quotes.quote: In pure starburst galaxies the excitation of the nuclear starburst as measured from the [ Ne iii ] / [ Ne ii ] line ratio is usually low and in the range of 0.05--0.2 thornley00,bernard-salas09 A notable exception may be the pure starburst galaxy NGC 7714, which has a measured [ Ne iii ] / [ Ne ii ] ratio of 0.75 bernard-salas09 . In our sample of sixteen excitation conditions like these are met for the systemic components of IRAS 05024--1941, IRAS 07598+6508, IRAS 12127--1412NE, IRAS 15462--0450 and IRAS 15225+2350. Among these IRAS 15225+2350 is the only source optically classified as a starburst.

Given the high radio luminosities for IRAS 13451+1232, IRAS F00183--7111, IRAS 11119+3257, IRAS 12127--1412NE, and IRAS 05024--1941, the apparent correlation between radio luminosity and [ Ne iii ] line width in Fig. fig4 may indicate that for these sources interaction of expanding radio jets with the dense ISM surrounding the AGN may explain the observed neon line kinematics. Four of the sources in Fig. fig2 which show outflow signatures in their neon lines do not show the tell-tale mid-infrared signature of AGN activity, the presence of a 14.32 m [ Ne v ] line, in their spectrum. While this may indicate their outflows to be starburst-driven, three of these sources, IRAS F00183--7111, IRAS 05024--1941 and IRAS 12127--1412NE, display strong evidence for the presence of a powerful AGN -- e.g. 1.4 GHz radio fluxes 4--10 times in excess of the value expected for star forming galaxies. In addition, IRAS F00183--7111 and IRAS 12127--1412NE have silicate strengths S _ sil ; spoon07 below -2.5, indicating an apparent silicate optical depth of at least 2.5 toward the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS 12127 1412 as a faint-lined ULIRG whose weak molecular emission drives large model uncertainties and exclusion from Bayesian analysis; its continuum shape is visibly concave.

quotes.quote: Overall Trend of Molecular Gas Properties at (U)LIRGs' Nuclei In Figure fig:ULIRGfit , the uncertainty in the derived gas density and temperature is large for IRAS 12112 + 0305 SW and IRAS 12127 1412 simply because molecular line emission is fainter than other (U)LIRGs' nuclei (Figure fig:profile ). Figure fig:AGNvsSB shows the contours of the derived gas density and temperature for (U)LIRGs with small uncertainty (excluding IRAS 12112 + 0305 SW and IRAS 12127 1412). For our MCMC analysis, IRAS 12112 1412 SW and IRAS 12127 1412 are excluded, because of their faint molecular emission lines.

However, in many (U)LIRGs, the observed continuum shape was concaved in such a way that 1.1--1.4 mm (band 6) flux was significantly smaller than the power law interpolation between 0.8--1.1 mm (band 7) and 1.4--1.8 mm (band 5) (e.g., clearly seen for IRAS 12127 1412, the Superantennae, and IRAS 20551 4250 in Figures fig:cont f, fig:cont i, and fig:cont j).

gmw_00005138.name: IRAS 01166 0844
gmw_00005138.overview: IRAS 01166 0844 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy that likely hosts an active galactic nucleus with quasar-like luminosity [arXiv:1103.3212]. This active nucleus is thoroughly absorbed, and the galaxy remained completely undetected during Chandra X-ray observations [arXiv:1103.3212].

sources.summary: IRAS 01166 0844 is treated as an unpublished Chandra ULIRG likely hosting a quasar-like AGN, closely resembling IRAS 01298 0744 and remaining undetected in X-rays.

quotes.quote: IRAS 01166 0844 and IRAS 07251 0248 Two other objects within our selection of ULIRGs that are missed by the optical diagnostics as Seyfert galaxies but likely harbour an AGN with quasar-like luminosity, although observed in the X-rays, have never been published to date. We have therefore reduced as outlined above and then analysed the 15 ks long observations of IRAS 01166 0844 and IRAS 07251 0248 retrieved from the Chandra archives. The former source is a close equivalent (although much fainter) to IRAS 01298 0744 at 5--8 m; strikingly, it turns out to be completely undetected as well.

In both cases, the upper limit (or estimated) 2--10 keV flux is of the order of 10 ^ 15 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 . Assuming again the far-IR to X-ray conversion of Ranalli et al. (2003), such a tiny value implies not only that the AGN is thoroughly absorbed, but also that the SB provides very little contribution to the bolometric luminosity of the host galaxy.

gmw_00005139.name: IRAS 07251 0248
gmw_00005139.overview: IRAS 07251 0248 is a bright ultraluminous infrared galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.088 [arXiv:1103.3212]. It likely hosts a heavily absorbed active galactic nucleus with quasar-like luminosity, though it exhibits only weak X-ray emission [arXiv:1103.3212].

sources.summary: IRAS 07251 0248 is a bright Revised Bright Galaxy Sample ULIRG with weak Chandra emission, a steep power-law fit, and evidence for heavy AGN absorption.

quotes.quote: IRAS 01166 0844 and IRAS 07251 0248 Two other objects within our selection of ULIRGs that are missed by the optical diagnostics as Seyfert galaxies but likely harbour an AGN with quasar-like luminosity, although observed in the X-rays, have never been published to date. We have therefore reduced as outlined above and then analysed the 15 ks long observations of IRAS 01166 0844 and IRAS 07251 0248 retrieved from the Chandra archives.

The latter, instead, which is a very bright source in the IRAS Revised Bright Galaxy Sample ( z 0.088 , f_ 60 6.5 Jy; Sanders et al. 2003) delivers a 3.5 detection, and its weak emission can be fitted with a steep power law of photon index 3.6( 1.6 ). In both cases, the upper limit (or estimated) 2--10 keV flux is of the order of 10 ^ 15 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 . Assuming again the far-IR to X-ray conversion of Ranalli et al. (2003), such a tiny value implies not only that the AGN is thoroughly absorbed, but also that the SB provides very little contribution to the bolometric luminosity of the host galaxy.

gmw_00005140.name: IRAS 00091 0738
gmw_00005140.overview: IRAS 00091 0738 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1103.3212, arXiv:2101.12058] that displays strong evidence of buried nuclear activity [arXiv:1103.3212]. It is a probable radio active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:2101.12058] with optically confirmed tidal tails [arXiv:2101.12058].

sources.summary: IRAS 00091 0738 is one of the remaining buried-activity ULIRGs, classified as a weak X-ray ULIRG whose Chandra flux allows only hardness-ratio constraints.

quotes.quote: The remaining four sources in our sample similarly display strong evidence for buried nuclear activity at mid-IR wavelengths, with spectral trends that are well represented by the ones shown in Fig. f1 . At high energies, IRAS 00091 0738 and IRAS 11095 0238 have been classified as weak X-ray ULIRGs by Teng Veilleux (2010): the small count rate does not allow traditional spectral fitting, and the only possible analysis is based on hardness ratio methods. We have reviewed these 15 ks Chandra observations to obtain a measure of the received 2--10 keV flux, and found that it is consistent with a SB origin of only less than 15 and 30 per cent, respectively, of the far-IR emission (see Table t2 ), assuming that no X-ray contribution from the AGN is detected.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 00091 0738 as a probable radio-AGN candidate with a GHz-peaked nonthermal component, high-frequency steepening, and optically confirmed tidal tails.

quotes.quote: In Figure fig:spectra , the nonthermal components of IRAS 00091 0738, IRAS 01004 2237, and IRAS 01298 0744 show a GHz-peaked spectrum. Although supernova remnants show similar peaked spectra e.g. 1998ApJ...502..218A,2004ApJ...604L..97C , such a spectrum from AGNs has also been found in ULIRGs e.g. 2004AJ....127..239G,2011AJ....142...17H , and thus these objects are still probable candidates for AGNs.
However, in the spectra of IRAS 00091 0738, IRAS 00188 0856, and IRAS 01298 0744, we find steepening toward higher frequencies (see ^ 5.5 _ 9.0 and ^ 9.0 _ 14.0 in Table tbl:result ).
This scenario could be the case for IRAS 00091 0738 and IRAS 01298 0744, whose tidal tails are optically confirmed by 2002ApJS..143..315V .

gmw_00005141.name: J0005-28
gmw_00005141.overview: J0005-28 is a bright blue compact dwarf galaxy that features a detectable stellar continuum [arXiv:1403.6903]. Its emission line rich spectrum includes twelve Balmer lines and a strong auroral line [arXiv:1403.6903].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0005-28 as a bright blue compact dwarf with detectable stellar continuum, rich emission lines, prominent Balmer lines, and a strong auroral line used for abundance work.

quotes.quote: In fact, the stellar continuum was extremely faint, with the exception of the object J0005-28 (see Figure fig_7 , displayed on a log-intensity scale).

Figure fig_7 shows the emission line rich spectrum of the bright BCD J0005-28 with flux on a logarithmic scale. Twelve Balmer lines can be seen, allowing particularly accurate de-reddening to be calculated.

Figure cosray shows part of a raw WiFeS blue data frame including segments of 7 slitlets (of a total 25), centered on the H and [ O iii ] 4363 auroral line, for the galaxy J0005-28. The auroral line is very prominent in this frame, to the left of the H line.

gmw_00005142.name: J1118-17
gmw_00005142.overview: J1118-17 is a very faint, extended dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1403.6903]. It exhibits a nitrogen-poor emission line spectrum, which suggests that the galaxy has a particularly low metallicity [arXiv:1403.6903].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1118-17 as a very faint, extended bloater dwarf with an unusual nitrogen-poor spectrum, likely very low metallicity, and needing deeper follow-up.

quotes.quote: One of these is J1118-17. It is very faint, but as we show below, appears to have a very low metallicity. In the light of the results obtained for the relatively faint object, J1118-17 (s1 and s2 targets), it would have been desirable to undertake significantly longer integration times, but observing conditions (weather and moonlight) did not permit this. We intend to undertake further longer integration time observations for this unusual object.

An enlarged section of the spectrum of J1118-17s2 near H is shown in Figure fig_6 , illustrating the apparent absence (to within the noise) of [ N ii ] , although both [ S ii ] lines are apparent. This lack of any evidence for nitrogen suggests a particularly low metallicity, which we have estimated below using strong line diagnostic measurements. This object (and the associated J1118-17s1) warrants further observation to reduce the noise and establish the [ N ii ] flux.

gmw_00005143.name: J1225-06s2
gmw_00005143.overview: J1225-06s2 is a dwarf emission-line galaxy [arXiv:1403.6903]. It is characterized by a very low oxygen abundance and may contain increased nitrogen levels due to enrichment by WN stars [arXiv:1403.6903].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1225-06s2 as a low-abundance outlier, with direct and grid methods disagreeing and possible excess nitrogen from WN-star enrichment.

quotes.quote: J1225-06s2 has a very low oxygen abundance, 7.45, from the direct method, and 7.9 from the grids. This could be explained if there is more N ii than implied by the Mappings models parameters, although this is not obvious from Table ty . There may also be increased nitrogen due to enrichment by WN stars, as in the case of HS 0837+4717 2004A&A...419..469P,2011A&A...532A.141P .

gmw_00005144.name: J1609-04
gmw_00005144.overview: J1609-04 is an isolated dwarf irregular galaxy located on the edge of the Local Void [arXiv:1311.4152, arXiv:1403.6903]. It is currently in a vigorous star-forming phase containing several bright, active star-forming regions [arXiv:1311.4152, arXiv:1403.6903].

sources.summary: The paper describes HIPASS J1609-04 as an isolated dwarf irregular on the Local Void edge, more distant and baryon-rich than KK246, with several active star-forming regions and measured nebular metallicity.

quotes.quote: From the SIGRID sample of isolated dwarf galaxies, we have identified two exceptionally isolated objects, the Local Void galaxy [ KK98 ] 246 (ESO 461-G036) and another somewhat larger dwarf irregular on the edge of the Local Void, MCG-01-41-006 (HIPASS J1609-04).
