We report our measurements of the nebular metallicities in these objects. The first object has a single low luminosity region, while the second is in a more vigorous star forming phase with several bright regions.

Description and location Another interesting galaxy from the SIGRID sample in this context is HIPASS J1609-04. It is located on the edge of the Local Void, 7.65Mpc from its center Nasonova11 . It is one of the more isolated galaxies in the SIGRID sample Nicholls11 , with a tidal index of -2.9. It has an isolation index of 2.38 Karachentsev11 , compared to 3.22 for KK246. Its distance of 14.82 Mpc is estimated from local flow data for the Virgo, Shapley and GA fields.

sources.summary: The paper includes J1609-04 as a very isolated Local Void galaxy, handles its adjacent HII regions separately, and attributes one abundance discrepancy to weak auroral-line flux.

quotes.quote: We reported results for J1609-04, a very isolated galaxy at the edge of the Local Void, in a previous paper 2014ApJ...780...88N , and the results are included again here for completeness.

Test sample sizes showed that all the detectable H-alpha and [ OIII ] in each HII region lay within the sample aperture, except where there are closely adjacent HII regions (e.g., J1609-04), where limiting the sample size to 6 arc sec diameter avoids sampling a different region.
The oxygen abundance discrepancy for J1609-04(2) is very likely a result of uncertainty in the flux of the [ O iii ] 4363 auroral line, which is weak in this object.

gmw_00005145.name: J2254-26
gmw_00005145.overview: J2254-26 is a dwarf emission-line galaxy [arXiv:1403.6903]. It exhibits a high electron density exceeding 5 cm^-3, which is the highest calculated density among its observed sample [arXiv:1403.6903].

sources.summary: The paper highlights J2254-26 in the electron-density analysis: it is denser than most objects and cannot be accommodated on the higher-density diagnostic grid.

quotes.quote: Comparing the observed S ii line flux ratios to Table t8 , it is reasonable to conclude that the Mappings values show J0005-28, J1152-02A B, J1403-27, J2039-63A, J2234-04B, J2254-26 have electron densities n _e> 5 cm ^ 3 , while the remainder have n _e< 5 cm ^ 3 .
It is clear that all but J2254-26 can be accommodated even on the higher electron density grid.
It is interesting to note that J2254-26 has the highest calculated electron density of the observed objects.

gmw_00005146.name: NGC 278
gmw_00005146.overview: NGC 278 is a small, isolated spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0407068] that lacks a bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0407068, arXiv:1509.07094] and hosts an unusually large star-forming nuclear ring [arXiv:astro-ph/0407068]. Its outer neutral hydrogen disk is morphologically and kinematically distorted [arXiv:astro-ph/0407068], suggesting a recent minor merger with a smaller gas-rich galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0407068, arXiv:astro-ph/0412162].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 278 as an isolated spiral with no detected bar, disturbed H I morphology, recent minor-merger evidence, and an unusually large nuclear star-forming ring.

quotes.quote: A nice example is that of NGC 278, a small, nearby and isolated spiral galaxy ( v_ sys =640 km s ^ 1 ; D=11.8  Mpc; D_ 25 =7.2  kpc; M_B=-18.8 ). Although classified as SAB(rs)b in the RC3, there is no evidence for the presence of a bar in this galaxy from either HST WFPC2 or ground-based NIR imaging (Knapen et al. 2004a). The optical disk of NGC 278 shows two distinct regions, an inner one with copious star formation and clear spiral arm structure, shown in Fig.  278fig , and an outer one ( >27  arcsec or >1.5  kpc) which is almost completely featureless, of low surface brightness, and rather red. NGC 278 has a large \ disk, which is morphologically and kinematically disturbed, as seen from \ data (Knapen et al. 2004a).

These disturbances suggest a recent minor merger with a small gas-rich galaxy, perhaps similar to a Magellanic cloud. The scale and morphology of the region of star formation in NGC 278 indicate that this is in fact a nuclear ring, albeit one with a much larger relative size with respect to its host galaxy than practically all other known nuclear rings (the absolute radius of the nuclear ring is about a kiloparsec, normal for nuclear rings). Knapen et al. (2004a) postulate that it is in fact the past interaction which has set up a non-axisymmetry in the gravitational potential, which in turn, in a way very similar to the action of a classic bar, leads to the formation of the nuclear ring.

sources.summary: The paper uses UGC 528 as a local spiral-galaxy mock-observation test, describing its rotation, gas dispersion, maps, simulated DEEP2 spectra, and size-dependent recovery of kinematics.

quotes.quote: Mock Observations of UGC 528 UGC 528 is a small rotating local spiral galaxy of Hubble Type SAB(rs)b 2003A&A...399...51G . It has a rotation velocity V _ rot (i) 60 km s ^ 1 and a relatively high average dispersion across its face of _g = 26.0 9.6 km s ^ 1 . We build mock spectral cubes (3D: spatial spatial ) from the available velocity, dispersion and flux maps.

We create separate cubes in this manner, each time varying the spatial size to simulate different apparent sizes for UGC 528. The final product is a set of 100 mock observations of UGC 528 at z = 0.2 ranging in size 0.4 -2.2 with a 0.018 step size. Each mock observation results in a 2D spectrum of the emission line for a given apparent size.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 278 as a small non-barred spiral with a bright star-forming nucleus; its ionized gas shows clear vertical-velocity behavior near spiral arms.

quotes.quote: In optical images its morphology is that of a small, non-barred galaxy with a bright star-forming nucleus and spiral arms. Its nucleus is not only quite bright in H , but also in the infrared continuum 2000AJ....120..583D,2003JKAS...36..149S , and in the [ CII ] line 2001ApJ...561..766M , consistent with intense star-forming activity 1995ApJS...98..477H,2002ApJS..143...73E . There is no indication of a low-luminosity AGN 2007A&A...468..129T .

In NGC 278, the behaviour described by 2001ApJ...550..253A is clearly observed for the slit PA = 40.5 ^ , between galactocentric distances 1 and 0.5 kpc, with V_Z 30 \ (Figure fig6 ). This feature is in the concave side of the spiral arm, since it is located within the corotation radius ( 1.5 kpc, 2009ApJS..182..559B ). The gas reaches the density wave behind the spiral arm border -or concave side-, so the approaching (negative values) peak in V_Z occurs behind the \ emission maxima, or the corresponding spiral arm, when the gas is flowing up to higher galactic latitudes above the arm.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 278's outer profile as III and describes bar evidence, dust features, rings shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 278 (III; SAB(rs)b) : This galaxy appears to host a very weak bar; evidence for this is discussed in Section sec:bars see also garrido03 . The wiggles between about 11 and 30 come from a dust ring of radius 11 (0.6 kpc) and tightly wound arms forming a pseudoring at 20 (1 kpc) radius. There is no visible structure in the outer zone, and the galaxy is so close to face-on that we cannot discriminate between disk and spheroid morphologies for the outermost light.

sources.summary: The review presents NGC 278 as a small nuclear-ring host without an obvious bar, whose distorted outer H I suggests a recent minor merger that may have formed the ring.

quotes.quote: As an example, careful imaging in optical and near-IR bands, from both the ground and from the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) , confirms the absence of a bar in the disk of the small nuclear ring host galaxy NGC 278 (even though it has been classified as SAB in the RC3). However, H i imaging reveals that in the outer regions of this small galaxy both the gas morphology and kinematics are severely distorted, which indicates that NGC 278 has recently undergone a minor merger event with an even smaller galaxy, possibly similar to a Magellanic Cloud, which would have led to a destabilization and non-axisymmetry of the gravitational potential, and hence to gas inflow, the formation of resonances, and ultimately the formation of the star-forming nuclear ring (Knapen et al. 2004b).

For instance, in NGC 5248, Laine et al. (2001) and Maoz et al. (2001) found a ring of some 0.1 kpc (two arcsec) in radius (Jogee et al. 2002), NGC 278 contains a small ring of 0.2 kpc radius (about four arcsec; Knapen et al. 2004b), and IC 342 hosts a nuclear ring of less than 100 pc in diameter (B oker, F orster-Schreiber, Genzel 1997).

gmw_00005147.name: J 1332--3308
gmw_00005147.overview: J 1332--3308 is a peculiar radio galaxy associated with the northwestern nucleus of the dominant cluster galaxy in A 3560 [arXiv:1308.0992]. It features an active core, jets, and radio lobes, alongside an older diffuse emission component shaped by cluster gas sloshing [arXiv:1308.0992].

sources.summary: J 1332--3308 is presented as the peculiar radio galaxy in A 3560, combining active jets and lobes with older diffuse emission shaped by cluster gas sloshing after a minor merger.

quotes.quote: The radio galaxy J 1332--3308, associated with the north--western nucleus of the dominant cluster galaxy in A 3560, is very peculiar. It consists of an active component in the form of a core, jets and radio lobes, and of aged diffuse emission extending over a scale of 220 kpc. The multiband X--ray and optical analysis, performed to address the origin of this radio galaxy, supports the idea that A 3560 is a very interesting example of radio AGN/ICM interaction in a minor merger. The X--ray observations suggest a minor merger scenario between the main cluster and a group: the surface brightness X--ray residual image shows the presence of a spiral--like feature in the central region, a typical signature of gas sloshing.

A clear connection is found between the spiral--like feature detected in the residual X--ray image and the diffuse, aged (t 1.5 10^8 yr) component of the radio emission, whose morphology is most likely affected by the gas motion in the cluster central region. The BCG is currently active in the radio band, with an FRI radio galaxy associated with the north--western nucleus. The radio lobes have a radiative age of the order of 1--2 Myr. Their orientation has no connection with the older diffuse emission, and possibly reflects the relative motion between the BCG and the local ICM.

gmw_00005148.name: GALEXASC J010720.81-641621.4 galaxy
gmw_00005148.overview: GALEXASC J010720.81-641621.4 is a face-on galaxy with no obvious spiral structure [arXiv:1606.03125]. Its core contains a mixture of star formation and a weak active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1606.03125].

sources.summary: The host of OGLE16aaa is described as a GALEXASC galaxy with archival UV photometry, weak emission lines, mixed star formation and weak AGN signatures, and a face-on morphology.

quotes.quote: It was found at the centre of GALEXASC J010720.81-641621.4 galaxy of 17.00 0.01 mag as measured by OGLE in the I-band and 20.83 0.12 mag and 21.82 0.32 mag in GALEX NUV and FUV, respectively.

However, the La PHARE Ilbert2006 best matching template spectrum to the spectral energy distribution (SED) from archival UV, optical, near- and far- infrared observations is found for a galaxy with a stellar mass of M = 10.3 0.2 , with no strong evidence for star formation. Signatures of the host are already present in the PESSTO spectrum of the flare.

The line ratios (log( [ NII ] /H )=-0.43 vs log( [ OIII ] /H )=-0.25 ) indicate there is a mixture of star formation in the galaxy of OGLE16aaa as well as a weak AGN in its core. The line ratios are also similar to those in the host of SDSS J0748. Moreover, the WISE Wright2010 colours for OGLE16aaa and SDSS J0748 place those hosts near the region occupied by AGNs, following the classification method of . However, a spectrum taken after the transient emission fades is still required for a quantitative comparison to other TDE hosts and for disentangling the continuum level of the transient from the of the host. The host galaxy is seen face-on and has no obvious signature of spiral structure, apart from a faint hint of an extended structure to the West from the centre (Fig. fig:chart ).

gmw_00005149.name: PEG J232054+080838.8
gmw_00005149.overview: PEG J232054+080838.8 is a low surface brightness galaxy located in the Pegasus I cluster [arXiv:1810.00710]. It features a marginally resolved central nucleus that requires a two-component Sersic function to model its structure [arXiv:1810.00710].

sources.summary: PEG J232054+080838.8 is the exceptional Pegasus I LSB object in the sample, showing a marginally resolved nucleus and requiring a two-component Sersic fit rather than a single profile.

quotes.quote: All galaxies, excepting PEG J232054+080838.8 (Figure J232054+080838 ), were fitted with a single S ersic law, excluding the inner regions affected by seeing. In each case, the fit extended from an inner radius defined by the seeing FWHM, to a maximum radius set at the point where the profile begins to be significantly affected by small variations in the sky level. PEG J232054+080838.8 displays what seems to be a nucleus. As a first step, we built a PSF model of this star-like object with DAOPHOT within IRAF. After subtracting this model to the original images of the galaxy, the g' , ' , and i' frames displayed some residuals.

This was taken as an evidence that the nucleus is marginally resolved in all the images. Therefore, we decided to fit the sum of two S ersic functions to the whole g', ' and i' profiles, without excluding the very inner region with the aim at obtaining structural parameters for the central component.

gmw_00005150.name: KK2000 58
gmw_00005150.overview: KK2000 58 is a dwarf elliptical [arXiv:2001.02241] or dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:2303.05787] located in the Centaurus group [arXiv:2001.02241, arXiv:2303.05787]. It hosts a central nuclear star cluster [arXiv:2001.02241] that is also identified as a globular cluster resolved into red giant branch stars [arXiv:2303.05787].

sources.summary: The paper reports a newly discovered nuclear star cluster in the dwarf elliptical KKs58, confirms its central membership, and finds a metal-poor NSC consistent with globular-cluster inspiral.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a NSC in the dwarf galaxy KKs58 and investigate its properties together with those of another NSC in KK197. Both NSCs are hosted by dwarf elliptical galaxies of the Centaurus group. Combining ESO VLT MUSE data with photometry from VLT FORS2, CTIO Blanco DECam, and HST ACS, as well as high-resolution spectroscopy from VLT UVES, we analyse the photometric, kinematic and stellar population properties of the NSCs and their host galaxies. We confirm membership of the NSCs based on their radial velocities and location close to the galaxy centres.

Based on high signal-to-noise spectra taken with MUSE of the NSCs of both KKs58 and KK197 we measure low metallicities, [ Fe/H ] = 1.75 0.06 dex and [ Fe/H ] = 1.84 0.05 dex , and stellar masses of 7.3 10^5 M_ and 1.0 10^6 M_ , respectively. Both NSCs are more metal-poor than their hosts that have metallicities of 1.35 0.23 dex (KKs58) and 0.84 0.12 dex (KK197). This can be interpreted as NSC formation via the in-spiral of GCs.

sources.summary: KK2000 58 is described as a dwarf spheroidal near the Cen A group, with a revised TRGB distance and a central globular cluster resolved into red-giant-branch stars.

quotes.quote: KK2000 58. This dwarf spheroidal galaxy was first resolved into stars in images obtained with the earthbound 8-m VLT telescope (M u ller et al. 2019). The authors determined a distance to the galaxy of 3.36 0.11 Mpc, which placed this galaxy on the leading edge of the group around Cen A (NGC 5128). Our estimate of the distance, 3.75 0.18 , corresponds better to the average distance of the group Cen A (3.68 Mpc).

Near the center of the dSph-galaxy there is a globular stellar cluster, the radial velocity of which was measured by Fahrion et al. (2020). On the images obtained from ACS HST the globular cluster is well resolved into stars of the red giant branch (Fig. 3). The position of the TRGB in the cluster is consistent with the estimate of the TRGB over the entire body of the dwarf galaxy.

gmw_00005151.name: NGC 7713
gmw_00005151.overview: NGC 7713 is a late-type spiral galaxy with an estimated distance of 8.05 Mpc [arXiv:2303.05787]. It has a possible physical association with IC 5332, another late-type spiral galaxy located at a projected distance of 270 kpc [arXiv:2303.05787].

sources.summary: NGC 7713 is treated as a late-type spiral with many previous Tully-Fisher distances, a new TRGB distance of 8.05 Mpc, and a possible association with IC 5332.

quotes.quote: NGC 7713. According to the NASA Extragalactic Database (NED; ned.ipac.caltech.edu), this spiral galaxy of a late type has 23 estimates of distance by the Tully-Fisher method (Tully and Fisher 1977) which relate the luminosity of the galaxy to the amplitude of its rotation. The average value of the distance from it is 9.05 1.38 Mpc, which agrees, to within the standard deviation, with our estimate of 8.05 0.40 Mpc. At a projected distance of 1.9 ^ from it (or 270 kpc) there is another spiral galaxy of a late type, IC 5332, with a radial velocity V_ LG = 716 km/s and distance D = 9.01 Mpc (Anand et al 2021) that indicate a possible physical association of these galaxies.

gmw_00005152.name: Mrk 1265
gmw_00005152.overview: Mrk 1265 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2303.05787]. It has a compact structure with an abundance of blue stars, and its distance is estimated to be approximately 9.55 Mpc [arXiv:2303.05787].

sources.summary: Mrk 1265 is described as a blue compact dwarf whose bright-star aureole and dense blue stellar structure reduce photometric accuracy, with a TRGB distance above the NAM estimate.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1265. This blue compact dwarf galaxy is in the aureole of a bright star in the Milky Way. The compactness of the structure, abundance of blue stars, and presence of an aureole of the projected star lower the accuracy of the photometry. According to our estimate, the distance to the galaxy is D = 9.55 0.96 Mpc, which is somewhat greater than the distance estimate D_ NAM = 7.8 Mpc from the radial velocity taking into account the local field of the velocities in the model of the Numerical Action Method (NAM, Shaya et al. 2017).

gmw_00005153.name: ESO 373-007
gmw_00005153.overview: ESO 373-007 is a dwarf irregular galaxy located at a distance of approximately 9.77 Mpc [arXiv:2303.05787]. It forms an isolated galaxy pair with the nearby, brighter Sd-type galaxy ESO 373-008 [arXiv:2303.05787].

sources.summary: ESO 373-007 is described as a dwarf irregular whose prior distance estimates agree with the new TRGB distance, and as one member of an isolated pair with ESO 373-008.

quotes.quote: ESO 373-007=AM 0930-330= [ KK 2000 ] 31. The two estimates of the distance of this dIrr-galaxy, 8.32 Mpc according to Tully-Fisher and 9.65 Mpc in the NAM model are in good agreement with our estimate D = 9.77 0.98 Mpc via the TRGB. Nearby, at a projected distance of 15^ or 40 kpc there is a brighter Sd-galaxy ESO 373-008 with a radial velocity of V_ LG = 620 km/s and a TF-distance of 9.68 Mpc. The two galaxies evidently form an isolated pair.

gmw_00005154.name: NGC 6684
gmw_00005154.overview: NGC 6684 is an early-type S0a lenticular galaxy that features a bar, a ring, and a peripheral star loop and radial jet [arXiv:2303.05787]. Located at a measured TRGB distance of 10.23 Mpc, it is likely part of a single diffuse association with the spiral galaxy NGC 6744 [arXiv:2303.05787].

sources.summary: NGC 6684 is described as an early-type S0a galaxy with a bar, ring, star loop, radial jet, a mid-range TRGB distance, and probable association with NGC 6744.

quotes.quote: NGC 6684. This galaxy is of an early type S0a, in which structure a bar and a ring can are seen. A star loop and radial jet can be seen on the periphery. The NED presents 14 crude estimates of the distance by various approximate methods in a range from 5.5 to 15.5 Mpc. The TRGB distance that we have measured, 10.23 1.9 Mpc, lies right in the middle of this interval.

At a distance 2.6^ from NGC 6684 there is a spiral galaxy NGC 6744 with several satellites. Its TRGB-distance, 9.51 Mpc (Anand et al 2021) and radial velocity V_ LG = 720 km/s show that these galaxies are probably members of a single diffuse association.

gmw_00005155.name: UGC 12588
gmw_00005155.overview: UGC 12588 is a face-on dwarf irregular galaxy located in a region of substantial galactic extinction [arXiv:2303.05787]. Although it lies near the brighter spiral galaxy NGC 7640, UGC 12588 has a substantially greater tip of the red giant branch distance of 11.07 Mpc [arXiv:2303.05787].

sources.summary: UGC 12588 is described as a face-on dwarf irregular in a high-extinction zone, with a TRGB distance substantially larger than neighboring NGC 7640.

quotes.quote: UGC 12588. This dIrr-galaxy is seen face-on and lies in a zone of substantial galactic extinction. Near it, at a projected distance of 42^ there is a brighter spiral galaxy NGC 7640 with V_ LG = 668 km/s and a TRGB distance of 8.43 Mpc (Anand et al 2021). Our estimate of the TRGB-distance of UGC 12588 is 11.07 1.1 Mpc, which is substantially greater than the distance of the neighboring galaxy. To test the possible physical coupling of these galaxies it is necessary to make deeper observations of UGC 12588.

gmw_00005156.name: Mrk 475
gmw_00005156.overview: Mrk 475 is an isolated blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1004.2858, arXiv:2303.05787] that contains a single, compact central star-forming region [arXiv:1004.2858]. It features a southern elongated structure [arXiv:2008.04528], a disturbed velocity field [arXiv:2008.04528], and complex ionization patterns [arXiv:1004.2858].

sources.summary: Mrk 475 is discussed as a low-luminosity metal-poor BCD/Wolf-Rayet galaxy with compact central star formation, complex excitation, inhomogeneous extinction, WR bumps, and flat velocity maps.

quotes.quote: The galaxy Mrk 475 is a low luminosity object, included in Izotov's sample of metal poor galaxies Izotov1994,Izotov2007 , and is classified as a BCD in . B and R broad-band surface brightness photometry and \ imaging were published by . It is a WR galaxy, where nebular and broad He ii 4686 lines and C iv 5808 were detected Conti1991,Schaerer1999,Guseva2000 . Our IFU data cover a region of 920 920 pc, with a spatial sampling of 58 pc per spaxel.

This is a compact, regular object, with a single central SF knot. Emission lines and continuum maps display all the same morphology. The ionization maps show all the same complex pattern: while in the western galaxy regions the excitation ratio decreases with the distance from the central SF region, in the eastern part it displays a constant value. Both the \ and the [ O iii ] kinematical maps show a flat velocity field.

sources.summary: The paper finds MRK 475 has roughly twice the previously reported H I flux, compact intermediate/high-resolution emission, a southern elongated feature, an offset peak, and disturbed velocity field.

quotes.quote: MRK 475 Using the Nan ay 300-m telescope, Huchtmeier2005A&A...434..887H detected 0.15 0.04 Jy of line flux integral from MRK 475. Our detection is around 2 times of this value. Due to the low content and compact size, this galaxy is not clearly detected in the low resolution image cube. However, the emission is detected in intermediate and high resolution cubes.
An elongated feature is seen along the south of the galaxy. The peak of the emission has an offset from the optical center. The velocity field appears disturbed.

sources.summary: Mrk 475 is described as an isolated blue compact galaxy whose dense stellar body reduces photometric accuracy and whose TRGB distance exceeds the kinematic NAM estimate.

quotes.quote: Mrk 475. This blue compact galaxy lies far from the other galaxies with close radial velocities. The dense stellar field in the main body of the galaxy lowers the accuracy of the performed photometry. Our estimate of the TRGB-distance, 11.53 1.15 Mpc, turned out to be considerably higher than the kinematic estimate 9.18 Mpc (NAM).

gmw_00005157.name: KKH 68
gmw_00005157.overview: KKH 68 is a dwarf irregular galaxy with an estimated, though unreliable, tip of the red giant branch distance of 12.47 Mpc [arXiv:2303.05787]. It is likely a peripheral member of the NGC 3627 galaxy group in the Leo constellation [arXiv:2303.05787].

sources.summary: KKH 68 is described as a dwarf irregular with a near-limit TRGB measurement, an unreliable 12.47 Mpc distance estimate, and probable peripheral membership in the NGC 3627 group.

quotes.quote: KKH 68=AGC 212837. The position of the TRGB for this dIrr-galaxy is only 0.5^m higher than the photometric limit, which makes our estimate of the distance D_ TRBG = 12.47 1.25 Mpc unreliable. This galaxy lies at an angular distance of 2.9^ from the spiral Sb-galaxy NGC 3627 (M 66) with TRGB distance of D_ TRBG = 11.12 0.56 Mpc (Hoyt et al. 2019), which is the brightest member of the group in the Leo constellation. Probably KKH68 belongs to the peripheral members of this group.

gmw_00005158.name: KDG 178
gmw_00005158.overview: KDG 178 is a gas-rich dwarf galaxy located at a distance of approximately 13.0 Mpc [arXiv:2303.05787]. Although projected near the bright spiral galaxy NGC 4631, its distance and 21-cm line width confirm that it lies behind this dwarf group [arXiv:2303.05787].

sources.summary: KDG 178 is described as a gas-rich dwarf projected near NGC 4631 but lying behind that dwarf group, with its larger distance supported by its 21-cm line width.

quotes.quote: KDG 178=BTS 147. This gas-rich dwarf galaxy lies only at 26 ^ from the bright spiral galaxy NGC 4631, for which V_ LG = 581 km/s and D_ TRBG = 7.35 0.10 Mpc (EDD). Judging from the distance 13.0 1.3 Mpc, which we obtained, KDG 178 lies behind the group of dwarfs around NGC 4631. The large radial distance of this object from the group NGC 4631 is indirectly confirmed by relatively wide width of the 21-cm line, W_ 50 = 52 km/s, which corresponds to a TF-distance of more than 11 Mpc.

gmw_00005159.name: ESO 219-010
gmw_00005159.overview: ESO 219-010 is an early-type dwarf galaxy [arXiv:0811.0329] located approximately 15 Mpc away [arXiv:2303.05787]. It is situated at a 3D distance of 10.5 Mpc from NGC 4945 [arXiv:0811.0329] and exhibits very low neutral hydrogen limits [arXiv:0811.0329].

sources.summary: The paper treats ESO219-G010 as an uncertain faint-emission detection, noting low H I limits, early-type dwarf-like properties, continuum-subtraction issues, and its NGC4945 separation.

quotes.quote: ESO219-G010 This galaxy seems to have faint diffuse emission that is not accompanied by any point source. However, previous investigations failed to detect for this object beaulieu2006, bouchard2007 . The upper-limits, M_ HI < 7 10^5 M_ and M_ HI /L_ B < 0.03 M_ L_ ^ 1 are very low and the properties are consistent with that of other early-type dwarfs. The detection could be interpreted as a spurious detection. Careful inspection of the , off band and residual images reveal no signs of an under-subtracted continuum but different seeing conditions in the off-band and on-band images is likely to have altered our capacity of making a good continuum estimate.
No CMD is available for this galaxy to help resolve this issue, additional data is required to confirm this detection. It is situated at 1 0.5 Mpc (3D) from NGC4945.

sources.summary: ESO 219-010 is discussed as a SNAP 15922 galaxy whose earlier 4.29 Mpc SBF distance is contradicted by ACS texture suggesting a rough distance near 15 Mpc.

quotes.quote: Jerjen, et al. (2000) estimated a distance of 4.29 Mpc for the galaxy ESO 219-010 (PGC 44110)by the method of fluctuations in surface brightness. This galaxy is essentially unresolved into stars in the images taken with the ACS. In terms of texture, a distance of this galaxy can be roughly estimated as 15 Mpc.

gmw_00005160.name: RUBIES-EGS-QG-1
gmw_00005160.overview: RUBIES-EGS-QG-1 is a massive quiescent galaxy at redshift z=4.896 that experienced a rapid burst of star formation followed by fast quenching [arXiv:2404.05683]. Its spectrum reveals an evolved stellar population, while its emission lines suggest the presence of an active galactic nucleus or shocked gas [arXiv:2404.05683].

sources.summary: RUBIES-EGS-QG-1 is confirmed as a massive quiescent galaxy at z=4.896, with old stellar light, rapid early mass assembly, fast quenching, possible AGN emission, and tension with simulations.

quotes.quote: Here we report the spectroscopic confirmation with the James Webb Space Telescope of the quiescent galaxy \ at redshift z=4.896, 1.2 billion years after the Big Bang. Deep stellar absorption features in the spectrum reveal that the galaxy's stellar mass of 10^ 10.9 , corroborated by the mass implied by its gas kinematics, formed in a short 340 Myr burst of star formation, after which star formation activity dropped rapidly and persistently. According to current galaxy formation models, systems with such rapid stellar mass growth and early quenching are too rare to plausibly occur in the small area probed spectroscopically with JWST. Instead, the discovery of \ implies that early massive quiescent galaxies can be quenched earlier or exhaust gas available for star formation more efficiently than currently assumed.

A marginal detection of \ implies N II _ 6585 / H 0.6 and exceeds the ratio that can be explained by photoionization from massive stars by a factor 4 . Therefore, the emission lines do not appear to be connected to ongoing star formation activity, but rather suggest the presence of an active galactic nucleus (AGN), although we cannot rule out the presence of shocked gas . Despite evidence for an AGN, the deep Balmer lines, indicative of a post-starburst system , suggest that the continuum emission of the spectrum is dominated by an evolved stellar population.

gmw_00005161.name: VR7
gmw_00005161.overview: VR7 is a luminous Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy at z=6.532 [arXiv:1909.06376] with no clear indications of active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:1909.06376]. Its rest-frame ultraviolet morphology consists of two components [arXiv:1903.08171], while its extended carbon emission suggests possible outflowing gas [arXiv:1903.08171].

sources.summary: The paper presents VR7 as a bright z=6.5 Ly-alpha emitter with resolved UV and [C II] structure, moderate metallicity, and possible outflowing gas.

quotes.quote: VR7, which is a bright (M _ 1500 =-22.4 ) galaxy with moderate color ( =-1.4 0.3 ) and Ly EW _0 = 34 , is clearly detected in [ C ii ] emission (S/N=15). VR7's rest-frame UV morphology can be described by two components separated by 1.5 kpc and is globally more compact than the [ C ii ] emission. The global [ C ii ] UV ratio indicates Z 0.2 Z_ , but there are large variations in the UV- [ C ii ] ratio on kpc scales. We also identify diffuse, possibly outflowing, [ C ii ] emitting gas at 100 km s ^ 1 with respect to the peak. VR7 appears assembling its components at a slightly more evolved stage than other luminous LAEs, with outflows already shaping its direct environment at z 7 .

[ C ii ] emission in VR7 is extended (major/minor axes (7.8 0.8) (3.8 0.4) kpc ^2 ), a factor 1.8 larger than the UV emission. The faint and low surface brightness [ C ii ] emission extends mostly in the east-west direction. The high surface brightness [ C ii ] emission also extends 45 towards to south-west ( sec:resolvedVR7 ).

sources.summary: The paper presents resolved MUSE Ly spectroscopy of VR7 at z=6.532, finding detected, extended Ly/[CII] emission beyond the UV, a broader redshifted Ly profile with spatial variations, and no clear AGN indication.

quotes.quote: We present spatially resolved Ly spectroscopy with VLT/MUSE targeting VR7, a UV-luminous galaxy at z=6.532 with moderate Ly equivalent width (EW _0 38 ). These data are combined with deep resolved [ CII ] _ 158 m spectroscopy obtained with ALMA and UV imaging from HST and we also detect UV continuum with MUSE. Ly emission is clearly detected with S/N 40 and FWHM of 374 km s ^ 1 . Ly and [ CII ] are similarly extended beyond the UV, with effective radius _ eff = 2.1 0.2 kpc for a single exponential model or _ eff, Ly , halo = 3.45^ +1.08 _ 0.87 kpc when measured jointly with the UV continuum. The Ly profile is broader and redshifted with respect to the [ CII ] line (by 213 km s ^ 1 ), but there are spatial variations that are qualitatively similar in both lines and coincide with resolved UV components.

Despite its luminosity, there are no clear indications of AGN activity in VR7. The Ly EW _0 is easily explained by a star-forming nature. No strong rest-frame high-ionisation UV lines as NV, CIV or HeII are detected Matthee2017SPEC , the Ly line is relatively narrow, significantly narrower than most (narrow-line) AGN Sobral2018 .

sources.summary: The paper presents VR7 as a luminous z=6.532 Ly-alpha emitter with no detected metal lines, strong UV luminosity, elongated HST morphology, high equivalent width, and ionizing properties.

quotes.quote: The source VR7 is a Ly emitter at z=6.532 0.001 , see Fig. fig:spectra , with a Ly luminosity of 2.4 0.2 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 . We do not detect continuum, allowing us to place a lower limit on the equivalent width of EW _0 > 196 . The Ly line-width is v_ FWHM = 340 14 km s ^ 1 , the S-statistic is 0.33 0.04 , resulting in a Skewness of 6.9 0.8 .

HST follow-up VR7 is detected at 3 significance in the UKIDSS DXS J band imaging ( J=24.2 ), resulting in an absolute UV magnitude of M _ 1500 = -22.5 0.2 . This luminosity places the source in the transition region between luminous galaxies and faint AGN e.g. Willott2009,Matsuoka2016 and is 0.3 dex brighter than CR7 e.g. Sobral2015 . We also obtained HST /WFC3 imaging in the F110W and F160W filters (PI Sobral, program 14699), with integration times of 2612 s and 5223 s. These observations reveal a relatively large elongated galaxy ( _ 1/2 = 1.7 0.1 kpc, elongation of 1.4), with F110W= 24.33 0.09 and F160W= 24.32 0.10 in a 0.6 '' aperture, see Fig. fig:thumb_VR7 .

gmw_00005162.name: ID 141
gmw_00005162.overview: ID 141 is a strongly lensed submillimeter disk galaxy at a redshift of z=4.24 [arXiv:2006.01147, arXiv:2112.03936]. It exhibits a complex, irregular source morphology [arXiv:1801.01831, arXiv:1705.05413] and hosts a very high star formation rate [arXiv:1705.05413, arXiv:2006.01147].

sources.summary: The paper treats HATLASJ142413.9+022303 as a 500-micron riser with two foreground galaxies, no near-infrared background detection, and complex source morphology not recoverable with one Sersic profile.

quotes.quote: HATLASJ142413.9+022303 : This source a 500 m ``riser'' was first presented in while the lens modelling, based on SMA data, was performed in . Observations carried out with HST /WFC3 and Keck /AO 2014ApJ...797..138C revealed two foreground galaxies, separated by 0.3^ , although only one currently has a spectroscopic redshift, z=0.595 (B13). No emission from the background galaxy is detected in the near-IR.

B13 modelled the system using two SIE profiles. We attempted the same but found no significant improvement in the results compared to the case of a single SIE mass distribution, which we have adopted here. We find _ E =0.97^ , consistent with the value derived from the lens modelling of ALMA data performed by , which also assumed a single SIE profile.

It is also important to note, as shown in Fig. fig:lens_modelling_results [ see also ] , that the background source has a complex extended morphology, which cannot be recovered by a single S e rsic profile. modelled this system assuming two S e rsic profiles for the background galaxy but their results, particularly for the position of the second knot of emission in the SP, disagree with ours and with the findings of .

sources.summary: The paper models H-ATLAS J142413.9+022303 as a strong galaxy-galaxy lens with two compact lens galaxies, acceptable single-power-law lensing, irregular source morphology, and high source star formation.

quotes.quote: Object notes H-ATLAS J142413.9+022303 Keck K-band imaging of this system see calanog14 reveals two compact galaxies interior to the Einstein ring, each consistent with an early-type morphology. Follow-up spectroscopy by B12 hereafter bussmann12 gives a redshift of z=0.595 but due to lack of spatial resolution, it is unclear if this corresponds to solely the brighter primary galaxy or whether both galaxies have the same redshift. In this work, we have used a single power-law profile, finding that this gives a perfectly acceptable fit to the data. The lens profile centre, which is a free parameter of the fit, aligns within 0.05 arcsec of the centre of the brighter of the two galaxies.

B12 found that a source model comprising two sersic profiles gives a significantly better fit than a single sersic profile source model. At a qualitative level, this is consistent with the irregular morphology of the reconstructed source we have obtained in the current work.
The lensed source in this system has a very high star formation rate (SFR) of 2200 M_ /yr (see below for more discussion).

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA imaging spectroscopy of the lensed z=4.24 SMG ID 141, resolving lensed images and inferring a magnified rotating disk with high star formation and compact emission.

quotes.quote: We present an Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) observation of the Submillimeter galaxy (SMG) ID 141 at z=4.24 in the 205 m line (hereafter ) and the underlying continuum at (rest-frame) 197.6 m. Benefiting from lensing magnification by a galaxy pair at z=0.595 , ID 141 is one of the brightest z>4 SMGs. At the angular resolutions of 1.2 to 1.5 \ (1 6.9 kpc), our observation clearly separates and moderately resolves the two lensed images in both continuum and line emission at a signal-to-noise ratio >5 .

Our continuum-based lensing model implies an averaged amplification factor of 5.8 and reveals that the delensed continuum image has the S ersic index 0.95 and the S ersic radius of 0.18 \ ( 1.24 kpc). Furthermore, the reconstructed velocity field in the source plane is dominated by a rotation component with a maximum velocity of 300 km/s at large radii, indicating a dark matter halo mass of 10 ^ 12 M_ . This, together with the reconstructed velocity dispersion field being smooth and modest in value ( < 100 km/s) over much of the outer parts of the galaxy, favors the interpretation of ID 141 being a disk galaxy dynamically supported by rotation. The observed /CO (7-6) and / 158 m line-luminosity ratios, which are consistent with the corresponding line ratio vs. far-infrared color correlation from local luminous infrared galaxies, imply a delensed star formation rate of (1.8 0.6) 10^3M_ yr^ 1 and provide an independent estimate of the size of the star-forming region 0.7^ +0.3 _ 0.3 kpc in radius.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA observations of the strongly lensed z=4.24 submillimetre galaxy ID141, describing its luminosity, high star-formation rate, redshift, and repeated millimetre/submillimetre study.

quotes.quote: One of the brightest strongly-lensed SMGs, H-ATLAS J 142413.9+022303 , hereafter referred to as 'ID141' following its label assigned by was discovered in the Herschel Astrophysical Terrahertz Large-Area Survey eales2010,cox2011,negrello2017 . ID141 is not only bright because it is strongly-lensed but also because it is intrinsically very luminous. After correction for the lens magnification factor of 6 , the star-formation rate in ID141, estimated from the bolometric luminosity of the dust and assuming the initial mass function of kroupa2003 , is 2400 \ M_ \ yr^ 1 , five times higher than SDP81. With a redshift of z=4.24 cox2011 , ID141 is also one of the most distant strongly-lensed SMGs. As a result of its interest, ID141 has been observed many times in the submillimetre/millimetre wavebands cox2011,bussmann2012,bussmann2013,omont2013,dye2018,cheng2020 .

gmw_00005163.name: galaxy 4
aliases_external: 3C196 field galaxy 4 from Le Brun et al. 1997
gmw_00005163.overview: Galaxy 4 is a spiral galaxy located 1.5 arcseconds away from the quasar 3C196 [arXiv:astro-ph/0609502]. It is classified as a giant Sbc galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.4376, exhibiting high metallicity and active star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0609502].

sources.summary: The paper identifies galaxy 4 as the bright DLA candidate toward 3C196, measures z=0.4376, high metallicity, and a star-formation rate consistent with a giant Sbc galaxy.

quotes.quote: We have carried out a spectroscopic study of a spiral galaxy (galaxy 4 from Le Brun et al. 1997) 1.5 '' away from 3C196 (Q0809+483). This QSO has a 21 cm absorption line (Brown Mitchell 1983) as well as a DLA system at z _ abs = 0.4368 with N _ HI = 20.8 0.2 (Boiss e et al. 1998). The spiral galaxy with AB(F702) 1 AB magnitude will be used throughout the paper. AB(F702) refers to the AB magnitude in the HST/WFPC2 filter F702W ( = 6940.1 ). = 19.9 has been reported as a candidate host galaxy for the DLA (Le Brun et al. 1997). The galaxy was suggested to be responsible for the DLA in the spectroscopic program of , based on the detection of H and [ O II ] emission lines in the spectra of the galaxy at z = 0.43745 138 km s ^ 1 away from the redshift of the DLA.

We have obtained spectra spanning 3500 \ to 5700 \ and 6700 \ to 9700 \ of a candidate DLA galaxy toward QSO 3C196. From emission line diagnostics, we found that the galaxy has a redshift of z = 0.4376 0.0006. The DLA is bright (M _B (AB) = -21.1), metal rich (0.8 - 2 times solar metallicity) and has a SMC extinction-corrected SFR 4.7 0.8 M _ yr ^ 1 (MW extinction-corrected SFR 5.4 1.0 M _ yr ^ 1 ). This DLA thus appears to be a giant Sbc galaxy undergoing large SFR.

gmw_00005164.name: galaxy 3
aliases_external: 3C196 field galaxy 3 from Le Brun et al. 1997
gmw_00005164.overview: Galaxy 3 is a compact dwarf galaxy located 1.1 arcseconds away from the quasar 3C196 [arXiv:astro-ph/0609502]. It is a less luminous system with established upper limits on its H-alpha and [O II] emission-line fluxes [arXiv:astro-ph/0609502].

sources.summary: The paper treats galaxy 3 as a compact nearby candidate, reports its color and emission-line upper limits, and says it cannot rule out galaxy 3 as the DLA.

quotes.quote: also detected a compact galaxy (galaxy 3) 1.1 '' away from 3C196. This galaxy has B R = 2.3 (Boiss e Boulade 1990), which is comparable to the typical B R of a Sbc galaxy at z = 0.4 or z = 0.9. We note however that have suggested that galaxy 3 may be associated with the QSO. In this paper, we study the spiral galaxy 4, and put limits on the line emissions for galaxy 3.

For galaxy 3, assuming the galaxy is at the same redshift as galaxy 4, we placed a 3 upper limit to the H and [ O II ] line fluxes of 4.8 10^ 17 ergs s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 and 1.2 10^ 17 ergs s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 respectively.
Finally, we can not rule out that galaxy 3, a dwarf galaxy which is slightly closer to the QSO and less luminous (M _B = -22.0, Le Brun et al. 1997) than galaxy 4, is the DLA.

gmw_00005165.name: UGC 1378
gmw_00005165.overview: UGC 1378 is a giant low-surface-brightness galaxy characterized by a barred, complex structure that includes a high-surface-brightness disk, a bulge, and an active nucleus [arXiv:2004.14458, arXiv:1108.0763]. Located in a low-density environment, its extended disk exhibits regular kinematics and low star-formation efficiency [arXiv:1908.11383, arXiv:2004.14458].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 1378 as a giant low-surface-brightness disc galaxy with HSB and LSB discs, low star-formation efficiency in the LSB disc, and an isolated gas-rich formation history.

quotes.quote: Galaxy UGC 1378 has both high surface brightness and an extended low surface brightness discs. Our stellar velocity dispersion data for the high-surface brightness, Milky Way-sized, disc appears inconsistent with a recent major merger, a widely discussed formation scenario for the very extended low surface brightness disc. We estimate the star formation rates (SFR) from archival Wide-Field Infrared Survey Explorer data. The SFR surface density in the LSB disc is low relative to its gas density, consistent with recent gas accretion. We argue that the unusually large size of UGC 1378's disc may be the product of a rich gas reservoir (e.g. a cosmic filament) and an isolated environment that has preserved the giant disc.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 1378 as a barred, complex gLSB with an HSB galaxy, bulge, and disc; its non-overheated disc, regular morphology and kinematics, and low-density environment favor gas accretion.

quotes.quote: On the left-hand side is UGC 1378 which has a bar and complex structure consisting of normal HSB galaxy with bulge and disc. According to our estimates of the stellar velocity dispersion, its disc is not dynamically overheated which is expected in major merger scenario Saburova2019 . We do not see any peculiarities in its morphology and kinematics. It is located in a low-density environment Saburova2019 .

sources.summary: The paper summarizes UGC 1378 as an LSB galaxy with a prominent bulge, diffuse stellar disk, active nucleus, and diffuse bulge X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: UGC 1378: This galaxy has a prominent bulge and a diffuse stellar disk. It is classified as an LSB galaxy with an active nucleus by . The bulge shows diffuse x-ray emission, possibly associated with an old stellar population das.etal.xray.2009 .

gmw_00005166.name: DOG1
gmw_00005166.overview: DOG1 is a point-like galaxy dominated by an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0904.1231, arXiv:1102.4616]. It exhibits heavy Balmer reddening [arXiv:1102.4616], though its nitrogen-to-hydrogen line ratios are more consistent with active star formation [arXiv:1102.4616].

sources.summary: DOG 11 is described as point-like: both one- and two-component GALFIT models collapse to PSF-sized components, leaving any underlying resolved galaxy too faint to detect.

quotes.quote: For DOGs 3 and 11, the single Sersic fits were already consistent with point sources. Recall, the single component Sersic fits to DOGs 3 and 11 had effective radii of 0.02 and 0.03 respectively, i.e. the size of PSFs. Therefore, any additional components were difficult to measure.

In the case of DOG 11, the GALFIT two component Sersic + PSF model has a Sersic component with an effective radius the size of a PSF. Thus GALFIT effectively chose to fit the galaxy with a PSF + PSF model. This again suggests that the single Sersic fit is more appropriate than a two component fit. Thus, both DOGs 3 and 11 appear to be point-like and any underlying resolved galaxy component is too faint to detect.

sources.summary: DOG1 shows heavy Balmer reddening, likely much larger extinction-corrected emission, point-source dominated AO light, AGN-dominated flux, and [NII] ratios more consistent with star formation.

quotes.quote: DOG1 was observed with the Gemini NIRI spectrograph and the results were published in object J143027.1+344007 Brand07 . In this system, the broad-line Balmer decrement \ 22.5 , which, assuming a Milky Way dust curve Cardelli89 , translates to a reddening, E(B-V) 2.09 mag, or A(H ) 4.86 mag. Thus the extinction corrected \ emission in this system is likely to be a factor of 70 larger (or more) than measured.

In fact, we have a high spatial resolution AO image of DOG1, which is actually point-source dominated Melbourne09 , suggesting that the AGN could be contributing a much higher fraction of the light in this system. This example suggests that in the absence of imaging we should attempt some other method for determining the AGN contribution to the galaxy luminosity.
For DOG1, the [ NII ] / \ line ratios are more consistent with star formation.

gmw_00005167.name: DOG2
gmw_00005167.overview: DOG2 is a galaxy modeled as a three-component system containing a main galaxy, a second resolved component, and an unresolved point source [arXiv:0904.1231]. This system likely hosts an active galactic nucleus, as indicated by its power-law spectral energy distribution [arXiv:0904.1231] and high nuclear narrow-line emission ratios [arXiv:1102.4616].

sources.summary: DOG 10 is modeled as a three-component system with a main galaxy, unresolved point source, and second resolved component; its power-law SED and high concentration suggest AGN activity.

quotes.quote: The best fit model for DOG 10 is actually a 3 component model: the main galaxy (Sersic 1), an unresolved point source associated with the main galaxy (PSF), and a second resolved component (Sersic 2). The total flux ratio of Sersic 2 to Sersic 1 is 20 . If any of these components are not included in the fit, there is significant structure in the residuals, and the quality code, Q, degrades.

DOGs 10 and 13 show two resolved components with separations on the order of 1 kpc and total flux ratios on the order of one to five (possibly minor mergers). They also both appear to contain an additional unresolved component, suggestive of an AGN. DOG 10 has a power-law SED and a very high concentration.

sources.summary: DOG2 has nuclear narrow-line emission with a high [NII] ratio suggesting AGN contamination; its X-ray-derived black hole mass exceeds the optical OSIRIS lower limit.

quotes.quote: Except for DOG2, these narrow-line regions are separated from the nuclear region which hosts the AGN (indicated by green contour lines).

In contrast, DOG2 has [ NII ] / [ ] =0.74 . Such a high ratio likely indicates that the production of [ NII ] in this galaxy is being enhanced by AGN activity Swinbank04 , a reasonable assumption given that the emission is located spatially atop the nuclear broad \ flux. Shocks in the nuclear gas could also result in high [ NII ] / [ ] ratios.
The X-ray derived BH mass for DOG2 is estimated to be 1.5 - 2.5 times the lower limit from the OSIRIS data, while DOG3 has a BH mass about 2-3 times larger.

gmw_00005168.name: DOG3
gmw_00005168.overview: The high-redshift galaxy DOG3 hosts a supermassive black hole [arXiv:1102.4616]. It features extended narrow emission in several distinct knots separated by up to 5 kiloparsecs, which exhibit kinematic offsets relative to the galaxy's broad-line component [arXiv:1102.4616].

sources.summary: DOG3 hosts the sample's most massive black hole and shows extended narrow emission knots, small line widths, and kinematic offsets relative to the broad-line component.

quotes.quote: DOG3 hosts the most massive BH, not surprising given the width of the broad \ line in that system.

The other two galaxies, DOG3 and DOG4, appear to show extended narrow \ emission in several distinct knots with separations as far as 5 kpc. Each of the knots has a very small \ line width of 150-200 km/s, and there are no large velocity gradients across these multiple emitting regions. In each case, the central wavelength of the narrow-line component is offset from the broad-line component. These kinematics offsets range from 150 km s ^ 1 , in DOGs 2 and 3, to 350 and 500 km s ^ 1 , in DOGs 1 and 4 respectively.
The X-ray derived BH mass for DOG2 is estimated to be 1.5 - 2.5 times the lower limit from the OSIRIS data, while DOG3 has a BH mass about 2-3 times larger.

gmw_00005169.name: DOG4
gmw_00005169.overview: DOG4 is a high-redshift galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1102.4616]. The galaxy features extended narrow emission in several distinct knots separated by up to 5 kiloparsecs, which exhibit small line widths and a kinematic offset from the broad-line component [arXiv:1102.4616].

sources.summary: DOG4 lacks a direct H measurement, so the paper adopts minimum extinction; its extended narrow emission knots have small widths and large kinematic offsets.

quotes.quote: For DOG4 we do not have a measurement of H , and therefore do not have a measure of the dust extinction. We choose to adopt the minimum dust extinction measured from the other 3 DOGs ( A(H ) 1.84 mag) as a lower limit on the extinction for DOG4.

The other two galaxies, DOG3 and DOG4, appear to show extended narrow \ emission in several distinct knots with separations as far as 5 kpc. Each of the knots has a very small \ line width of 150-200 km/s, and there are no large velocity gradients across these multiple emitting regions. In each case, the central wavelength of the narrow-line component is offset from the broad-line component. These kinematics offsets range from 150 km s ^ 1 , in DOGs 2 and 3, to 350 and 500 km s ^ 1 , in DOGs 1 and 4 respectively.

gmw_00005170.overview: The irregular starburst galaxy CGCG 097-079 is located in the Abell 1367 cluster [arXiv:1507.04305, arXiv:1904.08803]. It is undergoing ram-pressure stripping, which has displaced its cold gas from the optical center [arXiv:1211.0523, arXiv:1507.04305] and created a 100 kpc ionized gas tail [arXiv:2202.03443].

sources.summary: The paper describes CGCG097-079 as a blue late-type, likely edge-on irregular galaxy in A1367, undergoing ram-pressure stripping with a roughly 100 kpc ionized tail.

quotes.quote: CGCG097-073 and CGCG097-079 Zwicky68 are two blue, late-type galaxies of (M_*/ M_ ) 9.27 and (M_*/ M_ ) 9.08 respectively, which are likely to be infalling for the first time into the cluster environment Gavazzi01 . Their recessional velocities are 7298  km s^ 1 and 7025  km s^ 1 , respectively or +814  km s^ 1 and +541  km s^ 1 compared to the average velocity of the cluster. They lie well within the A1367 virial radius, being at a projected distance of 0.5 R_ vir . CGCG097-073 is face-on and appears as a spiral galaxy while CGCG097-079 is possibly seen edge-on but its morphology appears irregular, with no evident spiral arms. These galaxies were selected from the works in radio continuum of Gavazzi78 and Gavazzi87 , who discovered their head-tail radio morphology indicative of an ongoing RPS event.

Since these discovery papers, CGCG097-073 and CGCG097-079 have been extensively studied at all wavelengths, including in optical Gavazzi95, Yagi17 , H Kennicutt84, Gavazzi01, Yagi17 , near infrared Gavazzi96 , CO Boselli94, Sivanandam14, Scott13, Scott15 , HI Gavazzi06, Scott10 , and radio continuum Gavazzi95, Scott10 . All works consistently indicate that both galaxies are suffering RPS from the ICM of A1367. In particular, the H narrow-band imaging works by Gavazzi01 and Yagi17 revealed that the ionised gas of CGCG097-073 and CGCG097-079 extends in two tails of 70  kpc and 100  kpc , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper treats 079 as an archetypal ram-pressure-stripped spiral, finding an abnormal, offset CO distribution aligned with the H I intensity maximum.

quotes.quote: In two cases, 079 and 102(N), the offset between the CO and optical intensity maxima was significantly larger than the pointing uncertainty and the FWHMs of the fits were significantly greater than those of the other spirals, irrespective of optical size. Both signatures are indicators of an abnormal molecular gas distribution. In the case of 079, which is considered an archetype for ram pressure stripping, our observations indicate the CO intensity maximum lies 15.6 8.5 arcsec (6 kpc) NW of the optical centre at the same projected position as the \ intensity maximum.

In 079, the \ observations are compatible, within errors, with a nearly flat radial profile that hints at an off-centre peak at 17 arcsec. In 079 and 102(N), the CO distributions seem to be significantly shifted from the optical centres. The clearest exceptions are 079 and 102(N), which have significant offsets between their CO and optical intensity maxima of 6 and 4.5 kpc, respectively.

sources.summary: IRAM mapping of 079 finds molecular gas offset from the optical/NIR center; the paper links its recent starburst and cold-gas displacement to interaction with 073 and moderate ram pressure.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: References to the optical centre are to the position 11 ^ h 43 ^ m 13 4, +20 00^ 17^ from the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) .

quotes.quote: We report on \ mapping with the IRAM 30--m HERA receiver array of 079, an irregular galaxy in the merging galaxy cluster Abell 1367 ( z = 0.022). We find that 80 of the detected \ is projected within a 16 arcsec ^ 2 (6.5 kpc ^ 2 ) region to the north and west of the optical/NIR centre, with the intensity maximum offset 10 arcsec (4 kpc) NW of the optical/NIR centre and 7 arcsec (3 kpc) south--east of the \ intensity maximum. Evolutionary synthesis models indicate 079 experienced a burst of star formation 10 ^8 yr ago, most likely triggered by a tidal interaction with 073. For 079 we deduce an infall velocity to the cluster of 1000 \ and moderate ram pressure (P _ ram 10^ 11 dyn cm ^ 2 ). The observed offset in 079 of the highest density \ and \ from the stellar components has not previously been observed in galaxies black currently undergoing ram pressure stripping, although previous detailed studies of gas morphology and kinematics during ram pressure stripping were restricted to significantly more massive galaxies with deeper gravitational potential wells.
We conclude the observed black cold gas density maxima offsets are most likely the result of ram pressure and/or the high--speed tidal interaction with 073.

sources.summary: The paper treats CGCG 097-079 as a starburst galaxy near the A1367 shock region, with radio emission, an estimated star-formation rate of 2.1 M_ yr^ 1, and gas stripping that may seed the radio relic.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, we note there are three starburst galaxies (CGCG 097-073, 097-079, and 097-087/ UGC 6697) with H and radio tails ( 1987A&A...186L...1G ; 1995A&A...304..325G ; 2017ApJ...839...65Y ) close to the shock region. Two of them are brighter than NGC 3842 at 1.4 GHz (12.3 mJy from NVSS ): UGC 6697 (53.9 mJy), CGCG 097-073 (21.5 mJy), CGCG 097-079 (5.0 mJy). We estimate their star formation rate (SFR) from the GALEX FUV and WISE 22 m data with the relation derived by 2011ApJ...741..124H : UGC 6697 --- 5.1 M_ yr^ 1 , CGCG 097-073 --- 1.0 M_ yr^ 1 , CGCG 097-079 --- 2.1 M_ yr^ 1 .

All three also have X-ray tails opposite to their direction of motion as marked in Fig.  fig:multi (A). The ram pressure stripping could strip the gas from the galaxies, including the relativistic electrons. Star formation also can proceed in the stripped gas in the intracluster space e.g. 2010ApJ...708..946S .

sources.summary: The paper presents higher-resolution H i imaging of CGCG 097079 and argues that ram pressure pushes its gas backward while leaving it rotating and likely bound.

quotes.quote: We present the results on the distribution and kinematics of H i gas with higher sensitivity and in one case of higher spectral resolution as well than reported earlier, of three irregular galaxies CGCG 097073, 097079 and 097087 (UGC 06697) in the cluster Abell 1367. These galaxies are known to exhibit long (50 75 kpc) tails of radio continuum and optical emission lines (H ) pointing away from the cluster centre and arcs of starformation on the opposite sides of the tails. These features as well as the H i properties, with two of the galaxies (CGCG 097073 and 097079) exhibiting sharper gradients in H i intensity on the side of the tails, are consistent with the H i gas being affected by the ram pressure of the intracluster medium.

A comparison of the distribution and kinematics of the H i gas in CGCG 097079 with the results of these simulations shows that the gas in the disk of the galaxy is pushed backwards by the ram pressure of the ICM. The gas exhibits systematic rotation about the systemic velocity and is possibly still bound to the parent galaxy.

gmw_00005171.overview: CGCG 097-073 is a blue, late-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:2202.03443] that is classified as a starburst galaxy [arXiv:1904.08803]. It is undergoing strong ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:2009.08159] and a high-speed tidal interaction with CGCG 097-079 [arXiv:1507.04305], which has produced a 70 kpc ionized-gas tail [arXiv:2202.03443, arXiv:1507.04305].

sources.summary: The paper describes CGCG097-073 as a blue late-type, face-on spiral in A1367, suffering ram-pressure stripping with a roughly 70 kpc ionized-gas tail.

quotes.quote: CGCG097-073 and CGCG097-079 Zwicky68 are two blue, late-type galaxies of (M_*/ M_ ) 9.27 and (M_*/ M_ ) 9.08 respectively, which are likely to be infalling for the first time into the cluster environment Gavazzi01 . Their recessional velocities are 7298  km s^ 1 and 7025  km s^ 1 , respectively or +814  km s^ 1 and +541  km s^ 1 compared to the average velocity of the cluster. They lie well within the A1367 virial radius, being at a projected distance of 0.5 R_ vir . CGCG097-073 is face-on and appears as a spiral galaxy while CGCG097-079 is possibly seen edge-on but its morphology appears irregular, with no evident spiral arms. These galaxies were selected from the works in radio continuum of Gavazzi78 and Gavazzi87 , who discovered their head-tail radio morphology indicative of an ongoing RPS event.

Since these discovery papers, CGCG097-073 and CGCG097-079 have been extensively studied at all wavelengths, including in optical Gavazzi95, Yagi17 , H Kennicutt84, Gavazzi01, Yagi17 , near infrared Gavazzi96 , CO Boselli94, Sivanandam14, Scott13, Scott15 , HI Gavazzi06, Scott10 , and radio continuum Gavazzi95, Scott10 . All works consistently indicate that both galaxies are suffering RPS from the ICM of A1367. In particular, the H narrow-band imaging works by Gavazzi01 and Yagi17 revealed that the ionised gas of CGCG097-073 and CGCG097-079 extends in two tails of 70  kpc and 100  kpc , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper describes 073 as interacting with 079, showing a 70 kpc ionized tail, possible molecular-gas offset, and gas stripping enhanced by their high-speed encounter.

quotes.quote: The deep \ image of 079 and 073 in shows each galaxy to have a spectacular \ tail extending 100 kpc black and 70 kpc from their respective optical disks and meeting in projection at 11:42:57.7, +20:02:29.42 (see Figure overv ). At the black vertex of the \ tails the complex \ morphology strongly suggests a high velocity tidal encounter occurred between the two galaxies earlier in their infall to the cluster as proposed in . black There are indications in (at low significance) and that the molecular gas in 073 is also offset in the direction of its tail.

073 and 079 display impressive black 70 and 100 kpc \ tails respectively in the deep \ image which meet each other in projection NW of 079. Modelling in suggests black a high-speed tidal interaction , at the vertex of the \ tails, subsequently enhanced the efficiency of ram pressure stripping of \ from both galaxies. The \ tails of both galaxies only become visible in the deep \ image from the tails' vertex onwards.

sources.summary: The paper treats CGCG 097-073 as a starburst galaxy near the A1367 shock region, with radio emission, an estimated star-formation rate of 1.0 M_ yr^ 1, and gas stripping that may seed the radio relic.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, we note there are three starburst galaxies (CGCG 097-073, 097-079, and 097-087/ UGC 6697) with H and radio tails ( 1987A&A...186L...1G ; 1995A&A...304..325G ; 2017ApJ...839...65Y ) close to the shock region. Two of them are brighter than NGC 3842 at 1.4 GHz (12.3 mJy from NVSS ): UGC 6697 (53.9 mJy), CGCG 097-073 (21.5 mJy), CGCG 097-079 (5.0 mJy). We estimate their star formation rate (SFR) from the GALEX FUV and WISE 22 m data with the relation derived by 2011ApJ...741..124H : UGC 6697 --- 5.1 M_ yr^ 1 , CGCG 097-073 --- 1.0 M_ yr^ 1 , CGCG 097-079 --- 2.1 M_ yr^ 1 .

All three also have X-ray tails opposite to their direction of motion as marked in Fig.  fig:multi (A). The ram pressure stripping could strip the gas from the galaxies, including the relativistic electrons. Star formation also can proceed in the stripped gas in the intracluster space e.g. 2010ApJ...708..946S .

sources.summary: The paper studies CGCG 097073 as a long-tailed irregular in Abell 1367 whose H i distribution, tail geometry, and sharp gas gradient indicate ram-pressure effects.

quotes.quote: We present the results on the distribution and kinematics of H i gas with higher sensitivity and in one case of higher spectral resolution as well than reported earlier, of three irregular galaxies CGCG 097073, 097079 and 097087 (UGC 06697) in the cluster Abell 1367. These galaxies are known to exhibit long (50 75 kpc) tails of radio continuum and optical emission lines (H ) pointing away from the cluster centre and arcs of starformation on the opposite sides of the tails. These features as well as the H i properties, with two of the galaxies (CGCG 097073 and 097079) exhibiting sharper gradients in H i intensity on the side of the tails, are consistent with the H i gas being affected by the ram pressure of the intracluster medium.

In CGCG 097073 the H i gas appears to have a sharper gradient of the contours on the down-stream side roughly opposite to the arc of starformation region which is possibly caused by compression of gas due to the ram pressure of the ICM.

sources.summary: The paper identifies CGCG 097-073 as a strong ram-pressure-stripping case in Abell 1367, citing a long ionized gas tail, asymmetric distribution, and comparison to another tailed galaxy.

quotes.quote: Particularly, CGCG 097-073 in Abell 1367 cluster (No. 1 in Fig. fig:S_RPS ) has long, extended ionized ( H ) gas tail due to ram pressure Gavazzi01, Yagi17 . In addition, the asymmetric distribution of CGCG 097-073 suggests that this galaxy is undergoing strong RPS Scott10 .

Among the three strong-RPS galaxies in high-mass clusters excluded due to insufficient number of control galaxies (Fig. fig:S_RPS_nocontrol ), J114313.3+200017 (CGCG 097-079 in Abell 1367 cluster) also has an extended ionized gas tail Gavazzi01, Yagi17 , as CGCG 097-073 does.

gmw_00005172.name: J1145+5742
gmw_00005172.overview: J1145+5742 is an extremely red quasar surrounded by a luminous, asymmetric Lyman-alpha halo [arXiv:2303.12835]. This halo may possess a two-component structure of compact and extended gas, showing strong blueshifted absorption features across the field of view [arXiv:2303.12835].

sources.summary: J1145+5742 has a luminous asymmetric Ly halo, strong blueshifted absorption across the field, inner-halo absorption near 950 km/s, and possible compact/extended halo structure.

quotes.quote: For example, J1145+5742 has a luminous Ly halo that extends East from the quasar position, and is visibly asymmetric about the halo centroid, with a measured e_ weight 0.74 . Asymmetric extending of the halo away from the quasar position is also reflected in the large e_ unweight 0.71 .

One ERQ shows definite blueshifted absorption, J1145+5742, similar to what was found in J0006+1215, by L22. J1145+5742 has strong absorption features in its integrated spectra, and consistently shows deep and narrow absorption in apertures across the FOV, out to 50 kpc, blueshifted at 400 km s ^ 1 . In its inner halo spectrum, only within 25 kpc, there is blueshifted absorption at about 950 km s ^ 1 .
This boundary could be evidence of a 2-component halo structure of compact and extended gas (e.g., J1145+5742 and J1652+1728).

gmw_00005173.name: J1705+2736
gmw_00005173.overview: J1705+2736 is an extremely red quasar characterized by a narrow C IV emission line [arXiv:2303.12835]. The galaxy features a highly asymmetric Lyman-alpha halo with a luminous arm extending over 70 kiloparsecs, alongside uniformly blueshifted extended emission [arXiv:2303.12835].

sources.summary: J1705+2736 has the sample's most asymmetric Ly halo morphology, a luminous arm beyond 70 kpc, narrow C IV width, and uniformly blueshifted extended emission.

quotes.quote: The ERQ in our sample with narrowest C IV emission line FWHM, J1705+2736, is similar to the other Type-IIs, and has the largest unweighted eccentricity in our sample with e_ unweight = 0.78 . J1705+2736 also has the largest flux weighted eccentricity of our sample, and greater than of most of the Type-I blue quasars, with e_ weight = 0.91 .

One ERQ in our sample, J1705+2736, has considerable asymmetry in its outer Ly halo morphology, and has an asymmetric and luminous arm that extends >70 kpc from the quasar. J1705+2736 has substantially narrower FWHM(C IV) than the other ERQs, similar to other Type-II quasars, although FWHM(C IV) alone is known not to be a good discriminator for Type-I versus Type-II classification Greene+14, Hamann+17 . Fig. fig:fig_annular_spec shows J1705+2736's extended emission, that is uniformly blueshifted from the central halo by about 100 km s ^ 1 , and out to > 70 kpc in the southern direction.

gmw_00005174.name: J1451+0132
gmw_00005174.overview: J1451+0132 is an extremely red quasar characterized by an asymmetric large-scale Lyman-alpha halo [arXiv:2303.12835]. This halo exhibits a coherent 1,000 km/s velocity gradient, while blueshifted absorption features in the inner halo suggest the presence of outflows [arXiv:2303.12835].

sources.summary: J1451+0132 has an asymmetric large-scale halo, a coherent 1000 km/s velocity gradient, and inner-halo blueshifted absorption consistent with outflow-like Ly spectra.

quotes.quote: Offset between the profile fit and data in the outer regions (>32kpc, eg. J1451+0132 and J1652+1728) are generally from ERQs with an asymmetric shape in the Ly halo at large scales. These profiles are better fit by exponential functions than power laws, and show that SB steeply declines at large distances from the quasar.

Some ERQs show a velocity gradient from one edge of the halo to the other of 1,000 km s ^ 1 (e.g., J1232+0912 and J1451+0132), and the coherent transition from redshift to blueshift helps confirm the measured velocities are valid.

J1451+0132 has blueshifted absorption in its emission profile of the innermost and spatially unresolved inner halo, at 800 km s ^ 1 and 1250 km s ^ 1 , but not in the outer halo profile. These blueshifted absorption features resemble those of spatially resolved Ly halo spectra that require outflows e.g., Li+21b .

gmw_00005175.name: J2215 0056
gmw_00005175.overview: J2215 0056 is an extremely red quasar characterized by low ultraviolet line ratios [arXiv:2001.03379]. The system is associated with a northern Lyman-alpha emitting cloud located approximately 30 kiloparsecs from its halo peak [arXiv:2303.12835].

sources.summary: J2215 0056 has a northern Ly-emitting cloud about 30 kpc from the halo peak; the paper treats it as associated but possibly distinct and excludes it from halo totals.

quotes.quote: J2215 0056 has a cloud in the north of the quasar which appears to be associated with the quasar as part of the same large scale structure, and blueshifts the spatially-integrated halo spectra. The associated object is omitted in measuring the ERQ's total Ly halo emission.

Figure fig:fig_J2215 displays evidence of an extended Ly emitter (LAE) whose emission centroid coincides with absorption in the quasar spectrum. The centroid of the LAE is 30 kpc projected distance from the halo peak, and spectroscopically < 500 km s ^ 1 from the redshift measured by the Ly halo.

In the case of J2215 0056, the northern emitting region has unusually uniform dispersion across its area, unlike any other extended emission cloud we detect. It is not clear if this Ly emitter is a distinct object. For J2215 0056 we determine the systemic redshift by extracting the extended halo excluding the extended object.

sources.summary: The paper describes J2215-0056, with J0834+0159, as a core-ERQ with among the lowest UV line ratios, best matched by dense, metal-rich photoionization models.

quotes.quote: J0834+0159 and J2215-0056. They have among the lowest UV line ratios of core-ERQ. Based on their location in the diagrams NV/CIV vs. NV/HeII, NV/CIV vs. CIV/CIII ] , CIV/HeII vs. CIV/CIII ] , the optimum models have the following parameters (see Fig. 1 in Silva2019 ): Secondary N and primary C models. Models with n 10 ^ 6-8 cm ^ 3 and 3-5 Z_ are consistent with the location of the two objects in all diagrams mentioned above. These models are also consistent with NIV ] /CIV 0.3 measured in these two objects. No adequate secondary N and secondary C models are found.

gmw_00005176.name: KUG 0152+311
gmw_00005176.overview: KUG 0152+311 is a compact host galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.036 [arXiv:2208.09010]. It exhibits approximately solar metallicity [arXiv:2208.09000], a radio-derived star formation rate of 4.3 solar masses per year [arXiv:2208.09000], and likely hosts a weak central active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2208.09000].

sources.summary: KUG 0152+311 is analyzed beyond transient location context: the paper measures near-solar local metallicity, host-associated radio continuum, a radio-derived SFR, and a likely weak central AGN.

quotes.quote: It is clearly associated to the host galaxy KUG 0152+311 at z= , corresponding to d_ L = Mpc (NASA Extragalactic Database, NED) assuming cosmological parameter (flat CDM, H_0=67.8 Mpc ^ 1 , _ M =0.31 ) and correcting to the reference frame of the Cosmic Microwave Background.

Using the calibration in Pettini04 , we determine the metallicity at the location of \ to be 12 + log(O/H) = 8.7 0.2 dex (the 1 uncertainty here is dominated by the intrinsic scatter in the N2-metallicity index). The more recent calibrations of and give consistent values of 8.6 0.2 and 8.7 0.1 dex respectively. These values for the metallicity are approximately solar, although we must caution that this is the average metallicity measured over a large physical region in the host galaxy, using a diagnostic with relatively large scatter.

Both surveys show significant continuum emission at 1.4 GHz associated with KUG 0152+311, with a flux density of about 3 mJy. This corresponds to a monochromatic radio luminosity of 8.9 10^ 21 W Hz ^ 1 and a radio-derived star formation rate of 4.3 M _ yr ^ 1 Greiner2016 , in remarkably good agreement with the rate estimated from the optical SED fitting. At higher angular resolution, the radio emission associated to star formation is resolved out and a much more compact (subarcsecond), yet fainter (sub-mJy), source is detected with e-MERLIN and ALMA (see Appendix sect:radio ). This source is most likely a weak AGN (with \ L_ 1.7 10^ 37 erg s ^ 1 , from the 5 GHz e-MERLIN observations) at the center of the host galaxy. (1) Region (the entire host or the SN site). (2) Redshift determined from the emission-weighted frequency of the CO line. (3) Integrated flux within the dotted lines of the top panel in Fig. fig:cospec . (4) CO line luminosity using equation 3 in Solomon1997a . (5) Molecular hydrogen mass using the Galactic CO-to- H_2 conversion factor _ CO =5 M_ ( K km s ^ 1 pc ^2)^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies KUG 0152+311 as AT2019wxt's compact host at z=0.036 and finds its resolved radio emission likely contaminates the transient-location radio flux.

quotes.quote: It was localised to RA = 01:55:41.941, Dec = +31:25:04.55 and found to be associated with the compact host galaxy, KUG 0152+311 Galaxy morphology and classification from NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu .
The association of with KUG 0152+311 was confirmed by early optical spectra gcnspectra1 , which displayed standard galaxy lines at a redshift of z=0.036 (luminosity distance, d = 144 Mpc).

Figure fig:radio_lc shows the radio flux density measurements at the location of (top panel) and the host galaxy, KUG 0152+311 (bottom panel) in the VLA X (10 GHz) and Ku (15 GHz) bands (see Table tab:radio_obs ). The host galaxy (KUG 0152+311) is resolved in images taken in X (10 GHz) and Ku (15 GHz) bands with the VLA in its C configuration and is marginally resolved in both these bands for the B configuration observations. Moreover, as evident from Figure fig:B_C_comp , the host galaxy emission is likely contaminating our measurements at the location of in observations taken with the VLA in its more compact C configuration. A strong host galaxy contribution to the flux measured at the location is also suggested by the fact that observations taken with the VLA in the more extended B configuration (see colored regions in Figure fig:radio_lc ) show a drastic decrease in the measured flux density of the host galaxy, and return a non detection at the location of (see also Table tab:radio_obs ).

gmw_00005177.name: B1152+199
gmw_00005177.overview: B1152+199 is a gravitational lens galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.44 [arXiv:astro-ph/0003098]. It is modeled as an exponential disk galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0003098] and is accompanied by a faint companion galaxy known as the X-galaxy [arXiv:2202.06926].

sources.summary: The paper remeasures and models the B1152+199 companion X-galaxy, finding its mass contribution crucial to lens modelling and the secondary lensing effects seen in HST images.

quotes.quote: In rusin.02.mn 's investigation, there is a faint blob identified as the X galaxy west to the lens galaxy. Due to the inadequate number of observables, there is a degeneracy between the mass profile and the perturber mass. Therefore, no significant effect of the X galaxy on the model could be addressed.

The mass ratio between the lensing galaxy and the X-galaxy is conventionally derived from the Faber-Jackson relation ( L^ 1/4 ) and the mass-velocity dispersion relation ( b ^2 ), together with the magnitude relation ( F_1/F_2 2.512^ m ), as done by rusin.02.mn . Since the photometry is essential to speculate the mass scale of the X-galaxy, we re-measured the WFPC2 V / I band and NICMOS H band photometries of both the lens galaxy and the X-galaxy with the drizzled image data from Hubble Legacy Archive Based on observations made with the NASA/ESA Hubble Space Telescope, and obtained from the Hubble Legacy Archive, which is a collaboration between the Space Telescope Science Institute (STScI/NASA), the Space Telescope European Coordinating Facility (ST-ECF/ESA) and the Canadian Astronomy Data Centre (CADC/NRC/CSA).

In other words, the up-to-date optical and radio observations testify the role of the X-galaxy in this lens system is not ignorable. Nevertheless, our modelling has shown that the lens model can easily tackle this slight misalignment within the given measurement errors, as long as the X-galaxy perturber is considered in the mass model, i.e., no jet curvature or extra substructure is needed for that.

sources.summary: The paper detects and models the z=0.44 lens galaxy in B1152+199, deriving a Galactic-like extinction curve, high reddening, and DLA-level neutral hydrogen column.

quotes.quote: The z=0.44 lens galaxy is clearly detected in B , R , I and z^ and its position is found to be almost coincident with the faint QSO image which is heavily extincted (relative to the brighter QSO image) by dust in the lens galaxy. The extinction curve of the lens galaxy derived from the relative photometry is well fitted by a Galactic extinction law with 1.3 R_V 2.0 and E(B-V) 1 .
