The lens galaxy in B1152+199 was well fitted by the numerical model and an exponential disk law, whereas a de Vaucouleurs model gave significantly bad residuals. The best fitting exponential disk law was centered at ( , )= (0 29 0 02 ,-0 89 0 04) relative to the bright QSO image.

With the inferred E(B-V) 1 for the lens galaxy: N ( H I )_ B1152+199 =k 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 , where 5 k 100 is the parameterization of the range in the observed < N ( H I )/E(B-V) > in different systems. Thus, the lensing galaxy of B1152+199 would qualify as a DLA (N( H I ) >2 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 ) regardless of the value of k .

gmw_00005178.name: Cen A-MM-dw2
gmw_00005178.overview: Cen A-MM-dw2 is a dwarf satellite galaxy of Centaurus A [arXiv:1907.02012]. It forms a satellite pair with Cen A-MM-dw1, despite a revised distance estimate that places Cen A-MM-dw2 approximately 200 kpc further away than previously assumed [arXiv:1907.02012].

sources.summary: The paper discusses Cen A-MM-dw2 as the paired satellite of Cen A-MM-dw1, noting their projected separation, revised distance offset, and low chance-alignment probability.

quotes.quote: 2014ApJ...795L..35C announced the first discovery of a pair of satellites outside of the Local Group, the dwarf galaxies Cen A-MM-dw1 and Cen A-MM-dw2, which are satellites of Cen A. Their projected separation is only 3 arcmin (= 3.9 kpc at Cen A's distance), and they shared the same distance estimates. But with their updated distances 2019ApJ...872...80C , Cen A-MM-dw2 seems to be located 200 kpc further away than what was previously assumed. 2019ApJ...872...80C still argue that the probability for a chance alignment is negligible and, therefore, that they are indeed a pair of satellites given the distance uncertainties.

gmw_00005179.name: Cen A-MM-dw3
gmw_00005179.overview: Cen A-MM-dw3 is a tidally disrupted dwarf galaxy located near Centaurus A [arXiv:1607.04024, arXiv:1907.02012]. It is a visibly disturbed system featuring tidal tails that span over 120 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1607.04024, arXiv:1907.02012].

sources.summary: The paper describes CenA-MM-Dw3 as a tidally disrupted dwarf with 120 kpc tails whose elongation is almost parallel to the proposed Centaurus A satellite planes.

quotes.quote: discovered the tidally disrupted dwarf galaxy CenA-MM-Dw3 with tails spanning over 1 ^ .5 ( 120 kpc). They measured distances at five separate high surface brightness locations along the elongation. Defining the directional vector of CenA-MM-Dw3 as the vector between CenA-MM-Dw3 and CenA-MM-Dw3-SE, the vector is e_ Dw3 =(0.8983,-0.4266, 0.1056) in supergalactic coordinates.

Astonishingly, we find that angles between this vector and the plane 1 and 2 normal vectors are 82 ^ and 99 ^ , respectively, meaning that the tidally disrupted dwarf is almost parallel to the two planes. This is again reminiscent of the Local Group where the Magellan Stream aligns with the VPOS. In contrast to the Local Group the angle between e_ Dw3 and the normal of the dust plane is 109 ^ , meaning that the tidal dwarfs is almost parallel to the dust plane. The tail itself lies along the line of sight, having the positive effect that the distance errors only move along this direction, hence the uncertainties do not change the angles between the tail and the planes.

sources.summary: The paper describes Cen A-MM-dw3 as a disturbed, tidally disrupted dwarf near Cen A, paired in projection with Cen A-MM-dw1 but excluded from pair analysis.

quotes.quote: In contrast, the dwarf galaxies Cen A-MM-dw3 2016ApJ...823...19C and KK 208 2002A&A...385...21K are visibly disturbed in the integrated-light images, see for example Fig. 10 in MuellerTRGB2018 for the latter. These two galaxies are also, on average, closer to the large host (Cen A and M83) than our targets.

There are two other galaxies, Cen A-MM-dw1 and Cen A-MM-dw3, with a projection separation of 18 arcmin (= 20 kpc at a distance of 3.89 Mpc) and a 3D separation of 36 kpc. However, Cen A-MM-dw3 is a tidally disrupted dwarf extending over several hundred kpc, which complicates the analysis. We therefore excluded these two galaxies from the list of pairs.

gmw_00005180.name: KK 197
gmw_00005180.overview: KK 197 is a dwarf elliptical [arXiv:1002.0341] or dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:2011.04990] that forms a physical satellite pair with KKs 55 near Centaurus A [arXiv:1907.02012]. The galaxy has no recent star formation [arXiv:1002.0341], is dark-matter dominated [arXiv:2011.04990], and hosts globular clusters [arXiv:2011.04990].

sources.summary: The paper studies KK197 as a dwarf elliptical in the Centaurus A subgroup, finding no recent star formation, little HI, metal-poor populations, and differing stellar-population properties among the target dwarfs.

quotes.quote: The six early--type dwarfs of our sample have a quite wide range of luminosities (see Table infogen ), and their morphological types vary from dwarf spheroidals (KKs57, KKs55, CenN and KK189), to dwarf ellipticals (KK197 and ESO269-66, the latter being a nucleated galaxy). What they have in common is the absence of any recent star formation (as traced by H , or young populations from CMD), nor is there any significant amount of HI detected that would allow for ongoing or future star formation. For example, bouchard07 investigated the gas content in three of our target dwarfs (KK189, KK197 and ESO269-66) and found only upper limits for their HI masses ( <1.6 , <2.9 and <1.0 10^ 5 M _ , respectively).

Just as the complex and different star formation histories seen in our neighbourghood, we see that the metallicity and stellar population properties in our target dwarfs differ from each other (e.g., median metallicity, metallicity spread, fraction of intermediate--age stars). However, it looks like in our sample there are no purely old dwarfs such as Ursa Minor or Draco in the Local Group. Coming to our results, all of the studied dwarfs are relatively metal--poor, as is expected for galaxies of such low luminosities.

sources.summary: The paper identifies KK 197 with KKs 55 as a likely physical satellite pair near Cen A, giving projected and 3D separations and a brightness comparison.

quotes.quote: We found that one other potential pair, KK 197 and KKs 55, have a projection separation of 12 arcmin (= 13 kpc at a distance of 3.85 Mpc) and a 3D separation of 17 kpc.
The chance alignments of KK 197 and KKs 55 on-sky projections is with 0.1 percent, which is still quite low.

Thus, with their measured separations, the two galaxies could indeed be identified as a physical pair. KK 197 is 2.5 times brighter than KKs 55 2017A&A...597A...7M , that is 2.5 times more massive assuming the same mass-to-light ratio for both galaxies. Assuming that these four dwarf galaxies are indeed two physical pairs, the abundance of pairs of galaxies within the virial radius of Cen A is approximately 14 percent , that is 4 out of 28 dwarf galaxies .

sources.summary: The paper identifies KK 197 as a bright Cen A dwarf spheroidal with globular clusters, a possible planetary nebula, measured velocity dispersion and dynamical mass, and dark-matter-dominated dynamics.

quotes.quote: In the three brightest dwarf galaxies (KK 197, KKs 55, and KKs 58), we identify globular clusters, as well as a planetary nebula in KK 197, although its association with this galaxy and/or the extended halo of Cen A is uncertain. Using four discrete tracers, we measure the velocity dispersion and dynamical mass of KK 197. This dSph appears dark matter dominated and lies on the radial acceleration relation of star-forming galaxies within the uncertainties.

For the brightest dSph -- KK 197 -- we estimated the intrinsic velocity dispersion using discrete tracers. Depending on whether the PN is considered or not, the velocity dispersion ranges from 8.5 to 25.3 km s ^ 1 and the corresponding dynamical mass-to-light ratio from 4 to 37 M _ /L _ .

gmw_00005181.name: KKs 55
gmw_00005181.overview: KKs 55 is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy located in the Centaurus A subgroup [arXiv:1002.0341] and is considered a likely physical pair with the brighter dwarf galaxy KK 197 [arXiv:1907.02012]. The galaxy is characterized by a metal-poor stellar population, an absence of recent star formation, and a lack of detected neutral hydrogen [arXiv:1002.0341].

sources.summary: The paper studies KKs55 as a dwarf spheroidal in the Centaurus A subgroup, finding no recent star formation, little HI, metal-poor populations, and differing stellar-population properties among the target dwarfs.

quotes.quote: The six early--type dwarfs of our sample have a quite wide range of luminosities (see Table infogen ), and their morphological types vary from dwarf spheroidals (KKs57, KKs55, CenN and KK189), to dwarf ellipticals (KK197 and ESO269-66, the latter being a nucleated galaxy). What they have in common is the absence of any recent star formation (as traced by H , or young populations from CMD), nor is there any significant amount of HI detected that would allow for ongoing or future star formation. For example, bouchard07 investigated the gas content in three of our target dwarfs (KK189, KK197 and ESO269-66) and found only upper limits for their HI masses ( <1.6 , <2.9 and <1.0 10^ 5 M _ , respectively).

Just as the complex and different star formation histories seen in our neighbourghood, we see that the metallicity and stellar population properties in our target dwarfs differ from each other (e.g., median metallicity, metallicity spread, fraction of intermediate--age stars). However, it looks like in our sample there are no purely old dwarfs such as Ursa Minor or Draco in the Local Group. Coming to our results, all of the studied dwarfs are relatively metal--poor, as is expected for galaxies of such low luminosities.

sources.summary: The paper treats KKs 55 and KK 197 as a likely physical pair within Cen A's virial radius, with small separations and KK 197 brighter.

quotes.quote: We found that one other potential pair, KK 197 and KKs 55, have a projection separation of 12 arcmin (= 13 kpc at a distance of 3.85 Mpc) and a 3D separation of 17 kpc.
The chance alignments of KK 197 and KKs 55 on-sky projections is with 0.1 percent, which is still quite low.

Thus, with their measured separations, the two galaxies could indeed be identified as a physical pair. KK 197 is 2.5 times brighter than KKs 55 2017A&A...597A...7M , that is 2.5 times more massive assuming the same mass-to-light ratio for both galaxies. Assuming that these four dwarf galaxies are indeed two physical pairs, the abundance of pairs of galaxies within the virial radius of Cen A is approximately 14 percent , that is 4 out of 28 dwarf galaxies .

gmw_00005182.name: FDS11 LSB1
gmw_00005182.overview: FDS11 LSB1 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy candidate located in the Fornax cluster [arXiv:1710.04616]. It forms a close pair with FDS11 LSB2 at a projected distance of 15 kpc, and it is a clear outlier towards blue colors [arXiv:1710.04616].

sources.summary: The paper lists FDS11 LSB1 among Fornax UDG candidates, notes a classification disagreement, highlights its close pairing with FDS11 LSB2, and identifies it as an unusually blue-color outlier.

quotes.quote: Additionally, two of the UDGs appear in Lisker et al. ( submitted ), but in that work FDS11 LSB1 is not considered as a galaxy.

In this sense an interesting pair is FDS11 LSB1 and FDS11 LSB2 (see Fig. fig:UDGstreams ), which galaxies are located at a projected distance of 15 kpc from each other. Both galaxies point toward a spectroscopically confirmed Fornax dE, FCC252 Drinkwater2001b , which has a B-band total magnitude of M _B = -15.2 mag Ferguson1989 .
The only exception is FDS11 LSB1, which has g'-' = 0.18 mag, being a clear outlier towards blue colors.

gmw_00005183.name: IC 1263
gmw_00005183.overview: IC 1263 is a bright spiral galaxy located within a galaxy group [arXiv:1104.4701]. It is believed to have undergone ram-pressure stripping while passing through the dense core of its group, potentially creating a bow shock and heating the stripped interstellar medium to X-ray temperatures [arXiv:1104.4701].

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 1263 as a bright spiral member whose ram-pressure stripping during passage through its group core could produce a bow shock and heat stripped ISM to X-ray temperatures.

quotes.quote: In addition to numerical models of the galaxy-IGM interaction in SQ, we also studied the galaxy group around IC 1262. Chandra and XMM-Newton observations have uncovered an extended cool X-ray ridge at the centre of this group Tri:07 . The authors speculate that this peculiar structure may have been created by efficient ram pressure stripping of the bright spiral member IC 1263 as it had passed through the group's dense core ( _ IGM =8.0 10^ 27 g cm ^ 3 , T_ IGM =2.0 10^7 K) at a relative velocity of 1000 km s ^ 1 (translating to a Mach number of about 5). Then, gas supersonically streaming behind the bow shock wave may have heated up the stripped ISM to the observed X-ray temperatures. A result of our numerical and analytical computation of this interaction is shown in the lower right panel of Fig. im:compa1 .

gmw_00005184.name: J160943.35+522550.8
gmw_00005184.overview: J160943.35+522550.8 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1403.0958]. Multi-epoch observations reveal a high-velocity outflow component that has increased in strength over time, rather than undergoing physical acceleration [arXiv:1403.0958].

sources.summary: The paper highlights J160943.35+522550.8 as a BAL quasar whose apparent outflow acceleration was tested with a third epoch; matching MDM and HET spectra favor a strengthening high-velocity component.

quotes.quote: J160943.35+522550.8 : This object showed an apparent increase in the maximum velocity of the BAL outflow between the SDSS and HET observations. While the red edge remained fixed, the blue edge extends to shorter wavelengths in the later HET data, in not only C IV but also Si IV and N V (Figure 9). Systematic changes in the velocity structure of a BAL are unusual, for either BAL RQQs or BAL RLQs (e.g., Hall et al. 2007; Gibson et al. 2008). We obtained a new MDM spectrum to check whether a high-velocity component had accelerated between epochs, leaving the main absorption trough unchanged due to the putative presence of additional absorbers partially overlapping in velocity space and together fully covering the continuum.

The MDM spectrum aligns with the HET spectrum (Figure 9), arguing against acceleration and suggesting instead that a high-velocity component was always present but increased in strength (e.g., through a highly ionized clump cooling to a greater C IV opacity), or that a high-velocity cloud transversely entered the line of sight. This illustrates the importance of obtaining a third epoch spectrum to check cases of potential acceleration.

gmw_00005185.name: NGC1022
gmw_00005185.overview: NGC 1022 is a barred galaxy that likely experienced a past merger, which may have triggered a central starburst and formed its bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0104087]. This weak bar exhibits an exponential profile [arXiv:1405.6726], a barlens [arXiv:1405.6726], and dust emission concentrated almost exclusively in the central region [arXiv:astro-ph/0104087].

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 1022 to simulation gtr120 as a weak-bar case, citing radial profiles with small major/minor-axis differences, an exponential bar profile, and a prominent barlens.

quotes.quote: Finally, in the bottom row we compare the t =6 Gyr image of gtr120 to the S ^4 G image of NGC 1022. This is the weakest bar in this comparison, and one of the weakest bars in the AMR13 simulations. This can be witnessed also from the radial surface density profiles, which shows clearly that the difference between the profiles along the directions of the bar major and minor axes is less than in the previous cases and that the bar has an exponential profile. The latter according to is a sign of a bar weaker than those having a flat profile. In contrast, the barlens is still very prominent.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC1022 as an example where DiskFit and galfit disk-bulge-bar models closely agree, with similar component surface-brightness profiles and comparable disk/bar position angles and ellipticities.

quotes.quote: Figure Figure7 shows an example of a galaxy, NGC1022, for which the best fitting 2015ApJS..219....4S galfit (panel b) and DiskFit (panel ) disk-bulge-bar models closely resemble each other. The surface brightness profiles of individual components (panel d) are also similar, with DiskFit trading some disk light (solid green line) for bar light (solid blue line) at 10px << 30px (7.5 arcsec < < 23 arcsec) compared to galfit (dashed green and blue lines, respectively). NGC1022 is indicated by the red triangles in Figure Figure10 ; the DiskFit and galfit disk and bar position angles and ellipticities are similar.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1022 as a merger-affected barred galaxy whose dust emission is almost entirely central, with a CO source larger than the innermost infrared region.

quotes.quote: We also qualify that observation by noting that two galaxies, NGC 1022 and NGC 4691, have likely experienced a merger; gas may therefore have sunk to the center as a result of the violent energy dissipation in the merger, and not simply under the influence of the bar, which actually may have been formed during the interaction. We warn that NGC 1022 and NGC 4691 should be considered apart: their dust emission comes almost exclusively from central regions of 1 kpc, but this is more likely due to a past merger than to the influence of the bar, which may have been formed or transformed simultaneously as the starburst event was triggered. In NGC 1022, the source HPBW is estimated to be 17 , which is 2.9 times that of the innermost infrared regions.

gmw_00005186.name: NGC 4236
gmw_00005186.overview: NGC 4236 is a nearby, nearly edge-on low-surface-brightness barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0504393, arXiv:astro-ph/9912417]. Its disk contains bright star-forming knots [arXiv:0905.3995] and emission regions distributed along its bar, which exhibits solid-body rotation [arXiv:astro-ph/0504393].

sources.summary: The paper notes continuum knots in NGC 4236 without polarized counterparts, a polarized background source seen through its disk, and possible magneto-ionic ISM effects.

quotes.quote: NGC 4236 In this galaxy, braun_etal_2007 report detecting continuum emission from the bright knots in the disk. There is no polarized counterpart associated with these features. The double background radio source behind the disk of NGC 4236 is detected in polarization, at about the 7 polarized fraction level with an RM of 3 2 rad m ^ 2 . The two unconfused background double sources in the field provide a consistent estimate of the Galactic foreground RM of +15 2 rad m ^ 2 . The difference may well be due to the magneto-ionic ISM of NGC 4236.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4236 as a nearly edge-on late-type barred galaxy with emission regions along the bar, bright regions near the bar ends, and solid-body rotation coincident with the bar.

quotes.quote: NGC 4236: This late type SBdm galaxy is seen nearly edge-on. Its kinematical inclination is 76 . The image shows that the \ regions are distributed along the bar, with two bright regions near the end of the bar. These features are also seen in \ ( 1973A&A....24..411S 1973A&A....24..411S ). An extensive region of solid-body rotation coincides with the bar.

sources.summary: NGC 4236 is described as a nearby low-surface-brightness spiral whose H I includes a filamentary CNM network, loss of CNM beyond R25, and about 70 percent of flux in CNM.

quotes.quote: Several low mass spirals were observed in the high resolution sample of Braun ( , ), including the nearby SBm galaxy NGC 2366 and two of the nearest low surface brightness spirals, NGC 247 (of type Sc) and NGC 4236 (SBd). A filamentary network of CNM gas is seen in all these systems, which accounts for about 80 of the line flux from only about 15 of the surface area within R_ 25 (the radius where the average face-on surface brightness of light in the B band has declined to 25 mag arsec ^ 2 ). Just beyond R_ 25 the flux fraction of CNM gas disappears entirely, even though the WNM continues out to a radius of almost twice R_ 25 . The result is that the total \ flux fraction due to CNM in these galaxies is about 70 , substantially more than the 40 CNM fraction in the CHVCs.

gmw_00005187.name: GRB 090323A host
gmw_00005187.overview: The GRB 090323A host is a high-redshift galaxy system located at a redshift of z=3.57 [arXiv:1201.3730, arXiv:1507.03909]. It features a strong double absorption system with super-solar metallicity [arXiv:1201.3730, arXiv:1507.03909], and its complex emission profiles suggest the presence of two interacting galaxies [arXiv:1709.06574, arXiv:2310.15967].

sources.summary: The paper highlights the GRB 090323 host as a high-redshift system with a strong double absorber, super-solar metallicity, and excited Si II in both absorbers.

quotes.quote: However, the host of the distant GRB 090323 ( z=3.57 ) has a peculiar strong double absorption system with super-solar metallicity (Savaglio et al. \ 2011). The properties of the ISM in these galaxies are summarized in Table tab1 . GRB afterglow spectra are often characterized by the presence of excited lines of silicon S ii *, indicating the vicinity of a bright UV source.

This is further proven by the fact that S ii * is present in both the absorbers detected in the GRB 090323 afterglow spectrum. The separation of the two absorbers is 660 km s ^ 1 (Savaglio et al. \ 2011), the GRB can be close to one of the two, but not both.
Striking is the recent super-solar metallicity measured in the z=3.57 GRB 090323, and the universe at that time was only 1.7 Gyr old.

sources.summary: The paper presents the GRB 090323 host as a high-redshift case with super-solar metallicity, showing that GRB hosts are not necessarily metal poor even early in cosmic history.

quotes.quote: For instance, the two cases seen in Fig. f2 , where the two afterglow spectra for GRB 090926A at z=2.11 (Rau et al. 2010) and GRB 090323 at z=3.57 (Savaglio et al. 2012) are compared. Their metallicity are at two extremes: the former is about 1/100 the solar value (large HI absorption and the weak metal lines), the latter is over solar (strong metal lines and relatively low HI absorption). It is remarkable in the latter that a high chemical enrichment is measured in a galaxy when the Universe was less than 2 Gyr old. Unfortunately the host is detected, but its mass is not known.

sources.summary: The paper highlights the host's unusually large absorption velocity width, possible interacting-galaxy components, and an inferred emission metallicity that differs significantly from the absorption-based value.

quotes.quote: In the case of the host of GRB 090323A, the of 843 is significantly larger than the rotational velocity expected from its stellar mass of M_* = 10^ 10.3 M_ which is 213 based on the sTF relation presented in Ubler17-2017arXiv170304321U .

Indeed for the hosts of GRB 090323A and GRB 121024A, the two host galaxies with the largest , the absorption profiles contain two main components separated by a few hundreds in velocity space, which could be due to two interacting galaxies for GRBs 090323 see Savaglio12-2012MNRAS.420..627S , and for GRB 121024A see Friis15-2015MNRAS.451..167F .

Similarly, we infer an emission metallicity of 8.04 0.16 for GRB 090323A host with _ H =60 13 . For the host galaxy of GRB 050820A, we use the = 300 measurement and infer an emission metallicity of 7.97 0.18 . The three hosts with these inferred emission metallicities are presented as magenta circles in Fig. fig:ZXH . While acknowledging the very small statistics, the inferred emission metallicities appear to better follow the GRB host sample on the MZ plane compared to when the absorption metallicities are assumed to be identical to the emission metallicities. Also, in the host of GRB 090323A, the difference between the two values is significant.

sources.summary: The paper detects the GRB 090323 host continuum and emission lines, links unusual line profiles to possible interacting galaxies, and notes high absorption-based metallicity.

quotes.quote: The exception is in the \ and \ emission lines from the host galaxy of GRB 090323, where there is evidence of additional emission that likely originates from unresolved additional components in velocity space (section ssec:fluxes ).
In the case of the host galaxy of GRB 090323, emission from \ and \ was also detected as well as the galaxy continuum, consistent with the high absorption-based metallicity ( >2 Z_ ).

The afterglow of GRB 090323 had strong absorption from two systems separated by just v 660 km/s, which are proposed to be the signature of two interacting galaxies srg+12 . It is therefore possible that our NIRSpec spectrum contains the combined emission from two galaxies, which may explain why our single Gaussian fits cannot fully describe the data, although no spatial offset is evident around the \ and \ lines in the 2D spectrum (Fig. fig:grb090323A-2Dspec ).

gmw_00005188.name: GRB 121024A host galaxy
gmw_00005188.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 121024A is a massive, star-forming galaxy at a redshift of approximately 2.3 [arXiv:2103.15269, arXiv:2301.07131]. It is a rare system with both absorption and emission metallicity measurements [arXiv:1709.06574, arXiv:2301.07131], and its absorption profiles suggest it may consist of two interacting galaxies [arXiv:1709.06574].

sources.summary: The paper treats the GRB121024A host as a bright, massive z=2.3 galaxy with both absorption and emission metallicities, rederiving Z_emiss and comparing it to high-redshift mass-metallicity relations.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of GRB121024A at a redshift of z=2.3 is relatively bright ( m_ AB = 22.6 ) and massive (estimated stellar mass of 10^ 9.9 M_ ), and absorption and emission metallicity measurements for this galaxy have previously been reported to be in broad relative agreement ( ( [ O/H ] )+12 = 8.41^ +0.11 _ 0.12 using emission-line spectroscopy and 8.29 0.12 using absorption spectroscopy; see Arabsalmani+18 and references therein). However, the absorption-based metallicity determination for this galaxy has been recently redetermined by Bolmer+19 showing a significant difference to the emisison-line metallicity of this galaxy published by Friis+15 .

Current data At time of writing, GRB121024A is the only GRB whose host galaxy has been determined both using absorption-line and emission-line methods Arabsalmani+18 . The emission-line metallicity was first measured by Friis+15 and then revised by Kruhler+15 , who quote a value of 12+ ( [ O/H ] ) =8.41^ +0.11 _ 0.12 . This value was obtained using strong-line metallicity diagnostics calibrated from local Universe observations. One challenge with measuring gas metallicities at high-redshift with emission lines is that conditions in the emitting regions likely evolve with redshift Kewley+13, Steidel+14, Strom+17 .

Using a local Universe calibration of the O3N2 diagnostic PettiniPagel04 , we find that the host galaxy of GRB121024A is highly consistent with z 2.3 mass-metallicity relation measurements from both KBSS Steidel+14 and MOSDEF Sanders15 samples.

sources.summary: The paper identifies this host as uniquely measured in all tracked parameters, with consistent absorption and emission metallicities, an inferred emission metallicity, and possible interacting components.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of GRB 121024A is the sole host with measurements for both absorption and emission metallicities. The two metallicity measurements for this galaxy are consistent with each-other Friis15-2015MNRAS.451..167F .

GRB 121024A host galaxy has a _ H of 88 4 . Using equation eq:SZ (see the lower panel in Fig. fig:VS ), this leads to an inferred emission metallicity of 8.26 0.13 (which includes the intrinsic scatter of the used correlation, 0.13 dex, added in quadrature to the measurement error). Note that our inferred emission metallicity is consistent with the measured value of 8.4 0.4 reported by Friis15-2015MNRAS.451..167F and 8.41^ +0.11 _ 0.12 reported by Kruhler15-2015A&A...581A.125K for this GRB host.

Indeed for the hosts of GRB 090323A and GRB 121024A, the two host galaxies with the largest , the absorption profiles contain two main components separated by a few hundreds in velocity space, which could be due to two interacting galaxies for GRBs 090323 see Savaglio12-2012MNRAS.420..627S , and for GRB 121024A see Friis15-2015MNRAS.451..167F .

sources.summary: The paper uses the host galaxy of GRB121024A as the sole observed case with both absorption and emission metallicities, finding it marginally favors Illustris/TNG-style chemical structure.

quotes.quote: Using data for the host galaxy of GRB121024A for which both \ and \ have been measured, we find marginal support for the Illustris simulation as producing the most-realistic internal metallicity distributions within star-forming galaxies at cosmic noon.

As discussed in Section ssec:GRB121024 , currently only one GRB host galaxy has had its metallicity measured with both strong emission line diagnostics and by examining the absorption spectrum of the transient. A significant difference is seen between \ and \ ( 0.35^ + 0.14 _ 0.25 dex; ZZ_TNG ).

A statistical analysis of the \ and \ values determined for the host galaxy of GRB121024A was constructed. This single data point favours the TNG and Illustris models with a metallicity cutoff at 0.3Z_ , and specifically prefers Illustris over TNG. The EAGLE simulation was significantly disfavoured, as bright galaxies with low values of <0.5Z_ are rare in this simulation.

sources.summary: The paper uses GRB 121024A as a prior benchmark host with both absorption and emission metallicities, noting the emission metallicity exceeded the absorption value and its stellar mass estimate.

quotes.quote: Prior to the launch of JWST, only the host galaxy of GRB 121024A at z=2.298 fdk+15 had a well-measured absorption line metallicity together with sufficient emission line detections to be able to obtain an emission line metallicity. This is due to the need for restframe optical galaxy spectroscopy to capture the emission lines required for the metallicity diagnostic (e.g. ) kmf+15,pvs+19,gsf23 as well as time-sensitive rest-frame UV GRB afterglow spectra for deriving the absorption metallicity. Ly absorption can only be detected from the ground for GRBs at z 1.7 , but at such redshifts it becomes challenging to detect the weaker emission lines from the same, generally low-mass host galaxies. In the case of GRB 121024A, the emission line metallicity was larger than the absorption metallicity by 0.2--0.7 dex fdk+15,kmf+15 , depending on the emission-line metallicity diagnostic used.

Aside from the sample of GRB host galaxies observed with NIRSpec, we also include the host galaxies of GRB 120815A and GRB 121024A, which have absorption and emission lines detected from ground-based observations klf+13,fdk+15 .

The host galaxies with mass estimates or limits are GRB 030323 M_ /M_ <9.23 ; lbc11 , GRB 050820A M_ /M_ =9.29 ; cpp+09 , GRB 080804 M_ /M_ =9.28 ; pth+16 , GRB 090323 M_ /M_ =11.2 ; mkr+10 , and GRB 121024A M_ /M_ =9.9 ; fdk+15 .

gmw_00005189.name: ESO 149-G003
gmw_00005189.overview: ESO 149-G003 is a nearby, isolated dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:2012.01751]. Radio observations of the galaxy reveal an asymmetric morphology, an extraplanar neutral hydrogen component, and a fraction of gas with anomalous kinematics [arXiv:2012.01751].

sources.summary: The paper studies ESO 149-G003 as an isolated dwarf irregular with anomalous and extraplanar H i; MeerKAT data and tilted-ring modeling reveal asymmetric morphology and unexplained anomalous gas.

quotes.quote: ESO 149-G003 is a close-by, isolated dwarf irregular galaxy. Previous observations with the ATCA indicated the presence of anomalous neutral hydrogen ( H i ) deviating from the kinematics of a regularly rotating disc. We conducted follow-up observations with the MeerKAT radio telescope during the 16-dish Early Science programme as well as with the MeerLICHT optical telescope. Our more sensitive radio observations confirm the presence of anomalous gas in ESO 149-G003, and further confirm the formerly tentative detection of an extraplanar H i component in the galaxy.

Employing a simple tilted-ring model, in which the kinematics is determined with only four parameters but including morphological asymmetries, we reproduce the galaxy's morphology, which shows a high degree of asymmetry. By comparing our model with the observed H i , we find that in our model we cannot account for a significant (but not dominant) fraction of the gas. From the differences between our model and the observed data cube we estimate that at least 7 -8 of the H i in the galaxy exhibits anomalous kinematics, while we estimate a minimum mass fraction of less than 1 for the morphologically confirmed extraplanar component.

gmw_00005190.name: VCC856
gmw_00005190.overview: VCC856 is a rotationally supported, early-type dwarf galaxy in the Virgo cluster that is nearly gas-free [arXiv:1911.05094]. It features faint, low-contrast spiral arms [arXiv:1911.05094, arXiv:0812.3272] that likely formed during a weak tidal encounter with a neighboring galaxy approximately 0.85 Gyr ago [arXiv:1911.05094].

sources.summary: The paper models VCC856 as a gas-poor, bulgeless Virgo dwarf with faint spiral arms, matching those arms to a weak companion encounter about 0.85 Gyr ago.

quotes.quote: Galaxy Models In kwak17 , we chose VCC856, one of dEdis in the Virgo cluster, as our standard model to represent infalling progenitors and constructed 15 isolated galaxy models allowing for uncertainties in its observed properties. Photometric studies show that it is nearly gas free, has an exponential stellar disk without a bulge component, and possesses faint m=2 spirals with amplitudes 3 - 4 of the disk jerjen00 . In terms of kinematics, it is rotationally supported simien02 and is not dominated by a dark halo within the effective radius toloba14 . These properties suggest that VCC 856 might have experienced ram-pressure stripping in the cluster, but undergone neither strong tidal interactions nor major mergers, preserving the early shapes of infalling late-type dEs.

The grey circle corresponds to the faint arms in VCC856, in excellent agreement with the arms in Model G4 at t 1.4 - 1.5 . This suggests that VCC856 can be well described by Galaxy S1 that underwent tidal interactions of strength =0.015 with its neighbor about 0.85 ago.

sources.summary: VCC 856 is identified as the first dE/dS0 dwarf with detected low-contrast spiral arms; its age profile is flat while metallicity varies radially.

quotes.quote: VCC 856 = IC 3328 (Fig figvcc0856 ). This was the first dwarf dE/dS0 galaxy, where low-contrast spiral arms were detected by . The data allowed us to derive the stellar population properties out to 1 r_ e . The age profile is flat with the mean value of about 4 Gyr, whereas the metallicity distribution exhibits a gradient. With the presently available data we were not able to detect any statistically significant difference between the ages of the stellar populations in the nuclear region and in the spiral disc.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes IC 3328 as an early-type dwarf with weak spiral structure, finding mass-to-light ratios and rotation indicating a dynamically hot component in addition to its disk.

quotes.quote: Kinematical information of acceptable quality is available for one of these galaxies, IC 3328. We investigated its dynamical configuration, taking the effect of asymmetric drift into account, and using the Toomre parameter, as well as density wave considerations. The derived mass-to-light ratios and rotational velocities indicate a significant dynamically hot component in addition to the disk. However, any unambiguous conclusions will need to await further data for this and other early-type dwarfs with spiral structure.
The first discovery of spiral structure in a dE was reported by jer00a for the galaxy IC 3328 (VCC 856), which is also the focus of the work presented here (Fig. fig:profimage ).

sources.summary: The paper chooses VCC856 as a fiducial Virgo progenitor dwarf disk galaxy, citing its spiral arms, dEdi reclassification, and observationally inspected properties as motivation for simulation models.

quotes.quote: VCC856 As an initial condition of our simulations, it is ideal to choose a dwarf disk galaxy that maximally represents the infalling population without undergoing environmental effects. Among numerous dEdis in the Virgo cluster, we take VCC856 as our fiducial progenitor galaxy for the following reasons. The morphological classification of VCC856 was a nucleated dE in , but first reported the presence of faint logarithmic spiral arms after subtracting mean axisymmetric light distribution. The pitch angle of the spirals is 12.^ 1 , similar to those in Sb or Sbc galaxies. For this disk feature, VCC856 was reclassified as a dEdi or dE(di) by , and its properties have been inspected observationally in the literature barazza02, simien02, geha03, jerjen04, chilingarian09,lisker09, toloba11, kormendy12 .

gmw_00005191.name: GRB 050922C
gmw_00005191.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 050922C is a high-redshift system located at a redshift of approximately 2.199 [arXiv:0809.3247, arXiv:astro-ph/0701558]. It exhibits a high neutral hydrogen column density [arXiv:0809.3247] and is characterized by low, undersolar metallicity [arXiv:0809.3247, arXiv:astro-ph/0701558].

sources.summary: The GRB 050922C host sightline has a strong offset component, multi-component absorption over 280 km/s, high neutral column density, low metallicity, and no detected high-velocity components.

quotes.quote: GRB 050922C =2.1990 A single, strong component in , , \ and \ is detected at 65  \ relative to . \ is also detected in this component in both 1854.716 and 1862.789. Our best-fit to the \ line has b =24 2  ; for the \ line, we find b =16 3  .

\ and \ show a complex series of at least six components spread over 280  , but with no evidence for a negative-velocity wing. The \ and \ data are blended and not shown on Figure 3. This GRB shows a high log N ( ) value of 21.60, and a low metallicity of 2.0 Pr08b . This is the only GRB afterglow spectrum in our sample in which no HV \ components are detected.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes GRB050922C through host-galaxy absorption, reporting four absorption systems and undersolar host-galaxy abundances, while noting uncertainty from hydrogen columns and dust depletion.

quotes.quote: We focus here on the study of the local, GRB surrounding medium and on the analysis of the absorption lines produced by the gas in the host galaxy throughout observations of three GRBs: GRB050730, GRB050922C and GRB060418.
The GRB050922C afterglow was observed 3.5 hours after the trigger. We find four main absorption systems at z=2.199, 2.077, 2.008 and 1.9985.

The metallicity of the GRB host galaxies is computed using the hydrogen absorption features. We find undersolar abundances for our GRBs, namely, Z_ odot 10^ 3 10^ 2 . However, Z_ odot can be underestimated since the H column presents large uncertainties and dust depletion has not been taken into account.

gmw_00005192.name: GRB 060607
gmw_00005192.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 060607 is located at a redshift of z=3.0749 [arXiv:0809.3247, arXiv:2006.09377]. It is an extremely faint galaxy with a very low neutral hydrogen column density [arXiv:0809.3247, arXiv:2006.09377] and is undetected in deep Hubble Space Telescope imaging [arXiv:2006.09377].

sources.summary: The GRB 060607 host sightline differs from the others, with compact multi-component high-ion absorption, non-detections in several ions, a weak wing, low neutral column, and a separate high-velocity absorber.

quotes.quote: GRB 060607 =3.0749 The high ions in the GRB 060607 spectrum behave differently than in the other afterglow spectra. The , , and \ profiles show a four-component absorber centered at \ and covering only 100  \ of total width. However, \ is not detected at ; we derive a 3 upper limit of log N ( ) < 12.70 in the velocity range 140 to 50  , where \ absorption is seen. The , , and \ data are blended near \ and are of no use. \ shows a non-detection.

The \ profiles also show a weak (low optical depth) wing extending from 60 to 120  . This GRB has an extremely low \ column density, log N ( )=16.8 Pr08b , 3.5 orders of magnitude lower than the DLA columns seen in five of the other six cases. A HV absorber seen in \ and \ is centered at 1 850  \ relative to , with nearby low-ion absorption present in \ and (Figure 5).

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 060607A host as an extremely faint z=3.0749 LyC-leaking galaxy with very low column density, no HST-detected galaxy, and uncertain interloper contamination.

quotes.quote: GRB 060607A occurred in a galaxy at z=3.0749 . It has the lowest column density observed among the LGRB afterglow sample ( =16.95 0.03 ; Tanvir2019 ) and, like GRB 191004B, the only metal absorption lines at the GRB redshift are those of C iv and Si iv . The VLT/UVES spectrum of its afterglow shows a very complex line of sight, rich in intervening absorbers Fox2008, Prochaska2008, Fynbo2009 .

There is evidence of LyC emission over 16 below the LL, likely absorbed blueward from an intervening system. It is not firmly possible to exclude that the apparent LyC emission is due to an interloper. Nonetheless, deep HST-F775W imaging observations are available for the GRB 060607A field (see Blanchard2016 ) and no galaxy is detected at m_ AB >28.9 mag over a radius of 15 kpc from the afterglow position.

gmw_00005193.name: GRB 071031
gmw_00005193.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 071031 is located at a redshift of z = 2.6922 [arXiv:0809.3247]. Its sightline exhibits strong high-ion absorption, including a blueward absorption wing and three high-velocity high-ion components [arXiv:0809.3247].

sources.summary: The GRB 071031 host sightline contains strong high-ion absorption, saturated lines, a blueward absorption wing, related ion profiles, and three high-velocity high-ion components.

quotes.quote: GRB 071031 =2.6922 A strong component in , , , and \ is detected at . The least-saturated high-ion line, , shows a narrow line width of b ( )=11 2  , implying log T < 5.08 for the gas containing the N ^ +4 ions, regardless of ionization mechanism. No information is available on the line widths of \ and \ at \ because the absorption in these lines is saturated.

A notable feature of the , , , and (at lower S/N) the \ profiles is the wing extending blueward from 0  \ down to 160  . A component at 115  \ is superimposed on the wing. The \ and \ profiles follow one another closely, suggesting these two ions form in the same physical regions of gas. However, the slope d N_ a (v) /d v is shallower for \ than for \ and \ in the range 90 to 50  \ (see 4.3). Three strong components of HV high-ion absorption are seen (Figure 5), at velocities of 560, 510, and 370 .

gmw_00005194.name: GRB 080310
gmw_00005194.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 080310 is a high-redshift system at a redshift of z=2.42743 [arXiv:1207.6102] that exhibits a low neutral hydrogen column density [arXiv:0809.3247, arXiv:1207.6102]. Its interstellar medium shows time-variable absorption lines [arXiv:1207.6102] and chemical enrichment suggesting episodic massive star formation [arXiv:1207.6102].

sources.summary: The GRB 080310 host sightline shows strong high-ion absorption, two separated components, an extended negative-velocity wing, a narrow high-velocity absorber, and measured DLA column density and metallicity.

quotes.quote: GRB 080310 =2.4274 Strong high-ion absorption is seen in , , , , and \ centered within 50  \ of . The \ profile shows two components separated by 50 3  \ and with b values of 18 3 and 19 3  .

Similarly the \ 1062.662 profile is too noisy to measure a reliable column, but we can measure a lower limit of log N ( ) > 14.77 over the velocity range 70 to 30  . Combining this with the constraint log N ( ^* ) < 14.38 (3 ) from the non-detection of ^*   1072.973, we find N ( ^* )/ N ( ) < 0.41.

The , , and \ profiles show a clear negative-velocity absorption-line wing extending from 120 to 260  . This is the most extended absorption-line wing in our sample. Finally, a HV absorber at 1400  \ is seen (see Figure 5) with two \ components, one of which is very narrow ( b =4 2  ). For this GRB-DLA we derive an \ column density log N ( )=18.80 0.10 and a metallicity [ O/H ] of 1.39 0.10.

sources.summary: The paper studies the GRB 080310 host-galaxy ISM, finding low neutral hydrogen, time-variable absorption, unusual Fe and Cr enrichment, and evidence for episodic massive star formation.

quotes.quote: We analyse high-resolution near-UV and optical spectra of the afterglow of , obtained with the Very Large Telescope Ultraviolet and Visual Echelle Spectrograph (VLT/UVES), to investigate the circumburst environment and the interstellar medium of the gamma-ray burst (GRB) host galaxy. The VLT rapid-response mode (RRM) enabled the observations to start only 13 minutes after the Swift trigger and a series of four exposures to be collected before dawn. A low neutral-hydrogen column-density (log N ( ) =18.7 ) is measured at the host-galaxy redshift of z=2.42743 .

Our time-series of high-resolution VLT/UVES spectra of the afterglow has revealed the unique features of the ISM of the GRB host galaxy. We have reported the detection of several resonance absorption lines commonly observed in the ISM, as well as fine-structure lines sometimes associated with GRB afterglows. In the case of , both the ground-state and the fine-structure lines vary with time.

The strong overabundance of iron compared to silicon and carbon also suggests that there has been negligible recent star formation along the line-of-sight. The occurrence of the GRB then indicates that there has been episodic massive-star formation in the GRB region.

gmw_00005195.name: Object 1051264
gmw_00005195.overview: Object 1051264 is a variable nuclear point source located at the center of an edge-on disk galaxy [arXiv:2403.16138]. The source has a photometric redshift of 1.887 and is identified as a probable active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2403.16138].

sources.summary: 1051264 is a variable nuclear point source in an edge-on disk galaxy; host subtraction and dual-filter variability make the paper identify it as a probable AGN at photometric redshift 1.887.

quotes.quote: Object 1051264 This source lies in the center of an edge-on disk galaxy. Image subtraction shows the object has decreased substantially in brightness between 2009 and 2012, although the magnitude of variation is formally small ( 0.08 0.025  mag) by global (Kron) photometry because of dilution by the host galaxy. This is only a 2 variable, but variability is confirmed in both F105W and F160W imaging. The source is obviously point-like after the host is subtracted, and is located at the nucleus of the galaxy suggesting probable AGN activity. The source is identified by Rafelski.2015 and has a photometric redshift of 1.887.

gmw_00005196.name: Object 1052126
gmw_00005196.overview: Object 1052126 is an active galactic nucleus with a spectroscopic redshift of 3.1905 [arXiv:2403.16138]. The object exhibits high-ionization emission lines and has varied in brightness, brightening by 0.53 magnitudes between 2009 and 2012 before dimming by 0.50 magnitudes between 2012 and 2023 [arXiv:2403.16138].

sources.summary: 1052126 is a variable z=3.1905 AGN with high-ionization lines, broad permitted emission, variable luminosity, and an absolute magnitude comparable to an L-star galaxy.

quotes.quote: Object 1052126 This object has a spectroscopic redshift of 3.1905 from VLT/MUSE. It is identified as a strong emitter of 1640, 1550, and 1883, as well as other high-ionization lines Bacon.2023 and is clearly an AGN. It is comparably bright, with m_ F140 =24.1 and has varied by 0.53 and +0.50 magnitudes from 2009--2012 and 2012--2023, respectively (brightening then dimming). The flux in the variable component alone amounts to an absolute magnitude of 21.3 , corresponding approximately to the luminosity of an \ galaxy.

The F150W filter shows an obvious line of [ ] 3869 , but more interestingly there is a weak line at _ obs =14400  A. This is identified as the [ ] 3426 \ line, which requires a source of photons with h >97  eV or dense coronal gas with temperature T>10^5  K. This can only be produced by an AGN. A strong \ feature is also identified in the F115W filter.

gmw_00005197.name: Object 1052123
gmw_00005197.overview: Object 1052123 is a point-like, high-redshift variable source with a photometric redshift estimated between 6.44 and 6.74 [arXiv:2403.16138]. Its observed variability timescale and constant color suggest that the source is likely an active galactic nucleus rather than an extreme supernova [arXiv:2403.16138].

sources.summary: 1052123 is the strongest z>6 variable source, point-like and color-consistent with a Lyman break; its variability timescale leads the paper to favor an AGN over an extreme supernova.

quotes.quote: Object 1052123 This is our highest significance variable source at z>6 . It is detected as variable in all three photometric bands and is visible in the pair-subtracted images of Figure  fig:z7phot . Its light curve is shown in Figure  fig:lightcurve . It is point-like, and brightens between 2009 and 2012 (both F105W and F160W) by about 0.3 magnitudes, with a very similar change in brightness detected at both wavelengths implying a constant color of the variable source.

The source is assigned a photometric redshift of 6.44 by Rafelski.2015 and 6.74 in the JADES redshift catalog, and we lean toward the higher value because the spectral break must lie within the F105W filter. Assuming this redshift, and that the entire variable component can be attributed to an AGN, it would have an equivalent absolute magnitude of 19.3 AB.
We therefore favor an AGN over an extreme supernova interpretation.

gmw_00005198.name: Object 1052156
gmw_00005198.overview: Object 1052156 is a high-redshift galaxy at a spectroscopic redshift of 6.23 [arXiv:2403.16138]. It is classified as an active galactic nucleus candidate based on slow variability detected in the F105W and F160W filters [arXiv:2403.16138].

sources.summary: 1052156 is a z=6.23 variable source detected in two filters; the paper treats the slow variability as genuine and includes it among high-redshift AGN candidates.

quotes.quote: Object 1052156 This object is identified as variable in the F105W and F160W filters (2009--2012), but is not found to be variable in the later epoch. The variability is clear in the pair-subtracted images, and if all the variability can be attributed to a single new source, its equivalent magnitude would be 18.5 . This magnitude is also consistent with the object being a SN, but given the change in brightness of 0.2 mags in five year, seems implausible (using the same argumentation as for 1052123). The combined significance across the two filters is only 3.5 in this case, but even at 28 mag there is still not an enormous parent sample of sources, and we do not expect to find one with magnitude difference that is 3.5 outside its distribution from pure chance.
This source has a spectroscopic redshift of 6.23 Bunker.2023 , as well as various photometric redshift estimates consistent with this.
Based upon their absolute magnitudes, which are measured at a rest wavelength of 1300 , we believe these variable sources to be AGN.

gmw_00005199.name: Object 101159
gmw_00005199.overview: Object 101159 is a point-like, high-redshift variable source associated with three faint, diffuse clumps [arXiv:2403.16138]. It was visible in 2009 but faded by 2012, and it is considered a likely active galactic nucleus that outshines its host galaxy [arXiv:2403.16138].

sources.summary: 101159 is a point-like high-redshift variable candidate associated with faint diffuse JADES clumps; it faded after 2009, may outshine its host, and is included among likely AGN candidates.

quotes.quote: Object 101159 The final object, 101159, is a remarkable variable source. It is clearly identified by the image subtraction method as a point-like variable source that was visible in 2009, but not in 2012 or 2023. It is not visible in the ACS optical imaging obtained in 2006, implying a possible high redshift. It is clearly detected in the NIRCam/F277W image from JADES, which is the deepest in the JADES survey. The source likely comprises three diffuse clumps within the 1 \ region centered on the coordinates of the transient source, each of which is particularly faint, with magnitudes around 29 in the F277W filter.

The source could be a SLSN or an AGN, but in either case it outshines the host galaxy, which is only detectable by JWST at later epochs in redder images. We have verified that the source is visible in a deep stack of all the JADES imaging at NIR wavelengths, and invisible in a stack of the ACS optical filters again suggesting the object lies at high- z .
Based upon their absolute magnitudes, which are measured at a rest wavelength of 1300 , we believe these variable sources to be AGN.

gmw_00005200.name: G07-1
gmw_00005200.overview: G07-1 is an emission-line galaxy that exhibits strong emission lines in its spectrum [arXiv:1003.0693]. The galaxy may host an active galactic nucleus, which could contribute to its observed line fluxes and affect star formation rate estimates [arXiv:1003.0693].

sources.summary: G07-1 is an emission-line galaxy near the absorber sightline with strong lines; the paper reports uncertain metallicity and possible active-nucleus contamination of its star-formation estimate.

quotes.quote: The two galaxies with b=61.3 kpc (slit 1; hereafter G07-1 ) and b=36.1 kpc (slit 2; G07-2 ) to the north-east of the sightline both exhibit strong emission lines of , , \ and \ at redshifts similar (within 50 250 ) to that of the absorber; see figure Fig:0747 . Their spectra are shown in figure Fig:0747specs . We also detect 3869 (6828 \ observed) and tentatively identify 3426 (6045 \ observed) in the spectrum of G07-1 .

For G07-1, we were unable to use the iterative method as our measured value of R_ 23 is unphysically high -- suggesting that the value we determine for \ in that spectrum may indeed be inaccurate. We thus report for G07-1 a value of [ O/H ] from R_ 23 and / \ using the prescription of McGaugh (1991), though we caution that this may not be accurate. Finally, we note that the possible detections of \ in the spectrum of G07-1 indicates a possibility that an active nucleus is contributing to the observed line fluxes. We consider typical ranges of / \ (e.g., Netzer, 1990) for Seyfert 1 galaxies to estimate the possible AGN contamination, and conclude our SFR estimate may be overestimated by 10 to 70 per cent in G07-1 . This possible contamination is small compared to the uncertainty due to the unknown dust correction, and in any case does not significantly affect our conclusions.

gmw_00005201.name: G07-2
gmw_00005201.overview: G07-2 is an optically bright emission-line galaxy with a rest-frame g-band luminosity of 0.9 L* [arXiv:1003.0693]. It exhibits typical colors for a bright galaxy and shows clear evidence of ongoing star formation [arXiv:1003.0693].

sources.summary: G07-2 is an emission-line galaxy near the absorber; the paper describes it as optically bright, typical in color for bright galaxies, and clearly star-forming.

quotes.quote: The two galaxies with b=61.3 kpc (slit 1; hereafter G07-1 ) and b=36.1 kpc (slit 2; G07-2 ) to the north-east of the sightline both exhibit strong emission lines of , , \ and \ at redshifts similar (within 50 250 ) to that of the absorber; see figure Fig:0747 . Their spectra are shown in figure Fig:0747specs . We also detect 3869 (6828 \ observed) and tentatively identify 3426 (6045 \ observed) in the spectrum of G07-1 .

The z z_ abs galaxies are bright, with rest-frame g band luminosities 2.4 L^* and 0.9 L^* for G07-1 and G07-2 respectively, and 1.8 L^* and 0.3 L^* for G14-1 and G14-2, where L^* is given at similar redshift by Gabasch et al. \ (2004). Comparing the rest-frame u-g colours to local galaxies in the SDSS from Blanton et al. \ (2003) Although the magnitudes in Blanton et al. \ are k corrected to z=0.1 , the effect on the colours is negligible. , we find that G07-1 and G07-2 are typical of bright galaxies, while G14-1 and G14-2 are considerably bluer than most bright galaxies, suggesting SEDs dominated by young OB stars. While we lack accurate dust-corrected SFRs estimates, all four of the galaxies clearly exhibit significant ongoing star formation.

gmw_00005202.name: G14-1
gmw_00005202.overview: G14-1 is a bright, blue emission-line galaxy located near the Q1417+011 absorber [arXiv:1003.0693]. It is a relatively low-mass system characterized by a high specific star formation rate and is currently undergoing a starburst episode [arXiv:1003.0693].

sources.summary: G14-1 is a blue emission-line galaxy near the Q1417+011 absorber; the paper finds high specific star formation and classifies it as undergoing a starburst.

quotes.quote: Also similar to the previous field, the two next-brightest galaxies in the field (hereafter G14-1 and G14-2) exhibit strong , , \ and \ emission lines with redshifts similar (within 200 350 ; see figure Fig:1417 ) to z_ abs . Both may also exhibit 3869 . The impact parameters are b=58.3 kpc and b=28.7 kpc, respectively. Their spectra are displayed in figure Fig:1417specs and the absolute magnitudes and line-fluxes presented in table 2.

To summarise our observational results: (i) each field contains a pair of emission-line galaxies with z_ gal z_ abs , at impact parameters b 30 kpc and b 60 kpc to the absorption sightlines; (ii) G07-1 and G07-2 appear to be fairly massive, bright galaxies; (iii) G14-1 and G14-2 are also bright but less massive and have very blue rest-frame u-b colours; (iv) all four galaxies have high sSFRs and have metallicities typical for their mass; (v) G07-1 and G07-2 are roughly 1 Gyr removed from a starburst phase, though both exhibit ongoing star formation; and (vi) G14-1 and G14-2 appear to currently be undergoing a starburst episode.

gmw_00005203.name: G14-2
gmw_00005203.overview: G14-2 is a blue emission-line galaxy located near the Q1417+011 absorber [arXiv:1003.0693]. The galaxy has a high specific star formation rate and is currently undergoing a starburst episode [arXiv:1003.0693].

sources.summary: G14-2 is a blue emission-line galaxy near the Q1417+011 absorber; the paper finds high specific star formation and classifies it as undergoing a starburst.

quotes.quote: Also similar to the previous field, the two next-brightest galaxies in the field (hereafter G14-1 and G14-2) exhibit strong , , \ and \ emission lines with redshifts similar (within 200 350 ; see figure Fig:1417 ) to z_ abs . Both may also exhibit 3869 . The impact parameters are b=58.3 kpc and b=28.7 kpc, respectively. Their spectra are displayed in figure Fig:1417specs and the absolute magnitudes and line-fluxes presented in table 2.

To summarise our observational results: (i) each field contains a pair of emission-line galaxies with z_ gal z_ abs , at impact parameters b 30 kpc and b 60 kpc to the absorption sightlines; (ii) G07-1 and G07-2 appear to be fairly massive, bright galaxies; (iii) G14-1 and G14-2 are also bright but less massive and have very blue rest-frame u-b colours; (iv) all four galaxies have high sSFRs and have metallicities typical for their mass; (v) G07-1 and G07-2 are roughly 1 Gyr removed from a starburst phase, though both exhibit ongoing star formation; and (vi) G14-1 and G14-2 appear to currently be undergoing a starburst episode.

gmw_00005204.name: NGC 1210
gmw_00005204.overview: NGC 1210 is a shell galaxy [arXiv:1101.0898] that exhibits complex tidal debris, including an extended far-ultraviolet tidal tail [arXiv:0909.4020]. The presence of neutral hydrogen gas suggests a wet merger [arXiv:0909.4020], which likely triggered an accretion episode that rejuvenated the galaxy's nucleus 2 to 4 Gyr ago [arXiv:0909.4020, arXiv:1101.0898].

sources.summary: GALEX imaging shows NGC 1210 has a dominant FUV tidal tail, UV/H I-correlated structures, H I gas, and a nucleus likely rejuvenated by a 2-4 Gyr accretion episode.

quotes.quote: The GALEX Near UV (NUV) and Far UV (FUV) images of and NGC 1210 show complex tidal tails and debris structures. The far UV morphology of both galaxies appears so different from the optical morphology that the early-type classification may not apply. In both GALEX bands, the polar ring of is the dominant feature, whereas an extended tidal tail dominates the FUV bands of NGC 1210. In and NGC 1210, there is a strong correlation between structures detected in the FUV and NUV bands and in HI.
The presence of HI in both and NGC 1210 argues in favour of wet mergers.
Our models suggest that also the nuclei of NGC 1210 and NGC 5329 could have been rejuvenated by an accretion episode about 2-4 Gyr ago.

sources.summary: NGC 1210 is described as a shell galaxy whose GALEX FUV flux indicates accretion and whose rejuvenated nucleus traces star formation from an event 2-4 Gyr ago.

quotes.quote: NGC 1210 is a beautiful example of shell galaxy. In Figure fig4 , the GALEX FUV flux distribution provides clear evidence for an accretion event. Marino09 suggest that also the nucleus of NGC 1210 has been rejuvenated and the accretion episode, that triggered the star formation, should be dated between 2 and 4 Gyr ago.

gmw_00005205.name: NGC 3509
gmw_00005205.overview: NGC 3509 is a perturbed galaxy featuring a single, well-defined nucleus surrounded by dust lanes [arXiv:astro-ph/0309377, arXiv:0708.2645]. It is bound to a nearby companion galaxy by a low-surface-brightness stellar stream that spans a projected length of approximately 92 kpc [arXiv:1102.2905].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a low-surface-brightness stream at NGC 3509, measures it at about 92 kpc projected length, and argues the companion is bound to the host.

quotes.quote: NGC 3509 The first clear stream we found was at NGC 3509. hsttoomre analyzed this galaxy with the Hubble space telescope and concluded, that its shape is not the result of an ongoing major merger and mentioned that they have made deep images of the galaxy, and write about the small (dwarf) galaxy below NGC 3509 that " This possible companion also has faint low surface brightness features pointing both toward and away from NGC 3509 ", though they have not analyzed it further. The sword-like, low surface brightness feature, detected using our method, is shown in Fig. 3509 .

The stream was measured to have a length of 182 2", which translates to a projected length of 92^ +5.3 _ 4.7 Kpc. This companion galaxy has, according to the SDSS spectral analysis, a very similar redshift as NGC 3509. It has a redshift of z = 0.02544 0.000023 According to NED and the companion has a redshift of z = 0.0257 0.0001. These redshifts translate to velocities of 7530 km/s for the host galaxy and 7606 km/s for the companion, which yields a velocity difference of 76 km/s.
This definitely means that both galaxies are very close together and that the companion is highly likely bound to the host galaxy.

sources.summary: NGC 3509 is interpreted as a perturbed system with bright blue stellar associations, a single nucleus, H emission, and evidence favoring minor merger or interaction over a major merger.

quotes.quote: NGC 3509 The second-most distant galaxy of this sample, NGC 3509, falls in the middle of the Toomre Sequence. The sketch of this system by tmr77 suggests that he envisioned a large tail curving to the northwest and a shorter tail extending to the southwest. Deep ground-based CCD imagery obtained by one of us (JEH) suggests that the southeastern feature is not a tail, but rather the bright ridge of an inclined disk. There is a very bright rectangular-shaped feature at the head of the southeastern feature, directly south of a central bulge see also arp66 , which may have been interpreted as the bulge of the second system.

Our HST/WFPC2 broad-band imagery shows that this region resolves into a number of bright, blue stellar associations (Figure f:n3509 ), and we believe it is much more likely to be a collection of bright star forming regions within the perturbed disk than the nucleus of a second system. The extremely blue colors of this region ( V-I colors around 0.5; the tip of this region is seen at the bottom center of Figure f:n3509 ) are in spectacular contrast to the rest of the galaxy (typical V-I values from 1.1 to 1.4), and indicate active star formation. In our images we can clearly identify a single relatively undisturbed nucleus, surrounded by a swirl of dust lanes (center of Figure f:n3509 ).

sources.summary: NGC 3509 is described as having a single resolved nucleus, possible past minor merger evidence, and suppressed star formation relative to other mid-stage Toomre mergers.

quotes.quote: The nuclear region of NGC 3509 looks rather inconspicuous, with only a single, well-defined nucleus (Figure f:thirtyfivezeroninevk ), appearing as a concentrated but clearly resolved source. As noted already in Paper I, NGC 3509 has possibly experienced a minor merger in the past, as only one clear tidal tail is visible in deep optical images cf. arp66 . The relatively low value of the IRAS flux density ratio at 60 m over 100 m ( f_ 60 /f_ 100 0.33) also favors a scenario in which star formation has been suppressed compared to the other mid-stage mergers in the Toomre sequence, which show more vigorous signs of recent or ongoing star formation (cf. Figures 7 and 8 of Paper I).

gmw_00005206.name: PGC 1421380
gmw_00005206.overview: PGC 1421380 is a galaxy projected below NGC 4206 [arXiv:1102.2905]. It is surrounded by a verified stellar stream-like feature that is visible in both SDSS and Hubble Space Telescope imaging [arXiv:1102.2905].

sources.summary: The paper describes a stream-like feature around PGC 1421380 visible in both SDSS and HST data, with image-sampling differences but a verified stream.

quotes.quote: PGC 1421380 Another curious feature we found in the MAST database is the feature at PGC 1421380, shown in Fig. PGC1421380comp , which lies projected below NGC 4206. This stream like feature surrounding PGC 1421380 is also visible in the HST image, although the preview images are not perfectly calibrated. Curiously, in the SDSS data, the stream looks like it starts at the bottom of the galaxy and streams clockwise up to the small source, but in the HST image, it looks like it starts on top of the galaxy and streams counterclockwise, not even connecting to the small source. The reason for this is probably the lower sampling of the SDSS image, which happens to be in an area, where noise diminishes the SNR. But nonetheless, the stream is considered verified.

gmw_00005207.name: NGC 7711
gmw_00005207.overview: NGC 7711 is a seemingly isolated lenticular galaxy located at a distance of approximately 55.5 Mpc [arXiv:1102.2905]. It features a disk extension interpreted as tidal streams from a smaller dwarf galaxy that has completed at least two orbits [arXiv:1102.2905].

sources.summary: NGC 7711 is presented as an isolated S0 with a disk extension interpreted as dwarf-galaxy tidal streams, including a 19.4 kpc outer stream and central photometric contrast.

quotes.quote: NGC 7711 Another interesting finding is the S0 according to NED galaxy NGC 7711 which lies at the J2000 coordinates RA = 23h 35m 39.4s, DEC = +15 ^ 17m 7s, at a distance of 55.5^ +2.9 _ 2.6 Mpc. This seemingly isolated galaxy seems to be not mentioned in any obvious way in literature. We found in the stacked image, that there is an extension of the disc with a gap in the middle. It looks to us like this extensions are in fact tidal streams of a smaller dwarf galaxy which completed at least two orbits around NGC 7711. This is supported by a bright spot which is very close to the disk of NGC 7711, so close that it could be interpreted as part of the disk, which would then appear very asymmetrically.
The outer stream has an extent of 72" 2" from the d25 line, which translates to about 19.4 kpc.

As Tab. 7711phot shows, point 5 has the lowest surface brightness of all points, which clearly means that this in fact is a gap between the inner and outer stream. Point 9 has the highest value, which is what one would expect, since point 9 lies on the center of the galaxy.

gmw_00005208.name: J1104+59
gmw_00005208.overview: J1104+59 is a candidate Type II active galactic nucleus identified by its high-excitation emission lines [arXiv:1404.0677]. The galaxy exhibits tidal tails and other debris indicative of a recent major or minor merger [arXiv:1404.0677].

sources.summary: J1104+59 is treated as a candidate Type II AGN with high-excitation line evidence, merger debris, and a substantial compact light fraction whose origin remains ambiguous.

quotes.quote: Based on the strength of the high-excitation emission lines, [ O III ] and [ Ne V ] , we consider the following three galaxies as candidate Type II AGN: J1104+59 (ID 3), J1506+54 (ID 4), and J1713+28 (ID 8). Both J1104+59 and J1713+28 have high [ O III ] /H ratios relative to our purely star forming comparison sample (Fig. fig:bpt ). However, the line ratios could still be consistent with star formation given the unusual physical conditions in the galaxies kewley13a,kewley13b .

Tidal tails and other debris indicative of a recent major or minor merger are evident in two-thirds of the sample (J0826+43, J1104+59, J1506+54, J1558+39, J1613+28, J1713+28, J2118+00, and J1634+46; see Figs. fig:cutouts and fig:smoothed_images and Fig. fig:data_fits in the Appendix).

In five of these galaxies (J0826+43, J0944+09, J1104+59, J1506+54, J1506+61), the PSF light fraction is substantial (40 -- 60 ), and thus we would expect broad Mg II and H emission lines to be visible if the PSF light were due to an unobscured AGN with a normal UV-optical spectrum.

gmw_00005209.name: J1506+54
gmw_00005209.overview: J1506+54 is a compact starburst galaxy that hosts a very young stellar population [arXiv:2106.02366, arXiv:2102.11287]. The galaxy exhibits strong [Ne V] emission, which may be powered by extreme starburst conditions or a subdominant, obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1404.0677, arXiv:2102.11287].

sources.summary: J1506+54 has very strong [Ne V], possible obscured AGN activity, and mid-IR luminosity dominated by star formation rather than the AGN.

quotes.quote: The line is very strongly detected ( 10 ) in one of these galaxies (J1506+54) and is only weakly detected ( 4 ) in two other cases (J1104+59 and J1713+28). The significant detection of [ Ne V ] in the two latter galaxies is consistent with the finding in section:BPT that these galaxies are most likely to host obscured AGN activity. However, assessing the [ Ne V ] contribution in these latter two cases could be controversial and extremely challenging: 1) the significance and luminosity of the [ Ne V ] line is sensitive to the fit of the galaxy continuum model, which has an additional uncertainty not encapsulated above that is very difficult to quantify; 2) the measured strength of a line near the sensitivity limit can be overestimated rola94 ; and 3) without an AGN, very young stellar populations containing Wolf-Rayet and other O-stars (less than a few Myr) can produce some detectable [ Ne V ] because much higher fractions of high-energy photons are produced abel08 . Given these challenges applied to our unusual galaxies, we only analyze the very strong detection of [ Ne V ] in J1506+54 in detail.

While there is ambiguity about the origin of the [ Ne V ] in J1506+54, we can make the assumption that it traces obscured AGN activity and ask whether the AGN would be bolometrically dominant. Since both the AGN and starburst are heavily obscured, we consider their relative contributions to the 12 m luminosity. We adopt L_ 12 m,AGN = L_ bol,AGN /9 from richards06 and estimate L_ 12 m,AGN = 1.4 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 for J1506+54. Compared to the observed value (Table table:other_info ), this implies that roughly 11 of the galaxy's mid-IR luminosity is powered by the AGN and 89 is powered by star formation.

sources.summary: The paper presents J1506 as a young compact starburst with [NeV] emission that may imply a Compton-thick AGN but could also be powered by extreme starburst conditions.

quotes.quote: J1506 exhibits a clear ( 10 ) [ NeV ] 3426 detection; this ion has a high ionization potential and is commonly used to trace AGN activity e.g., gil10 . sel14 estimate a ratio of the X-ray to [ NeV ] luminosity L _x /L _ [ NeV ] = 4.9, implying a Compton-thick AGN (N _H > 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 ). Under the assumption of the emission line being produced by an obscured AGN, sel14 find that the AGN would contribute 10 of the mid-IR luminosity. However, [ NeV ] can also be powered by a very young (less than a few Myr) stellar population containing Wolf-Rayet and O stars abe08 . J1506 has a very young ( 3 Myr) stellar population and the highest _ SFR in our sample.
Therefore, the observed [ NeV ] could be produced by the extreme conditions of the central starburst sel14 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1506+5402 as a compact starburst galaxy with a weak X-ray detection, starburst-consistent X-ray/IR properties, and emission-line ratios consistent with either an extreme starburst or subdominant AGN.

quotes.quote: There are two galaxies with weak X-ray detections (J1506+5402 and J1613+2834, 4 X-ray counts each) from sel14 that indicate L_X 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 and four galaxies with X-ray upper limits (J0826+4305, J0944+0930, J1558+3957, J2140+1209) that imply L_X<10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 . All of these galaxies are consistent with the relationship between X-ray luminosity and mid-infrared luminosity for starburst galaxies asm11,min14,sel14 . One of the galaxies with a weak X-ray detection (J1506+5402) also has a clear detection of 3426 and an emission-line ratio / H 1 , which is the highest ratio in the sample and consistent with a composite BPT classification bal81,kew01,kau03,sel14 . These \ and \ emission lines could be produced either by an extreme ( t 3 Myr) starburst or an AGN that contributes 10 of the mid-infrared continuum dia12,gea13,sel14 .

gmw_00005210.name: J1506+61
gmw_00005210.overview: J1506+61 is a merging system featuring two distinct, bright cores [arXiv:1404.0677]. The galaxy exhibits a substantial compact light fraction, and its Mg II absorption line is affected by strong emission line infilling [arXiv:1404.0677].

sources.summary: J1506+61 has Mg II emission infilling that biases its average outflow velocity, two bright merger-related cores, and a substantial compact light fraction.

quotes.quote: In J1506+61, strong emission line in-filling of the Mg II absorption line causes the average velocity to be biased to larger negative values; the maximum velocity is less affected.

For the two galaxies with two distinct, clearly associated, bright cores, in the process of major or minor mergers, J1506+61 and J1713+28, we fit each core with a S e rsic profile. To accommodate the bright, compact cores of our galaxies, we also tried fitting each core with a S e rsic + PSF + sky model (again, all sky model parameters were frozen). In the case of the two galaxies with two distinct, clearly associated, bright cores, J1506+61 and J1713+28, we only added a PSF to the secondary core if there is a noticeable improvement to the fit (relatively large change in ^2/ ) and if the best-fit PSF magnitude was non-negligible (the PSF magnitude had to be 1 of the S e rsic magnitude).

In five of these galaxies (J0826+43, J0944+09, J1104+59, J1506+54, J1506+61), the PSF light fraction is substantial (40 -- 60 ), and thus we would expect broad Mg II and H emission lines to be visible if the PSF light were due to an unobscured AGN with a normal UV-optical spectrum.

gmw_00005211.name: J1558+39
gmw_00005211.overview: J1558+39 is a compact galaxy system that exhibits a highly disturbed morphology [arXiv:1404.0677]. It features very bright inner tidal structures and tidal debris indicative of a recent merger, suggesting the system is currently undergoing unrelaxed nuclear coalescence [arXiv:1404.0677].

sources.summary: J1558+39 is described as a disturbed, compact system with tidal debris, very bright inner tidal features, and an unrelaxed nuclear-coalescence morphology.

quotes.quote: Tidal tails and other debris indicative of a recent major or minor merger are evident in two-thirds of the sample (J0826+43, J1104+59, J1506+54, J1558+39, J1613+28, J1713+28, J2118+00, and J1634+46; see Figs. fig:cutouts and fig:smoothed_images and Fig. fig:data_fits in the Appendix).

Five of the nine narrow-line galaxies have PSF fractions in the range of 40 -- 60 , while the remaing four have PSF fractions less than 20 . Notably, galaxies in this later group show indications of having not reached complete nuclear coalescence including double nuclei (J1713+28), elliptical inner light profiles with large GALFIT residuals that may be consistent with dual nuclei slightly below the resolution limit (J1613+28 and J2118+00), and very bright inner tidal features (J1558+39, J1713+28, and J2118+00).
All of the galaxies have very compact light profiles. J1558+39, J1613+28, J1713+28, and J2118+00 appear to be in the midst of nuclear coalescence and have not relaxed yet.

gmw_00005212.name: J1613+28
gmw_00005212.overview: J1613+28 is a compact galaxy merger remnant that appears to be in the midst of nuclear coalescence [arXiv:1404.0677]. It exhibits inner light profiles that may be consistent with unresolved dual nuclei, and its X-ray emission is likely driven by an intense starburst [arXiv:1404.0677].

sources.summary: J1613+28 is a compact merger remnant candidate with Chandra four-count emission consistent with starburst X-rays, possible unresolved dual nuclei, and the sample's fastest outflow.

quotes.quote: For the galaxies with 4-count X-ray detections (J1506+54, J1613+28, J2118+00), the IR-based SFRs from restframe WISE data are 250, 230, and 130 M _ yr ^ 1 (see Table table:other_info and section:WISE for details). The predicted X-ray luminosities from star formation for these three galaxies are (L_X( erg s ^ 1 )) = 41.9, 41.9, and 41.6, respectively. Given that these numbers agree with the observations within the error bars and that the spectra are relatively soft (see section:data_red_Chandra ), we conclude that the X-rays from all of the galaxies except the two Type I AGN (J1359+51 and J1634+46) are likely from intense starbursts, not heavily obscured SMBHs.

Five of the nine narrow-line galaxies have PSF fractions in the range of 40 -- 60 , while the remaing four have PSF fractions less than 20 . Notably, galaxies in this later group show indications of having not reached complete nuclear coalescence including double nuclei (J1713+28), elliptical inner light profiles with large GALFIT residuals that may be consistent with dual nuclei slightly below the resolution limit (J1613+28 and J2118+00), and very bright inner tidal features (J1558+39, J1713+28, and J2118+00).
